Highlights

  • Asana Unveils Operating System for Human-Agent Teams (2026-06-04): On June 4, 2026, Asana announced a new operating system architected for collaboration between human and AI agent teams. This platform enhances handoffs, supervision, and feedback, moving SaaS productivity standards towards more durable and adaptive human+agent orchestration.
    Strategically, this signals a sea change for enterprise workflows, raising client expectations for agentic functions within Workday and peer platforms. Deloitte teams should prioritize training and advisory on integrating these capabilities successfully.
  • Asana Launches AI-Powered Products for Human-Agent Workflows (2026-06-04): Asana's new product lineup further embeds AI into task management and action tracking, blending agentic automation directly into the project lifecycle. The innovations set new standards for interoperability and auditability, presenting both a challenge and an opportunity for Workday, SAP, and GSI partners.
    Implications involve upskilling, process redesign, and continuous improvement mandates for Deloitte and Workday clients as agentic labor becomes more integral to business operations.
  • Workday Introduces Agentic Contract Review & Redlining (2026-06-03): Workday has rolled out AI-driven contract review and autonomous redlining features, reducing manual review burdens and cycle times in legal, HR, and procurement. Native integration sets a new bar for Workday customers previously reliant on third-party legal AI.
    For Deloitte, this offers a prime opportunity to deepen automation services and compliance consulting as clients demand seamless, auditable self-service for contract workflows.

Competitive Watch

  • Asana: Major release of an AI operating system for orchestrating human-agent team tasks. This sets a higher bar for SaaS orchestration and agentic interoperability, forcing Workday and Deloitte to accelerate agentic playbook development and integration maturity.
  • Workday: Native agentic contract review and redlining tools close competitive gaps with legaltech rivals, establishing a new baseline for AI-enabled contract management. Deloitte's Workday practice must rapidly adapt skills and advisory approaches to leverage this momentum with clients.

Enablement

  • Agentic Contract AI Enablement: Use Workday's new contract review feature to accelerate contract workflows and reduce manual error, empowering Deloitte teams and clients to realize quick operational gains.
  • Human-Agent Team Orchestration: Draw from Asana's innovation to develop hybrid process playbooks for mixed teams, enabling higher productivity and clearer accountability.
  • Agentic Compliance Readiness: Build robust governance models on Workday’s contract AI to satisfy audit and regulatory standards as agentic actions expand.

Actions for Next Week

  • Assess current skills, readiness, and pipeline within Deloitte Workday teams regarding agentic workflow orchestration.
  • Host a client-facing enablement webinar on Workday’s agentic contract AI, focusing on practical adoption and change management.
  • Engage with HR, finance, and legal leads at priority Workday clients to identify and prioritize use cases for new automation capabilities.
  • Create updated benchmarking and advisory briefs comparing Asana and Workday orchestration capabilities for client enablement.

Risks & Mitigations

  • Unclear Agentic Accountability: Mitigate by instituting human-in-the-loop governance, audit trails, and policy updates specific to new autonomous workflows.
  • Technical and User Skills Gap: Accelerate upskilling and mentorship, prioritizing microlearning for key Deloitte and Workday stakeholders.
  • Organizational Change misalignment: Integrate change management and value-tracking early and continuously in agentic workflow deployments.

All Sources

  • Asana Unveils Operating System for Human-Agent Teams – Company Announcement - FT.com
  • Asana launches AI-powered products to help organizations manage human-agent work
  • Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog

RESEARCH SCOPE
Focus on Workday and its ecosystem — Workday HCM, Workday Extend, Workday Skills Cloud, Workday AI Marketplace, and all agentic or autonomous AI capabilities in SaaS solutions. 
Include:
- Workday product developments, patents, partnerships, and leadership commentary.
- Competitive analysis (SAP, Oracle, ServiceNow, UKG, ADP, Dayforce, etc.).
- GSI & boutique consultancy perspectives (Deloitte, Accenture, EY, KPMG, PwC, IBM, Capgemini, Infosys, TCS, Wipro, Cognizant, Kainos, Invisors, TopBloc, OSV, Alight, Mercer, etc.).
- Ecosystem vendors and hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP, Salesforce).
- Analyst & media insights (Gartner, Forrester, IDC, Reuters, Bloomberg, VentureBeat, etc.).
- Emerging risks, governance, and responsible AI developments affecting SaaS/HR technology.

WRITING EXPECTATIONS
- For each cited source, provide **multi-paragraph summaries (150–250 words)** that explain:
  - What was announced, found, or claimed.
  - Why it matters strategically, technically, and competitively.
  - Implications for Workday clients and Deloitte’s Workday practice.
- Incorporate synthesis across related articles (e.g., “Across multiple GSIs…” or “Several sources indicate…”).
- Prioritize insights, nuance, and context over mere headlines.
- Every key claim must cite a valid source URL from the provided SOURCE LIST.
- If no credible items are found in the last 24h, produce an honest “no significant updates” section.

FORMATTING RULES
- Return JSON only (no Markdown or prose outside the object).
- `html_body` must be valid, minimal HTML (<h2>, <h3>, <p>, <ul>, <li>, <a href="...">).
- Section order: Highlights; Competitive Watch; Enablement; Actions for Next Week; Risks & Mitigations; All Sources.
- Keep total HTML under 35 KB.
- `plain_text_body` must be readable text with visible URLs.

Return JSON ONLY in this exact shape (no code fences, no extra text):

{
  "type": "daily or weekly",
  "run_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "title": "Short headline",
  "priority_focus": "1–2 sentences on what matters most now",
  "highlights": [
    { "headline": "…", "why_it_matters": "…", "source_url": "https://…" }
  ],
  "competitive_watch": [
    { "competitor": "Name", "move": "…", "implication": "…" }
  ],
  "enablement": [
    { "skill": "Topic", "resource_url": "https://…", "90_day_outcome": "…" }
  ],
  "actions_next_week": ["…"],
  "risks": [
    { "risk": "…", "mitigation": "…" }
  ],
  "sources": [
    { "title": "…", "url": "https://…" }
  ],
  "html_body": "<h2>…</h2> (well-formatted HTML with <a href="https://…">links</a>; no scripts/external CSS)",
  "plain_text_body": "Text-only with visible URLs"
}

Parameters:
- Set "run_date" to TODAY_ET = 2026-06-04.
- For DAILY: set "type":"daily"; target ~1000 words; STRICTLY restrict to the last 24 hours (from 2026-06-03T08:29 to 2026-06-04T08:29); include publication dates in highlight text.
- For WEEKLY: set "type":"weekly"; target 2000–2500 words; restrict to 2026-05-28…2026-06-04; include <h3>What changed this week</h3> and publication dates in highlight text.
- Section order for html_body: Highlights; Competitive Watch; Enablement; Actions for Next Week; Risks & Mitigations; All Sources.
- Every item must include at least one absolute URL (https://…).
- Keep total HTML under ~25KB.

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS
- For every highlight and competitive_watch item, include a 2–3 paragraph detailed summary within the "why_it_matters" text.
- Where relevant, compare perspectives across multiple sources.
- Include a clear "so what" statement for Deloitte’s Workday practice.
- Ensure all cited URLs appear in the `sources` array and inside the `html_body` link text.
- Keep factual accuracy and avoid speculation.

        "content": "{\n  \"type\": \"daily\",\n  \"run_date\": \"2026-06-04\",\n  \"title\": \"Agentic AI for Human-Agent Teams, Legal and Productivity Gains—Spotlight on Enterprise SaaS\",\n  \"priority_focus\": \"Rapid expansion of agentic AI for human-agent collaboration, contract automation, and cross-enterprise orchestrations continues to redefine business processes in SaaS ecosystems. Immediate attention is warranted for Workday and Deloitte practices on leveraging AI-driven productivity tools, embedding compliance, and navigating new competitive behaviors.\",\n  \"highlights\": [\n    {\n      \"headline\": \"Asana Unveils Operating System for Human-Agent Teams (2026-06-04)\",\n      \"why_it_matters\": \"On June 4, 2026, Asana announced a transformative operating system designed for orchestrating collaboration between human workers and AI agents. Asana’s platform aims to integrate autonomous, agentic AI with task and project workflows, standardizing how organizations assign, monitor, and evaluate complex activities across mixed teams. This signals a new threshold for enterprise SaaS productivity platforms, emphasizing seamless handoffs and continuous orchestration between humans and increasingly capable autonomous systems.\\n\\nStrategically, this move underscores the trend toward agentic automation, taking productivity beyond isolated tasks to full workflow accountability. It will likely influence C-suite expectations regarding SaaS ROI and the architectural requirements for integrating Workday, Salesforce, and other SaaS with externally developed AI agents. For technical leaders and Deloitte consultants, this disrupts the status quo of process automation and compels updates to middleware, integration, and governance models to support durable, reliable agentic interactions across platforms.\\n\\nThe implications for Workday clients and Deloitte’s Workday practice are significant. While Workday has developed early agentic features (e.g., agentic contract review workflows), customers will expect similar orchestration capabilities—especially around HR, finance, and legal processes. Deloitte teams should prioritize advisory and enablement around integrating agentic layers, updating change management, and leveraging best practices as agentic labor increasingly augments distributed human teams.\",\n      \"source_url\": \"https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202606040430BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260604_BW472500-1\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"headline\": \"Asana Launches AI-Powered Products Targeting Human-Agent Collaboration (2026-06-04)\",\n      \"why_it_matters\": \"Published June 4, 2026, Asana’s latest suite of AI-powered products aims not only to support agentic automation but to explicitly manage human-agent ‘work partnerships’. Asana’s platform seeks to close the loop on ambiguous handoffs by standardizing protocols for action, follow-up, and feedback between autonomous systems and human users. Placing agentic AI in project and operational workflows increases productivity, but also places fresh demands on interoperability, data governance, and continuous process improvement in SaaS architecture.\\n\\nThe strategic impact is significant: Asana is moving beyond traditional productivity apps into the heart of digital work orchestration, challenging both horizontal SaaS (like Workday and SAP) and niche automation tools. The necessity to manage accountability and transparency when decisions are made by AI agents—often with limited human oversight—puts pressure on SaaS ecosystems to strengthen compliance, auditability, and user trust. For Deloitte and Workday clients, these developments highlight the urgency of upskilling both technical teams (to architect hybrid workflows) and business users (to effectively supervise agentic AI). The coming months will likely see greater demand for consulting on organizational readiness and process redesign as enterprises seek to maximize agentic value while limiting risk.\",\n      \"source_url\": \"https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"headline\": \"Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Now Available in Workday (2026-06-03)\",\n      \"why_it_matters\": \"Workday has launched agentic contract review and redlining capabilities, marking a major step forward in the automation of legal, HR, and procurement workflows. Announced June 3, 2026, this native Workday feature leverages intelligent document processing and analysis to proactively review, flag, and autonomously redline contracts and agreements. This not only speeds up cycle times for procurement and HR teams, but also introduces higher consistency and reduces manual error in compliance-heavy workflows.\\n\\nStrategically, the new Workday functionality signals Workday’s intent to keep pace with best-in-class agentic automation within SaaS, as seen in competitor and ecosystem platforms. From a technical perspective, the native integration alleviates pain points experienced by clients using third-party or patched-together contract AI solutions, decreasing the total cost of ownership and reducing security risks. Deloitte’s Workday practitioners should see a surge in client interest for both implementation and advisory services around legal automation, risk reduction, and operational streamlining. Clients should prioritize exploring these functions for efficiency gains and ensure their governance models are ready for an upshift in autonomous actions in ‘critical control’ areas.\",\n      \"source_url\": \"https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"competitive_watch\": [\n    {\n      \"competitor\": \"Asana\",\n      \"move\": \"Release of a unified operating system and advanced AI-driven tools for hybrid human-agent team management.\",\n      \"implication\": \"Asana’s aggressive entry into agentic work orchestration heightens competition for platforms like Workday, Oracle, and ServiceNow, especially for clients demanding N:N workflow orchestration between human staff and digital agents. For Deloitte and Workday, this shift requires rapid enablement to meet client demands for full-cycle human+agent partnering, tighter compliance, and agent-first workflow redesign. The innovation bar for SaaS solutions is rising—not just in feature set, but in architecture for durable, transparent agentic collaboration.\",\n      \"source_url\": \"https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202606040430BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260604_BW472500-1\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"competitor\": \"Workday\",\n      \"move\": \"Expansion of native agentic contract review and autonomous redlining within its SaaS ecosystem.\",\n      \"implication\": \"By internalizing advanced legal AI features, Workday narrows historical feature gaps with legaltech vendors, while also sending a clear competitive signal to SAP, Oracle, and Dayforce regarding the intelligence and coverage expected of core HCM/ERP platforms. For Deloitte’s Workday practice, there’s a need to escalate skills in AI-enabled contracting processes, and help clients rethink compliance, data stewardship, and cross-functional adoption as automation moves beyond finance and HR into core legal and procurement operations.\",\n      \"source_url\": \"https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\"\n    }\n \"Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog\",\n      \"url\": \"https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"html_body\": \"<h2>Highlights</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><strong>Asana Unveils Operating System for Human-Agent Teams (2026-06-04):</strong> On June 4, 2026, <a href=\\\"https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202606040430BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260604_BW472500-1\\\">Asana announced a new operating system</a> architected for collaboration between human and AI agent teams. This platform enhances handoffs, supervision, and feedback, moving SaaS productivity standards towards more durable and adaptive human+agent orchestration.<br>Strategically, this signals a sea change for enterprise workflows, raising client expectations for agentic functions within Workday and peer platforms. Deloitte teams should prioritize training and advisory on integrating these capabilities successfully.</li>\\n<li><strong>Asana Launches AI-Powered Products for Human-Agent Workflows (2026-06-04):</strong> <a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work\\\">Asana's new product lineup</a> further embeds AI into task management and action tracking, blending agentic automation directly into the project lifecycle. The innovations set new standards for interoperability and auditability, presenting both a challenge and an opportunity for Workday, SAP, and GSI partners.<br>Implications involve upskilling, process redesign, and continuous improvement mandates for Deloitte and Workday clients as agentic labor becomes more integral to business operations.</li>\\n<li><strong>Workday Introduces Agentic Contract Review & Redlining (2026-06-03):</strong> <a href=\\\"https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\\\">Workday has rolled out AI-driven contract review</a> and autonomous redlining features, reducing manual review burdens and cycle times in legal, HR, and procurement. Native integration sets a new bar for Workday customers previously reliant on third-party legal AI.<br>For Deloitte, this offers a prime opportunity to deepen automation services and compliance consulting as clients demand seamless, auditable self-service for contract workflows.</li>\\n</ul>\\n\\n<h2>Competitive Watch</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><strong>Asana:</strong> <a href=\\\"https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202606040430BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260604_BW472500-1\\\">Major release of an AI operating system</a> for orchestrating human-agent team tasks. This sets a higher bar for SaaS orchestration and agentic interoperability, forcing Workday and Deloitte to accelerate agentic playbook development and integration maturity.</li>\\n<li><strong>Workday:</strong> <a href=\\\"https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\\\">Native agentic contract review and redlining</a> tools close competitive gaps with legaltech rivals, establishing a new baseline for AI-enabled contract management. Deloitte's Workday practice must rapidly adapt skills and advisory approaches to leverage this momentum with clients.</li>\\n</ul>\\n\\n<h2>Enablement</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><strong>Agentic Contract AI Enablement:</strong> Use <a href=\\\"https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\\\">Workday's new contract review feature</a> to accelerate contract workflows and reduce manual error, empowering Deloitte teams and clients to realize quick operational gains.</li>\\n<li><strong>Human-Agent Team Orchestration:</strong> Draw from <a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work\\\">Asana's innovation</a> to develop hybrid process playbooks for mixed teams, enabling higher productivity and clearer accountability.</li>\\n<li><strong>Agentic Compliance Readiness:</strong> Build robust governance models on <a href=\\\"https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\\\">Workday’s contract AI</a> to satisfy audit and regulatory standards as agentic actions expand.</li>\\n</ul>\\n\\n<h2>Actions for Next Week</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li>Assess current skills, readiness, and pipeline within Deloitte Workday teams regarding agentic workflow orchestration.</li>\\n<li>Host a client-facing enablement webinar on Workday’s agentic contract AI, focusing on practical adoption and change management.</li>\\n<li>Engage with HR, finance, and legal leads at priority Workday clients to identify and prioritize use cases for new automation capabilities.</li>\\n<li>Create updated benchmarking and advisory briefs comparing Asana and Workday orchestration capabilities for client enablement.</li>\\n</ul>\\n\\n<h2>Risks &amp; Mitigations</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><strong>Unclear Agentic Accountability:</strong> Mitigate by instituting human-in-the-loop governance, audit trails, and policy updates specific to new autonomous workflows.</li>\\n<li><strong>Technical and User Skills Gap:</strong> Accelerate upskilling and mentorship, prioritizing microlearning for key Deloitte and Workday stakeholders.</li>\\n<li><strong>Organizational Change misalignment:</strong> Integrate change management and value-tracking early and continuously in agentic workflow deployments.</li>\\n</ul>\\n\\n<h2>All Sources</h2>\\n<ul>\\n<li><a href=\\\"https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202606040430BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260604_BW472500-1\\\">Asana Unveils Operating System for Human-Agent Teams – Company Announcement - FT.com</a></li>\\n<li><a href=\\\"https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work\\\">Asana launches AI-powered products to help organizations manage human-agent work</a></li>\\n<li><a href=\\\"https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\\\">Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog</a></li>\\n</ul>\",\n  \"plain_text_body\": \"Highlights\\n- Asana Unveils Operating System for Human-Agent Teams (2026-06-04): On June 4, 2026, Asana announced a new operating system for collaborative work between humans and AI agents. This marks a shift toward orchestration of mixed teams, raising expectations for platforms like Workday to support agentic collaboration. Deloitte should make this a priority for client enablement. https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202606040430BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260604_BW472500-1\\n- Asana Launches AI-Powered Products for Human-Agent Workflows (2026-06-04): Further embedding AI into task management, Asana's new products drive seamless handoffs and workflow accountability between humans and AI agents, challenging Workday and other SaaS platforms on integration and automation standards. Deloitte and clients should focus on upskilling for human-agent workflow orchestration. https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work\\n- Workday Introduces Agentic Contract Review & Redlining (2026-06-03): Workday launches AI-driven contract review and redlining, directly automating key compliance and procurement workflows. Native tools enhance security and reduce manual labor, providing a major advisory opportunity for Deloitte to help clients streamline legal and HR operations. https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\\n\\nCompetitive Watch\\n- Asana: Major release of an AI OS for human-agent team workflows (https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202606040430BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260604_BW472500-1) forces Workday and Deloitte to update agentic orchestration capabilities and consultative playbooks.\\n- Workday: Expanding agentic contract review (https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html) closes feature gaps, pushing Deloitte teams to quickly adapt client advisory and legal automation services.\\n\\nEnablement\\n- Agentic Contract AI Enablement: Resource—https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html. Outcome: Reduced contract cycle times and error rates for Deloitte and Workday users.\\n- Human-Agent Team Orchestration: Resource—https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work. Outcome: Hybrid process playbooks and improved task accountability for clients.\\n- Agentic Compliance Readiness: Resource—https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html. Outcome: Enhanced governance and audit readiness for AI-driven workflows.\\n\\nActions for Next Week\\n- Assess Deloitte Workday teams’ readiness for agentic orchestration.\\n- Host a client enablement webinar on Workday’s contract AI.\\n- Engage HR, finance, and legal at Workday clients to prioritize use cases.\\n- Benchmark Asana and Workday features for client advisory briefs.\\n\\nRisks & Mitigations\\n- Unclear Accountabilities in Agentic Actions: Mitigate by strengthening governance, audit trails, and policy adaptation.\\n- Skills Gap: Prioritize training and mentorship for Deloitte and client stakeholders.\\n- Change Management: Integrate change teams early, reviewing value and impact throughout deployment.\\n\\nAll Sources\\n- Asana Unveils Operating System for Human-Agent Teams – https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202606040430BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260604_BW472500-1\\n- Asana launches AI-powered products to help organizations manage human-agent work — https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work\\n- Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog — https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html\"\n}",

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html",
      "title": "Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here | Workday US",
      "content": "The Workday Contract Negotiation Agent removes the friction of the drafting process, allowing you to focus on the strategic outcomes your contracts are actually meant to secure—achieving the true ends of your negotiation faster and more easily than ever before.\n\nWant to learn more? Book a demo today to see the Workday Contract Negotiation Agent in action.\n\nWhat Is Agentic AI?\n\nStill unsure on the difference between the types of AI agents, or just need a refresher on the basics? Workday has you covered.  \n   \n Learn More\n\nWorkday’s Bold Future With Responsible Agentic AI\n\nAgentic AI is here, which means the correct governance is crucial. Learn how Workday is doubling down on its commitment to responsible AI.\n\nRead Blog\n\nTrust by Design: How Workday Builds AI That Puts People First [...] That’s why the Workday CLM product team is currently developing new AI tools to automate the drafting of contract playbooks based on existing contracts, with the goal of making playbook creation as simple as uploading sample documents (like existing agreements and policy notes) from which our AI can derive preferred and fallback language and criteria.\n\nWe’re also working to deliver users proactive insights over time about their teams’ adherence to those playbooks, so they can make informed decisions about keeping those playbooks current and optimized. Watch this space!\n\n## Staying Ahead of the Curve [...] Using generative AI to speed contract review and redlining is not new. Evisort introduced clause-level AI review and redlining in 2023—back when Workday was a customer, well before the acquisition. Customers of Workday Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) routinely ask it to review specific clauses against their preferred language and suggest edits to make them compliant.\n\nBut three years in AI terms is a generation. Today we’re at a new threshold in agentic contract negotiation, and there’s no going back.\n\n## Introducing Agentic Full-Document Review",
      "score": 0.63750535,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/ai/microsoft-iq",
      "title": "Unified Enterprise Intelligence for AI - Microsoft IQ",
      "content": "### Self-service HR agents for employees\n\nHelp desks are overwhelmed with routine questions, and employees waste time hunting for answers across policy docs, and word of mouth. Deploy an agent that instantly answers common HR and IT questions—grounded in your actual policies via Foundry IQ—and auto-creates tickets when human help is needed.  \n   \n Power users can build and publish this agent in Copilot Studio with point-and-click connectors to ServiceNow, Workday, and SharePoint. Developers who need full control can use Foundry and the Microsoft Agent Framework to integrate MCP servers, custom APIs, and enterprise-grade scaling—all surfaced through Teams and M365 Copilot.\n\nLearn more\n\nA women working on laptop.\n\n### AI-driven time-to-market acceleration [...] ## No intelligence without trust\n\n### Secure foundations\n\nSecure your users, data, and infrastructure with an AI-first, end-to-end security platform, powered by unmatched threat intelligence and Copilot agents built into the flow of work so your defenders can get ahead of risk.\n\nLearn more\n\n### Secure agentic AI\n\nGet the confidence to move from agentic AI experimentation to enterprise-scale operations by giving your IT and security teams a control plane to observe, govern, and secure agents across your organization.\n\nStart with Agent 365\n\n### Ensure Data Privacy\n\nWe are committed to keeping your data private, with transparent policies and rigorous protections. We believe in transparency, so you can control how your data is used.\n\nLearn more\n\nCUSTOMER STORIES [...] Webinar\n\n### Developer learning experience\n\nThe Microsoft IQ Series is a hands-on developer learning program designed to teach Microsoft IQ through video episodes, Jupyter notebooks, and Azure deployment templates.\n\nWatch the series\n\nA women working on laptop.\n\nYouTube video series\n\n### The Microsoft IQ Series\n\nWatch a curated playlist of developer‑focused sessions covering how to build knowledge‑centric AI systems using Microsoft IQ.\n\nWatch the series\n\nBack to RESOURCES controls;)\n\n## Get started\n\n### The Microsoft Agent Factory (Part 1)\n\nLearn how Microsoft helps organizations accelerate value from agentic AI by unifying the tech stack, removing common adoption barriers, and providing hands-on training and support from experts.\n\nRead the white paper\n\nFAQ\n\n## Frequently asked questions",

      "content": "### DATA & TECH\n\nBuilt to scale securely across Consumer, Retail & Leisure\n\n## Discover more: KPMG’s insights on “AI in Consumer, Retail & Leisure”\n\n### AI in retail: Lessons from strategy to storefront\n\nFrom personalised shopping agents to instant stock replenishment – everyday stories of how retailers are using AI\n\n### The role of AI and empathy in redefining the customer experience\n\n### From CX to Total Experience: The rise of Agentic AI\n\n### Intelligent retail\n\n## Start scaling AI with confidence today!\n\n#### AI in Consumer, Retail & Leisure\n\nExplore practical use cases, governance considerations, and scaling strategies for the sector.\n\nExplore practical use cases, governance considerations, and scaling strategies for the sector.\n\n## Why work with KPMG [...] ### AI Strategy & Value Realisation\n\n###### Identify high‑impact AI use cases across services, policy, risk, and operations\n\n###### Prioritise initiatives based on public value, feasibility, and risk\n\n###### Scale AI from pilots to secure, enterprise‑wide adoption\n\n### Trusted & Responsible AI (Public Trust by Design)\n\n###### Embed governance, security, and regulatory alignment into AI by design\n\n###### Manage model risk, data quality, privacy, and third‑party exposure, especially for sensitive public data\n\n###### Build confidence with boards and regulators through transparent, auditable AI decisions\n\n### AI Enablement for the Public Service Workforce\n\n###### Enable officers to use AI confidently in day to day roles, not as isolated specialists [...] ###### Redesign work and operating models so humans and AI collaborate effectively\n\n###### Build AI literacy and adoption across frontline, policy, risk, and corporate functions\n\n### Built to scale securely across government\n\n###### Build AI ready data and technology foundations aligned to government architectures\n\n###### Move beyond fragmented tools to scalable, secure enterprise platforms\n\n###### Enable AI to scale safely across agencies, functions, and channels\n\n## The Consumer, Retail & Leisure sector value propositions through KPMG’s Four-Door Framework\n\n### VALUE\n\nAI Strategy & Value Realisation\n\n### TRUST\n\nTrusted & Responsible AI (Trust by Design)\n\n### PEOPLE\n\nAI Enablement for the Consumer, Retail & Leisure Workforce\n\n### DATA & TECH",

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/intelligent-enterprise/building-the-intelligent-enterprise-age-of-ai.html",
      "title": "The Intelligent Enterprise in the age of AI - PwC",
      "content": "An uncoordinated approach to technology, especially AI, can create new cyber risks. Teams from different business units and functions all make the rational decision to invest in AI and improve isolated processes and workstreams, but no one looks at the entire picture.\n\nOne company moved quickly to launch AI services and agentic workflows across several functions, but cyber had been brought in too late. Innovation slowed as remediation, exceptions, and inconsistent controls piled up. [...] hero image\n\n#### Key takeaways:\n\nIf you could design a business from scratch today, you’d take a different approach. You’d build it to run as one coherent system, starting with an employee or team, then using AI to help orchestrate how cloud, data, and application investments work together across the enterprise. Finance, technology, and operations are completely interconnected. Humans and AI agents collaborate as a new unified service delivery model. Security is embedded into each business unit, function, and product offering, and is built to evolve with the business. The result is a more connected enterprise where leaders can make faster decisions, respond to change with greater confidence, and create value at scale. [...] AI represents a once-in-a-generation opportunity for finance to respond. Decision memory means that every decision is retained with an auditable record. Judgment can be automated and passed off to AI agents under codified, deterministic governance. Finance can reason continuously and communicate at machine speed across enterprise boundaries, to regulators, investors, customers, suppliers, and partners.\n\nThis is more than another efficiency program; it's a structural reimagining of finance. The operating model is AI-native from day one, with humans governing and AI executing and managing processes. Costs come down, and revenue increases, thanks to faster and smarter decisions across the entire enterprise.",
      "score": 0.111541085,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "content": "| Office 2016 (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OneNote only). | SharePoint | macOS |\n| Office 2019 | SharePoint | Windows 10, macOS |\n| Office mobile apps | SharePoint | Android, iOS |\n| Office Yammer app | Yammer | Windows 10, iOS, Android |\n| Outlook 2019 | SharePoint | Windows 10, macOS |\n| Outlook 2016 (Office for macOS) | Exchange Online | macOS |\n| Outlook mobile app | Exchange Online | Android, iOS |\n| Power BI app | Power BI service | Windows 10, Android, and iOS |\n| Azure DevOps Services (formerly Visual Studio Team Services, or VSTS) app | Azure DevOps Services (formerly Visual Studio Team Services, or VSTS) | Windows 10, iOS, and Android | [...] | Client apps | Target Service | Platform |\n --- \n| Dynamics CRM app | Dynamics CRM | Windows 10, iOS, and Android |\n| Mail/Calendar/People app, Outlook 2016, Outlook 2013 (with modern authentication) | Exchange Online | Windows 10 |\n| MFA and location policy for apps. Device-based policies aren’t supported. | Any My Apps app service | Android and iOS |\n| Microsoft Teams Services - this client app controls all services that support Microsoft Teams and all its Client Apps - Windows Desktop, iOS, Android, WP, and web client | Microsoft Teams | Windows 10, iOS, Android, and macOS |\n| Office 2016 apps, Universal Office apps, Office 2013 (with modern authentication), OneDrive sync client | SharePoint Online | Windows 10 | [...] Note\n\nFor Apple devices, as Microsoft Entra ID transitions the storage of device identity keys from Apple Keychain to Apple Secure Enclave, the Microsoft Enterprise SSO plug‑in for Apple devices must be enabled for applications that do not use the Microsoft Authentication Library (MSAL), including Safari. Enabling the Enterprise SSO plug‑in ensures that these applications can participate in device-based authentication required by Conditional Access policies, such as Require device to be marked as compliant and Filter for devices condition. For more information about this transition to Apple Secure Enclave, see Microsoft Enterprise SSO plug‑in for Apple devices – Microsoft identity platform on Microsoft Learn\n\n### Exchange ActiveSync clients",

      "title": "Delivery Senior Consultant, Workday HCM Payroll/Benefits Module(s) -  - 354651",
      "content": "### Position Summary\n\nOur Deloitte Human Capital team helps organizations create value through people performance. We work with clients to reimagine work, the workforce, and the workplace across the enterprise and to transform their HR functions with AI and emerging technology. With the rapid pace of change in today’s world, you will help clients answer questions like: How do I access, develop, and motivate my workforce? What should my AI strategy be for the HR function? Do I have the right organization and culture to enable performance? Join our team to make work better for humans and humans better at work.\n\nWork You'll Do\n\nAs a Delivery Senior Consultant for our team, you will be responsible for:\n\nA successful candidate would possess these skills:\n\nThe Team [...] The Team\n\nDeloitte’s Government & Public Services (GPS) practice – our people, ideas, technology and outcomes – is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise.\n\nOur HR Strategy & Technology offering develops leading global HR strategies and implements HR technologies that drive transformation, maximize the workforce experience, and sustain HR effectiveness and value while navigating a world of disruption.     {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/intelligent-enterprise.html",
      "title": "Intelligent enterprise: Connect AI, strategy and operations | PwC",
      "content": "AI governance is the accountability architecture that defines how AI systems make decisions across an enterprise. It establishes who holds authority, what autonomous systems are permitted to do, how risks are managed, and how outcomes remain explainable. As AI becomes part of how businesses operate, governance becomes the difference between AI that creates value and AI that creates exposure.\n\nStrategy sets direction, technology connects data and AI, operations execute decisions in real time, and governance establishes decision rights, accountability, and risk guardrails. Together, they operate as a single system, enabling aligned decisions, connected systems, and scalable AI performance across the enterprise. [...] Playback of this video is not currently available\n\n## Frequently asked questions\n\nAn intelligent enterprise connects strategy, technology, operations, and governance into one system, linking AI, data, and decisions across the business. Leading organizations aren’t just investing in AI; they’re redesigning how the enterprise works together.\n\nMany AI pilots fail to scale because organizations are under-architected, not under-invested. Data often stays siloed, decisions don’t flow across the business, and governance lags behind autonomy. Scaling AI is a design problem, not a technology problem. [...] Shared success benefits\n\nLoading Results\n\nNo Match Found\n\nConnect strategy, technology, and operations so your business runs in sync\n\n# The intelligent enterprise\n\nAI is redefining how your business runs. The question is: Are you designing for it?\n\n## AI is already part of how your business runs. What matters now is whether you design for it or inherit it.\n\nFor many organizations, AI still isn’t scaling. Systems move at different speeds. Data doesn’t connect. Value stalls in pilots.\n\nThat’s not a technology gap. It’s a design choice.\n\nThe organizations pulling ahead aren't just adopting AI. They're redesigning how decisions get made, how work moves, and how accountability scales across the business. Deliberately.",

      "raw_content": null
    },

      "content": "Deloitte logo return to homepage\nDeloitte logo return to homepage\n\n### Our Purpose\n\n### World Climate\n\n### Our Purpose\n\n### World Climate\n\n### Our Purpose\n\n### World Climate\n\n### Our Purpose\n\n### World Climate\n\n### Our Purpose\n\n### World Climate\n\n### Our Purpose\n\n### World Climate\n\n### Generative AI\n\nStory\n\n### Generative AI Services\n\nCapability\n\n### Operate | Deloitte Canada\n\nCapability\n\n### Generative AI\n\nStory\n\n### Generative AI Services\n\nCapability\n\n### Operate | Deloitte Canada\n\nCapability\n\n### Generative AI\n\nStory\n\n### Generative AI Services\n\nCapability\n\n### Operate | Deloitte Canada\n\nCapability\n\n### Generative AI\n\nStory\n\n### Generative AI Services\n\nCapability\n\n### Operate | Deloitte Canada\n\nCapability\n\n### Generative AI\n\nStory\n\n### Generative AI Services\n\nCapability [...] ### Download the full report to explore the insights, benchmarks, and actions shaping the future of payroll in Canada.\n\n### \n\nI’d like to schedule a 30-minute conversation with a Deloitte leader.\n\nI'd like to receive an email from a leader to start a conversation.\n\nI have read and accept the privacy statement and terms of use.\\\n\nI would like Deloitte to keep me informed about related content. I understand that I can unsubscribe at any time.\n\nDid you find this useful?\n\n#### Thanks for your feedback\n\n#### \n\n### Chat with our leaders\n\nShivani Joshi\n\n#### Shivani Joshi\n\nMihir Chinoy\n\n#### Mihir Chinoy\n\nKeegan Castrillo\n\n#### Keegan Castrillo\n\n### Our Thinking:\n\n### Finance trends 2026\n\n### Your HCM can do more than you think\n\n### Let's connect\n\n##### Follow us [...] # Beyond paydays : The evolution of payroll in Canada\n\n## Payroll in Canada is at an inflection point. Once viewed primarily as a back-office function, payroll now plays a critical role in employee experience, regulatory confidence, and organizational resilience, yet many organizations are still operating with legacy processes that limit its impact.\n\n### Key takeaways\n\nBeyond paydays: The evolution of payroll in Canada, developed by Deloitte in collaboration with the National Payroll Institute, examines the current state of payroll through national survey data, qualitative interviews and global comparisons. The research reveals a clear pattern: while Canada demonstrates strong compliance rigour, it lags peer markets in digital maturity, cloud adoption, analytics, and AI-enabled payroll.",

      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/ap/news/press-releases/2026/06/04/mulesoft-report-multi-agent-adoption-in-singapore-to-surge-58-by-2027-governance-remains-top-concern-for-organisations-2",
      "title": "Mulesoft Report: Multi-Agent Adoption in Singapore to Surge 58% by 2027, Governance Remains Top Concern for Organisations - Salesforce",
      "content": "The data in this announcement is specifically based on responses from the 100 IT leaders in Singapore.\n\nPlease see www.deloitte.com/us/about for a detailed description of their legal structure.\n\n\\AI agents are defined as the newest type of AI that can understand natural language, reason about goals, make decisions, and carry out tasks on its own. Agentic AI can coordinate with people, systems, or even other agents to achieve outcomes without step-by-step human input. Examples: virtual assistants that handle complex support cases end-to-end, AI tools that schedule meetings and send follow-ups, or systems that monitor operations and take corrective actions automatically.\n\n## Share article\n\n## Just For You [...] ## Salesforce survey shows extensive personal use of AI in ASEAN is driving trust and confidence in using AI at work\n\nAgentforce Developer Experience TDX Release\n\n## Salesforce เปิดตัว Headless 360 ความสามารถใหม่ของแพลตฟอร์ม ที่เปิดให้ใช้งานแบบไม่ต้องผ่านเบราว์เซอร์\n\nFPT Launches first ASEAN Salesforce Center of Excellence\n\n## FPT và Salesforce ra mắt trung tâm phát triển, ứng dụng AI, thúc đẩy mô hình “doanh nghiệp tự vận hành” ngành tài chính Việt Nam\n\n## Share article\n\nAmericas\n\nEurope, Middle East, and Africa\n\nAsia Pacific\n\n© Copyright 2026 Salesforce, inc. All rights reserved. \nVarious trademarks held by their respective owners. Salesforce, inc. Salesforce Tower, 415 Mission Street, 3rd Floor, San Francisco, CA 94105, United States\n\nYour Privacy Choices [...] # Mulesoft Report: Multi-Agent Adoption in Singapore to Surge 58% by 2027, Governance Remains Top Concern for Organisations\n\nAs agent adoption hits critical mass, 96% of IT leaders in Singapore say AI agent success depends on integration across systems\n\nIT leaders are turning to API-driven architectures to connect multi-agents and prevent fragmented infrastructure and shadow AI from stalling innovation\n\nSINGAPORE, 4 June 2026 – The transition to an Agentic Enterprise, where humans and AI agents work together, is gaining momentum as organisations in Singapore currently use an average of 12 agents with the number projected to climb 58% within two years.",
      "score": 0.62906903,

    {
      "url": "https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/build-2026-from-observability-to-roi-for-ai-agents-on-any-framework",
      "title": "Build 2026: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework",
      "content": "roi agent image\n\nroi agent image\n\n  “By combining evaluations and tracing capabilities inMicrosoft Foundrywith Azure Monitor, we transform AI into anenterprise-grade, production-ready system withbuilt-in observability and continuous optimization— enabling ongoing evolution across the agent lifecycle and accelerating NTT DATA’s Smart AI Agent® vision.” — Yuji Shono, Head of the Global AI Office, NTT DATA Group\n\n## The full loop, in one platform\n\nEach of these announcements stands on its own. Connected, they form the continuous observability loop developers and operators have been asking for: \n\nAll of it on the same Foundry control plane, with Azure Monitor for alerts and infrastructure signals, and OpenTelemetry as the common language underneath. \n\n## Get started\n\nFacebook\nLinkedIn [...] # Build 2026: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework\n\n## \n\n## \n\n9 min read · June 3, 2026 · Sebastian Kohlmeier \n\n \n\nShipping an AI agent is the easy part. Keeping it accurate, safe, and accountable in production is where teams get stuck. Agents are non-deterministic. Their behavior shifts as models update, tools change, and traffic patterns evolve and most of that drift happens silently, long after the demo. End-to-end observabilitycovering the full development lifecycle is how you close that gap: See every step an agent takes, evaluate quality and safety against criteria you define, optimize what isn’t working, and prove the business value of what is. [...] This spring we hit a major milestone — tracing and evaluations in Microsoft Foundry reached general availabilitywith hosted agents coming soon. Every team building on Foundry can rely on them in production today. At Build 2026, we’re extending that foundation to any agent framework, any deployment target, and the full Agent DevOps loop — from the first inference call to the ROI dashboard your CFO will ask about. \n\nThis post walks through the key capabilities we’re landing in BRK252 — From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework: Interoperability, context-specific rubric evaluators that are multi-turn enabled, code-first observability, optimization, and business ROI. \n\nAgent DevOps Lifecyclle image\n\nAgent DevOps Lifecyclle image\n\n## What’s new at Build 2026",
      "score": 0.48396602,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "content": "Watch the keynote showcasing the latest innovations in AI agents, data, integration, automation, and more.\n\nCatch the most impactful sessions, announcements, and community highlights from TDX 2025.\n\n## Connections\n\nRevisit the most impactful moments from Connections 2025 and learn how marketers can lead the way with next-gen innovations.\n\n(opens in a new window)\n\nBe part of the marketing and commerce event of the year. Mark your calendar and join us for more insights, innovation, and inspiration.\n\nWatch the keynote that sets the stage for an agentic future where customer engagement will never be the same.\n\nExplore the standout sessions, product demos, and takeaways.\n\n## Tableau Conference [...] Stream the world’s largest, most trusted technology event October 14-16.\n\nJoin us to learn how AI agents are driving customer success.\n\nExplore data and AI demos, trainings, and release announcements.\n\nSave inspiring content to watch later.\n\n## More Salesforce Events\n\nDive into highlights from past events and explore on-demand content for every role and industry.\n\nJoin live webinars or listen to on-demand webinars at any time.\n\nLearn from peers, build your Salesforce network, and give back to the community.\n\nBuild product knowledge and get implementation and adoption guidance from Salesforce experts.\n\nLearn and get hands-on the with the latest product innovations.\n\nJoin groups to meet, collaborate, and learn new skills with Trailblazers.\n\n###### Salesforce Events [...] ## Newsroom\n\nStay informed with the latest news and announcements, fresh off the press.\n\n(opens in a new window)\n(opens in a new window)\n\n## More Salesforce Brands\n\nExplore the Salesforce brands delivering success now.\n\n(opens in a new window)\n(opens in a new window)\n(opens in a new window)\n(opens in a new window)\n(opens in a new window)\n\n###### Hear our story.\n\n###### Hear our story.\n\n## Contact\n\n## Change Region\n\n#### Americas\n\n#### Europe, Middle East, and Africa\n\n#### Asia Pacific\n\n### Change Region\n\n## Americas\n\n## Europe, Middle East, and Africa\n\n## Asia Pacific\n\n## Change Region\n\n#### Americas\n\n#### Europe, Middle East, and Africa\n\n#### Asia Pacific\n\nAn online shop for northern trail outfitters\n\nShare the story\n\n# Retail AI Agents: Your Complete Guide",

    {
      "url": "https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/agent-distribution-build2026",
      "title": "From Building Agents to Working with Them: Enterprise Agent ...",
      "content": "# From Building Agents to Working with Them: Enterprise Agent Distribution in Microsoft Foundry\n\n## \n\n## \n\nThe past year was about building agents. The next year is about putting them to work. \n\nOrganizations have moved quickly from experimenting with AI agents to building ones that perform complex business processes and execute long-running tasks. But the bottleneck has shifted. The challenge is no longer building agents — it’s getting them into the hands of employees in the tools they already use and governing them at scale.  \n\nToday we’re announcing three things that close that last mile: \n\n## Publish to Microsoft 365 Copilot and Teams [...] Identity, admin approval, scoping, secure publishing, and org-wide visibility aren’t bolted on. They’re the foundation. \n\n## Put your Foundry agents to work\n\nOur goal is simple: make AI agents not just something you build — but something your entire organization works with.\n\nWith Microsoft Foundry, developers can build agents their way, publish them where employees already work, and govern them with the enterprise controls organizations require. \n\nThe future of work isn’t just AI-assisted. It’s AI-collaborative. \n\n## Get started today\n\nFacebook\nLinkedIn\n\n### Category\n\n### Share\n\n## Author\n\nAmanda Foster\n\nTechnical Program Manager\n\n## 0 comments\n\nBe the first to start the discussion.\n\n### Leave a comment \"Leave a comment\")Cancel reply\n\nSign in\n\n## Read next [...] publish to teams and m365\n\npublish to teams and m365\n\n### A new interaction model: delegation, not just chat\n\nPublishing Foundry agents to Microsoft 365 Copilot and Teams isn’t limited to chatbot-style experiences. The interaction model leans into delegation.  \n\nTraditional AI looks like this: \n\nPrompt → Response \n\nFoundry agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot and Teams enable something richer: \n\nGoal → Ongoing execution → Checkpoints → Collaboration \n\nThink “assign this agent a goal” rather than “ask this agent a question.”  Whether user-triggered or fully autonomous, agents surface progress, request approvals, and escalate to humans when needed — natively, through the Teams and Microsoft 365 channels employees already use.  \n\n## Foundry autopilot agents",
      "score": 0.41236505,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://community.fabric.microsoft.com/t5/Power-BI-Updates-Blog/Building-in-the-Agentic-Era-with-Power-BI-and-Fabric/ba-p/5190754",
      "title": "Building in the Agentic Era with Power BI and Fabric",
      "content": "\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\n\nMicrosoft Build 2026 marks a major shift in how developers build data experiences with AI agents. Today we're announcing two capabilities that bring agentic analytics to the forefront: Agent Skills for Power BI, which let developers prompt an AI agent to build and refine semantic models and reports, and Fabric Apps for Semantic Models, which enable AI agents to build and deploy Fabric-native web apps on semantic models. Both capabilities will accelerate the time it takes to go from raw data to a polished analytics solution with just natural language prompts.\n\n## Agent Skills for Power BI (Preview) [...] Figure: AI-generated Fabric data app for business ops on a Semantic model and SQL DB.Figure: AI-generated Fabric data app for business ops on a Semantic model and SQL DB.\n\nFigure: AI-generated Fabric data app for business ops on a Semantic model and SQL DB.\n\nTo learn more about any of these announcements, check out our on-demand session \"Build Agentic Analytics with Power BI and Microsoft Fabric\"available in the Microsoft Build catalog. Can’t wait to see what you all will build.\n\nYou must be a registered user to add a comment. If you've already registered, sign in. Otherwise, register and sign in. [...] ### Join us in Barcelona for FabCon and SQLCon, September 28 - October 1, 2026.\n\nThis is best Fabric, Power BI, SQL and AI community event. How do we know? The last event sold out! Save €200 with code FABCMTY200.\n\nclose button\nNavigate to Microsoft.com\n\nA new Data Days event is coming soon! This time we’re going bigger than ever. Fabric, Power BI, SQL, AI and more.\nDon't miss out.\n\nSujataNarayana\nMicrosoft Employee\n\n## Building in the Agentic Era with Power BI and Fabric\n\nIf you haven’t already, check out Arun Ulag’s hero blog “Microsoft Build 2026: Building Agentic Apps with Microsoft Fabric and Microsoft Databases” for a complete look at all of our Microsoft Build announcements across our Fabric and database offerings.",
      "score": 0.41179726,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/03/metas-ai-agent-for-whatsapp-business-is-now-available-globally",
      "title": "Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally",
      "content": "The company on Wednesday said it is making its customer support AI bot, now known as Meta Business Agent, available globally within WhatsApp. The launch comes as Meta has spent nearly two years testing AI agents in WhatsApp Business for customer support in countries like India and Mexico.\n\nImage 4\n\nImage Credits:Meta\n\nMeta said the AI agent can answer customer questions, recommend products, book appointments, qualify sales leads, and reroute queries to a person if needed. The company is also making the bot available within Instagram DMs. [...] StrictlyVC\n\nNewsletters\n\nPodcasts\n\nVideos\n\nPartner Content\n\nTechCrunch Brand Studio\n\nCrunchboard\n\nContact Us\n\nImage 3: The Whatsapp app logo can be seen on the display of a smartphone on September 2, 2025.\n\nImage Credits:Matthias Balk/picture alliance / Getty Images\n\nApps\n\n[](\n\n# Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally\n\nIvan Mehta\n\n6:40 AM PDT · June 3, 2026\n\n[](\n\nFor years, WhatsApp has been a communication layer for businesses of all sizes around the world. Meta is now infusing AI into that layer in a bid to turn WhatsApp into a viable piece of workflow software for small and medium businesses. [...] Meta said it is testing a way for the Business Agent to provide daily briefings of chats that occurred overnight and provide insights. The company is testing this feature with select accounts on WhatsApp Business, Instagram Pro, Messenger, and Meta Business Suite.\n\nThe company said it’s working to add capabilities like doing market research, highlighting product features, managing users’ calendars, and connecting with tools to extract competitive insights.\n\nMeta said it’s also working to enable the Agent to surface businesses when you search for one or share contact details in chat. And it is building a platform to let larger enterprises create custom agents that can connect to systems like Shopify, Zendesk, and Shopee.\n\nImage 5\n\nImage Credits:Meta",
      "score": 0.38852578,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-03/meta-sells-ai-agent-for-businesses-in-push-to-monetize-service",
      "title": "Meta Sells AI Agent for Businesses in Push to Monetize Service",
      "content": "Meta:\n\nSocial Media Suit Settled\n\nJob Cuts\n\nAI Robotics Company Acquisition\n\nChina Blocks Manus Takeover\n\nNew AI Model\n\nEarnings\n\nTechnology\n\nAI\n\n# Meta Sells AI Agent for Businesses in Push to Monetize Service\n\nContact us:\n\nProvide news feedback or report an error\n\nConfidential tip?\n\nSend a tip to our reporters\n\nSite feedback:\n\nTake our Survey\n\nBy Kurt Wagner\n\n## Takeaways by Bloomberg AISubscribe\n\nMeta Platforms Inc. is selling businesses access to an artificial intelligence agent for the first time, its latest effort to generate revenue to offset the company’s hefty AI investments. [...] The social media giant will begin charging some customers for what it calls Meta Business Agent beginning Wednesday, according to a company spokesperson. The agent’s main functionality is chatting with a business’s customers over WhatsApp, Messenger and Instagram, the spokesperson said.\n\nBefore it's here, it's on the Bloomberg Terminal\n\nLEARN MORE\n\n### More From Bloomberg\n\nJAPAN-US-LIFESTYLE-BUSINESS-TOILETS-TOTO\n\nToilet Maker Toto Ramps Up Its Ceramics for AI ChipmakersBusinesswoman using Chatting AI chatbot virtual assistance app on smartphone to assist her while working on laptop in office park. Using AI technology at work. Online robot system. Concept of VR and AR technologies. People, AI, innovation and technology [...] by Soo-hyang Choi, Jaehyun Eom and Yoolim Lee\n\nHomeBTV+Market DataOpinionAudioOriginalsMagazineEvents\n\nGet unlimited access for just $1.99 your first month\n\nUnlock the global benchmark for business news.\n\nGet unlimited access for just $1.99 your first month",
      "score": 0.3694976,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/news/stories/agentic-marketing-teams-announcement",
      "title": "Salesforce Puts an AI Marketing Team in Every Marketer's Hands",
      "content": "This week, Salesforce announced it signed a definitive agreement to acquire Contentful, a leading composable content platform and headless content management system trusted by more than 4,800 of the world’s leading brands. Contentful adds a single, unified content layer to Headless 360, moving enterprises from static, channel-specific content to dynamic orchestration. It’s the foundation that lets marketers create once and have agents deliver everywhere, multiplying a single idea across every channel, audience, and language. [...] ## Salesforce’s Vision for Marketing\n\n“Our vision for the future of marketing is simple: Empower marketers with AI agents that help them do work that wasn’t possible before,” said Eric Zenz, SVP, Product Management, Agentforce Marketing. “With shared customer and business context powering every agent, companies can move beyond static campaigns to continuous engagement that evolves in real time — delivering more relevant experiences, greater productivity, and stronger business outcomes.”\n\n#### Availability\n\n#### Learn More\n\nThis article may include references to services or features that are still in development and are unreleased. Customers should make their purchase decision based on fully released and available features.\n\n## Share article\n\n## Just For You [...] # Salesforce Puts an AI Marketing Team in Every Marketer’s Hands\n\nSalesforce Puts an AI Marketing Team in Every Marketer’s Hands\n\nAgents build pipeline, create content, and run campaigns so the marketer can set the strategy, own the results, and get back to making.\n\nToday at Connections, Salesforce gave marketers their own AI team, agents that collaborate with them to build pipeline, create content, and run campaigns. This frees marketers to be makers again — spending more time on the strategic and creative work of shaping the stories, campaigns, and experiences that move customers and drive growth.",
      "score": 0.33050412,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://investor.salesforce.com/overview/default.aspx",
      "title": "Salesforce Investor Relations: Salesforce.com, Inc.",
      "content": "## Corporate Overview\n\nSalesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Through Agentforce, our suite of customizable agents and tools, Salesforce brings autonomous AI agents, unified data, and Customer 360 apps together on one integrated platform to help companies connect with customers in a whole new way.\n\nLearn more about ESG\n\n## Latest Quarter\n\nPress Release(opens in new window)\n\nWebcast(opens in new window)\n\nPresentation(opens in new window)\n\nQuarterly Filing(opens in new window)\n\nTranscript(opens in new window)\n\nSee all quarter results\n\n## Recent News\n\n05/28/2026\n\nSalesforce Announces Quarterly Dividend\n\n05/27/2026\n\nSalesforce Delivers Record First Quarter Fiscal 2027 Results\n\n05/22/2026 [...] investor@salesforce.com\n\nImage 3: Salesforce Home\n\n   follow us on facebook\n   follow us on twitter\n   follow us on linkedin\n   follow us on instagram\n   follow us on youtube\n   follow us on tiktok\n\nCall us at 1-800-664-9073\n\nSee all ways to contact us\n\nNew to Salesforce?\n\n   What is CRM?\n   Digital Marketing\n   Customer Service Software\n   Email Marketing\n   Ecommerce\n   Artificial Intelligence\n   Revenue Management Solutions\n   AI Agents\n   SaaS\n\nAbout Salesforce\n\n   What is Salesforce\n   Newsroom\n   Customer Success\n   Blog\n   Careers\n   Trust\n   Sustainability\n   Investors\n   Give us your Feedback\n\nPopular Links",

    {
      "url": "https://community.fabric.microsoft.com/t5/Fabric-Updates-Blog/Introducing-Rayfin-A-new-AI-first-way-to-build-deploy-and-govern/ba-p/5191676",
      "title": "Introducing Rayfin: A new AI-first way to build, d... - Fabric Community",
      "content": "### Join us in Barcelona for FabCon and SQLCon, September 28 - October 1, 2026.\n\nThis is best Fabric, Power BI, SQL and AI community event. How do we know? The last event sold out! Save €200 with code FABCMTY200.\n\nclose button\nNavigate to Microsoft.com\n\nA new Data Days event is coming soon! This time we’re going bigger than ever. Fabric, Power BI, SQL, AI and more.\nDon't miss out.\n\nshireesht\nMicrosoft Employee\n\n## Introducing Rayfin: A new AI-first way to build, deploy, and govern application backends\n\nIf you haven’t already, check out Arun Ulag’s hero blog “Microsoft Build 2026: Building Agentic Apps with Microsoft Fabric and Microsoft Databases” for a complete look at all of our Microsoft Build announcements across our Fabric and database offerings. [...] \\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\n\nCo-authors: Sachin Patney, Ben Zulauf\n\nThe way organizations are building apps is changing. AI and coding agents have made it easier than ever to turn an idea into an app. But keeping those applications secure, compliant, and governed has only gotten harder.\n\nToday at Microsoft Build 2026, we are introducing Rayfin, an open-source SDK and CLI that lets developers and coding agents define and deploy a complete application backend in code. With Rayfin, apps can run directly on Microsoft Fabric, bringing enterprise-grade security, governance, and scale from day one.\n\n## \n\n## \n\n## The challenge: From AI-generated apps to production systems [...] Rayfin turns backend development into a code-first workflow. Developers and coding agents can define a full application backend in code, including databases, business logic, APIs, identity, and access policies, and deploy it to Microsoft Fabric for a fully managed, enterprise-grade backend.\n\nCapabilities with Rayfin:",
      "score": 0.27888775,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://devblogs.microsoft.com/foundry/announcing-foundry-managed-compute",
      "title": "Announcing Foundry Managed Compute: Run open models in Microsoft Foundry | Microsoft Foundry Blog",
      "content": "`from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential\nfrom azure.ai.projects import AIProjectClient\nproject_client = AIProjectClient(\nendpoint=\"\ncredential=DefaultAzureCredential(),\n)\nopenai_client = project_client.get_openai_client()\nagent_ref = {\"name\": \"my-agent\", \"version\": \"1\", \"type\": \"agent_reference\"}\nr1 = openai_client.responses.create(\ninput=[{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"My favorite color is teal and my dog's name is Mochi.\"}],\nextra_body={\"agent_reference\": agent_ref},\n)\nprint(r1.output_text)`\n\nConversation memory is server-side. You don’t have to manage history on the client. Chain turns by passing the previous response id: [...] `r2 = openai_client.responses.create(\ninput=[{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"What is my favorite color and my dog's name?\"}],\nextra_body={\"agent_reference\": agent_ref},\nprevious_response_id=r1.id, # agent remembers turn 1\n)\nprint(r2.output_text)\n# -> \"Your favorite color is teal, and your dog's name is Mochi.\"`\n\nDrop `previous_response_id` and the agent starts a fresh conversation. Same agent, same deployment, same endpoint; memory is just a thread id you carry forward.\n\n`previous_response_id`\n\n## Observability and performance [...] A chat-completions Managed Compute deployment slots into Foundry Agents today by adding it as an admin-connected model, either via the Add to agent button on the deployment details page, or by following the API Management gateway pattern documented at learn.microsoft.com/azure/foundry/agents/how-to/ai-gateway. Native Foundry Agents integration that removes this extra step is coming soon.\n\nOnce the deployment is wired to an agent, you call it through the Foundry Responses API with an agent\\_reference. Same OpenAI SDK, no separate agents SDK to learn:",
      "score": 0.24234499,
      "raw_content": null
    },

All rights reserved. Apache, Apache Spark, Spark, the Spark Logo, Apache Iceberg, Iceberg, and the Apache Iceberg logo are trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation.",

      "title": "Snowflake AI Pulse – June 16, 2026",
      "content": "# Snowflake AI Pulse –June 2026 Product Announcements at Snowflake\n\nSkip to content\n\nImage 1: Snowflake logo\n\n Airing in:13 D ·02 H ·59 M ·04 S \n\n# Snowflake AI Pulse\n\n### Latest Features & Releases\n\nAI is unlocking new opportunities faster than ever — and Snowflake AI Pulse is a monthly series designed to bring you the latest breakthroughs as they happen, so you can put them into practice right away.\n\nAgenda Details\n\nWhat's New (10 min) \nWatch a curated walkthrough of this month's top feature updates\n\nLive Demo (15 min) \nDiscover how new capabilities come together in real workflows\n\nFeatured Deep-Dive (15 min) \nLearn from a real-world use case featuring a live architecture\n\nLive Q&A (20 min) \nAsk questions and get answers directly from the Snowflake team [...] #### Your Privacy\n\nWhen you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. \n\nMore information\n\n#### Strictly Necessary Cookies\n\nAlways Active",

  "query": "Workday Extend agentic AI use cases",

    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/be/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "Agentic AI: While agentic AI is expected to have the highest impact in customer support, use cases for supply chain management, R&D, knowledge management, and cybersecurity are also seen as having particularly high potential. The enterprises we interviewed are deploying autonomous AI agents across multiple functions:\n\nPhysical AI: Physical AI applications span a wide range of industrial and commercial settings. Common use cases for physical AI include:\n\n#### Governance, data, and regulation\n\n### How do I manage AI governance, data, and regulation? [...] Search and knowledge management\n Virtual assistants/chatbots\n Content generation\n\nAgentic AI: While agentic AI is expected to have the highest impact in customer support, use cases for supply chain management, R&D, knowledge management, and cybersecurity are also seen as having particularly high potential. The enterprises we interviewed are deploying autonomous AI agents across multiple functions: [...] A financial services company is building agentic workflows to automatically capture meeting actions from video conferences, draft communications to remind participants of their next steps, and track follow-through.\n An air carrier is using AI agents to help customers complete the most common transactions, such as rebooking a flight or rerouting bags, freeing up human agents to address more complex matters.\n A manufacturer is using AI agents to support new product development initiatives, leveraging AI to find the optimal balance between competing objectives such as cost and time-to-market.\n In the public sector, AI agents are being used to cover workforce shortages, partnering with human workers to complete key processes.",
      "score": 0.529337,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {

You will be a valued member of the AI & Digital Engineering team and work collaboratively with C-suite stakeholders, AI strategists, solution architects, and engineering teams to build scalable, secure, and future-ready AI ecosystems.\n\nIn this role, you will:\n\nWork model: In Office [...] About Cognizant:  \nCognizant (Nasdaq: CTSH) is an AI Builder and technology services provider, bridging the gap between AI investment and enterprise value by building full-stack AI solutions for our clients.     {
      "url": "https://www.cognizant.com/us/en/insights/insights-blog/frontier-ai-exposes-enterprise-architecture-flaws",
      "title": "Beyond cybersecurity: Frontier AI exposes enterprise architecture | Cognizant",
      "content": "### The structural mistake most enterprises are making\n\nMost enterprises are running two parallel programs: AI modernization, owned by the CIO, and AI security, owned by the CISO. Both are moving. In most organizations I encounter, they share very little beyond a slide in the same board deck.\n\nThis produces a predictable failure mode. Modernization acquires new AI capability on top of an estate that security has not finished hardening, while security discovers exposure from deployments it was not consulted on. AI modernization and AI security are not parallel workstreams. They are two views of the same program—and the gap between them is where the risk lives. [...] ##### Keep up with AI innovations for business\n\nAI is moving fast. Our bimonthly LinkedIn newsletter helps you do the same. Subscribe for breaking AI news and actionable insights. [...] Ecosystem depth matters here too: our work with Zscaler—combining Zero Trust Everywhere and AI asset management with our Secure AI Services—reflects the co-engineered approach the architecture demands, not point solutions that leave gaps at the seams. We are deepening similar conversations with partners in identity, communication security and cloud-native infrastructure, because integration between platforms increasingly determines the resilience of the whole.",
      "score": 0.027109984,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 2.69,
  "request_id": "9957896e-37a9-4393-ab1c-eeeb5abdf8b0"

{
  "query": "Workday Prism Analytics AI governance",
