  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/ingestion/default-parsers/macos",
      "title": "Collect macOS System Logs  |  Google Security Operations  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "## Configure macOS syslog forwarding\n\nmacOS uses the Unified Logging system. To forward logs to the Bindplane agent via syslog, configure the built-in `syslogd` service on each macOS endpoint.\n\n`syslogd`\n\n### Option 1: Configure syslog forwarding using syslog.conf\n\nEdit the syslog configuration file:\n\n`sudo nano /etc/syslog.conf`\n\nAdd the following line at the end of the file to forward all logs via UDP to the Bindplane agent:\n\n`. @<BINDPLANE_IP_ADDRESS>:514`\n`<BINDPLANE_IP_ADDRESS>`\n`192.168.1.100`\n`@`\n`@@`\n\nSave the file and exit.\n\nRestart the syslog daemon:\n\n`sudo launchctl unload /System/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.apple.syslogd.plist\nsudo launchctl load /System/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.apple.syslogd.plist`\n\n### Option 2: Configure syslog forwarding using asl.conf [...] ### Windows installation\n\nRun the following command:\n\n`msiexec /i \" /quiet`\n\n### Linux installation\n\nRun the following command:\n\n`sudo sh -c \"$(curl -fsSlL  install_unix.sh`\n\n### Additional installation resources\n\nFor additional installation options, consult this installation guide.\n\n## Configure the Bindplane agent to ingest Syslog and send to Google SecOps\n\nAccess the Configuration File:\n\n`config.yaml`\n`/observiq-otel-collector/`\n`nano`\n`vi`\n\nEdit the `config.yaml` file as follows: [...] ### Option 2: Configure syslog forwarding using asl.conf\n\nEdit the ASL configuration file:\n\n`sudo nano /etc/asl.conf`\n\nAdd the following line to forward logs to the remote syslog server:\n\n`? [= Sender kernel] forward <BINDPLANE_IP_ADDRESS>:514\n? [<= Level notice] forward <BINDPLANE_IP_ADDRESS>:514`\n`<BINDPLANE_IP_ADDRESS>`\n\nSave the file and exit.\n\nRestart the syslog daemon:\n\n`sudo launchctl unload /System/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.apple.syslogd.plist\nsudo launchctl load /System/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.apple.syslogd.plist`\n\n### Verify log forwarding\n\nOn the macOS endpoint, generate a test log message:\n\n`logger -p user.notice \"Test syslog message for Bindplane\"`\n\nOn the Bindplane agent host, check the agent logs for incoming messages:\n\n`sudo journalctl -u observiq-otel-collector -f`",
      "score": 0.014349489,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/ingestion/default-parsers/servicenow-cmdb",
      "title": "Collect ServiceNow CMDB data  |  Google Security Operations  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "`cmdb_ci`\n`cmdb_ci_server`\n`cmdb_ci_computer`\n`cmdb_ci_linux_server`\n`cmdb_ci_win_server`\n`cmdb_ci_vm_instance`\n`cmdb_ci_network_adapter`\n`cmdb_ci_ip_address`\n`cmdb_ci_service`\n`cmdb_ci_appl`\n`cmdb_ci`\n\n## Verify REST API access\n\nBefore creating the feed, verify that your ServiceNow API credentials work correctly.\n\n### Test using REST API Explorer (optional)\n\n`cmdb_ci`\n\n### Test using curl (optional)\n\nAlternatively, test the API using curl:\n\n`curl \" \\\n--request GET \\\n--header \"Accept: application/json\" \\\n--user 'your-username':'your-password'`\n`your-instance.service-now.com`\n`your-username`\n`your-password`\n\nA successful response will return JSON data with configuration items.\n\n## Set up feeds\n\nTo configure a feed, follow these steps: [...] | `company` | `entity.asset.attribute.labels.value` | Owning company/organization | [...] ## Set up feeds\n\nTo configure a feed, follow these steps:\n\n`ServiceNow CMDB - All CIs`\n`ServiceNow CMDB - Servers`\n\nSpecify values for the following input parameters:\n\n`google_secops_integration`\n`myinstance.servicenow.com`\n`\n`/api/now/table/`\n`instance.service-now.com`\n`instance.service-now.eu`\n`cmdb_ci`\n`cmdb_ci_server`\n`cmdb_ci_computer`\n`cmdb_ci_linux_server`\n`cmdb_ci_win_server`\n\nClick Next.\n\nReview your new feed configuration in the Finalize screen, and then click Submit.\n\nAfter setup, the feed begins to retrieve CMDB records from the ServiceNow instance. The initial sync may take several minutes depending on the number of records in the table.\n\n## Ingesting multiple CMDB tables\n\nTo ingest data from multiple CMDB tables, create separate feeds for each table:\n\n`cmdb_ci_server`",
      "score": 0.013543917,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/investigate-detections-in-search",
      "title": "Investigate detections in Search | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "`detection.created_time.nanos > 621828000 AND detection.feedback_summary.priority = \"PRIORITY_CRITICAL\"`\n\nFind all detections where the investigation reason is marked as malicious:\n\n`detection.investigation.reason = \"REASON_MALICIOUS\"`\n\nBasic detection search:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\ndetection.detection.rule_type = \"SINGLE_EVENT\"\ndetection.detection.rule_name = /.+/`\n\nExport to datatable:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name = /.+/\noutcome:\n$x = detection.id\nexport:\n%new_detection_table.write_row(\ntesting: $x\n)`\n\nStatistical query (search on aggregated results):\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\nmatch:\ndetection.detection.rule_name\noutcome:\n$detection_count = count(detection.id)\norder:\n$detection_count desc`\n\n## Enable programmatic API [...] Use this table to map specific issues to actionable fixes.\n\nIssue | Description | Fix || Exceeded result limit | The query returns more than 100,000 results (or 10,000 for statistical queries). | Refine your search query with filters or reduce the time range to narrow down the dataset. |\n| Exceeded time range | The search spans more than 365 days. | Adjust the start and end times in the search parameters to span one year or less. |\n| API sorting error | Sorting fails when using collection proto fields. | Ensure the sorting fields are prefixed with `detection.` instead of `collection.`. |\n\n`detection.`\n`collection.`\n\n### Validation and testing\n\nTo determine if detections search is enabled, check if the Run search button\nstays disabled after entering a query. If it does, contact Support. [...] Value: Lets you identify compromised entities that are\nconsistently generating alerts across the environment. Instead of manually\nchecking individual rules, you can perform a single search to see the full\nscope of an entity's suspicious activity.\n\n### Granular attribute analysis\n\nObjective: Search for detections based on specific fields, such as\nparticular network connection types or log sources, rather than just rule names.\n\nValue: Enables targeted searching within the detection fields. Since\nmultiple rules might trigger on similar network behaviors, searching by the\nspecific attribute helps you isolate related security findings that would\notherwise be buried across disparate rule sets.\n\n### Incident scope identification",
      "score": 0.012204752,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/investigate-detections-in-search?hl=zh-CN",
      "title": "在 Google 搜索中调查检测结果  |  Google Security Operations  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "查找调查原因标记为“恶意”的所有检测结果：\n\n`detection.investigation.reason = \"REASON_MALICIOUS\"`\n\n基本检测结果搜索：\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\ndetection.detection.rule_type = \"SINGLE_EVENT\"\ndetection.detection.rule_name = /.+/`\n\n导出到数据表：\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name = /.+/\noutcome:\n$x = detection.id\nexport:\n%new_detection_table.write_row(\ntesting: $x\n)`\n\n统计查询（搜索汇总结果）：\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\nmatch:\ndetection.detection.rule_name\noutcome:\n$detection_count = count(detection.id)\norder:\n$detection_count desc`\n\n## 启用程序化 API\n\n用于搜索检测结果的程序化 API 遵循与标准搜索操作相同的结构和工作流。如需了解详情，请参阅\n异步搜索 API\n。\n\n查询示例: [...] 最后更新时间 (UTC)：2026-06-03。\n\n### 产品和价格\n\n### 支持\n\n### 资源\n\n### 互动 [...] 查询支持的最长时间范围为一年。\n\n不支持涉及检测字段的联接。\n\n检测结果搜索受标准搜索限制和配额的约束。\n\n检测结果表仅显示与检测结果相关的事件和实体。\n\n## 已知问题\n\n搜索检测结果时，结果表中不会显示案例名称。虽然您可以通过点击特定行来打开\n提醒查看器以查看案例名称，但无法直接按案例名称查询检测结果。\n例如，以下查询会返回空结果：\n\n`detection.case_name != \"\"`\n\n## 问题排查\n\n查看这些限制和配额，以排查平台行为方面的问题。\n\n### 错误修复\n\n下表列出了具体问题以及可采取的修复措施。\n\n| 问题 | 说明 | 修复措施 |\n --- \n| 超出结果限制 | 查询返回的结果超过 10 万条（或统计查询返回的结果超过 1 万条）。 | 使用过滤条件优化搜索查询，或缩短时间范围以缩小数据集。 |\n| 超出时间范围 | 搜索范围超过 365 天。 | 调整搜索参数中的开始时间和结束时间，使搜索范围不超过一年。 |\n| API 排序错误 | 使用集合 proto 字段时排序失败。 | 确保排序字段以 `detection.` 而不是 `collection.` 为前缀。 |\n\n`detection.`\n`collection.`\n\n### 验证和测试\n\n如需确定是否启用了检测结果搜索，请检查输入查询后运行搜索 按钮是否保持停用状态。如果是，请与支持团队联系。\n\n需要更多帮助？获得社区成员和 Google SecOps 专业人士的解答。已更新。请注意，本部分是我们遵循的基于新历程的文档模板的一部分。\n\n如未另行说明，那么本页面中的内容已根据知识共享署名 4.0 许可获得了许可，并且代码示例已根据 Apache 2.0 许可获得了许可。有关详情，请参阅 Google 开发者网站政策。Java 是 Oracle 和/或其关联公司的注册商标。",
      "score": 0.011925401,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/ingestion/default-parsers/bluecat-ddi",
      "title": "Collect BlueCat DDI logs | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "### Windows installation\n\nRun the following command:\n\n`msiexec /i \" /quiet`\n\n### Linux installation\n\nRun the following command:\n\n`sudo sh -c \"$(curl -fsSlL  install_unix.sh`\n\n### Additional installation resources\n\nFor additional installation options, consult the installation guide.\n\n## Configure the Bindplane agent to ingest syslog and send to Google SecOps\n\nAccess the configuration file:\n\n`config.yaml`\n`/observiq-otel-collector/`\n`nano`\n`vi`\n\nEdit the `config.yaml` file as follows: [...] Edit the syslog-ng configuration file:\n\n`sudo vi /etc/syslog-ng/syslog-ng.conf`\n\nAdd the following destination and log statements:\n\n`destination d_bindplane {\nudp(\"<BINDPLANE_IP>\" port(514));\n};\nlog {\nsource(s_local);\ndestination(d_bindplane);\n};`\n\nReplace `<BINDPLANE_IP>` with the IP address of the Bindplane agent host.\n\n`<BINDPLANE_IP>`\n\nSave the file and restart the syslog-ng service:\n\n`sudo systemctl restart syslog-ng`\n\nVerify that logs are being forwarded by checking the Bindplane agent logs.\n\n## UDM mapping table [...] | `src_ip` | `network.dhcp.yiaddr` | Directly mapped |\n| `log_type` | `network.dns.answers` | Mapped: `named` → `response` |\n| `response` | `network.dns.answers` | Merged |\n| `log_type` | `network.dns.questions` | Mapped: `named` → `query` |\n| `query` | `network.dns.questions` | Merged |\n| `question` | `network.dns.questions` | Merged |\n| `ses` | `network.session_id` | Directly mapped |\n| `hostname` | `principal.hostname` | Directly mapped |\n| `server_host` | `principal.hostname` | Directly mapped |\n| `kv.src` | `principal.ip` | Merged |\n| `log_type` | `principal.ip` | Mapped: `named` → `src_ip`, `dhcpd` → `src_ip`, `syslog-ng` → `src_ip` |\n| `src_ip` | `principal.ip` | Merged |\n| `client_mac` | `principal.mac` | Merged |\n| `log_type` | `principal.mac` | Mapped: `dhcpd` → `client_mac` |",
      "score": 0.009634603,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 3.13,
  "request_id": "19a04888-95fb-4b63-9f13-d83b94c4b513"
}

Learn what it takes to design your intelligent enterprise.\n\nLearn more\n\n### Your business doesn’t need more buzzwords\n\nPlayback of this video is not currently available\n\n## Formula 1®: Rewiring race operations for what’s next\n\nGrowth at Formula 1® isn’t just about scale—it’s a chance to reengineer race operations.       "content": "For more details, read the full Tax Insight linked below.\n\n## Trump administration further adjusts Section 232 tariffs on aluminum, steel, and copper imports\n\n## {{filterContent.facetedTitle}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\n## Related content\n\n## Contact us\n\nEd Geils\n\nEd Geils\n\nGlobal and US Tax Knowledge Management Leader, PwC US\n\nLinkedin Follow\nLinkedin Follow\nFacebook Follow\nX Follow\nSocialFollow_Youtube\nInstagram Follow\nSocialFollow_TikTok\n\n© 2017\n- 2026 PwC. The Proclamation establishes a temporary tariff framework, effective June 8, 2026 through December 31, 2027, for certain products identified in Annex I-C, including a generally applicable 25% duty rate, alternative tariff treatment for products from specified trading partners, and special rules for qualifying United States–Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) products.",

Real support.\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nTech Effect\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nShared success benefits\n\nLoading Results\n\nNo Match Found\n\nForensics Today [...] In each example, a seemingly harmless or undisclosed relationship snowballed into a costly outcome that could have been avoided with some additional due diligence or better monitoring and follow-through.\n\n## The price of disappointing regulators and markets",
      "score": 0.02590068,

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/industries/health-industries/library/develop-a-deep-understanding-of-consumers.html",
      "title": "Develop a deep understanding of consumers: Owning the doctor’s visit is no longer enough: PwC",
      "content": "Innovator’s move: Design segmented journeys that extend beyond your walls by partnering, offloading, and orchestrating across ecosystems so resources follow need‑states, not organizational boundaries.\n\n## 4. Make hyper‑personalization practical without waiting for perfection. [...] Intake data, scheduling behavior, missed appointments, and Social Determinants of Health indicators can be used today to tailor journeys, anticipate needs, and intervene earlier if organizations are set up to act on them. The real constraint is the operating model: who owns personalization, how insights trigger action, and how teams coordinate across care, access, and engagement. Treating personalization as an enterprise capability (not a technology feature) can unlock meaningful impact quickly while building the foundation for more advanced capabilities over time.\n\nInnovator’s move: Treat personalization as an enterprise autopilot. AI orchestrates cross‑team actions in real time, continuously adapting journeys using the data you already have, while governance defines the guardrails. [...] {{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\nLinkedin Follow\nFacebook Follow\nX Follow\nSocialFollow_Youtube\nInstagram Follow\nSocialFollow_TikTok\n\n© 2017\n- 2026 PwC.       "title": "Embrace care engineering: Unlock better outcomes and stronger financial sustainability: PwC",
      "content": "More\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nAmerica in motion\n\nExecutive leadership hub - What’s important to the C-suite?\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nTech Effect\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nShared success benefits\n\nLoading Results\n\nNo Match Found [...] ### What is care engineering?\n\nCare engineering is a disciplined approach to analyzing, designing, and improving operational workflows to help drive efficiency, quality, and performance. Care engineering investigates how care is performed today, identifying bottlenecks and waste with the aim of redesigning processes to achieve the organization’s goals. This process uses data, insights from stakeholders, and leading practices to streamline operations, reduce costs, and improve outcomes and patient, caregiver, and employee experience.\n\nPwC, “A vision for 2035: The future of healthcare providers: Empowering care, elevating outcomes,” January 2026, .\n\nAAMC, “AAMC Report Reinforces Mounting Physician Shortage”, June 2021, [...] AHA, “The Financial Stability of America’s Hospitals and Health Systems Is at Risk as the Costs of Caring Continue to Rise”, April 2023, \n\nGet started with PwC's preference center\n\nOur insights. Your choices.\n\n## {{filterContent.facetedTitle}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\nLinkedin Follow\nFacebook Follow\nX Follow\nSocialFollow_Youtube\nInstagram Follow\nSocialFollow_TikTok\n\n© 2017\n- 2026 PwC.       "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/event-processing/entity-graph",
      "title": "Using the Entity Context Graph (ECG)  |  Google Security Operations  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "`entity.domain.admin.attribute.labels`\n`entity.domain.audit_update_time`\n`entity.domain.billing.attribute.labels`\n`entity.domain.billing.office_address.country_or_region`\n`entity.domain.contact_email`\n`entity.domain.creation_time`\n`entity.domain.expiration_time`\n`entity.domain.iana_registrar_id`\n`entity.domain.name_server`\n`entity.domain.private_registration`\n`entity.domain.registrant.company_name`\n`entity.domain.registrant.office_address.state`\n`entity.domain.registrant.office_address.country_or_region`\n`entity.domain.registrant.email_addresses`\n`entity.domain.registrant.user_display_name`\n`entity.domain.registrar`\n`entity.domain.registry_data_raw_text`\n`entity.domain.status`\n`entity.domain.tech.attribute.labels`\n`entity.domain.update_time`\n`entity.domain.whois_record_raw_text` [...] `Entity graph`\n\nTo build a comprehensive ECG, prioritize data sources that contribute\nhigh-value identifiers and relationship data. For example: [...] ECG aliasing uses the following UDM fields as merge-keys:\n\n`Asset`\n`entity.asset.product_object_id`\n`entity.asset.hostname`\n`entity.asset.asset_id`\n`entity.asset.mac`\n`User`\n`entity.user.product_object_id`\n`entity.user.userid`\n`entity.user.windows_sid`\n`entity.user.email_addresses`\n`entity.user.employee_id`\n`Resource`\n`entity.resource.product_object_id`\n`entity.resource.name`\n`Group`\n`entity.group.product_object_id`\n`entity.group.email_addresses`\n`entity.group.windows_sid`\n\n### Merging specific entity types (File, URL, Domain)\n\nIn addition to merge-keys, the ECG merges context for specific entity types (File, URL, and Domain) using the following unique identifiers:\n\n`File`\n\n`File`\n`entity.file.md5`\n`entity.file.sha1`\n`entity.file.sha256`\n`entity.file.product_object_id`\n\n`URL`",

      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/investigate-detections-in-search",
      "title": "Investigate detections in Search | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "Use this table to map specific issues to actionable fixes.\n\nIssue | Description | Fix || Exceeded result limit | The query returns more than 100,000 results (or 10,000 for statistical queries). | Refine your search query with filters or reduce the time range to narrow down the dataset. |\n| Exceeded time range | The search spans more than 365 days. | Adjust the start and end times in the search parameters to span one year or less. |\n| API sorting error | Sorting fails when using collection proto fields. | Ensure the sorting fields are prefixed with `detection.` instead of `collection.`. |\n\n`detection.`\n`collection.`\n\n### Validation and testing\n\nTo determine if detections search is enabled, check if the Run search button\nstays disabled after entering a query. If it does, contact Support. [...] `detection.created_time.nanos > 621828000 AND detection.feedback_summary.priority = \"PRIORITY_CRITICAL\"`\n\nFind all detections where the investigation reason is marked as malicious:\n\n`detection.investigation.reason = \"REASON_MALICIOUS\"`\n\nBasic detection search:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\ndetection.detection.rule_type = \"SINGLE_EVENT\"\ndetection.detection.rule_name = /.+/`\n\nExport to datatable:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name = /.+/\noutcome:\n$x = detection.id\nexport:\n%new_detection_table.write_row(\ntesting: $x\n)`\n\nStatistical query (search on aggregated results):\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\nmatch:\ndetection.detection.rule_name\noutcome:\n$detection_count = count(detection.id)\norder:\n$detection_count desc`\n\n## Enable programmatic API [...] Need more help? Get answers from Community members and Google SecOps professionals.Updated. FYI, this section is a part of the new journey-based doc template that we follow.\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-03 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage",
      "score": 0.012723961,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/investigate-detections-in-search?hl=zh-CN",
      "title": "在Google 搜索中调查检测结果| Google Security Operations",
      "content": "最后更新时间 (UTC)：2026-06-03。\n\n### 产品和价格\n\n### 支持\n\n### 资源\n\n### 互动 [...] 查找调查原因标记为“恶意”的所有检测结果：\n\n`detection.investigation.reason = \"REASON_MALICIOUS\"`\n\n基本检测结果搜索：\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\ndetection.detection.rule_type = \"SINGLE_EVENT\"\ndetection.detection.rule_name = /.+/`\n\n导出到数据表：\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name = /.+/\noutcome:\n$x = detection.id\nexport:\n%new_detection_table.write_row(\ntesting: $x\n)`\n\n统计查询（搜索汇总结果）：\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\nmatch:\ndetection.detection.rule_name\noutcome:\n$detection_count = count(detection.id)\norder:\n$detection_count desc`\n\n## 启用程序化 API\n\n用于搜索检测结果的程序化 API 遵循与标准搜索操作相同的结构和工作流。如需了解详情，请参阅\n异步搜索 API\n。\n\n查询示例: [...] ## 对检测结果执行搜索\n\n登录 Google SecOps。\n\n依次点击调查 > 搜索 。\n\n在搜索 字段中，输入搜索查询。系统会显示与搜索查询匹配的检测结果。\n\n您可以使用以下功能分析搜索结果：\n\n检测结果表： 显示所选检测结果的所有关联事件的详细视图，以便进行彻底调查。\n\n汇总面板： 通过对字段值进行分组来提供查询结果的摘要视图，让您能够识别数据分布和模式。\n\n列管理器： 通过允许您选择要显示的特定字段来自定义数据表界面。\n\nCSV 下载： 将当前结果集导出到逗号分隔值 (CSV) 文件中，以供外部使用。\n\n分页： 将大型结果集划分为可管理的部分，以优化呈现和导航，而不会使显示内容过多。\n\n### 搜索查询示例\n\n本部分列出了用于搜索检测结果的查询示例。\n\n查找来自特定规则的所有检测结果：\n\n `detection.detection.rule_name = \"\"`\n\n查找所有提醒检测结果：\n\n `detection.detection.alert_state = \"ALERTING\"`\n\n查找所有严重程度为“中”的 GCTI 检测结果：\n\n`detection.type = \"GCTI_FINDING\" AND detection.detection.severity = \"MEDIUM\"`\n\n查找源自 SOAR 提醒或与 SOAR 提醒相关的所有检测结果：\n\n`detection.id_namespace = \"SOAR_ALERT\"`\n\n查找在特定时间（精确到纳秒）之后创建的所有检测结果，并将反馈优先级设置为“严重”：\n\n`detection.created_time.nanos > 621828000 AND detection.feedback_summary.priority = \"PRIORITY_CRITICAL\"`",
      "score": 0.012261391,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/ingestion/default-parsers/bluecat-ddi",
      "title": "Collect BlueCat DDI logs | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "| N/A | `metadata.product_name` | Constant: `Bluecat DDI` |\n| N/A | `metadata.vendor_name` | Constant: `Bluecat Networks` |\n| N/A | `network.application_protocol` | Constant: `DNS` |\n| N/A | `network.dhcp.opcode` | Constant: `BOOTREQUEST` |\n| N/A | `network.dhcp.type` | Constant: `DISCOVER` | [...] Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Collect BlueCat DDI logs\n\nThis document explains how to ingest BlueCat DDI logs to Google Security Operations using Bindplane. BlueCat DDI is an integrated DNS, DHCP, and IP Address Management (IPAM) solution that provides centralized management and automation of core network services. It helps organizations manage DNS zones, DHCP scopes, and IP address allocations across complex enterprise environments.\n\n## Before you begin\n\nMake sure you have the following prerequisites:\n\n## Get Google SecOps ingestion authentication file\n\n## Get Google SecOps customer ID\n\n## Install the Bindplane agent\n\nInstall the Bindplane agent on your Windows or Linux operating system according to the following instructions.\n\n### Windows installation [...] `message`\n`metadata.event_type`\n`session closed`\n`USER_LOGOUT`\n`operation_type`\n`metadata.event_type`\n`view`\n`USER_UNCATEGORIZED`\n`csv.event_id`\n`metadata.product_event_type`\n`log_type`\n`network.application_protocol`\n`named`\n`DNS`\n`dhcpd`\n`DHCP`\n`client_mac`\n`network.dhcp.chaddr`\n`client_ip`\n`network.dhcp.ciaddr`\n`src_ip`\n`network.dhcp.ciaddr`\n`relay_ip`\n`network.dhcp.giaddr`\n`log_type`\n`network.dhcp.opcode`\n`dhcpd`\n`BOOTREQUEST`\n`dhcpd`\n`BOOTREPLY`\n`message_type`\n`network.dhcp.opcode`\n`DHCPDISCOVER`\n`BOOTREQUEST`\n`\"DHCPREQUEST\", \"BOOTREQUEST\"`\n`src_ip`\n`network.dhcp.siaddr`\n`server_host`\n`network.dhcp.sname`\n`log_type`\n`network.dhcp.type`\n`dhcpd`\n`DISCOVER`\n`dhcpd`\n`REQUEST`\n`dhcpd`\n`OFFER`\n`message_type`\n`network.dhcp.type`\n`DHCPDISCOVER`\n`DISCOVER`\n`\"DHCPREQUEST\", \"BOOTREQUEST\"`",

      "url": "https://www.computerworld.com/article/4181180/google-brings-local-ai-agents-to-laptops-with-gemma-4-12b-2.html",
      "title": "Google brings local AI agents to laptops with Gemma 4 12B – Computerworld",
      "content": "Image\n\n### AI saves workers a day a week, but they don’t know what to do with it\n\nImage\n\n### EU sets out plans to reduce reliance on US cloud providers\n\nImage\n\n### Microsoft Copilot Growth, ClaudeBleed Risk, LinkedIn GDPR Complaint | Ep. 84\n\nImage\n\n### Chrome Gemini, AI Agents, CISA Infrastructure Cyber Resilience | Ep. 83\n\nImage\n\n### AI Triage Gains, Model Reviews, Ask Jeeves Shutdown | Ep. 82\n\nImage\n\n### What happens when AI starts selling to AI?\n\nImage\n\n### AI is making business scams harder than ever to spot\n\nImage\n\n### Microsoft Copilot Growth, ClaudeBleed Risk, LinkedIn GDPR Complaint | Ep. 84\n\nImage\n\n### About\n\n### Policies\n\n### More\n\n### Our Network [...] ## More from this author\n\n### RTX Spark may split the AI PC market into mainstream laptops and premium workstations\n\n### Developers on H-1B face a tighter job market as AI shifts hiring priorities\n\n### Microsoft, Google push AI agent governance into enterprise IT mainstream\n\n### Xiaomi releases MIT‑licensed MiMo models for long‑running AI agents\n\n### Meta to track employee keystrokes, screen activity to train AI agents\n\n### Z.ai unveils GLM-5.1, enabling AI coding agents to run autonomously for hours\n\n### Microsoft adds multi-model AI to Copilot Researcher, raising accuracy stakes\n\n### Google targets AI inference bottlenecks with TurboQuant\n\n## Show me more\n\n### Google brings local AI agents to laptops with Gemma 4 12B\n\nImage [...] “The framework for local deployment of agentic AI is different from that of a data center,” Joshi said. “The models are smaller; you can run only one instance of a large model at a time. You are limited by memory, CPU, and so on.”\n\nSecurity and governance are also likely to become bigger concerns as AI agents move closer to enterprise endpoints. Agentic AI is designed to take actions, creating new security risks when local models are given access to employee files or allowed to interact directly with applications and scripts.",

      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise-agent-platform/machine-learning/training-overview",
      "title": "Train and use your own models | Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform",
      "content": "Except as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-01 UTC.\n\n  \n\n,,[\"Last updated 2026-06-01 UTC.\"],[],[]] [...] Console  \n\n English\n Deutsch\n Español – América Latina\n Français\n Indonesia\n Italiano\n Português – Brasil\n 中文 – 简体\n 中文 – 繁體\n 日本語\n 한국어\n  Sign in\n\n Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform\n\nStart free\n\n \n\n Home\n Documentation\n AI and ML\n Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform\n Models\n\n \n\n# Train and use your own models Stay organized with collections Save and categorize content based on your preferences.\n\n \n\nThis page provides an overview of the workflow for training and using your own machine learning (ML) models on Agent Platform. Agent Platform offers a spectrum of training methods designed to meet your needs, from fully automated to fully custom. [...] ### Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform training clusters\n\nFor large-scale, high-performance, and mission-critical training, you can reserve a dedicated cluster of accelerators. This provides assured capacity and eliminates queues, ensuring your jobs start immediately.\n\nWhile you have exclusive use of these resources, Agent Platform still handles the operational overhead of managing the cluster, including hardware maintenance and OS patching. This \"managed serverful\" approach gives you the power of a dedicated cluster without the management complexity.\n\n## Ray on Vertex AI",
      "score": 0.07529205,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/dialogflow/cx/docs/concept/agent",
      "title": "Agents  |  Dialogflow CX  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "`engine`\n\nVertex AI Agents Data Stores:\nAll references to data stores will be overwritten in the target agent,\naccording to the following rules:\n\nExample: If the source agent refers to a data store named\n`projects/123/locations/eu-west2/collections/default_collection/dataStores/myDataStore1`\nand the App of the target agent is named\n`projects/321/locations/us-east1/collections/default_collections/engines/app123`,\nthen the resulting data store reference in the target agent becomes:\n`projects/321/locations/us-east1/collections/default_collection/dataStores/myDataStore1` [...] Except as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-03 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage [...] ## Create an agent\n\nTo create an agent:\n\n### Console\n\n### API\n\nIf you have not already configured\nlocation settings\nfor your project, you must configure these settings with the console\nbefore creating agents with the API. You cannot configure location settings\nwith the API.\n\nTo create an agent, see the `create` method for the `Agent` type.\nGo to the Agent API reference\n\nSelect a protocol and version for the Agent reference:",
      "score": 0.055091754,

    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise-agent-platform/models/tuning",
      "title": "Introduction to tuning | Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform",
      "content": "## What's next\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-01 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage [...] ### Full fine-tuning\n\nFull fine-tuning updates all parameters of the model, making it suitable for\nadapting the model to highly complex tasks, with the potential of achieving\nhigher quality. However full fine tuning demands higher computational resources\nfor both tuning and serving, leading to higher overall costs.\n\n### Parameter efficient tuning compared to full fine tuning\n\nParameter-efficient tuning is more resource efficient and cost effective\ncompared to full fine-tuning. It uses significantly lower computational\nresources to train. It's able to adapt the model faster with a smaller dataset.\nThe flexibility of parameter-efficient tuning offers a solution for multi-task\nlearning without the need for extensive retraining.\n\n## Supported tuning methods [...] ### Preference tuning\n\nGemini Enterprise Agent Platform preference tuning builds on supervised fine-tuning by letting\nyou tune your Gemini models with human feedback data.\n\nPreference tuning enables the model to learn from subjective user preferences\nthat are hard to define by using specific labels and supervised fine-tuning\nalone.\n\n#### Models that support preference tuning\n\nThe following Gemini models support preference tuning:\n\n#### Click to expand supported models\n\nFor more information on preference tuning, see About preference tuning for\nGemini\nmodels.\n\n### Tuning checkpoints\n\nTuning checkpoints let you save tuning progress, compare checkpoint performance,\nand choose the best performing checkpoint.\n\n#### Models that support tuning checkpoints",
      "score": 0.047465697,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/apigee/docs/apihub/manage-agent-registry-integration",
      "title": "Manage Agent Registry integration  |  Apigee  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Manage Agent Registry integration Stay organized with collections Save and categorize content based on your preferences.\n\nThis page\napplies to Apigee and Apigee hybrid.\n\nPreview\n\nThis product or feature is\nsubject to the \"Pre-GA Offerings Terms\" in the General Service Terms section\nof the Service Specific\nTerms.\nPre-GA products and features are available \"as is\" and might have limited support.\nFor more information, see the\nlaunch stage descriptions.\n\nAPI hub allows you to automatically synchronize Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools and server metadata with Agent Registry. This integration enables AI agents to discover and utilize the MCP-styled APIs registered in your hub.\n\n## Enable Agent Registry integration\n\n### Console [...] ## Enable Agent Registry integration\n\n### Console\n\nWhen you enable sync, it may take some time for the process to complete and for your data to be reflected in Agent Registry.\n\n## Disable Agent Registry integration\n\n### Console\n\n## Limitations\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-03 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage",
      "score": 0.04478529,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise-agent-platform/models/start",
      "title": "Get started with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "To get the permissions that\nyou need to use Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform,\nask your administrator to grant you the\nAgent Platform User  (`roles/aiplatform.user`) IAM role on your project.\nFor more information about granting roles, see Manage access to projects, folders, and organizations.\n\n`roles/aiplatform.user`\n\nYou might also be able to get\nthe required permissions through custom\nroles or other predefined\nroles.\n\n## Install the SDK and set up your environment\n\nOn your local machine, click one of the following tabs to install the SDK for\nyour programming language.\n\n### Python\n\nInstall and update the Google Gen AI SDK for Python by running this command.\n\nSet environment variables:\n\n### Go\n\nInstall and update the Google Gen AI SDK for Go by running this command. [...] Set environment variables:\n\n### Node.js\n\nInstall and update the Google Gen AI SDK for Node.js by running this command.\n\nSet environment variables:\n\n### Java\n\nInstall and update the Google Gen AI SDK for Java by running this command.\n\n#### Maven\n\nAdd the following to your `pom.xml`:\n\n`pom.xml`\n\nSet environment variables:\n\n### REST\n\nSet environment variables:\n\nReplace GOOGLE\\_CLOUD\\_PROJECT\\_ID with your Google Cloud project ID.\n\n## Make your first request\n\nUse the\n`generateContent`\nmethod to send a request to the Gemini API in Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.\n\n`generateContent` [...] .build()) {\nGenerateContentResponse response =\nclient.models.generateContent(modelId, \"How does AI work?\", null);\nSystem.out.print(response.text());\n// Example response:\n// Okay, let's break down how AI works. It's a broad field, so I'll focus on the ...\n//\n// Here's a simplified overview:\n// ...\nreturn response.text();\n}\n}\n}`",
      "score": 0.041357696,
      "raw_content": null

      "content": "Sign in\n\nImage 2\n   Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform\n\nStart free\n\nOverviewStudioAgentsModelsNotebooks Pricing \n\n   Agent Platform\n   Generative AI\n\nEngineering Blog\n\nImage 3: Google Cloud Documentation\n\n   Technology areas\n        More \n\n       Overview\n       Studio\n       Agents\n       Models\n       Notebooks\n        Pricing \n            More \n\n       Engineering Blog\n\n   Cross-product tools\n        More \n\n   Console [...] Send feedback \nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-01 UTC.\n\n Need to tell us more?  ,,[\"Last updated 2026-06-01 UTC.\"],[],[]] \n\n   ### Products and pricing\n\n       See all products\n       Google Cloud pricing\n       Google Cloud Marketplace\n       Contact sales\n\n   ### Support\n\n       Community forums\n       Support\n       Release Notes\n       System status\n\n   ### Resources [...] Cross-product tools\n        More \n\n   Console\n\n   Overview\n   Beginner's guide\n   Glossary\n   Get started \n   Get started with Agent Platform\n   Develop Gemini API code with the Gen AI SDK\n   Get an API key\n   Configure application default credentials\n   Migrate from Google AI Studio to Agent Platform\n   Get started with Gemini 3\n   Gemini 3 prompting guide\n   Google GenAI libraries\n   Generative AI cookbook\n   Access Gemini models using OpenAI libraries\n   \n\n           Mistral Small 3.1 (25.03)\n           Codestral 2\n\n       Deploy partner models from Model Garden\n       Model deprecations (MaaS)\n       Open Models \n       Overview\n       \n \n           Multilingual E5 Small\n           Multilingual E5 Large",
      "score": 0.04121853,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise-agent-platform/machine-learning/predictions/serve-gemma-with-saxml-tpu",
      "title": "Serve Gemma open models using TPUs on Gemini Enterprise Agent ...",
      "content": "Replace LOCATION with the region where you created your model in\na previous section.\n\n## Limitations\n\n`us-west1`\n\n## What's next\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-01 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage [...] `ENDPOINT_ID=$(gcloud ai endpoints list \\\n --region=LOCATION \\\n --filter=display_name=ENDPOINT_NAME \\\n --format=\"value(name)\")\nDEPLOYED_MODEL_ID=$(gcloud ai endpoints describe $ENDPOINT_ID \\\n --region=LOCATION \\\n --format=\"value(deployedModels.id)\")\ngcloud ai endpoints undeploy-model $ENDPOINT_ID \\\n --region=LOCATION \\\n --deployed-model-id=$DEPLOYED_MODEL_ID\ngcloud ai endpoints delete $ENDPOINT_ID \\\n --region=LOCATION \\\n --quiet`\n\nReplace LOCATION with the region where you created your model in\na previous section.\n\nTo delete your model, run the following command in your shell:\n\n`MODEL_ID=$(gcloud ai models list \\\n --region=LOCATION \\\n --filter=display_name=DEPLOYED_MODEL_NAME \\\n --format=\"value(name)\")\ngcloud ai models delete $MODEL_ID \\\n --region=LOCATION \\\n --quiet` [...] ### Gemma\n\nGemma is a set of openly available, lightweight, and generative\nartificial intelligence (AI) models released under an open license. These AI\nmodels are available to run in your applications, hardware, mobile devices, or\nhosted services. You can use the\nGemma models for text generation, however you can also tune these\nmodels for specialized tasks.\n\nTo learn more, see the Gemma documentation.\n\n### Saxml\n\nSaxml is an experimental system that serves\nPaxml, JAX,\nand PyTorch models for inference.\nFor the sake of this tutorial\nwe'll cover how to serve Gemma on TPUs that are more cost efficient for Saxml.\nSetup for GPUs is similar.\nSaxml offers scripts to build containers for Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform that we are going to use in this tutorial.\n\n### TPUs",
      "score": 0.038053043,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise-agent-platform/models",
      "title": "Overview of models on Agent Platform  |  Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "### Partner Models\n\nModels from leading AI providers integrated into the platform, such as Anthropic Claude, Grok, and Mistral AI.\n\n### Open Models\n\nSupport for a wide range of open-weights models like DeepSeek, Llama, and Qwen.\n\n## Model Access and Deployment\n\nDepending on the model and your requirements, you can access models in two ways:\n\n### Model as a Service (MaaS)\n\nUse managed APIs for partner and open models without managing infrastructure.\n\n### Model Garden\n\nDiscover, test, and customize a wide variety of first-party, partner, and open-source models.\n\n## Model Capabilities\n\nExplore advanced capabilities supported by the models:\n\n### Long Context\n\nUnderstand how to work with models that support large context windows.\n\n### Thinking Models [...] Learn about the Standard pricing model.\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-05-29 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage [...] Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Overview of models on Agent Platform Stay organized with collections Save and categorize content based on your preferences.\n\nThe Agent Platform provides access to broad classes of large language models\n(LLMs) and generative AI models, including Google first-party models, partner\nmodels, and open-weights models. This page provides an index of documentation for\nthese models, organized by model types, access options, capabilities,\ncustomization, evaluation, and safety.\n\n## Get started\n\nGet started with using models on the Agent Platform.\n\n### Quickstart\n\nQuickly start calling APIs on the platform.\n\n### Get an API key\n\nLearn how to create and manage API keys.\n\n### Setup Authentication\n\nConfigure application default credentials.",
      "score": 0.03315172,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise-agent-platform",
      "title": "Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform | Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "### Build\n\n### Scale\n\n### Govern\n\n### Optimize\n\n## Built for developers, designed for enterprise\n\n## Get started with Agent Platform\n\nStart using Agent Platform to build, scale, govern, and optimize enterprise-grade agents today.\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-05-29 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage [...] Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform Stay organized with collections Save and categorize content based on your preferences.\n\n# Build, scale, govern, and optimize with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform\n\nGemini Enterprise Agent Platform is our open and comprehensive platform that empowers\nbusinesses to rapidly build, scale, govern and optimize enterprise-grade agents grounded\nin your enterprise data. It provides the full-stack foundation and extensive developer\nchoice you need to transform your applications and workflows into powerful, reliable\nagentic systems at global scale.\n\nAPI quickstart\nGet started in Agent Studio\nGet an API key\n\n## Everything you need to build production-ready agents\n\n### Build\n\n### Scale\n\n### Govern\n\n### Optimize",
      "score": 0.031502426,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/customer-engagement-ai/conversational-agents/ps/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.apps/getExtendedAgentCard",
      "title": "Method: projects.locations.apps.getExtendedAgentCard  |  CX Agent Studio  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "`protected`\n\n`string`\n\n`string`\n\nRequired. Required. The protected JWS header for the signature. This is always a base64url-encoded JSON object.\n\n`signature`\n\n`string`\n\n`string`\n\nRequired. The computed signature, base64url-encoded.\n\n`header`\n\n`object (Struct format)`\n\n`object (Struct format)`\n`Struct`\n\nThe unprotected JWS header values.\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-03 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage [...] | `securitySchemes` | `map (key: string, value: object (SecurityScheme))`  The security scheme details used for authenticating with this agent.  An object containing a list of `\"key\": value` pairs. Example: `{ \"name\": \"wrench\", \"mass\": \"1.3kg\", \"count\": \"3\" }`. |\n| `securityRequirements[]` | `object (SecurityRequirement)`  Security requirements for contacting the agent. |\n| `defaultInputModes[]` | `string`  Required. protolint:enable REPEATED\\_FIELD\\_NAMES\\_PLURALIZED The set of interaction modes that the agent supports across all skills. This can be overridden per skill. Defined as media types. |\n| `defaultOutputModes[]` | `string`  Required. The media types supported as outputs from this agent. | [...] `defaultInputModes[]`\n\n`string`\n\n`string`\n\nRequired. protolint:enable REPEATED\\_FIELD\\_NAMES\\_PLURALIZED The set of interaction modes that the agent supports across all skills. This can be overridden per skill. Defined as media types.\n\n`defaultOutputModes[]`\n\n`string`\n\n`string`\n\nRequired. The media types supported as outputs from this agent.\n\n`skills[]`\n\n`object (AgentSkill)`\n\n`object (AgentSkill)`\n`AgentSkill`\n\nRequired. Skills represent the abilities of an agent. It is largely a descriptive concept but represents a more focused set of behaviors that the agent is likely to succeed at.\n\n`signatures[]`\n\n`object (AgentCardSignature)`\n\n`object (AgentCardSignature)`\n`AgentCardSignature`\n\nJSON Web Signatures computed for this `AgentCard`.\n\n`AgentCard`\n`documentationUrl`\n\n`string`\n\n`string`",
      "score": 0.028829927,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs",
      "title": "BigQuery documentation - Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# BigQuery documentation\n\nBigQuery is Google Cloud's fully managed, petabyte-scale, and\ncost-effective analytics data warehouse that lets you run analytics over\nvast amounts of data in near real time. With BigQuery, there's\nno infrastructure to set up or manage, letting you focus on finding meaningful\ninsights using GoogleSQL or Python and taking advantage of flexible\npricing models across on-demand and flat-rate options.\n\nGo to the BigQuery product page for more.\n\n#### Start your proof of concept with $300 in free credit\n\n#### Keep exploring with 20+ always-free products.\n\nAccess 20+ free products for common use cases, including AI APIs, VMs, data warehouses,\nand more.\n\n## Documentation resources\n\n### Guides [...] ## Documentation resources\n\n### Guides\n\nQuickstarts:\nConsole,\nCommand line,\nClient libraries,\nor\nBigQuery DataFrames (Python)\n\nCreating and using tables\n\nIntroduction to partitioned tables\n\nIntroduction to BigQuery AI\n\nPredefined roles and permissions\n\nIntroduction to loading data\n\nLoading CSV data from Cloud Storage\n\nExporting table data\n\nCreate machine learning models in BigQuery ML\n\nQuerying external data sources\n\nIntroduction to vector search\n\n### Reference\n\nFunctions in GoogleSQL\n\nOperators in GoogleSQL\n\nConditional expressions in GoogleSQL\n\nDate functions in GoogleSQL\n\nQuery syntax in GoogleSQL\n\nString functions in GoogleSQL\n\nUsing the bq command-line tool\n\nEnd-to-end journey for machine learning models\n\nBigQuery DataFrames (Python)\n\nBigQuery API Client Libraries [...] Learn to create a data processing pipeline using Apache Spark with Managed Service for Apache Spark on Google Cloud. It is a common use case in data science and data engineering to read data from one storage location, perform transformations on it and write it into another storage location.\n\n### BigQuery For Data Analysis\n\nLearn how to query, ingest, optimize, visualize, and even build machine learning models in SQL inside of BigQuery.\n\n### BigQuery for Marketing Analysts\n\nGet repeatable, scalable, and valuable insights into your data by learning how to query it using BigQuery.\n\n### BigQuery for Machine Learning\n\nExperiment with different model types in BigQuery Machine Learning, and learn what makes a good model.\n\n### Migrating data warehouses to BigQuery",
      "score": 0.016179273,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 3.65,
  "request_id": "ad41e4c4-ddd0-4af5-aa70-d8ec96e48c53"
}

      "content": "### Unleashing the power of generative AI in the workplace\n\nKPMG firms can assist your organization in realizing the value of Microsoft 365 Copilot.\n\n### Making an impact: Navigating the journey towards a Microsoft 365 Copilot strategy\n\n### KPMG achieves the 2025-2026 Microsoft AI Business Solutions Inner Circle award\n\n### KPMG earns 2024 Microsoft Partner of the Year recognitions\n\n### KPMG named a “Leader” in Worldwide Microsoft Services\n\n### KPMG and Microsoft enter landmark agreement to put AI at the forefront of professional services\n\nMulti-year cloud and AI alliance to supercharge the employee experience and.....\n\n## Awards & recognitions\n\ninner circle 25 I 26 logo maker badges 300x300 vector\n\n## Our people\n\nCherie Gartner\n\n###### Cherie Gartner\n\nMicrosoft Global 360° Lead Partner [...] ### Check – a store operations tool for all Coop locations\n\nThe workday for retail employees has become simpler with Microsoft Power Platform.\n\n### Striving for operational resiliency, Polpharma implements analytics at scale\n\n### KPMG in New Zealand and Microsoft help to digitally enable the Maori community\n\nTe Puni Kōkiri engaged KPMG in New Zealand and Microsoft to create a tailored business assessment and grant management solution called “Te Tomokanga” – a gateway to transforming, uplifting and creating better outcomes for all.\n\n### Bank of Queensland embraces Microsoft Azure Cloud\n\nKPMG accelerates BOQ’s move to next generation, best-in-class Azure cloud platform.\n\n### Bournemouth, Christchurch & Poole Council [...] ### Bournemouth, Christchurch & Poole Council\n\nOvercoming fragmentation and inefficiency to deliver better public services.\n\n### Saigon Securities Incorporation (SSI);       "url": "https://careers.cognizant.com/global-en/jobs/00068835141/program-manager",
      "title": "Program Manager, Bangalore, Karnataka, India | Cognizant Careers",
      "content": "### These will help you stand out\n\n Exposure to enterprise GenAI solutions such as Salesforce Einstein GPT, ServiceNow Now Assist, SAP Joule, Workday AI, or Oracle AI Services\n Experience with hybrid and multi-cloud AI deployment models\n Knowledge of AI observability, agent lifecycle management, and interoperability standards\n Experience mentoring architects and leading high-performing AI engineering teams\n Active participation in AI communities, industry forums, or thought leadership initiatives\n\n### Why consider this role?\n\nWe're excited to meet professionals who are passionate about shaping the future of AI and driving real-world impact through innovation. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply and bring your unique perspective to the role.\n\n--- [...] ### What you need to have to be considered\n\n 15+ years of IT experience, including 5+ years in AI/ML and hands-on experience in Generative AI / Agentic AI\n Strong expertise in designing and implementing Generative AI and multi-agent systems at enterprise scale\n Proven experience in enterprise architecture, including frameworks such as TOGAF or Zachman\n Hands-on experience with cloud AI platforms such as AWS (SageMaker, Bedrock), Azure AI (Studio/Foundry), or Google Vertex AI\n Experience defining AI governance models, compliance frameworks, and enterprise AI strategies\n Ability to design scalable architectures integrating AI platforms across enterprise ecosystems\n Strong stakeholder management skills with experience working with senior leadership\n\n### These will help you stand out [...] ### Step 1: Apply to the right opportunity\n\nFind an open role that matches your skills and career goals and show us why you’re the person for the job. Don’t see the right opportunity? Consider joining our Talent Community.\n\n### Step 2: Connect with your recruiter\n\nIf one of our recruiters sees a fit, they’ll set up a short intro call to learn more about you and how your experiences and skills match the role.\n\n### Step 3: Show off your skills\n\nIf you and our team would like to continue the process, you’ll interview with our hiring team. Some roles may also require technical assessments and/or client interviews.\n\n### Step 4: Decision time",
      "score": 0.097777575,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/ai-and-ml/2026/06/03/no-longer-just-a-copilot-microsofts-ai-wants-to-take-the-wheel/5250718",
      "title": "No longer just a Copilot, Microsoft's AI wants to take the wheel",
      "content": "## Intel bit off more than it could chew with 18A process node\n\nCFO Zinsner insists the troubled node was a one-off as 14A stays on track\n\n## ZTE and partners nurture global ICT talent through 2026 engineering capacity building program\n\nGlobal ICT experts gather in Shenzhen to master cutting-edge engineering practices and foster international collaboration\n\n## Don’t repeat 5G mistakes with 6G, plead mobile operators\n\nNGMN wants a clear migration path before next-gen network rollouts begin\n\n## AWS reportedly to tuck Elon Musk's Grok into Bedrock, despite zero enterprise demand\n\nThe energy drink of frontier models\n\n## The tech that could make Marvell the next trillion dollar company\n\nCU later, rivals? That's if Broadzilla doesn't eat its lunch first\n\n### MOST POPULAR [...] TOPICS\n\nGo to frontpage. Logo, The Register\nGo to frontpage. Logo, The Register\nGo to frontpage. Logo, The Register\n\nAI AND ML\n\n# No longer just a Copilot, Microsoft's AI wants to take the wheel\n\nAlways-on agent promises to keep work moving, provided you trust it with practically everything\n\nMove over, Copilot: Microsoft is introducing a new category of agentic AI called \"Autopilot,\" starting with Scout, its first agent. And it doesn't take much guessing to understand how Microsoft expects these things to operate: By constantly watching your every move and taking action in the background to ostensibly streamline your workday. [...] Microsoft announced Autopilot, and the first Autopilot agent, Scout, at Microsoft Build on Tuesday, describing it and other future Autopilots as “always-on agents that work autonomously,” stay active in the background to “understand how work gets done across your apps and systems,” and can “take action without needing to be prompted each time.”\n\nScout, for example, can be interacted with in Teams when one feels the need, but outside of instances when users need to query it directly, it’s always there.\n\n“It operates across cloud, desktop, and web, connecting to Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, and SharePoint, and to the data that powers your day, including chats, email, calendar, and contacts,” Omar Shahine, corporate VP of Microsoft Scout, wrote in the announcement.",
      "score": 0.07399697,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 1.42,
  "request_id": "1d2d2ee6-2ed4-49d0-8fd5-e7aa87231440"

      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/ingestion/default-parsers/macos",
      "title": "Collect macOS System Logs  |  Google Security Operations  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "`config.yaml`\n`receivers:\n udplog:\n # Replace the port and IP address as required\n listen_address: \"0.0.0.0:514\"\nexporters:\n chronicle/chronicle_w_labels:\n compression: gzip\n # Adjust the path to the credentials file you downloaded in Step 1\n creds_file_path: '/path/to/ingestion-authentication-file.json'\n # Replace with your actual customer ID from Step 2\n customer_id: <PLACEHOLDER_CUSTOMER_ID>\n endpoint: malachiteingestion-pa.googleapis.com\n # Add optional ingestion labels for better organization\n log_type: 'MACOS'\n raw_log_field: body\n ingestion_labels:\nservice:\n pipelines:\n logs/source0__chronicle_w_labels-0:\n receivers:\n - udplog\n exporters:\n - chronicle/chronicle_w_labels`\n`<PLACEHOLDER_CUSTOMER_ID>`\n`/path/to/ingestion-authentication-file.json` [...] ## Configure macOS syslog forwarding\n\nmacOS uses the Unified Logging system. To forward logs to the Bindplane agent via syslog, configure the built-in `syslogd` service on each macOS endpoint.\n\n`syslogd`\n\n### Option 1: Configure syslog forwarding using syslog.conf\n\nEdit the syslog configuration file:\n\n`sudo nano /etc/syslog.conf`\n\nAdd the following line at the end of the file to forward all logs via UDP to the Bindplane agent:\n\n`. @<BINDPLANE_IP_ADDRESS>:514`\n`<BINDPLANE_IP_ADDRESS>`\n`192.168.1.100`\n`@`\n`@@`\n\nSave the file and exit.\n\nRestart the syslog daemon:\n\n`sudo launchctl unload /System/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.apple.syslogd.plist\nsudo launchctl load /System/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.apple.syslogd.plist`\n\n### Option 2: Configure syslog forwarding using asl.conf [...] | `eventType` | `metadata.product_event_type` | Directly mapped |\n| `attributes.host` | `principal.asset.hostname` | Directly mapped |\n| `hostname` | `principal.asset.hostname` | Directly mapped |\n| `source` | `principal.asset.hostname` | Directly mapped |\n| `senderImagePath` | `principal.file.full_path` | Directly mapped |\n| `attributes.host` | `principal.hostname` | Directly mapped |\n| `hostname` | `principal.hostname` | Directly mapped |\n| `source` | `principal.hostname` | Directly mapped |\n| `command_line` | `principal.process.command_line` | Directly mapped |\n| `processImagePath` | `principal.process.file.full_path` | Directly mapped |\n| `attributes.pid` | `principal.process.pid` | Directly mapped |\n| `processID` | `principal.process.pid` | Directly mapped |",
      "score": 0.012404132,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/investigate-detections-in-search",
      "title": "Investigate detections in Search | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "`detection.created_time.nanos > 621828000 AND detection.feedback_summary.priority = \"PRIORITY_CRITICAL\"`\n\nFind all detections where the investigation reason is marked as malicious:\n\n`detection.investigation.reason = \"REASON_MALICIOUS\"`\n\nBasic detection search:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\ndetection.detection.rule_type = \"SINGLE_EVENT\"\ndetection.detection.rule_name = /.+/`\n\nExport to datatable:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name = /.+/\noutcome:\n$x = detection.id\nexport:\n%new_detection_table.write_row(\ntesting: $x\n)`\n\nStatistical query (search on aggregated results):\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\nmatch:\ndetection.detection.rule_name\noutcome:\n$detection_count = count(detection.id)\norder:\n$detection_count desc`\n\n## Enable programmatic API [...] Use this table to map specific issues to actionable fixes.\n\nIssue | Description | Fix || Exceeded result limit | The query returns more than 100,000 results (or 10,000 for statistical queries). | Refine your search query with filters or reduce the time range to narrow down the dataset. |\n| Exceeded time range | The search spans more than 365 days. | Adjust the start and end times in the search parameters to span one year or less. |\n| API sorting error | Sorting fails when using collection proto fields. | Ensure the sorting fields are prefixed with `detection.` instead of `collection.`. |\n\n`detection.`\n`collection.`\n\n### Validation and testing\n\nTo determine if detections search is enabled, check if the Run search button\nstays disabled after entering a query. If it does, contact Support. [...] Need more help? Get answers from Community members and Google SecOps professionals.Updated. FYI, this section is a part of the new journey-based doc template that we follow.\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-03 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage",
      "score": 0.010377202,
      "raw_content": null
    }

      "content": "Find up-to-date information and status about our technology bugs and workarounds.\n\nFind tutorials, sample code, developer guides and API references.\n\nExplore diagrams, decision guides and resources to build trusted, easy and adaptable solutions.\n\n## Communities\n\nMeet people, learn skills, find apps and experts and share feedback by joining Salesforce communities.\n\nConnect with peers to learn skills and grow.\n\nConnect with Slack end users, admins and developers.\n\nConnect with MuleSoft developers, architects and business users.\n\nConnect with Tableau analysts, admins, developers and business users.\n\nCollaborate, learn and manage your partner business.\n\nHelp us build the Salesforce roadmap by sharing your feedback.\n\n## Services & Plans\n\nReach your goals with our customer success experts. [...] ## New to Salesforce\n\nSalesforce is the #1 AI CRM, with AI agents, data and CRM apps on a single, unified platform.\n\nLearn all about what CRM is, what it does and how it can improve customer relationships.\n\nSalesforce’s category-leading apps for sales, service, marketing, commerce, IT and more.\n\nLearn more about AI agents and how they can help your business.\n\nIt’s always-on digital labour augmenting every employee, department and business process to improve customer experiences.\n\n## Blogs\n\nDiscover tips and insights from experts to supercharge your digital journey.\n\nGet the latest on AI, data, digital labour and more.\n\nKeep up with Salesforce’s AI research team and stay on the cutting edge of technology.\n\nGet the latest tooling innovation news, insights and inspiration.       "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-us/perspectives/ai/values-outlast-regulations.html",
      "title": "Leading with Values in the Age of AI Regulation | Workday US",
      "content": "## 4. Assemble A Socio-Technical Team\n\nBuilding a durable program is a company-wide effort. Because AI systems are sociotechnical by nature, governing their development and use requires expertise across technology, science, law, human behavior, organizational change, and risk. Most modern org charts don’t put all of that expertise in one place, which is why responsible AI requires coordinated, cross-functional teams rather than isolated compliance silos.\n\nAt Workday, that effort is led by Trindel and the dedicated Responsible AI team she built. The team brings together scientists, governance experts, and training and enablement specialists to evaluate AI systems, develop and manage scalable processes, and build organizational literacy around responsible AI across the company. [...] ## 4. Assemble A Socio-Technical Team\n\nBuilding a durable program is a company-wide effort. Because AI systems are sociotechnical by nature, governing their development and use requires expertise across technology, science, law, human behavior, organizational change, and risk. Most modern org charts don’t put all of that expertise in one place, which is why responsible AI requires coordinated, cross-functional teams rather than isolated compliance silos.\n\nAt Workday, that effort is led by Trindel and the dedicated Responsible AI team she built. The team brings together scientists, governance experts, and training and enablement specialists to evaluate AI systems, develop and manage scalable processes, and build organizational literacy around responsible AI across the company.       "title": "What Is an HRIS? How IT and HR Are Evolving Together | Workday IN",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management [...] #### Workday Ventures\n\n### Careers\n\n#### Overview\n\n#### Life at Workday\n\n#### Early Career\n\n#### Job Search\n\n### Corporate Responsibility\n\n#### Overview\n\n#### Foundation\n\n#### Sustainability\n\n#### Workforce Development\n\n### Trust\n\n### Investor Relations\n\n### Newsroom\n\n### Perspectives\n\n## Welcome,\n\nYou are automatically signed in to workday.com with your Workday Digital ID account.\n\nNeed help signing in?\n\nTo access your Workday account, please reach out to your HR or IT department for a link to your company's unique sign in page.\n\n# What is an HRIS? How IT and HR are evolving together.\n\nMore than ever, the companies that succeed are those where HR and IT work in conjunction. A human resources information system, or HRIS, is the perfect tool to bring those departments in sync. [...] ### 5. Employee interface.\n\nWhen we talk about the future of HR, we often talk about manager and employee self-service. The benefit of an HRIS is that it streamlines HR requests into one platform. Employees can use one solution to view job role information, perform basic time and expense tracking, and update personal information. That makes an HRIS an essential part of modern HR service delivery.\n\nWhen considering which HR tool is best for your company, it’s worth evaluating how user-friendly the interface is. Employees increasingly expect workplace technology to have the same level of sophistication as their personal smart devices. Providing a user-friendly interface for your HR information solution can be a quick way to increase employee engagement.\n\n \n\n### 6. Benefits management.",

      "title": "What is AI in Finance? | Workday IN",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management [...] Workday has embedded AI into the very core of our platform. Leading finance organizations are already using AI technology in Workday to help deliver better employee experiences, improve operational efficiencies, and provide insights for faster data-driven decision-making.\n\nIMC Financial Markets logo\n\n“Workday Journal Insights means one less thing for our end users to check off their list at the end of the month. They can correct issues and can fix them throughout the month. It makes it a continuous process.”\n\n—ERP Business Analyst, IMC Financial Markets\n\n“Workday Journal Insights means one less thing for our end users to check off their list at the end of the month. They can correct issues and can fix them throughout the month. It makes it a continuous process.” [...] Companies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations.\n\nWe believe that for AI to truly deliver on its future promise, it must be trustworthy. That’s why Workday has a Machine Learning Trust program that provides governance in defining procedures for the development and management of AI at Workday. This work helps us deliver technology benefits to our customers in line with our core values.​ As part of our commitment to trustworthy AI, we embrace the following principles:",

      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html",
      "title": "Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog",
      "content": "The Workday Contract Negotiation Agent removes the friction of the drafting process, allowing you to focus on the strategic outcomes your contracts are actually meant to secure—achieving the true ends of your negotiation faster and more easily than ever before.\n\nWant to learn more? Book a demo today to see the Workday Contract Negotiation Agent in action.\n\nWhat Is Agentic AI?\n\nStill unsure on the difference between the types of AI agents, or just need a refresher on the basics? Workday has you covered.  \n   \n Learn More\n\nWorkday’s Bold Future With Responsible Agentic AI\n\nAgentic AI is here, which means the correct governance is crucial. Learn how Workday is doubling down on its commitment to responsible AI.\n\nRead Blog\n\nTrust by Design: How Workday Builds AI That Puts People First [...] ## Driving AI Responsibility\n\nIn-house legal teams are the stewards of compliance and risk management for their organizations, with legal leaders routinely serving on strategy committees pursuing safe adoption of AI across the enterprise. That’s why it’s critical for legal teams to choose AI for their own use that meets strict criteria for accuracy, reliability, and data privacy.\n\nIn 2024, Evisort became one of the first AI companies in the world to achieve an accredited certification for ISO/IEC 42001, a global standard for responsible, reliable AI development and management. In 2025, Workday achieved the same. [...] It leverages sophisticated AI orchestration, enabling it to choose the best AI tool to support the task at hand. Instead of relying exclusively on one large language model (LLM), its capabilities grow naturally with the latest advances in AI.\n Its foundational, purpose-built AI helps ensure accurate results from LLMs by conducting core analysis of the customer’s contract repository and creating a trusted base of structured data to enable retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\n It’s fully customizable, enabling customers to create their own AI models to extract and generate all the contract data points they care about over time, rather than being limited to a finite set of core extractions.\n\n## Driving AI Responsibility",

      "title": "What is AI in Finance? | Workday IN",
      "content": "Companies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations.\n\nWe believe that for AI to truly deliver on its future promise, it must be trustworthy. That’s why Workday has a Machine Learning Trust program that provides governance in defining procedures for the development and management of AI at Workday. This work helps us deliver technology benefits to our customers in line with our core values.​ As part of our commitment to trustworthy AI, we embrace the following principles: [...] As organizations continue to place a greater focus on AI, it’s critical that business leaders can trust their AI. At Workday, our approach leverages ethical AI principles that are built into the architecture of our finance solutions.\n\nCompanies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations. [...] Workday has embedded AI into the very core of our platform. Leading finance organizations are already using AI technology in Workday to help deliver better employee experiences, improve operational efficiencies, and provide insights for faster data-driven decision-making.\n\nIMC Financial Markets logo\n\n“Workday Journal Insights means one less thing for our end users to check off their list at the end of the month.       "title": "Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog",
      "content": "That’s why the Workday CLM product team is currently developing new AI tools to automate the drafting of contract playbooks based on existing contracts, with the goal of making playbook creation as simple as uploading sample documents (like existing agreements and policy notes) from which our AI can derive preferred and fallback language and criteria.\n\nWe’re also working to deliver users proactive insights over time about their teams’ adherence to those playbooks, so they can make informed decisions about keeping those playbooks current and optimized.
