      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/careers/jobdetails?id=R00333881_en&title=Workday+Certified+Financials+Adaptive+Consultant",
      "title": "Workday Certified Financials Adaptive Consultant",
      "content": "You are…\n\nAs a member of one of the world’s largest Workday-certified teams, you’ll be part of a team of advisory professionals who deliver more than just technology. From rapid deployments to entire transformations, you’ll deliver integrated, payroll, cloud-based HR and financial management solutions that put organizations on the fast track to a better way to work.\n\nAs a Workday Adaptive Insights Consultant, your primary responsibilities may include: [...] Identification of business requirements, functional design, process design (including scenario design, flow mapping), prototyping, testing, training, defining support procedures\n Lead workshops for client education\n Conduct working sessions with clients to gather, understand, and analyze business requirements\n Lead in architecting Workday Adaptive Insights solutions to ensure that all work streams are cohesive and cross functional impacts are considered and mitigated  Advise clients on industry standards and leading practices\n Demonstrate design options through the use of prototyping\n Understand and apply Workday and Accenture methodologies\n\nWhy should I join the Accenture Workday team? [...] #### Technology jobs: Be the catalyst\n\nGet hands-on with the technologies that our clients need to reinvent, work in new ways and change the world for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n#### Jobs in technology platforms: Create foundations for the future\n\nYou’ll build the tech that transforms how business works for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n## Learn more about the hiring process at Accenture\n\n Benefits information\n Hiring process (Q&A)\n Interview tips\n Using AI",
      "score": 0.5012457,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/ai-and-ml/2026/06/03/no-longer-just-a-copilot-microsofts-ai-wants-to-take-the-wheel/5250718",
      "title": "No longer just a Copilot, Microsoft's AI wants to take the wheel",
      "content": "TOPICS\n\nGo to frontpage. Logo, The Register\nGo to frontpage. Logo, The Register\nGo to frontpage. Logo, The Register\n\nAI AND ML\n\n# No longer just a Copilot, Microsoft's AI wants to take the wheel\n\nAlways-on agent promises to keep work moving, provided you trust it with practically everything\n\nMove over, Copilot: Microsoft is introducing a new category of agentic AI called \"Autopilot,\" starting with Scout, its first agent. And it doesn't take much guessing to understand how Microsoft expects these things to operate: By constantly watching your every move and taking action in the background to ostensibly streamline your workday. [...] Say, for example, you need to schedule a meeting: Scout can handle scheduling on its own while accounting for time zones; it can flag meetings it considers particularly important for its users and generate materials it believes users need to prepare before the scheduled time. Scout can also identify looming deadlines and block off time on a user’s calendar so that they can work on a particular project, “spot risks, like stalled decisions,” and basically act like a work nanny that schedules your day by being hyper-aware of every single little thing that needs to get done.\n\nHopefully, your new Microsoft nannybot is more reliable than its Copilot predecessor, whose outputs Microsoft itself warns may not always be accurate.\n\n### Get ready for a Claw-shaped hole in your environment [...] Microsoft announced Autopilot, and the first Autopilot agent, Scout, at Microsoft Build on Tuesday, describing it and other future Autopilots as “always-on agents that work autonomously,” stay active in the background to “understand how work gets done across your apps and systems,” and can “take action without needing to be prompted each time.”\n\nScout, for example, can be interacted with in Teams when one feels the need, but outside of instances when users need to query it directly, it’s always there.\n\n“It operates across cloud, desktop, and web, connecting to Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, and SharePoint, and to the data that powers your day, including chats, email, calendar, and contacts,” Omar Shahine, corporate VP of Microsoft Scout, wrote in the announcement.",
      "score": 0.48850358,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/mergers-acquisitions",
      "title": "Mergers and Acquisitions - CIO",
      "content": "news ### BlackRock’s $40B data center deal opens a new infrastructure battle for CIOs As the data center market consolidates and hyperscalers buy up capacity years in advance, IT leaders must adopt new strategies to secure compute power. By Gyana Swain Oct 16, 2025 5 mins Artificial Intelligence Data Center Mergers and Acquisitions\n\nnews ### Workday buys Sana to address the ‘era of superintelligence for work’ By acquiring the Swedish artificial intelligence company and integrating its technology into its existing people management software, Workday aims to \"create the work experience of the future,\" a scenario of human employees empowered as never before by AI agents. By Esther Macías and Anirban Ghoshal Sep 17, 2025 5 mins Mergers and Acquisitions Microsoft Workday [...] news ### SoftBank expands AI infrastructure ambitions with $4B DigitalBridge acquisition DigitalBridge’s assets in data centers, fiber, and towers would bolster SoftBank’s $500B Project Stargate and other AI bets. By Varun Aggarwal Dec 29, 2025 3 mins Artificial Intelligence Mergers and Acquisitions Technology Industry\n\nnews ### ServiceNow’s $7.75 billion cash deal for Armis illustrates shifting strategies ServiceNow’s Tuesday announcement of its largest acquisition to date, the latest in a series of similar cybersecurity moves for the company, is a signal that both CIOs and CISOs have to rethink enterprise strategy in an AI world. By Evan Schuman Dec 23, 2025 8 mins Industry Mergers and Acquisitions ServiceNow [...] Image 7: Image\n\nView all\n\nSponsored Links\n\n   Transform Customer Experience with AI Agents | Aragon Research Names Five9 a Leader\n   Build for What’s Next: Your AI Blueprint for Contact Center Readiness\n   Build, buy, or borrow? About Us\n2.   Advertise\n3.   Contact Us\n4.   Editorial Ethics Policy\n5.   Foundry Careers\n6.   Reprints\n7.   Newsletters\n8.   Add CIO as a Preferred Source in Google Search\n\n### Policies",

      "url": "https://apply.deloitte.com/en_US/careers/JobDetail/Delivery-Senior-Consultant-Workday-HCM-Payroll-Benefits-Module-s/354651",
      "title": "Delivery Senior Consultant, Workday HCM Payroll/Benefits Module(s) -  - 354651",
      "content": "### Position Summary\n\nOur Deloitte Human Capital team helps organizations create value through people performance. We work with clients to reimagine work, the workforce, and the workplace across the enterprise and to transform their HR functions with AI and emerging technology. With the rapid pace of change in today’s world, you will help clients answer questions like: How do I access, develop, and motivate my workforce? What should my AI strategy be for the HR function? Do I have the right organization and culture to enable performance? Join our team to make work better for humans and humans better at work.\n\nWork You'll Do\n\nAs a Delivery Senior Consultant for our team, you will be responsible for:\n\nA successful candidate would possess these skills:\n\nThe Team [...] The Team\n\nDeloitte’s Government & Public Services (GPS) practice – our people, ideas, technology and outcomes – is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise.\n\nOur HR Strategy & Technology offering develops leading global HR strategies and implements HR technologies that drive transformation, maximize the workforce experience, and sustain HR effectiveness and value while navigating a world of disruption. [...] © 2026. See  Terms of Use  for more information.\n\nDeloitte refers to one or more of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee (\"DTTL\"), its network of member firms, and their related entities. DTTL and each of its member firms are legally separate and independent entities. DTTL (also referred to as \"Deloitte Global\") does not provide services to clients. In the United States, Deloitte refers to one or more of the US member firms of DTTL, their related entities that operate using the \"Deloitte\" name in the United States and their respective affiliates. Certain services may not be available to attest clients under the rules and regulations of public accounting. Please see www.deloitte.com/about to learn more about our global network of member firms.",

      "content": "Applicants may be required to attend interviews in person or by video conference. In addition, candidates may be required to present their current state or government issued ID during each interview.\n\nAbout Cognizant:  \nCognizant (Nasdaq: CTSH) is an AI Builder and technology services provider, bridging the gap between AI investment and enterprise value by building full-stack AI solutions for our clients. Our deep industry, process and engineering expertise enables us to build an organization’s unique context into technology systems that amplify human potential, drive tangible outcomes and keep global enterprises ahead in a fast-changing world. See how at cognizant.ai or @cognizant. [...] Assist the Lead Consultant(s) in gathering business requirements, designing and prototyping, testing, recommending and implementing business solutions.\n\nBe a problem solver and find creative solutions, analyzing from multiple perspectives.\n\nConfigure the system to the customers’ expectations and requirements.\n\nMaster the art of “follow through”. Frequently update your project Lead and Engagement Manager on timeline, tasks, risks, roles & responsibilities, etc.\n\nAct as both a mentor and mentee, be willing and able to help others, and to ask for guidance.\n\nProactively keep yourself abreast of Workday updates and project changes. Seek out opportunities to further your skillset and professional growth. We love initiative!\n\nSkills and Requirements: [...] Cognizant Careers\n\n### Change region\n\n#### Americas\n\n#### India\n\n#### Europe, Middle East, and Africa\n\n#### Asia Pacific and Japan\n\nCognizant Careers\n\n# Workday Financials Associate Consultant\n\n00069011001\n\nCognizant Workday practice is recruiting for upcoming graduates who are looking for an exciting, entry level consulting opportunity starting in the Summer of 2026. We proudly partner with Workday, one of the leading cloud-based software solutions for HR, finance and payroll management. As a Financials Associate Consultant at Cognizant Workday practice, you will be part of a project team working with customers to get the most out of their Workday HCM implementation.",

    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/xx/en/what-we-do/services/ai/trusted-ai-framework.html",
      "title": "KPMG Trusted AI framework",
      "content": "Human-centric\n\nWe prioritize human impact as we deploy AI and recognize the needs of our clients and our people. We are embracing this technology to empower and augment human capabilities — to unleash creativity and improve productivity in a way that allows people to reimagine how they spend their days.\n\nTrustworthy\n\nWe will adhere to our principles and the ethical pillars that guide how and why we use AI across its lifecycle. We will strive to ensure our data acquisition, governance and usage practices upholds ethical standards and complies with applicable privacy and data protection regulations, as well as any confidentiality requirements. [...] ### Privacy\n\nAI solutions should be designed to comply with applicable privacy and data protection laws and regulations.\n\n### Sustainability\n\nAI solutions should be designed to be energy efficient, reduce carbon emissions and support a cleaner environment.\n\n### Explainability\n\nAI solutions should be developed and delivered in a way that answers the questions of how and why a conclusion was drawn from the solution.\n\n### Integrity\n\nData used in AI solutions should be acquired in compliance with applicable laws and regulations and assessed for accuracy, completeness, appropriateness and quality to drive trusted decisions.\n\n### Transparency [...] ### Transparency\n\nAI solutions should include responsible disclosure to provide stakeholders with a clear understanding of what is happening in each solution across the AI lifecycle.\n\n### Fairness\n\nAI solutions should be designed to reduce or eliminate bias against individuals, communities and groups.\n\n### Accountability\n\nHuman oversight and responsibility should be embedded across the AI lifecycle to manage risk and comply with applicable laws and regulations.\n\n### Reliability\n\nAI solutions should consistently operate in accordance with their intended purpose and scope and at the desired level of precision.\n\n### Security\n\nRobust and resilient practices should be implemented to safeguard AI solutions against bad actors, misinformation or adverse events.\n\n### Safety",
      "score": 0.06841928,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/be/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] A financial services company is building agentic workflows to automatically capture meeting actions from video conferences, draft communications to remind participants of their next steps, and track follow-through.\n An air carrier is using AI agents to help customers complete the most common transactions, such as rebooking a flight or rerouting bags, freeing up human agents to address more complex matters.\n A manufacturer is using AI agents to support new product development initiatives, leveraging AI to find the optimal balance between competing objectives such as cost and time-to-market.\n In the public sector, AI agents are being used to cover workforce shortages, partnering with human workers to complete key processes.",
      "score": 0.058094297,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cognizant.com/us/en/insights/insights-blog/frontier-ai-exposes-enterprise-architecture-flaws",
      "title": "Beyond cybersecurity: AI exposes your entire architecture - Cognizant",
      "content": "##### Keep up with AI innovations for business\n\nAI is moving fast. Our bimonthly LinkedIn newsletter helps you do the same. Subscribe for breaking AI news and actionable insights. [...] Consider what enterprise landscapes contain: software built and layered over decades, logic written by teams that no longer exist, dependencies that nobody has fully mapped. Frontier AI can surface vulnerabilities in that estate at 50 to 100 times the speed of a human analyst and adversaries, including state-level actors, already have access to the same capability.  median time to fully patch critical vulnerabilities has now risen to 43 days. The discovery-to-exploit gap has inverted. [...] ### The structural mistake most enterprises are making\n\nMost enterprises are running two parallel programs: AI modernization, owned by the CIO, and AI security, owned by the CISO. Both are moving. In most organizations I encounter, they share very little beyond a slide in the same board deck.\n\nThis produces a predictable failure mode. Modernization acquires new AI capability on top of an estate that security has not finished hardening, while security discovers exposure from deployments it was not consulted on. AI modernization and AI security are not parallel workstreams. They are two views of the same program—and the gap between them is where the risk lives.",
      "score": 0.04741274,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 2.06,

From rapid deployments to entire transformations, you’ll deliver integrated, payroll, cloud-based HR and financial management solutions that put organizations on the fast track to a better way to work.\n\nAs a Workday Adaptive Insights Consultant, your primary responsibilities may include: [...] #### Technology jobs: Be the catalyst\n\nGet hands-on with the technologies that our clients need to reinvent, work in new ways and change the world for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n#### Jobs in technology platforms: Create foundations for the future\n\nYou’ll build the tech that transforms how business works for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n## Learn more about the hiring process at Accenture\n\n Benefits information\n Hiring process (Q&A)\n Interview tips\n Using AI [...] Identification of business requirements, functional design, process design (including scenario design, flow mapping), prototyping, testing, training, defining support procedures\n Lead workshops for client education\n Conduct working sessions with clients to gather, understand, and analyze business requirements\n Lead in architecting Workday Adaptive Insights solutions to ensure that all work streams are cohesive and cross functional impacts are considered and mitigated  Advise clients on industry standards and leading practices\n Demonstrate design options through the use of prototyping\n Understand and apply Workday and Accenture methodologies\n\nWhy should I join the Accenture Workday team?",

    {
      "url": "https://www.cognizant.com/us/en/insights/insights-blog/frontier-ai-exposes-enterprise-architecture-flaws",
      "title": "Beyond cybersecurity: AI exposes your entire architecture - Cognizant",
      "content": "### The structural mistake most enterprises are making\n\nMost enterprises are running two parallel programs: AI modernization, owned by the CIO, and AI security, owned by the CISO. Both are moving. In most organizations I encounter, they share very little beyond a slide in the same board deck.\n\nThis produces a predictable failure mode. Modernization acquires new AI capability on top of an estate that security has not finished hardening, while security discovers exposure from deployments it was not consulted on. AI modernization and AI security are not parallel workstreams. They are two views of the same program—and the gap between them is where the risk lives. [...] There is a broader point here that most businesses are not yet pricing in. When you open an enterprise estate, even for a security scan, you find more than vulnerabilities. You find migration debt, modernization candidates and re-platforming work that has been sitting invisible for years. Security is the entry point; what comes through the door behind it is the entire enterprise transformation agenda. Leaders who grasp this will not treat the security mandate as a cost to absorb, but rather as the forcing function that finally unlocks modernization at scale.\n\n### What the architecture response actually requires\n\nFour decisions define whether an enterprise is building for this moment or deferring it: [...] 1. Which systems get retired, and when. Unsupported stacks are not technical debt awaiting a budget cycle. They are mapped attack surface. Frontier AI changes the cost calculation on the other side of the ledger.\n2. Zero-trust as architecture, not a project. Redesigning the estate around zero-trust principles removes entire categories of risk. Deploying zero-trust controls on top of the existing architecture does not. Most enterprises I work with are somewhere in the middle, and they know it.\n3. AI systems governed from day one. Model inventory, behavior baselines and runtime guardrails for agentic AI are architectural requirements—not features to layer on after an incident forces the conversation.",
      "score": 0.055152804,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/careers/jobdetails?id=ATCI-5311864-S1946389_en",

    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/investigate-detections-in-search",
      "title": "Investigate detections in Search | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "`detection.created_time.nanos > 621828000 AND detection.feedback_summary.priority = \"PRIORITY_CRITICAL\"`\n\nFind all detections where the investigation reason is marked as malicious:\n\n`detection.investigation.reason = \"REASON_MALICIOUS\"`\n\nBasic detection search:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\ndetection.detection.rule_type = \"SINGLE_EVENT\"\ndetection.detection.rule_name = /.+/`\n\nExport to datatable:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name = /.+/\noutcome:\n$x = detection.id\nexport:\n%new_detection_table.write_row(\ntesting: $x\n)`\n\nStatistical query (search on aggregated results):\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\nmatch:\ndetection.detection.rule_name\noutcome:\n$detection_count = count(detection.id)\norder:\n$detection_count desc`\n\n## Enable programmatic API [...] Value: Lets you identify compromised entities that are\nconsistently generating alerts across the environment. Instead of manually\nchecking individual rules, you can perform a single search to see the full\nscope of an entity's suspicious activity.\n\n### Granular attribute analysis\n\nObjective: Search for detections based on specific fields, such as\nparticular network connection types or log sources, rather than just rule names.\n\nValue: Enables targeted searching within the detection fields. Since\nmultiple rules might trigger on similar network behaviors, searching by the\nspecific attribute helps you isolate related security findings that would\notherwise be buried across disparate rule sets.\n\n### Incident scope identification [...] Use this table to map specific issues to actionable fixes.\n\nIssue | Description | Fix || Exceeded result limit | The query returns more than 100,000 results (or 10,000 for statistical queries). | Refine your search query with filters or reduce the time range to narrow down the dataset. |\n| Exceeded time range | The search spans more than 365 days. | Adjust the start and end times in the search parameters to span one year or less. |\n| API sorting error | Sorting fails when using collection proto fields. | Ensure the sorting fields are prefixed with `detection.` instead of `collection.`. |\n\n`detection.`\n`collection.`\n\n### Validation and testing\n\nTo determine if detections search is enabled, check if the Run search button\nstays disabled after entering a query. If it does, contact Support.",
      "score": 0.014889645,
      "raw_content": null
    }

      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/ai/ai-in-finance.html",
      "title": "What is AI in Finance? | Workday IN",
      "content": "Companies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations.\n\nWe believe that for AI to truly deliver on its future promise, it must be trustworthy. That’s why Workday has a Machine Learning Trust program that provides governance in defining procedures for the development and management of AI at Workday. This work helps us deliver technology benefits to our customers in line with our core values.​ As part of our commitment to trustworthy AI, we embrace the following principles: [...] As organizations continue to place a greater focus on AI, it’s critical that business leaders can trust their AI. At Workday, our approach leverages ethical AI principles that are built into the architecture of our finance solutions.\n\nCompanies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations. [...] Workday has embedded AI into the very core of our platform. Leading finance organizations are already using AI technology in Workday to help deliver better employee experiences, improve operational efficiencies, and provide insights for faster data-driven decision-making.\n\nIMC Financial Markets logo\n\n“Workday Journal Insights means one less thing for our end users to check off their list at the end of the month.       "title": "Workday GO | Medium Enterprise Software",
      "content": "### Questions? [...] Not at all. Workday GO actually reduces complexity with streamlined processes, automation, global payroll, and integration. Workday AI agents including BP Optimise Agent, Payroll Agent, Self-Service Agent, and Deployment Agent are also available at go-live to augment your team’s capacity.\n\nWorkday GO unifies your data so you get an accurate and complete picture of your business in real time across HR and Payroll—rather than separate, siloed views.\n\nWorkday GO delivers its all-in-one solution for HR, global payroll, and finance to medium enterprise businesses through packages designed to fit their business and budget.     {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html",
      "title": "Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog",
      "content": "That’s why the Workday CLM product team is currently developing new AI tools to automate the drafting of contract playbooks based on existing contracts, with the goal of making playbook creation as simple as uploading sample documents (like existing agreements and policy notes) from which our AI can derive preferred and fallback language and criteria.\n\nWe’re also working to deliver users proactive insights over time about their teams’ adherence to those playbooks, so they can make informed decisions about keeping those playbooks current and optimized. Watch this space!\n\n## Staying Ahead of the Curve [...] The Workday Contract Negotiation Agent removes the friction of the drafting process, allowing you to focus on the strategic outcomes your contracts are actually meant to secure—achieving the true ends of your negotiation faster and more easily than ever before.\n\nWant to learn more? Book a demo today to see the Workday Contract Negotiation Agent in action.\n\nWhat Is Agentic AI?\n\nStill unsure on the difference between the types of AI agents, or just need a refresher on the basics? Workday has you covered.  \n   \n Learn More\n\nWorkday’s Bold Future With Responsible Agentic AI\n\nAgentic AI is here, which means the correct governance is crucial. Learn how Workday is doubling down on its commitment to responsible AI.\n\nRead Blog\n\nTrust by Design: How Workday Builds AI That Puts People First [...] It leverages sophisticated AI orchestration, enabling it to choose the best AI tool to support the task at hand. Instead of relying exclusively on one large language model (LLM), its capabilities grow naturally with the latest advances in AI.\n Its foundational, purpose-built AI helps ensure accurate results from LLMs by conducting core analysis of the customer’s contract repository and creating a trusted base of structured data to enable retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\n It’s fully customizable, enabling customers to create their own AI models to extract and generate all the contract data points they care about over time, rather than being limited to a finite set of core extractions.\n\n## Driving AI Responsibility",
      "score": 0.78334314,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-us/perspectives/ai/values-outlast-regulations.html",
      "title": "Leading with Values in the Age of AI Regulation | Workday US",
      "content": "## 4. Assemble A Socio-Technical Team\n\nBuilding a durable program is a company-wide effort. Because AI systems are sociotechnical by nature, governing their development and use requires expertise across technology, science, law, human behavior, organizational change, and risk. Most modern org charts don’t put all of that expertise in one place, which is why responsible AI requires coordinated, cross-functional teams rather than isolated compliance silos.\n\nAt Workday, that effort is led by Trindel and the dedicated Responsible AI team she built. The team brings together scientists, governance experts, and training and enablement specialists to evaluate AI systems, develop and manage scalable processes, and build organizational literacy around responsible AI across the company. [...] ## 4. Assemble A Socio-Technical Team\n\nBuilding a durable program is a company-wide effort. Because AI systems are sociotechnical by nature, governing their development and use requires expertise across technology, science, law, human behavior, organizational change, and risk. Most modern org charts don’t put all of that expertise in one place, which is why responsible AI requires coordinated, cross-functional teams rather than isolated compliance silos.\n\nAt Workday, that effort is led by Trindel and the dedicated Responsible AI team she built. The team brings together scientists, governance experts, and training and enablement specialists to evaluate AI systems, develop and manage scalable processes, and build organizational literacy around responsible AI across the company. [...] Audio also available on Apple Podcasts and Spotify.\n\nA real gap is emerging between organizations building AI safeguards now and those waiting for the regulatory dust to settle. At Workday, Chief Responsible AI Officer Kelly Trindel and Chief Legal Officer Rich Sauer argue that waiting for perfect regulatory clarity is a losing game. Compliance deadlines arrive quickly, even as the technology moves too fast for lawmakers to stay ahead.",
      "score": 0.76385427,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/hr/hris.html",
      "title": "What Is an HRIS? How IT and HR Are Evolving Together - Workday",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management [...] #### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Payroll\n\n### Planning\n\nThe planning system that integrates with any ERP/GL or data source.\n\n#### Planning\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Close & Consolidation\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Operational Planning\n\n### Legal\n\nTake control of your contract data and drive results across your organization.\n\n#### Legal Overview\n\n#### Products\n\n#### Contract Intelligence\n\n#### Contract Lifecycle Management\n\n### Platform\n\nGain a flexible foundation that helps you move beyond the limitations of legacy ERP.\n\n#### IT Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Our Technology\n\n#### Extensions\n\n#### Orchestrate & Integrations\n\n#### Data Cloud\n\n#### Workday Extend\n\n### Workday ERP [...] HRIS: An HRIS collects and stores employee data. It compiles information on employee demographics, job status, contact information, compensation, and other areas.\n HRMS: An HRMS expands on the data management tools of an HRIS to include a wider range of software and utility. The term originated in the early twenty-first century to describe HR software suites supporting payroll, applicant tracking, benefits, and talent management.\n HCMS: An HCMS encompasses the functionality of an HRIS and HRMS while supporting a wider breadth of HR functions, including employee experience and analytics. HCM, in general, largely refers to HR suites designed with cloud computing at the core, ensuring they remain adaptive.",

    },

      "url": "https://www.cio.com/profile/anirban-ghoshal/page/7",
      "title": "Anirban Ghoshal | Page 7 | CIO",
      "content": "## Topics\n\n# Anirban Ghoshal\n\n## Articles by Anirban Ghoshal\n\n### Salesforce supercharges Agentforce with embedded AI, multimodal support, and industry-specific agents\n\nFrom auto-updating CRM fields to new industry agents and employee tools, Salesforce’s Summer ’25 update positions Agentforce as a no-code AI engine woven into every enterprise workflow.\n\n### Workday’s new dev tools help enterprises connect with external agents\n\nAmong the additions are some improvements to Workday’s Developer Copilot, which can now generate application code snippets, data queries, and functional orchestrations.\n\n### SAP teams up with Alibaba to host Cloud ERP workloads in China\n\nThe partnership will also see Alibaba adopt SAP offerings to run its own business. [...] With its acquisition of Dotmatics, a developer of software for managing data in life sciences research, Siemens hopes to help cut drug development costs.\n\n### Oracle’s AI Agent Studio is free for Fusion Cloud customers\n\nThe offering is expected to automate business processes, reducing manual effort and improving efficiency without heavy development overhead.\n\n### Salesforce takes on hyperscalers with Agentforce 2dx updates\n\nThe latest updates will help the CRM software provider leapfrog over its rivals in the agentic AI market, analysts say.\n\n### Salesforce’s AgentExchange targets AI agent adoption, monetization\n\nThe marketplace will initially feature action, topics, and templates from 200 partners for sales, service, finance, HR, productivity, and operations across various industries. [...] ### Why Microsoft is unifying data and AI within Fabric\n\nFabric is being transformed as a decision infrastructure layer to support faster decision making and easier development of agentic applications, analysts say.\n\n### New agentic AI tools bring new threat: agent sprawl\n\nVendors are lining up to offer enterprises tools to create AI agents for a plethora of different tasks — but it’s time to think about managing them.\n\n### Google’s AI innovations at Cloud Next 2025: What CIOs need to know\n\nThe announcements made at Google Cloud Next 2025 were designed to help CIOs adopt and scale agents while reducing integration complexity and costs.\n\n### Siemens grows its digital twin strategy into life sciences market",

      "title": "AI Consulting & Advisory service - You can with AI - KPMG in Ireland",
      "content": "## AI insights for your business\n\n## Unlock the value of AI with KPMG\n\n### AI, governance and organisational clarity\n\nEmma Coogan, AI campaign, AI consulting\n\n### Embedding AI in transformation programmes\n\nBreda O'Callaghan, AI campaign, KPMG Consulting\n\n### AI, risk and cyber security\n\nDani Michaux, EMA Cyber Lead\n\n### AI, capability and pace\n\nTania Kuklina, Director\n\n### Driving value from AI\n\nRory Timlin, Partner, Management Consulting\n\n### AI and data\n\nAlan Lavery AI campaign\n\n### Redefining leadership in the age of AI\n\nColm O'Neill AI campaign\n\n## Our AI strategic partnerships and alliances\n\nMicrosoft logo\nGoogle Cloud logo\nUniphore logo\nSAP logo\nServicenow logo\nWorkday logo\nDatabricks logo\nOnetrust logo\n\n## Get in touch with our AI Consulting team [...] #### \n\n###### \n\nKPMG Ireland - Audit, Tax, Deal Advisory & Consulting\nKPMG Ireland - Audit, Tax, Deal Advisory & Consulting\n\n#### \n\n###### \n\nYour search term was too short.\n\nSearch terms must include 3 or more characters.\n\n# You can with AI\n\nFrom driving value and growing margin, to unlocking insights in your data and from building resilience to empowering your people, we can show you how you can with AI.\n\nWe help organisations move beyond experimentation by applying a structured, enterprise-ready approach that’s grounded in governance, sectoral insights and delivery experience.\n\nOur trusted Al services can support your transition from pilot initiatives to scalable capabilities that are tied to the commercial value your business needs.\n\n## AI insights for your business [...] ## Get in touch with our AI Consulting team\n\nAt KPMG we understand the pressure business leaders are under to get it right on tech and AI.\n\nTo find out more about how KPMG perspectives and fresh thinking can help your business please contact our AI team today.\n\nOwen Lewis\n\n###### Owen Lewis\n\nPartner, Head of AI & Velocity, Head of Banking & Capital Markets, Global Lead for Financial Services Cost Transformation\n\nKPMG in Ireland\n\nRory Timlin\n\n###### Rory Timlin\n\nPartner, Management Consulting\n\nKPMG in Ireland\n\nAnna Scally\n\n###### Anna Scally\n\nPartner, Global Head of Technology, Media, and Telecommunications\n\nKPMG in Ireland\n\nDani Michaux\n\n###### Dani Michaux\n\nEMA Cyber Leader\n\nKPMG in Ireland\n\nTania Kuklina\n\n###### Tania Kuklina\n\nDirector\n\nKPMG in Ireland\n\nAlan Lavery\n\n###### Alan Lavery",

    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/xx/en/what-we-do/services/ai/trusted-ai-framework.html",
      "title": "KPMG Trusted AI framework",
      "content": "Human-centric\n\nWe prioritize human impact as we deploy AI and recognize the needs of our clients and our people. We are embracing this technology to empower and augment human capabilities — to unleash creativity and improve productivity in a way that allows people to reimagine how they spend their days.\n\nTrustworthy\n\nWe will adhere to our principles and the ethical pillars that guide how and why we use AI across its lifecycle. We will strive to ensure our data acquisition, governance and usage practices upholds ethical standards and complies with applicable privacy and data protection regulations, as well as any confidentiality requirements. [...] ### Privacy\n\nAI solutions should be designed to comply with applicable privacy and data protection laws and regulations.\n\n### Sustainability\n\nAI solutions should be designed to be energy efficient, reduce carbon emissions and support a cleaner environment.\n\n### Explainability\n\nAI solutions should be developed and delivered in a way that answers the questions of how and why a conclusion was drawn from the solution.\n\n### Integrity\n\nData used in AI solutions should be acquired in compliance with applicable laws and regulations and assessed for accuracy, completeness, appropriateness and quality to drive trusted decisions.\n\n### Transparency [...] ### Transparency\n\nAI solutions should include responsible disclosure to provide stakeholders with a clear understanding of what is happening in each solution across the AI lifecycle.\n\n### Fairness\n\nAI solutions should be designed to reduce or eliminate bias against individuals, communities and groups.\n\n### Accountability\n\nHuman oversight and responsibility should be embedded across the AI lifecycle to manage risk and comply with applicable laws and regulations.\n\n### Reliability\n\nAI solutions should consistently operate in accordance with their intended purpose and scope and at the desired level of precision.\n\n### Security\n\nRobust and resilient practices should be implemented to safeguard AI solutions against bad actors, misinformation or adverse events.\n\n### Safety",
      "score": 0.06841928,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment",
      "content": "### Additional Information\n\nWork Personas\n\nWe approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust. Work personas (flexible, remote, or required in office) are categories that are assigned to ServiceNow employees depending on the nature of their work and their assigned work location. Learn more here. To determine eligibility for a work persona, ServiceNow may confirm the distance between your primary residence and the closest ServiceNow office using a third-party service.\n\nEqual Opportunity Employer [...] # Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment\n\n### Company Description\n\nIt all started when engineer Fred Luddy wrote code that automated a tedious task for his coworker, Phyllis. She cried tears of joy. That moment inspired Fred to build a company that could do that for everyone—freeing people from busywork so they could focus on meaningful work. Today, ServiceNow is the AI control tower for business reinvention. Our ServiceNow AI platform brings together any AI, any data, and any workflow— helping 85% of the Fortune 500® work smarter, faster, and better. We're building an AI-native culture where technology and talent are unstoppable together. And we're just getting started.\n\nJoin us to put AI to work for people.\n\n### Job Description [...] Equal Opportunity Employer\n\nServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements.\n\nAccommodations",

      "title": "Banking",
      "content": "### AI Enablement for the Public Service Workforce\n\n###### Enable officers to use AI confidently in day to day roles, not as isolated specialists\n\n###### Redesign work and operating models so humans and AI collaborate effectively\n\n###### Build AI literacy and adoption across frontline, policy, risk, and corporate functions\n\n### Built to scale securely across government\n\n###### Build AI ready data and technology foundations aligned to government architectures\n\n###### Move beyond fragmented tools to scalable, secure enterprise platforms\n\n###### Enable AI to scale safely across agencies, functions, and channels\n\n## The Banking sector value propositions through KPMG’s Four-Door Framework\n\n### VALUE\n\nAI Strategy & Value Realisation (Banking outcomes by design)\n\n### TRUST [...] Explore practical use cases, governance considerations, and scaling strategies for the public sector.\n\n## Why work with KPMG\n\n###### Anchor AI in strategy, not hype\n\n###### Force clarity before investment\n\n###### Prove value before transformation\n\n###### Reduce risk while driving impact\n\n## Start small. Work with giants.\n\nAlliance Partners\n\n## Attend our latest event on trusted AI agents\n\nMay\n\n26\n\n### KPMG AI CoE Open Day\n\nExperience AI in action with real use cases, practical demos, and expert insights.\n\ndate\\_range\n26 May, 2026\n\nschedule\n09:00AM - 06:00PM SGT\n\n## More insights\n\n### Intelligent banking\n\n#### Intelligent banking\n\nBanking industry: A blueprint for creating value in the banking industry through AI-driven transformation\n\nfuturistic office people\n\n## Get in touch [...] AI CoE\n\n## The Government Sector value propositions through KPMG’s four-door Framework\n\n### AI Strategy & Value Realisation\n\n###### Identify high‑impact AI use cases across services, policy, risk, and operations\n\n###### Prioritise initiatives based on public value, feasibility, and risk\n\n###### Scale AI from pilots to secure, enterprise‑wide adoption\n\n### Trusted & Responsible AI (Public Trust by Design)\n\n###### Embed governance, security, and regulatory alignment into AI by design\n\n###### Manage model risk, data quality, privacy, and third‑party exposure, especially for sensitive public data\n\n###### Build confidence with boards and regulators through transparent, auditable AI decisions\n\n### AI Enablement for the Public Service Workforce",

Device-based policies aren’t supported. | Any My Apps app service | Android and iOS |\n| Microsoft Teams Services - this client app controls all services that support Microsoft Teams and all its Client Apps - Windows Desktop, iOS, Android, WP, and web client | Microsoft Teams | Windows 10, iOS, Android, and macOS |\n| Office 2016 apps, Universal Office apps, Office 2013 (with modern authentication), OneDrive sync client | SharePoint Online | Windows 10 | [...] Exchange Web Services (EWS) - A programming interface used by Outlook, Outlook for Mac, and non-Microsoft apps.\n       IMAP4 - Used by IMAP email clients.\n       MAPI over HTTP (MAPI/HTTP) - Used by Outlook 2010 and later.\n       Offline Address Book (OAB) - A copy of address list collections that are downloaded and used by Outlook.\n       Outlook Anywhere (RPC over HTTP) - Used by Outlook 2016 and earlier.\n       Outlook Service - Used by the Mail and Calendar app for Windows 10.\n       POP3 - Used by POP email clients.\n       Reporting Web Services - Used to retrieve report data in Exchange Online.",

    {
      "url": "https://techcrunch.com/sponsor/r1/a-hospital-can-save-your-life-in-four-hours-and-wait-three-months-to-get-paid-for-it",
      "title": "A hospital can save your life in four hours and wait three months to get paid for it | TechCrunch",
      "content": "## $1.4 billion in AI, and the same problems\n\nHealthcare organizations are adopting AI twice as fast as the rest of the U.S. economy and spent $1.4 billion on it last year alone, according to Menlo Ventures. And yet the economics of getting paid have not changed.\n\nThe pattern is familiar. HR ran on fragmented tools until Workday connected the full process. IT operations ran on disconnected dashboards until ServiceNow built a platform. Sales teams managed pipelines across three systems until Salesforce unified the workflow. In every case, the winning company built an operating system that connected all steps under a single intelligent layer, and the platform became more valuable with each additional customer. Hospital revenue is the next version of that story. [...] The patient rarely receives one bill; rather, they could receive several bills for the variety of services provided. The emergency physician group bills separately from the hospital. The radiologist bills separately. The anesthesiologist bills separately. A single ER visit generates three or four invoices from different entities, weeks apart, with no explanation of what the patient owes. Every step runs on different software, shares no intelligence with any other step, and costs American hospitals more than 40 cents of every dollar they spend.\n\n## $1.4 billion in AI, and the same problems [...] ## What changes when hospitals get paid in days instead of months\n\nWhen payment takes months, hospitals sit on billions in unpaid claims. Surgeons spend more time documenting for insurance companies than talking to patients. Patients receive surprise bills weeks after a procedure they assumed was covered.\n\n“Instead of adding another point solution, R1’s R37 Lab is working on AI that can reason across payer policy, coding accuracy, and denial prevention in one connected framework. Real-time adjudication is the goal and the holy grail in this space, and we’re within reach.”\n\n— Lee Kupferman, Co-CEO, R1 R37 AI Lab",
      "score": 0.48908922,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/ai/microsoft-iq",
      "title": "Unified Enterprise Intelligence for AI - Microsoft IQ",
      "content": "### Self-service HR agents for employees\n\nHelp desks are overwhelmed with routine questions, and employees waste time hunting for answers across policy docs, and word of mouth. Deploy an agent that instantly answers common HR and IT questions—grounded in your actual policies via Foundry IQ—and auto-creates tickets when human help is needed.  \n   \n Power users can build and publish this agent in Copilot Studio with point-and-click connectors to ServiceNow, Workday, and SharePoint. Developers who need full control can use Foundry and the Microsoft Agent Framework to integrate MCP servers, custom APIs, and enterprise-grade scaling—all surfaced through Teams and M365 Copilot.\n\nLearn more\n\nA women working on laptop.\n\n### AI-driven time-to-market acceleration [...] ### Sales-led engagement\n\nSellers waste hours researching prospects or skip it entirely, sending generic outreach. Deploy agents that identify warm paths, pull company news and relationship signals via Work IQ, score fit in Fabric, and deliver prioritized briefings with talking points—all before the first call. A B2B tech company saw outbound response rates increase double-digits while sellers spent far less time on pre-call research.\n\nLearn more\n\nA person smiling while working at a desk\n\nBack to Use-Cases section\n\nSecurity\n\n## No intelligence without trust\n\n### Secure foundations [...] Microsoft IQ is built on four integrated components that give AI a unified understanding of the enterprise:  \n  + Work IQ – Continuously builds a rich, up-to-date semantic understanding across Microsoft 365, organizational systems, and external sources, to help agents efficiently navigate vast, constantly changing organizational context.\n  + Fabric IQ – Grounds AI in how the business operates by using shared definitions, metrics, and relationships already modeled in enterprise data and analytics systems, enabling consistent reasoning.\n  + Foundry IQ – Unlocks and preserves enterprise knowledge across systems through policy‑compliant access, so agents reuse trusted knowledge while maintaining business meaning as they act.",
      "score": 0.4613328,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/profile/anirban-ghoshal/page/7",
      "title": "Anirban Ghoshal | Page 7 - CIO",
      "content": "## Topics\n\n# Anirban Ghoshal\n\n## Articles by Anirban Ghoshal\n\n### Salesforce supercharges Agentforce with embedded AI, multimodal support, and industry-specific agents\n\nFrom auto-updating CRM fields to new industry agents and employee tools, Salesforce’s Summer ’25 update positions Agentforce as a no-code AI engine woven into every enterprise workflow.\n\n### Workday’s new dev tools help enterprises connect with external agents\n\nAmong the additions are some improvements to Workday’s Developer Copilot, which can now generate application code snippets, data queries, and functional orchestrations.\n\n### SAP teams up with Alibaba to host Cloud ERP workloads in China\n\nThe partnership will also see Alibaba adopt SAP offerings to run its own business. [...] With its acquisition of Dotmatics, a developer of software for managing data in life sciences research, Siemens hopes to help cut drug development costs.\n\n### Oracle’s AI Agent Studio is free for Fusion Cloud customers\n\nThe offering is expected to automate business processes, reducing manual effort and improving efficiency without heavy development overhead.\n\n### Salesforce takes on hyperscalers with Agentforce 2dx updates\n\nThe latest updates will help the CRM software provider leapfrog over its rivals in the agentic AI market, analysts say.\n\n### Salesforce’s AgentExchange targets AI agent adoption, monetization\n\nThe marketplace will initially feature action, topics, and templates from 200 partners for sales, service, finance, HR, productivity, and operations across various industries. [...] ### Why Microsoft is unifying data and AI within Fabric\n\nFabric is being transformed as a decision infrastructure layer to support faster decision making and easier development of agentic applications, analysts say.\n\n### New agentic AI tools bring new threat: agent sprawl\n\nVendors are lining up to offer enterprises tools to create AI agents for a plethora of different tasks — but it’s time to think about managing them.\n\n### Google’s AI innovations at Cloud Next 2025: What CIOs need to know\n\nThe announcements made at Google Cloud Next 2025 were designed to help CIOs adopt and scale agents while reducing integration complexity and costs.\n\n### Siemens grows its digital twin strategy into life sciences market",
      "score": 0.4352539,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://careers.servicenow.com/jobs/744000129995998/staff-ai-agent-engineer-moveworks-customer-deployment",
      "title": "Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment",
      "content": "### Additional Information\n\nWork Personas\n\nWe approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust. Work personas (flexible, remote, or required in office) are categories that are assigned to ServiceNow employees depending on the nature of their work and their assigned work location. Learn more here. To determine eligibility for a work persona, ServiceNow may confirm the distance between your primary residence and the closest ServiceNow office using a third-party service.\n\nEqual Opportunity Employer [...] # Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment\n\n### Company Description\n\nIt all started when engineer Fred Luddy wrote code that automated a tedious task for his coworker, Phyllis. She cried tears of joy. That moment inspired Fred to build a company that could do that for everyone—freeing people from busywork so they could focus on meaningful work. Today, ServiceNow is the AI control tower for business reinvention. Our ServiceNow AI platform brings together any AI, any data, and any workflow— helping 85% of the Fortune 500® work smarter, faster, and better. We're building an AI-native culture where technology and talent are unstoppable together. And we're just getting started.\n\nJoin us to put AI to work for people.\n\n### Job Description [...] Equal Opportunity Employer\n\nServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements.\n\nAccommodations",

    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/ingestion/default-parsers/servicenow-cmdb",
      "title": "Collect ServiceNow CMDB data  |  Google Security Operations  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "`cmdb_ci`\n`cmdb_ci_server`\n`cmdb_ci_computer`\n`cmdb_ci_linux_server`\n`cmdb_ci_win_server`\n`cmdb_ci_vm_instance`\n`cmdb_ci_network_adapter`\n`cmdb_ci_ip_address`\n`cmdb_ci_service`\n`cmdb_ci_appl`\n`cmdb_ci`\n\n## Verify REST API access\n\nBefore creating the feed, verify that your ServiceNow API credentials work correctly.\n\n### Test using REST API Explorer (optional)\n\n`cmdb_ci`\n\n### Test using curl (optional)\n\nAlternatively, test the API using curl:\n\n`curl \" \\\n--request GET \\\n--header \"Accept: application/json\" \\\n--user 'your-username':'your-password'`\n`your-instance.service-now.com`\n`your-username`\n`your-password`\n\nA successful response will return JSON data with configuration items.\n\n## Set up feeds\n\nTo configure a feed, follow these steps: [...] Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Collect ServiceNow CMDB data\n\nThis document explains how to collect ServiceNow CMDB data by setting up a Google Security Operations feed using the Third party API.\n\n## Before you begin\n\nEnsure that you have the following prerequisites:\n\n`cmdb_read`\n\n## Configure IP allowlisting\n\nBefore creating the feed, you must allowlist Google SecOps IP ranges in your ServiceNow firewall or network settings.\n\n### Get Google SecOps IP ranges\n\n### Add IP ranges to ServiceNow\n\n`192.0.2.0/24`\n`GGoogle SecOps IP Range 1`\n\n## Configure ServiceNow API access\n\nTo enable Google SecOps to pull CMDB data, you need to create a ServiceNow user with appropriate permissions.\n\n### Create dedicated integration user (recommended) [...] | `company` | `entity.asset.attribute.labels.value` | Owning company/organization |",
      "score": 0.021766657,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/investigate-detections-in-search",
      "title": "Investigate detections in Search | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "`detection.created_time.nanos > 621828000 AND detection.feedback_summary.priority = \"PRIORITY_CRITICAL\"`\n\nFind all detections where the investigation reason is marked as malicious:\n\n`detection.investigation.reason = \"REASON_MALICIOUS\"`\n\nBasic detection search:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\ndetection.detection.rule_type = \"SINGLE_EVENT\"\ndetection.detection.rule_name = /.+/`\n\nExport to datatable:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name = /.+/\noutcome:\n$x = detection.id\nexport:\n%new_detection_table.write_row(\ntesting: $x\n)`\n\nStatistical query (search on aggregated results):\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\nmatch:\ndetection.detection.rule_name\noutcome:\n$detection_count = count(detection.id)\norder:\n$detection_count desc`\n\n## Enable programmatic API [...] Use this table to map specific issues to actionable fixes.\n\nIssue | Description | Fix || Exceeded result limit | The query returns more than 100,000 results (or 10,000 for statistical queries). | Refine your search query with filters or reduce the time range to narrow down the dataset. |\n| Exceeded time range | The search spans more than 365 days. | Adjust the start and end times in the search parameters to span one year or less. |\n| API sorting error | Sorting fails when using collection proto fields. | Ensure the sorting fields are prefixed with `detection.` instead of `collection.`. |\n\n`detection.`\n`collection.`\n\n### Validation and testing\n\nTo determine if detections search is enabled, check if the Run search button\nstays disabled after entering a query. If it does, contact Support. [...] Value: Lets you identify compromised entities that are\nconsistently generating alerts across the environment. Instead of manually\nchecking individual rules, you can perform a single search to see the full\nscope of an entity's suspicious activity.\n\n### Granular attribute analysis\n\nObjective: Search for detections based on specific fields, such as\nparticular network connection types or log sources, rather than just rule names.\n\nValue: Enables targeted searching within the detection fields. Since\nmultiple rules might trigger on similar network behaviors, searching by the\nspecific attribute helps you isolate related security findings that would\notherwise be buried across disparate rule sets.\n\n### Incident scope identification",
      "score": 0.021175781,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/event-processing/entity-graph",
      "title": "Using the Entity Context Graph (ECG) | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "| Derived context | Google SecOps-generated | Google SecOps generates statistical data based on analysis of ingested activity. It enriches events and entities from various sources within your environment (for example, Windows AD, Azure AD, Okta, Google Cloud, IAM).  For example:   Calculates and enriches each entity with a prevalence statistic that indicates its popularity in the environment.  Calculates and enriches the entity first-seen-time and last-seen-time. |\n| Global context | Google-sourced | Global sources provide internal and external threat intelligence and reputation data.  For example:   Ingests and stores Google Threat Intelligence data.  Enriches entities with information from Safe Browsing threat lists.  Enriches entities with WHOIS data. | [...] The ECG also calculates summary metrics for entities. These include\nprevalence (how often a specific entity occurs in your UDM data compared to other entities),\nand an entity's `first-seen-time` and `last-seen-time`. It also identifies key enrichment\nsources and indicators of compromise (IOC) sources like Google Threat Intelligence (GTI),\nSafe Browsing, WHOIS, and VirusTotal data.\n\n`first-seen-time`\n`last-seen-time`\n\nThe ECG uses UDM events to do the following:\n\n## Data sources for the ECG\n\nThe ECG pipeline combines data from the following sources: [...] `entity.domain.first_seen_time`\n`entity.domain.last_seen_time`\n`entity.file.first_seen_time`\n`entity.file.last_seen_time`\n`entity.artifact.first_seen_time`\n`entity.artifact.last_seen_time`\n`entity.asset.first_seen_time`\n`entity.user.first_seen_time`\n\n#### Exceptions for first-seen-time and last-seen-time calculations:\n\n`asset`\n`user`\n`first_seen_time`\n`last_seen_time`\n`events`\n`group`\n`resource`\n\n## Global context enrichments\n\nThese sources include external threat intelligence and reputation data from both\ninternal and third-party global sources.\n\n### Ingest Google Threat Intelligence data\n\nGoogle SecOps ingests data from Google Threat Intelligence (GTI)\ndata sources, providing contextual information for investigating activity in your environment.\n\nQuery the following data sources:",
      "score": 0.020459222,
      "raw_content": null
    },

`log_type` | `network.dns.questions` | Mapped: `named` → `query` |\n| `query` | `network.dns.questions` | Merged |\n| `question` | `network.dns.questions` | Merged |\n| `ses` | `network.session_id` | `principal.mac` | Mapped: `dhcpd` → `client_mac` | [...] `sudo systemctl status observiq-otel-collector`\n\nCheck logs for errors:\n\n`sudo journalctl -u observiq-otel-collector -f`\n\nTo restart the running:\n\n`sc query observiq-otel-collector`\n\nCheck logs for errors:\n\n`type \"C:\\Program Files\\observIQ OpenTelemetry Collector\\log\\collector.log\"`\n\n## Configure BlueCat DDI syslog forwarding\n\n### Configure syslog on BlueCat Address Manager\n\n`192.168.1.100`\n`514`\n\n### Configure syslog on BlueCat DNS/DHCP Server (BDDS) via CLI\n\nIf you prefer to configure syslog       "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/ai/ai-in-finance.html",
      "title": "What is AI in Finance? | Workday IN",
      "content": "Companies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations.\n\nWe believe that for AI to truly deliver on its future promise, it must be trustworthy. That’s why Workday has a Machine Learning Trust program that provides governance in defining procedures for the development and management of AI at Workday. This work helps us deliver technology benefits to our customers in line with our core values.​ As part of our commitment to trustworthy AI, we embrace the following principles: [...] As organizations continue to place a greater focus on AI, it’s critical that business leaders can trust their AI. At Workday, our approach leverages ethical AI principles that are built into the architecture of our finance solutions.\n\nCompanies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations. [...] Workday has embedded AI into the very core of our platform. Leading finance organizations are already using AI technology in Workday to help deliver better employee experiences, improve operational efficiencies, and provide insights for faster data-driven decision-making.\n\nIMC Financial Markets logo\n\n“Workday Journal Insights means one less thing for our end users to check off their list at the end of the month.       "title": "Workday GO | Medium Enterprise Software",
      "content": "### Questions?     {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html",
      "title": "Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog",
      "content": "That’s why the Workday CLM product team is currently developing new AI tools to automate the drafting of contract playbooks based on existing contracts, with the goal of making playbook creation as simple as uploading sample documents (like existing agreements and policy notes) from which our AI can derive preferred and fallback language and criteria.\n\nWe’re also working to deliver users proactive insights over time about their teams’ adherence to those playbooks, so they can make informed decisions about keeping those playbooks current and optimized. Watch this space!\n\n## Staying Ahead of the Curve [...] The Workday Contract Negotiation Agent removes the friction of the drafting process, allowing you to focus on the strategic outcomes your contracts are actually meant to secure—achieving the true ends of your negotiation faster and more easily than ever before.\n\nWant to learn more? Book a demo today to see the Workday Contract Negotiation Agent in action.\n\nWhat Is Agentic AI?\n\nStill unsure on the difference between the types of AI agents, or just need a refresher on the basics? Workday has you covered.  \n   \n Learn More\n\nWorkday’s Bold Future With Responsible Agentic AI\n\nAgentic AI is here, which means the correct governance is crucial. Learn how Workday is doubling down on its commitment to responsible AI.\n\nRead Blog\n\nTrust by Design: How Workday Builds AI That Puts People First [...] It leverages sophisticated AI orchestration, enabling it to choose the best AI tool to support the task at hand. Instead of relying exclusively on one large language model (LLM), its capabilities grow naturally with the latest advances in AI.\n Its foundational, purpose-built AI helps ensure accurate results from LLMs by conducting core analysis of the customer’s contract repository and creating a trusted base of structured data to enable retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\n It’s fully customizable, enabling customers to create their own AI models to extract and generate all the contract data points they care about over time, rather than being limited to a finite set of core extractions.\n\n## Driving AI Responsibility",
      "score": 0.78334314,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-us/perspectives/ai/values-outlast-regulations.html",
      "title": "Leading with Values in the Age of AI Regulation | Workday US",
      "content": "## 4. Assemble A Socio-Technical Team\n\nBuilding a durable program is a company-wide effort. Because AI systems are sociotechnical by nature, governing their development and use requires expertise across technology, science, law, human behavior, organizational change, and risk. Most modern org charts don’t put all of that expertise in one place, which is why responsible AI requires coordinated, cross-functional teams rather than isolated compliance silos.\n\nAt Workday, that effort is led by Trindel and the dedicated Responsible AI team she built. The team brings together scientists, governance experts, and training and enablement specialists to evaluate AI systems, develop and manage scalable processes, and build organizational literacy around responsible AI across the company. [...] ## 4. Assemble A Socio-Technical Team\n\nBuilding a durable program is a company-wide effort. Because AI systems are sociotechnical by nature, governing their development and use requires expertise across technology, science, law, human behavior, organizational change, and risk. Most modern org charts don’t put all of that expertise in one place, which is why responsible AI requires coordinated, cross-functional teams rather than isolated compliance silos.\n\nAt Workday, that effort is led by Trindel and the dedicated Responsible AI team she built. The team brings together scientists, governance experts, and training and enablement specialists to evaluate AI systems, develop and manage scalable processes, and build organizational literacy around responsible AI across the company. [...] Audio also available on Apple Podcasts and Spotify.\n\nA real gap is emerging between organizations building AI safeguards now and those waiting for the regulatory dust to settle. At Workday, Chief Responsible AI Officer Kelly Trindel and Chief Legal Officer Rich Sauer argue that waiting for perfect regulatory clarity is a losing game. Compliance deadlines arrive quickly, even as the technology moves too fast for lawmakers to stay ahead.",
      "score": 0.76385427,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://apply.deloitte.com/en_US/careers/JobDetail/Delivery-Senior-Consultant-Workday-HCM-Payroll-Benefits-Module-s/354651",
      "title": "Delivery Senior Consultant, Workday HCM Payroll/Benefits Module(s)",
      "content": "### Position Summary\n\nOur Deloitte Human Capital team helps organizations create value through people performance. We work with clients to reimagine work, the workforce, and the workplace across the enterprise and to transform their HR functions with AI and emerging technology. With the rapid pace of change in today’s world, you will help clients answer questions like: How do I access, develop, and motivate my workforce? What should my AI strategy be for the HR function? Do I have the right organization and culture to enable performance? Join our team to make work better for humans and humans better at work.\n\nWork You'll Do\n\nAs a Delivery Senior Consultant for our team, you will be responsible for:\n\nA successful candidate would possess these skills:\n\nThe Team [...] The Team\n\nDeloitte’s Government & Public Services (GPS) practice – our people, ideas, technology and outcomes – is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise.\n\nOur HR Strategy & Technology offering develops leading global HR strategies and implements HR technologies that drive transformation, maximize the workforce experience, and sustain HR effectiveness and value while navigating a world of disruption. [...] © 2026. See  Terms of Use  for more information.\n\nDeloitte refers to one or more of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee (\"DTTL\"), its network of member firms, and their related entities. DTTL and each of its member firms are legally separate and independent entities. DTTL (also referred to as \"Deloitte Global\") does not provide services to clients. In the United States, Deloitte refers to one or more of the US member firms of DTTL, their related entities that operate using the \"Deloitte\" name in the United States and their respective affiliates. Certain services may not be available to attest clients under the rules and regulations of public accounting. Touch device users, explore by touch or with swipe gestures.\n\ncancel\n\nYour search term was too short.\n\nSearch terms must include 3 or more characters.\n\n Previous search terms \n\nclose\n\n Cancel \n\nVideo 1\n\nImage 2\n\nVideo Player is loading.\n\nPlay Video\n\nPause Skip Backward Skip Forward Next playlist item\n\nUnmute [...] ## Help us improve your experience with cookies\n\nCookies help us run our website smoothly, understand how it's used and tailor information to your interests. Some cookies are essential and required for you to browse the website. Other are optional and can only be used with your consent. You can manage your preferences at any time by clicking manage choices. Read more in our privacy statement.\n\nReject Accept All\n\nImage 23: Company Logo\n\n## Privacy preference center\n\n   ### We value your privacy \n   ### Strictly Necessary Cookies \n   ### Functional Cookies \n   ### Performance Cookies \n   ### Targeting Cookies \n\n#### We value your privacy [...] #### We value your privacy\n\nWhen you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. \n\nPrivacy statement\n\n#### Strictly Necessary Cookies\n\nAlways Active",
