    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/xx/en/what-we-do/services/ai/trusted-ai-framework.html",
      "title": "KPMG Trusted AI framework",
      "content": "Human-centric\n\nWe prioritize human impact as we deploy AI and recognize the needs of our clients and our people. We are embracing this technology to empower and augment human capabilities — to unleash creativity and improve productivity in a way that allows people to reimagine how they spend their days.\n\nTrustworthy\n\nWe will adhere to our principles and the ethical pillars that guide how and why we use AI across its lifecycle. We will strive to ensure our data acquisition, governance and usage practices upholds ethical standards and complies with applicable privacy and data protection regulations, as well as any confidentiality requirements. [...] ### Privacy\n\nAI solutions should be designed to comply with applicable privacy and data protection laws and regulations.\n\n### Sustainability\n\nAI solutions should be designed to be energy efficient, reduce carbon emissions and support a cleaner environment.\n\n### Explainability\n\nAI solutions should be developed and delivered in a way that answers the questions of how and why a conclusion was drawn from the solution.\n\n### Integrity\n\nData used in AI solutions should be acquired in compliance with applicable laws and regulations and assessed for accuracy, completeness, appropriateness and quality to drive trusted decisions.\n\n### Transparency [...] ### Transparency\n\nAI solutions should include responsible disclosure to provide stakeholders with a clear understanding of what is happening in each solution across the AI lifecycle.\n\n### Fairness\n\nAI solutions should be designed to reduce or eliminate bias against individuals, communities and groups.\n\n### Accountability\n\nHuman oversight and responsibility should be embedded across the AI lifecycle to manage risk and comply with applicable laws and regulations.\n\n### Reliability\n\nAI solutions should consistently operate in accordance with their intended purpose and scope and at the desired level of precision.\n\n### Security\n\nRobust and resilient practices should be implemented to safeguard AI solutions against bad actors, misinformation or adverse events.\n\n### Safety",
      "score": 0.06841928,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/be/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] A financial services company is building agentic workflows to automatically capture meeting actions from video conferences, draft communications to remind participants of their next steps, and track follow-through.\n An air carrier is using AI agents to help customers complete the most common transactions, such as rebooking a flight or rerouting bags, freeing up human agents to address more complex matters.\n A manufacturer is using AI agents to support new product development initiatives, leveraging AI to find the optimal balance between competing objectives such as cost and time-to-market.\n In the public sector, AI agents are being used to cover workforce shortages, partnering with human workers to complete key processes.",

    },

Today, ServiceNow is the AI control tower for business reinvention. Our ServiceNow AI platform brings together any AI, any data, and any workflow— helping 85% of the Fortune 500® work smarter, faster, and better. We're building an AI-native culture where technology and talent are unstoppable together. And we're just getting started.\n\nJoin us to put AI to work for people.\n\n### Job Description [...] Equal Opportunity Employer\n\nServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer.       "title": "20 tricks for more efficient Android messaging - Computerworld",
      "content": "Surely you’ve been in that type of thread before, right?       "title": "E-News from KPMG’s EU Tax Centre: E-News 230",
      "content": "### Türkiye\n\nOmnibus law introducing tax reforms approved\n\nOn May 21, 2026, the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye approved an omnibus law introducing several tax reforms.\n\nKey measures include:\n\nThe legislation now awaits presidential approval and publication in the Official Gazette before entering into force.\n\n### United Kingdom\n\nHMRC adopts OECD transitional approach for Pillar Two GIR filing\n\nOn May 19, 2026, His Majesty's Revenue and Customs (HMRC) released guidance to reflect the OECD common understanding for the first reporting year. HMRC confirmed that it will not enforce local GIR filing requirements and will reduce certain penalties to nil where: [...] ### Ireland\n\nGuidance issued on GIR filing and notification requirements under Pillar Two\n\nOn May 26, 2026, Irish Revenue issued detailed guidance on the GIR filing and notification requirements under the Pillar Two rules.\n\nKey clarifications include:\n\nFor more information on the recent Pillar Two amendments in Ireland, please refer to E-News Issue 227.\n\n### Poland\n\nTax Ordinance amendment including revision of Polish Mandatory Disclosure Rules\n\nOn May 15, 2026, the lower chamber of the Parliament (Sejm) approved a bill amending the Tax Ordinance (Ordynacja Podatkowa) to amend, inter alia, the Polish Mandatory Disclosure Rules (MDR). [...] Where the GIR is not received within six months, the DGFiP may require local filing and apply late filing penalties until the obligation is fulfilled. The transitional approach does not apply where the GIR is filed in jurisdictions not included in the OECD annex.\n\n### Greece\n\nOmnibus tax reform law enacted\n\nOn May 15, 2026, Greece enacted the Omnibus Tax reform, which was published in the official government gazette. The law introduces a wide range of tax measures across corporate taxation, indirect taxes, and transparency rules.\n\nKey measures include:\n\nThe legislation is effective following its publication on May 15, 2026, with further administrative guidance expected.\n\nFor more information, please refer to an April report and a May report published by KPMG in Greece.\n\n### Ireland",

      "title": "What is AI in Finance? | Workday IN",
      "content": "Companies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations.\n\nWe believe that for AI to truly deliver on its future promise, it must be trustworthy. That’s why Workday has a Machine Learning Trust program that provides governance in defining procedures for the development and management of AI at Workday. This work helps us deliver technology benefits to our customers in line with our core values.​ As part of our commitment to trustworthy AI, we embrace the following principles: [...] As organizations continue to place a greater focus on AI, it’s critical that business leaders can trust their AI. At Workday, our approach leverages ethical AI principles that are built into the architecture of our finance solutions.\n\nCompanies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation.     {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html",
      "title": "Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog",
      "content": "That’s why the Workday CLM product team is currently developing new AI tools to automate the drafting of contract playbooks based on existing contracts, with the goal of making playbook creation as simple as uploading sample documents (like existing agreements and policy notes) from which our AI can derive preferred and fallback language and criteria.\n\nWe’re also working to deliver users proactive insights over time about their teams’ adherence to those playbooks, so they can make informed decisions about keeping those playbooks current and optimized. Watch this space!\n\n## Staying Ahead of the Curve [...] The Workday Contract Negotiation Agent removes the friction of the drafting process, allowing you to focus on the strategic outcomes your contracts are actually meant to secure—achieving the true ends of your negotiation faster and more easily than ever before.\n\nWant to learn more? Book a demo today to see the Workday Contract Negotiation Agent in action.\n\nWhat Is Agentic AI?\n\nStill unsure on the difference between the types of AI agents, or just need a refresher on the basics? Workday has you covered.  \n   \n Learn More\n\nWorkday’s Bold Future With Responsible Agentic AI\n\nAgentic AI is here, which means the correct governance is crucial. Learn how Workday is doubling down on its commitment to responsible AI.\n\nRead Blog\n\nTrust by Design: How Workday Builds AI That Puts People First [...] It leverages sophisticated AI orchestration, enabling it to choose the best AI tool to support the task at hand. Instead of relying exclusively on one large language model (LLM), its capabilities grow naturally with the latest advances in AI.\n Its foundational, purpose-built AI helps ensure accurate results from LLMs by conducting core analysis of the customer’s contract repository and creating a trusted base of structured data to enable retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\n It’s fully customizable, enabling customers to create their own AI models to extract and generate all the contract data points they care about over time, rather than being limited to a finite set of core extractions.\n\n## Driving AI Responsibility",
      "score": 0.78334314,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-us/perspectives/ai/values-outlast-regulations.html",
      "title": "Leading with Values in the Age of AI Regulation | Workday US",
      "content": "## 4. Assemble A Socio-Technical Team\n\nBuilding a durable program is a company-wide effort. Because AI systems are sociotechnical by nature, governing their development and use requires expertise across technology, science, law, human behavior, organizational change, and risk. Most modern org charts don’t put all of that expertise in one place, which is why responsible AI requires coordinated, cross-functional teams rather than isolated compliance silos.\n\nAt Workday, that effort is led by Trindel and the dedicated Responsible AI team she built. The team brings together scientists, governance experts, and training and enablement specialists to evaluate AI systems, develop and manage scalable processes, and build organizational literacy around responsible AI across the company. [...] ## 4. Assemble A Socio-Technical Team\n\nBuilding a durable program is a company-wide effort. Because AI systems are sociotechnical by nature, governing their development and use requires expertise across technology, science, law, human behavior, organizational change, and risk. Most modern org charts don’t put all of that expertise in one place, which is why responsible AI requires coordinated, cross-functional teams rather than isolated compliance silos.\n\nAt Workday, that effort is led by Trindel and the dedicated Responsible AI team she built. The team brings together scientists, governance experts, and training and enablement specialists to evaluate AI systems, develop and manage scalable processes, and build organizational literacy around responsible AI across the company. [...] Audio also available on Apple Podcasts and Spotify.\n\nA real gap is emerging between organizations building AI safeguards now and those waiting for the regulatory dust to settle. At Workday, Chief Responsible AI Officer Kelly Trindel and Chief Legal Officer Rich Sauer argue that waiting for perfect regulatory clarity is a losing game. Compliance deadlines arrive quickly, even as the technology moves too fast for lawmakers to stay ahead.",
      "score": 0.76385427,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/hr/hris.html",
      "title": "What Is an HRIS? How IT and HR Are Evolving Together - Workday",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management [...] #### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Payroll\n\n### Planning\n\nThe planning system that integrates with any ERP/GL or data source.\n\n#### Planning\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Close & Consolidation\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Operational Planning\n\n### Legal\n\nTake control of your contract data and drive results across your organization.\n\n#### Legal Overview\n\n#### Products\n\n#### Contract Intelligence\n\n#### Contract Lifecycle Management\n\n### Platform\n\nGain a flexible foundation that helps you move beyond the limitations of legacy ERP.\n\n#### IT Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Our Technology\n\n#### Extensions\n\n#### Orchestrate & Integrations\n\n#### Data Cloud\n\n#### Workday Extend\n\n### Workday ERP [...] HRIS: An HRIS collects and stores employee data. It compiles information on employee demographics, job status, contact information, compensation, and other areas.\n HRMS: An HRMS expands on the data management tools of an HRIS to include a wider range of software and utility. The term originated in the early twenty-first century to describe HR software suites supporting payroll, applicant tracking, benefits, and talent management.\n HCMS: An HCMS encompasses the functionality of an HRIS and HRMS while supporting a wider breadth of HR functions, including employee experience and analytics. HCM, in general, largely refers to HR suites designed with cloud computing at the core, ensuring they remain adaptive.",
      "score": 0.69399214,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://careers.cognizant.com/apj-ph/jobs/00069011002/workday-data-conversion-associate-consultant",
      "title": "Workday Data Conversion Associate Consultant, Plano,TX-8383 DominionPkwy, Texas, United States | Cognizant Careers",
      "content": "About us  \nCognizant (Nasdaq: CTSH) is an AI Builder and technology services provider, building the bridge between AI investment and enterprise value by building full-stack AI solutions for our clients. Our deep industry, process and engineering expertise enables us to build an organization’s unique context into technology systems that amplify human potential, realize tangible returns and keep global enterprises ahead in a fast-changing world. See how at www.cognizant.com or @cognizant. [...] Cognizant Careers\n\nCognizant Careers\n\n# Workday Data Conversion Associate Consultant\n\n Date published Jun 03 2026\n Location Plano,TX-8383 DominionPkwy / United States\n Job category Consulting\n Work model Hybrid\n\nCognizant Workday practice is recruiting for upcoming graduates who are looking for an exciting, entry level consulting opportunity starting in the Summer of 2026. We proudly partner with Workday, one of the leading cloud-based software solutions for HR, Finance and Payroll management. As a Data Conversion Associate Consultant at Cognizant Workday practice, you will be part of a project team working with customers to get the most out of their Workday Implementation. [...] Your career, your way\n\n### Your career, your way\n\nWith 90% of our associates building skills through GenAI training, job shadowing, industry certifications and more, you have everything you need to build a full career.\n\nReal-world impact\n\n### Real-world impact\n\nThink about the biggest brands you rely on. Chances are, they rely on us to help strengthen their business. Here, you’ll turn bold ideas into solutions that improve lives everywhere.\n\n> \"Cognizant has one of the best learning ecosystems you can ask for. From sponsored certifications to training platforms to mentorship, we have all that we need to grow.”\n\nDebashish Mishra SAP MM Developer",
      "score": 0.5726516,

    {
      "url": "https://www.theregister.com/ai-and-ml/2026/06/03/no-longer-just-a-copilot-microsofts-ai-wants-to-take-the-wheel/5250718",
      "title": "No longer just a Copilot, Microsoft's AI wants to take the wheel",
      "content": "TOPICS\n\nGo to frontpage. Logo, The Register\nGo to frontpage. Logo, The Register\nGo to frontpage. Logo, The Register\n\nAI AND ML\n\n# No longer just a Copilot, Microsoft's AI wants to take the wheel\n\nAlways-on agent promises to keep work moving, provided you trust it with practically everything\n\nMove over, Copilot: Microsoft is introducing a new category of agentic AI called \"Autopilot,\" starting with Scout, its first agent. And it doesn't take much guessing to understand how Microsoft expects these things to operate: By constantly watching your every move and taking action in the background to ostensibly streamline your workday. [...] Say, for example, you need to schedule a meeting: Scout can handle scheduling on its own while accounting for time zones; it can flag meetings it considers particularly important for its users and generate materials it believes users need to prepare before the scheduled time. Scout can also identify looming deadlines and block off time on a user’s calendar so that they can work on a particular project, “spot risks, like stalled decisions,” and basically act like a work nanny that schedules your day by being hyper-aware of every single little thing that needs to get done.\n\nHopefully, your new Microsoft nannybot is more reliable than its Copilot predecessor, whose outputs Microsoft itself warns may not always be accurate.\n\n### Get ready for a Claw-shaped hole in your environment [...] Microsoft announced Autopilot, and the first Autopilot agent, Scout, at Microsoft Build on Tuesday, describing it and other future Autopilots as “always-on agents that work autonomously,” stay active in the background to “understand how work gets done across your apps and systems,” and can “take action without needing to be prompted each time.”\n\nScout, for example, can be interacted with in Teams when one feels the need, but outside of instances when users need to query it directly, it’s always there.\n\n“It operates across cloud, desktop, and web, connecting to Teams, Outlook, OneDrive, and SharePoint, and to the data that powers your day, including chats, email, calendar, and contacts,” Omar Shahine, corporate VP of Microsoft Scout, wrote in the announcement.",
      "score": 0.48850358,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/careers/jobdetails?id=R00333881_en&title=Workday+Certified+Financials+Adaptive+Consultant",
      "title": "Workday Certified Financials Adaptive Consultant - Accenture",
      "content": "You are…\n\nAs a member of one of the world’s largest Workday-certified teams, you’ll be part of a team of advisory professionals who deliver more than just technology. From rapid deployments to entire transformations, you’ll deliver integrated, payroll, cloud-based HR and financial management solutions that put organizations on the fast track to a better way to work.\n\nAs a Workday Adaptive Insights Consultant, your primary responsibilities may include: [...] Identification of business requirements, functional design, process design (including scenario design, flow mapping), prototyping, testing, training, defining support procedures\n Lead workshops for client education\n Conduct working sessions with clients to gather, understand, and analyze business requirements\n Lead in architecting Workday Adaptive Insights solutions to ensure that all work streams are cohesive and cross functional impacts are considered and mitigated  Advise clients on industry standards and leading practices\n Demonstrate design options through the use of prototyping\n Understand and apply Workday and Accenture methodologies\n\nWhy should I join the Accenture Workday team? [...] #### Technology jobs: Be the catalyst\n\nGet hands-on with the technologies that our clients need to reinvent, work in new ways and change the world for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n#### Jobs in technology platforms: Create foundations for the future\n\nYou’ll build the tech that transforms how business works for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n## Learn more about the hiring process at Accenture\n\n Benefits information\n Hiring process (Q&A)\n Interview tips\n Using AI",

    {
      "url": "https://www.cognizant.com/us/en/insights/insights-blog/frontier-ai-exposes-enterprise-architecture-flaws",
      "title": "Beyond cybersecurity: AI exposes your entire architecture - Cognizant",
      "content": "##### Keep up with AI innovations for business\n\nAI is moving fast. Our bimonthly LinkedIn newsletter helps you do the same. Subscribe for breaking AI news and actionable insights. [...] Consider what enterprise landscapes contain: software built and layered over decades, logic written by teams that no longer exist, dependencies that nobody has fully mapped. Frontier AI can surface vulnerabilities in that estate at 50 to 100 times the speed of a human analyst and adversaries, including state-level actors, already have access to the same capability.  median time to fully patch critical vulnerabilities has now risen to 43 days. The discovery-to-exploit gap has inverted. [...] ### The structural mistake most enterprises are making\n\nMost enterprises are running two parallel programs: AI modernization, owned by the CIO, and AI security, owned by the CISO. Both are moving. In most organizations I encounter, they share very little beyond a slide in the same board deck.\n\nThis produces a predictable failure mode. Modernization acquires new AI capability on top of an estate that security has not finished hardening, while security discovers exposure from deployments it was not consulted on. AI modernization and AI security are not parallel workstreams. They are two views of the same program—and the gap between them is where the risk lives.",
      "score": 0.04741274,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 2.32,
  "request_id": "6215db00-3a6c-4b17-ae49-0b415d7b1fad"
}

    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/ai/ai-in-finance.html",
      "title": "What is AI in Finance? | Workday IN",
      "content": "Companies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations.\n\nWe believe that for AI to truly deliver on its future promise, it must be trustworthy. That’s why Workday has a Machine Learning Trust program that provides governance in defining procedures for the development and management of AI at Workday. This work helps us deliver technology benefits to our customers in line with our core values.​ As part of our commitment to trustworthy AI, we embrace the following principles: [...] As organizations continue to place a greater focus on AI, it’s critical that business leaders can trust their AI. At Workday, our approach leverages ethical AI principles that are built into the architecture of our finance solutions.\n\nCompanies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations. [...] Workday has embedded AI into the very core of our platform. Leading finance organizations are already using AI technology in Workday to help deliver better employee experiences, improve operational efficiencies, and provide insights for faster data-driven decision-making.\n\nIMC Financial Markets logo\n\n“Workday Journal Insights means one less thing for our end users to check off their list at the end of the month. They can correct issues and can fix them throughout the month. It makes it a continuous process.”\n\n—ERP Business Analyst, IMC Financial Markets\n\n“Workday Journal Insights means one less thing for our end users to check off their list at the end of the month. They can correct issues and can fix them throughout the month. It makes it a continuous process.”",

      "title": "Workday GO | Medium Enterprise Software",
      "content": "### Questions? We have answers.\n\nThe built-in AI in Workday GO helps medium enterprise and small businesses adopt enterprise AI affordably and without the complexity of building custom systems. [...] Not at all. Workday GO actually reduces complexity with streamlined processes, automation, global payroll, and integration. Workday AI agents including BP Optimise Agent, Payroll Agent, Self-Service Agent, and Deployment Agent are also available at go-live to augment your team’s capacity.\n\nWorkday GO unifies your data so you get an accurate and complete picture of your business in real time across HR and Payroll—rather than separate, siloed views.\n\nWorkday GO delivers its all-in-one solution for HR, global payroll, and finance to medium enterprise businesses through packages designed to fit their business and budget.       "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-be/homepage.html",
      "title": "Workday Belgium | AI Platform for Smarter Finance and HR",
      "content": "''\n\nThat’s a wrap! Thank you for joining us at Workday Horizon Brussels. Check back for next year’s dates, locations and how to register. Learn More\n\n# Superintelligence for work. Meet Sana.\n\nSana from Workday is the superintelligence that automates work across HR, Finance, IT, and beyond through the power of AI. It’s a new work day.\n\n# Superintelligence for work. Meet Sana.\n\nSana from Workday is the superintelligence that automates work across HR, Finance, IT, and beyond through the power of AI. It’s a new work day.\n\nExplore Sana\n\nPlay Video\n\nA new way of working. Sana is the last software your teams will ever need to learn. Find out why.\n\nImage of three people sitting at a table with their laptops open.\n\n'' [...] Watch Now\n\n''\n\nThe new work day is here – and we're leading the way with superintelligence.     {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html",
      "title": "Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog",
      "content": "That’s why the Workday CLM product team is currently developing new AI tools to automate the drafting of contract playbooks based on existing contracts, with the goal of making playbook creation as simple as uploading sample documents (like existing agreements and policy notes) from which our AI can derive preferred and fallback language and criteria.\n\nWe’re also working to deliver users proactive insights over time about their teams’ adherence to those playbooks, so they can make informed decisions about keeping those playbooks current and optimized. Watch this space!\n\n## Staying Ahead of the Curve [...] The Workday Contract Negotiation Agent removes the friction of the drafting process, allowing you to focus on the strategic outcomes your contracts are actually meant to secure—achieving the true ends of your negotiation faster and more easily than ever before.\n\nWant to learn more? Book a demo today to see the Workday Contract Negotiation Agent in action.\n\nWhat Is Agentic AI?\n\nStill unsure on the difference between the types of AI agents, or just need a refresher on the basics? Workday has you covered.  \n   \n Learn More\n\nWorkday’s Bold Future With Responsible Agentic AI\n\nAgentic AI is here, which means the correct governance is crucial. Learn how Workday is doubling down on its commitment to responsible AI.\n\nRead Blog\n\nTrust by Design: How Workday Builds AI That Puts People First [...] It leverages sophisticated AI orchestration, enabling it to choose the best AI tool to support the task at hand. Instead of relying exclusively on one large language model (LLM), its capabilities grow naturally with the latest advances in AI.\n Its foundational, purpose-built AI helps ensure accurate results from LLMs by conducting core analysis of the customer’s contract repository and creating a trusted base of structured data to enable retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\n It’s fully customizable, enabling customers to create their own AI models to extract and generate all the contract data points they care about over time, rather than being limited to a finite set of core extractions.\n\n## Driving AI Responsibility",
      "score": 0.78334314,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/hr/hris.html",
      "title": "What Is an HRIS? How IT and HR Are Evolving Together - Workday",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management [...] #### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Payroll\n\n### Planning\n\nThe planning system that integrates with any ERP/GL or data source.\n\n#### Planning\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Close & Consolidation\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Operational Planning\n\n### Legal\n\nTake control of your contract data and drive results across your organization.\n\n#### Legal Overview\n\n#### Products\n\n#### Contract Intelligence\n\n#### Contract Lifecycle Management\n\n### Platform\n\nGain a flexible foundation that helps you move beyond the limitations of legacy ERP.\n\n#### IT Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Our Technology\n\n#### Extensions\n\n#### Orchestrate & Integrations\n\n#### Data Cloud\n\n#### Workday Extend\n\n### Workday ERP [...] HRIS: An HRIS collects and stores employee data. It compiles information on employee demographics, job status, contact information, compensation, and other areas.\n HRMS: An HRMS expands on the data management tools of an HRIS to include a wider range of software and utility. The term originated in the early twenty-first century to describe HR software suites supporting payroll, applicant tracking, benefits, and talent management.\n HCMS: An HCMS encompasses the functionality of an HRIS and HRMS while supporting a wider breadth of HR functions, including employee experience and analytics. HCM, in general, largely refers to HR suites designed with cloud computing at the core, ensuring they remain adaptive.",
      "score": 0.69399214,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/careers/jobdetails?id=R00333881_en&title=Workday+Certified+Financials+Adaptive+Consultant",

      "content": "You are…\n\nAs a member of one of the world’s largest Workday-certified teams, you’ll be part of a team of advisory professionals who deliver more than just technology. By Gyana Swain Oct 16, 2025 5 mins Artificial Intelligence Data Center Mergers and Acquisitions\n\nnews ### Workday buys Sana to address the ‘era of superintelligence for work’ By acquiring the Swedish artificial intelligence company and integrating its technology into its existing people management software, Workday aims to \"create the work experience of the future,\" a scenario of human employees empowered as never before by AI agents.       "url": "https://apply.deloitte.com/en_US/careers/JobDetail/Delivery-Senior-Consultant-Workday-HCM-Payroll-Benefits-Module-s/354651",
      "title": "Delivery Senior Consultant, Workday HCM Payroll/Benefits Module(s)",
      "content": "### Position Summary\n\nOur Deloitte Human Capital team helps organizations create value through people performance. We work with clients to reimagine work, the workforce, and the workplace across the enterprise and to transform their HR functions with AI and emerging technology. With the rapid pace of change in today’s world, you will help clients answer questions like: How do I access, develop, and motivate my workforce? What should my AI strategy be for the HR function? Do I have the right organization and culture to enable performance? Join our team to make work better for humans and humans better at work.\n\nWork You'll Do\n\nAs a Delivery Senior Consultant for our team, you will be responsible for:\n\nA successful candidate would possess these skills:\n\nThe Team [...] The Team\n\nDeloitte’s Government & Public Services (GPS) practice – our people, ideas, technology and outcomes – is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise.\n\nOur HR Strategy & Technology offering develops leading global HR strategies and implements HR technologies that drive transformation, maximize the workforce experience, and sustain HR effectiveness and value while navigating a world of disruption. [...] © 2026. See  Terms of Use  for more information.\n\nDeloitte refers to one or more of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee (\"DTTL\"), its network of member firms, and their related entities. DTTL and each of its member firms are legally separate and independent entities. DTTL (also referred to as \"Deloitte Global\") does not provide services to clients. In the United States, Deloitte refers to one or more of the US member firms of DTTL, their related entities that operate using the \"Deloitte\" name in the United States and their respective affiliates. Certain services may not be available to attest clients under the rules and regulations of public accounting. Please see www.deloitte.com/about to learn more about our global network of member firms.",

      "content": "## Topics\n\n# Anirban Ghoshal\n\n## Articles by Anirban Ghoshal\n\n### Salesforce supercharges Agentforce with embedded AI, multimodal support, and industry-specific agents\n\nFrom auto-updating CRM fields to new industry agents and employee tools, Salesforce’s Summer ’25 update positions Agentforce as a no-code AI engine woven into every enterprise workflow.\n\n### Workday’s new dev tools help enterprises connect with external agents\n\nAmong the additions are some improvements to Workday’s Developer Copilot, which can now generate application code snippets, data queries, and functional orchestrations.\n\n### SAP teams up with Alibaba to host Cloud ERP workloads in China\n\nThe partnership will also see Alibaba adopt SAP offerings to run its own business. [...] ### Why Microsoft is unifying data and AI within Fabric\n\nFabric is being transformed as a decision infrastructure layer to support faster decision making and easier development of agentic applications, analysts say.\n\n### New agentic AI tools bring new threat: agent sprawl\n\nVendors are lining up to offer enterprises tools to create AI agents for a plethora of different tasks — but it’s time to think about managing them.\n\n### Google’s AI innovations at Cloud Next 2025: What CIOs need to know\n\nThe announcements made at Google Cloud Next 2025 were designed to help CIOs adopt and scale agents while reducing integration complexity and costs.\n\n### Siemens grows its digital twin strategy into life sciences market",

    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/xx/en/what-we-do/services/ai/trusted-ai-framework.html",
      "title": "KPMG Trusted AI framework",
      "content": "Human-centric\n\nWe prioritize human impact as we deploy AI and recognize the needs of our clients and our people. We are embracing this technology to empower and augment human capabilities — to unleash creativity and improve productivity in a way that allows people to reimagine how they spend their days.\n\nTrustworthy\n\nWe will adhere to our principles and the ethical pillars that guide how and why we use AI across its lifecycle. We will strive to ensure our data acquisition, governance and usage practices upholds ethical standards and complies with applicable privacy and data protection regulations, as well as any confidentiality requirements. [...] ### Privacy\n\nAI solutions should be designed to comply with applicable privacy and data protection laws and regulations.\n\n### Sustainability\n\nAI solutions should be designed to be energy efficient, reduce carbon emissions and support a cleaner environment.\n\n### Explainability\n\nAI solutions should be developed and delivered in a way that answers the questions of how and why a conclusion was drawn from the solution.\n\n### Integrity\n\nData used in AI solutions should be acquired in compliance with applicable laws and regulations and assessed for accuracy, completeness, appropriateness and quality to drive trusted decisions.\n\n### Transparency [...] ### Transparency\n\nAI solutions should include responsible disclosure to provide stakeholders with a clear understanding of what is happening in each solution across the AI lifecycle.\n\n### Fairness\n\nAI solutions should be designed to reduce or eliminate bias against individuals, communities and groups.\n\n### Accountability\n\nHuman oversight and responsibility should be embedded across the AI lifecycle to manage risk and comply with applicable laws and regulations.\n\n### Reliability\n\nAI solutions should consistently operate in accordance with their intended purpose and scope and at the desired level of precision.\n\n### Security\n\nRobust and resilient practices should be implemented to safeguard AI solutions against bad actors, misinformation or adverse events.\n\n### Safety",
      "score": 0.06841928,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/be/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] A financial services company is building agentic workflows to automatically capture meeting actions from video conferences, draft communications to remind participants of their next steps, and track follow-through.\n An air carrier is using AI agents to help customers complete the most common transactions, such as rebooking a flight or rerouting bags, freeing up human agents to address more complex matters.\n A manufacturer is using AI agents to support new product development initiatives, leveraging AI to find the optimal balance between competing objectives such as cost and time-to-market.\n In the public sector, AI agents are being used to cover workforce shortages, partnering with human workers to complete key processes.",
      "score": 0.058094297,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cognizant.com/us/en/insights/insights-blog/frontier-ai-exposes-enterprise-architecture-flaws",
      "title": "Beyond cybersecurity: AI exposes your entire architecture - Cognizant",
      "content": "##### Keep up with AI innovations for business\n\nAI is moving fast. Our bimonthly LinkedIn newsletter helps you do the same. Subscribe for breaking AI news and actionable insights. [...] Consider what enterprise landscapes contain: software built and layered over decades, logic written by teams that no longer exist, dependencies that nobody has fully mapped. Frontier AI can surface vulnerabilities in that estate at 50 to 100 times the speed of a human analyst and adversaries, including state-level actors, already have access to the same capability.  median time to fully patch critical vulnerabilities has now risen to 43 days. The discovery-to-exploit gap has inverted. [...] ### The structural mistake most enterprises are making\n\nMost enterprises are running two parallel programs: AI modernization, owned by the CIO, and AI security, owned by the CISO. Both are moving. In most organizations I encounter, they share very little beyond a slide in the same board deck.\n\nThis produces a predictable failure mode. Modernization acquires new AI capability on top of an estate that security has not finished hardening, while security discovers exposure from deployments it was not consulted on. AI modernization and AI security are not parallel workstreams. They are two views of the same program—and the gap between them is where the risk lives.",
      "score": 0.04741274,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 1.5,
  "request_id": "4c098e36-2058-423c-88d2-709c3d93393e"
}

      "content": "## AI insights for your business\n\n## Unlock the value of AI with KPMG\n\n### AI, governance and organisational clarity\n\nEmma Coogan, AI campaign, AI consulting\n\n### Embedding AI in transformation programmes\n\nBreda O'Callaghan, AI campaign, KPMG Consulting\n\n### AI, risk and cyber security\n\nDani Michaux, EMA Cyber Lead\n\n### AI, capability and pace\n\nTania Kuklina, Director\n\n### Driving value from AI\n\nRory Timlin, Partner, Management Consulting\n\n### AI and data\n\nAlan Lavery AI campaign\n\n### Redefining leadership in the age of AI\n\nColm O'Neill AI campaign\n\n## Our AI strategic partnerships and alliances\n\nMicrosoft logo\nGoogle Cloud logo\nUniphore logo\nSAP logo\nServicenow logo\nWorkday logo\nDatabricks logo\nOnetrust logo\n\n## Get in touch with our AI Consulting team [...] #### \n\n###### \n\nKPMG Ireland - Audit, Tax, Deal Advisory & Consulting\nKPMG Ireland - Audit, Tax, Deal Advisory & Consulting\n\n#### \n\n###### \n\nYour search term was too short.\n\nSearch terms must include 3 or more characters.\n\n# You can with AI\n\nFrom driving value and growing margin, to unlocking insights in your data and from building resilience to empowering your people, we can show you how you can with AI.\n\nWe help organisations move beyond experimentation by applying a structured, enterprise-ready approach that’s grounded in governance, sectoral insights and delivery experience.\n\nOur trusted Al services can support your transition from pilot initiatives to scalable capabilities that are tied to the commercial value your business needs.\n\n## AI insights for your business [...] Director\n\nKPMG in Ireland\n\nAlan Lavery\n\n###### Alan Lavery\n\nManaging Director, Applied Intelligence, GenAI Lead\n\nKPMG in Ireland\n\n## Discover more in Artificial Intelligence\n\n### Something went wrong\n\n###### Oops!!       "title": "Custom Software Engineer - Accenture",
      "content": "Project Role : Custom Software Engineer  \nProject Role Description : Assist in defining requirements and designing applications to meet business process and application requirements.   \nMust have skills : Workday Cloud Connect for Third Party Payroll  \nGood to have skills : NA  \nMinimum 3 year(s) of experience is required  \nEducational Qualification : 15 years full time education  \n  \nSummary: [...] Skip to footer\n\nCareers\n\n# Custom Software Engineer\n\nPackaged Application Development Senior Analyst  | Full time | Experience: 2-5 years\n\nJob No. ATCI-5311864-S1946389 | Hyderabad   | Required Skill: Workday Cloud Connect for Third Party Payroll\n\nApply for this job [...] - Provide solutions to problems for their immediate team and across multiple teams.  \n- Facilitate knowledge sharing sessions to enhance team capabilities.  \n- Mentor junior team members to foster their professional growth.  \n   \nProfessional & Technical Skills:   \n- Must To Have Skills: Proficiency in Workday Core HCM.  \n- Strong understanding of application design principles and methodologies.  \n- Experience with business process mapping and requirements gathering.  \n- Ability to troubleshoot and resolve application issues effectively.  \n- Familiarity with integration techniques and data migration strategies.  \n   \nAdditional Information:   \n- The candidate should have minimum 7.5 years of experience in Workday Core HCM.  \n- This position is based at our Bengaluru office.",

      "url": "https://kpmg.com/xx/en/what-we-do/services/ai/trusted-ai-framework.html",
      "title": "KPMG Trusted AI framework",
      "content": "Human-centric\n\nWe prioritize human impact as we deploy AI and recognize the needs of our clients and our people. We are embracing this technology to empower and augment human capabilities — to unleash creativity and improve productivity in a way that allows people to reimagine how they spend their days.\n\nTrustworthy\n\nWe will adhere to our principles and the ethical pillars that guide how and why we use AI across its lifecycle. We will strive to ensure our data acquisition, governance and usage practices upholds ethical standards and complies with applicable privacy and data protection regulations, as well as any confidentiality requirements. [...] We recognize that the field of AI is rapidly evolving — and so is our approach. As the technology advances and legal, ethical, risk and regulatory standards mature, we will continue to review and evolve our approach as necessary.\n\nHumans empowered: A new era of AI is here\n\n## KPMG's Trusted AI framework is how we drive principles into practice\n\nClick to interact\n\n### Reliability\n\nAI solutions should consistently operate in accordance with their intended purpose and scope and at the desired level of precision.\n\n### Security\n\nRobust and resilient practices should be implemented to safeguard AI solutions against bad actors, misinformation or adverse events.\n\n### Safety\n\nAI solutions should be designed and implemented to safeguard against harm to people, businesses and property.\n\n### Privacy [...] ### Transparency\n\nAI solutions should include responsible disclosure to provide stakeholders with a clear understanding of what is happening in each solution across the AI lifecycle.\n\n### Fairness\n\nAI solutions should be designed to reduce or eliminate bias against individuals, communities and groups.\n\n### Accountability\n\nHuman oversight and responsibility should be embedded across the AI lifecycle to manage risk and comply with applicable laws and regulations.\n\n### Reliability\n\nAI solutions should consistently operate in accordance with their intended purpose and scope and at the desired level of precision.\n\n### Security\n\nRobust and resilient practices should be implemented to safeguard AI solutions against bad actors, misinformation or adverse events.\n\n### Safety",
      "score": 0.07084848,
      "raw_content": null
    },

[...] hero image\n\n#### Key takeaways:\n\nIf you could design a business from scratch today, you’d take a different approach. You’d build it to run as one coherent system, starting with an employee or team, then using AI to help orchestrate how cloud, data, and application investments work together across the enterprise. Finance, technology, and operations are completely interconnected. Humans and AI agents collaborate as a new unified service delivery model. Security is embedded into each business unit, function, and product offering, and is built to evolve with the business.       "url": "https://careers.servicenow.com/jobs/744000129995998/staff-ai-agent-engineer-moveworks-customer-deployment",
      "title": "Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment",
      "content": "Your primary mission is to translate complex customer business process challenges into innovative, high-impact AI solutions built on our platform. This role provides full-stack ownership of technical success, allowing you to not only deploy capabilities in the current product but also directly influence the product's evolution by ensuring that learnings are folded back into our core platform.\n\nCore Responsibilities & Impact:\n\nAbout You:  \n You are a technical generalist & a “do-er” with a deep, intuitive understanding of complex systems and a relentless focus on customer impact. You thrive in a dynamic, high-growth environment where you can both build and lead.\n\n### Qualifications\n\nTo be successful in this role you have:\n\n​​​​​​​Preferred Qualifications\n\n### Additional Information [...] # Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment\n\n### Company Description\n\nIt all started when engineer Fred Luddy wrote code that automated a tedious task for his coworker, Phyllis. She cried tears of joy. That moment inspired Fred to build a company that could do that for everyone—freeing people from busywork so they could focus on meaningful work. Today, ServiceNow is the AI control tower for business reinvention. Our ServiceNow AI platform brings together any AI, any data, and any workflow— helping 85% of the Fortune 500® work smarter, faster, and better. We're building an AI-native culture where technology and talent are unstoppable together. And we're just getting started.\n\nJoin us to put AI to work for people.\n\n### Job Description [...] Equal Opportunity Employer\n\nServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements.\n\nAccommodations",

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/intelligent-enterprise.html",
      "title": "Intelligent enterprise: Connect AI, strategy and operations | PwC",
      "content": "An AI operating model defines how AI systems, workflows, people, and business decisions function together across the enterprise. It establishes how workflows, how decisions move across functions, and how humans and AI interact at scale.\n\nGovernance is the accountability layer within that system. It defines decision rights, delegation boundaries, escalation paths, and the controls needed to ensure AI-driven actions remain reliable, auditable, and aligned to business intent. [...] Shared success benefits\n\nLoading Results\n\nNo Match Found\n\nConnect strategy, technology, and operations so your business runs in sync\n\n# The intelligent enterprise\n\nAI is redefining how your business runs. The question is: Are you designing for it?\n\n## AI is already part of how your business runs. What matters now is whether you design for it or inherit it.\n\nFor many organizations, AI still isn’t scaling. Systems move at different speeds. Data doesn’t connect. Value stalls in pilots.\n\nThat’s not a technology gap. It’s a design choice.\n\nThe organizations pulling ahead aren't just adopting AI. They're redesigning how decisions get made, how work moves, and how accountability scales across the business. Deliberately. [...] AI governance is the accountability architecture that defines how AI systems make decisions across an enterprise. It establishes who holds authority, what autonomous systems are permitted to do, how risks are managed, and how outcomes remain explainable. As AI becomes part of how businesses operate, governance becomes the difference between AI that creates value and AI that creates exposure.\n\nStrategy sets direction, technology connects data and AI, operations execute decisions in real time, and governance establishes decision rights, accountability, and risk guardrails. Together, they operate as a single system, enabling aligned decisions, connected systems, and scalable AI performance across the enterprise.",
      "score": 0.05720304,
      "raw_content": null

06:00PM SGT\n\n## More insights\n\n### Intelligent banking\n\n#### Intelligent banking\n\nBanking industry: A blueprint for creating value in the banking industry through AI-driven transformation\n\nfuturistic office people\n\n## Get in touch [...] ### AI Enablement for the Public Service Workforce\n\n###### Enable officers to use AI confidently in day to day roles, not as isolated specialists\n\n###### Redesign work and operating models so humans and AI collaborate effectively\n\n###### Build AI literacy and adoption across frontline, policy, risk, and corporate functions\n\n### Built to scale securely across government\n\n###### Build AI ready data and technology foundations aligned to government architectures\n\n###### Move beyond fragmented tools to scalable, secure enterprise platforms\n\n###### Enable AI to scale safely across agencies, functions, and channels\n\n## The Banking sector value propositions through KPMG’s Four-Door Framework\n\n### VALUE\n\nAI Strategy & Value Realisation (Banking outcomes by design)\n\n### TRUST [...] AI CoE\n\n## The Government Sector value propositions through KPMG’s four-door Framework\n\n### AI Strategy & Value Realisation\n\n###### Identify high‑impact AI use cases across services, policy, risk, and operations\n\n###### Prioritise initiatives based on public value, feasibility, and risk\n\n###### Scale AI from pilots to secure, enterprise‑wide adoption\n\n### Trusted & Responsible AI (Public Trust by Design)\n\n###### Embed governance, security, and regulatory alignment into AI by design\n\n###### Manage model risk, data quality, privacy, and third‑party exposure, especially for sensitive public data\n\n###### Build confidence with boards and regulators through transparent, auditable AI decisions\n\n### AI Enablement for the Public Service Workforce",

These factors may help explain why the majority posture in healthcare is to be a ‘fast follower’ of new technology (55 percent) rather than an early adopter (35 percent) — one of the lowest early adopter percentages amongst any of the sectors surveyed. Nine in ten (90 percent) executives also say they take a long-term rather than reactive investment approach. [...] Healthcare services around the world are coming under increasing pressure as lengthening life expectancies and aging populations drive patient demand ever higher. At the same time, funding increases may not keep pace while workforce challenges are also acute through a combination of skills shortages and restrictions in some countries on migrant talent. The volatility of the external environment is keenly felt by healthcare executives, with 58 percent saying that they are often or frequently impacted by market, regulatory and/or technology shifts. Regulation is a key barrier to technology transformation for many, given the complexities of data privacy rules, data sovereignty issues and the still-emerging regimes around the regulation of AI. All rights reserved. PwC refers to the PwC network and/or one or more of its member firms, each of which is a separate legal entity. Please see www.pwc.com/structure for further details. [...] A finance director at a fast-growing tech company might disclose a significant but minority ownership stake in a small vendor during onboarding. The company documents that the disclosure occurred and that it was deemed immaterial and therefore required no action. After rapid growth, the company reorganizes, promoting the finance director to procurement officer without ever revisiting the disclosure. The employee selects that same vendor for a multimillion-dollar contract. Without monitoring or reassessment, the conflict scales with the business, creating financial and reputational risk.       "content": "Applicants may be required to attend interviews in person or by video conference.       "url": "https://careers.cognizant.com/global-en/jobs/00068835141/program-manager",
      "title": "Program Manager, Bangalore, Karnataka, India | Cognizant Careers",
      "content": "### These will help you stand out\n\n Exposure to enterprise GenAI solutions such as Salesforce Einstein GPT, ServiceNow Now Assist, SAP Joule, Workday AI, or Oracle AI Services\n Experience with hybrid and multi-cloud AI deployment models\n Knowledge of AI observability, agent lifecycle management, and interoperability standards\n Experience mentoring architects and leading high-performing AI engineering teams\n Active participation in AI communities, industry forums, or thought leadership initiatives\n\n### Why consider this role?\n\nWe're excited to meet professionals who are passionate about shaping the future of AI and driving real-world impact through innovation. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply and bring your unique perspective to the role.\n\n--- [...] Cognizant Careers\n\nCognizant Careers\n\n# Program Manager\n\n00068835141\n\n Date published Jun 04 2026\n Location Bangalore / India\n Job category Technology & Engineering\n Work model Hybrid\n\n### Senior Architect – Generative & Agentic AI\n\n### About the role\n\nAs a Senior Architect – Generative & Agentic AI, you will make an impact by defining and delivering enterprise-scale AI architectures that drive business transformation and innovation. You will be a valued member of the AI & Digital Engineering team and work closely with business leaders, AI strategists, and engineering teams to design scalable, secure, and high-performing AI solutions.\n\n### In this role, you will: [...] ### In this role, you will:\n\n Define and own enterprise-wide architecture for Generative AI and Agentic AI solutions aligned with business goals\n Design end-to-end AI systems, from model selection and context engineering to application integration and deployment\n Lead development of reusable frameworks, accelerators, and multi-agent architectures for enterprise use cases\n Drive prototyping, PoCs, and MVP initiatives to validate AI solutions across industries\n Establish governance, standards, and best practices for AI lifecycle, ethics, security, and performance\n\n### Work model",
      "score": 0.9804314,
      "raw_content": null

    {
      "url": "https://careers.servicenow.com/jobs/744000129995998/staff-ai-agent-engineer-moveworks-customer-deployment",
      "title": "Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment",
      "content": "Join us to put AI to work for people.\n\n### Job Description\n\nAs a Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment at Moveworks ServiceNow, you’ll be responsible for making customers successful with the Moveworks ServiceNow Platform by accelerating product adoption through various customer engagements. You are a critical, high-leverage technical individual that operates at the intersection of Customer Success, Product Management, and Engineering. [...] # Staff AI Agent Engineer – Moveworks | Customer Deployment\n\n### Company Description\n\nIt all started when engineer Fred Luddy wrote code that automated a tedious task for his coworker, Phyllis. She cried tears of joy. That moment inspired Fred to build a company that could do that for everyone—freeing people from busywork so they could focus on meaningful work. Today, ServiceNow is the AI control tower for business reinvention. Our ServiceNow AI platform brings together any AI, any data, and any workflow— helping 85% of the Fortune 500® work smarter, faster, and better. We're building an AI-native culture where technology and talent are unstoppable together. And we're just getting started.\n\nJoin us to put AI to work for people.\n\n### Job Description [...] Your primary mission is to translate complex customer business process challenges into innovative, high-impact AI solutions built on our platform. This role provides full-stack ownership of technical success, allowing you to not only deploy capabilities in the current product but also directly influence the product's evolution by ensuring that learnings are folded back into our core platform.\n\nCore Responsibilities & Impact:\n\nAbout You:  \n You are a technical generalist & a “do-er” with a deep, intuitive understanding of complex systems and a relentless focus on customer impact. You thrive in a dynamic, high-growth environment where you can both build and lead.\n\n### Qualifications\n\nTo be successful in this role you have:\n\n​​​​​​​Preferred Qualifications\n\n### Additional Information",
      "score": 0.95430803,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/intelligent-enterprise/building-the-intelligent-enterprise-age-of-ai.html",
      "title": "The Intelligent Enterprise in the age of AI - PwC",
      "content": "It’s not too late to design this system intentionally and realize the advantage before your competitors do. In an April 2026 PwC survey, 89% of operations leaders say that technology investments have not delivered expected results. And only 41% say they currently operate in a horizontal, networked way.\n\nAI is raising the stakes. It’s reshaping how work happens and how decisions get made. But with the right design, large-scale AI investments can do more than accelerate individual tasks. In a business environment that’s only becoming more complex, demanding faster, better-governed decisions, the advantage will go to companies that can turn information into coordinated action that improves business performance. [...] For CEOs, the intelligent enterprise means that AI evolves from a collection of disconnected pilots to become the core of the business. So, decisions happen in hours, not weeks. Data flows to the right places, with the right level of accuracy, and the right overarching governance. Risk is visible in real time, not surfaced quarterly. Front-office and back-office teams speak the same language and have the same goals: serving the customer, executing the company’s strategy, and continually improving performance. Across business units, functions, and geographic markets, the entire enterprise works as one.\n\nThat kind of orchestration can create competitive advantage:\n\n## An intelligent enterprise in action [...] Collectively, these attributes turn commercial threats into opportunities. What we're seeing across companies we've worked with is consistent: When AI is embedded into core commercial functions, conversion rates can improve by 30 to 40% and revenue per sales representative can improve by 10 to 20%. Many teams also become 10 to 20% more productive.\n\nAfter years of improvements to finance functions, companies are approaching the ceiling of what’s possible in terms of efficiency. The pace of disruption renders the traditional finance calendar obsolete. Modern capital structures require much faster and more precise engagement. CFOs have a growing opportunity to look ahead and steer the business, not just report past results.",
      "score": 0.14608724,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cognizant.com/us/en/insights/insights-blog/frontier-ai-exposes-enterprise-architecture-flaws",
      "title": "Beyond cybersecurity: AI exposes your entire architecture - Cognizant",
      "content": "### Related posts\n\napiUrl\n\n/content/cognizant-dot-com/us/en/insights/insights-blog/frontier-ai-exposes-enterprise-architecture-flaws/jcr:content/root/container/ctscontainerwidthcon\\_1391314226/relatedsection.search.json\n\nlimit\n\nerrorMsg\n\nOops! There is an error in the search. Please try again\n\nnoDataMsg\n\nNo Results found\n\nlayout\n\nvariation\n\npretitle\n\nfalse\n\ndescription\n\ntrue\n\ntruncate\n\nenabled\n\nreadmore\n\nKnow more\n\ntarget\n\n\\_self\n\nseemore\n\nSee more\n\nseeless\n\nSee less\n\nMaxDisplayTiles\n\nisShowAuthorName\n\nisShowReadTime\n\ninitialDisplayTiles\n\ndateFormat\n\nnumberOfLinesDescription\n\nSee more\n\nstyle\n\nBackground Transparent\n\n##### Keep up with AI innovations for business [...] Ecosystem depth matters here too: our work with Zscaler—combining Zero Trust Everywhere and AI asset management with our Secure AI Services—reflects the co-engineered approach the architecture demands, not point solutions that leave gaps at the seams. We are deepening similar conversations with partners in identity, communication security and cloud-native infrastructure, because integration between platforms increasingly determines the resilience of the whole. [...] The 260+ enterprise clients we work with in cybersecurity who are moving fastest share one thing: they stopped asking how to secure the current estate and started asking what an enterprise designed for a frontier-AI world would look like from scratch. That question is available to every organization. The constraint is how quickly leadership can align around the answer.\n\n### The verdict that is already in\n\nMythos was an architectural audit—one that frontier models are now capable of running continuously, on any estate, at scale. Leaders who respond at the level that verdict was delivered, with a genuine enterprise architecture mandate, will build the resilience the next decade demands.",
      "score": 0.030908957,
      "raw_content": null

    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/outlook/15-productivity-features-in-the-new-outlook-for-windows/4522796",
      "title": "15 productivity features in the new Outlook for Windows | Microsoft Community Hub",
      "content": "## Calendar and meeting features\n\nFor many people, the workday is shaped by meetings, schedule changes, and the need to stay aligned on what comes next. Outlook includes calendar and meeting capabilities that can help simplify planning, reduce coordination overhead, and make follow-up easier.\n\n1. Follow a meeting: Stay informed without attending live. The Follow RSVP option lets you indicate that you will not attend a meeting but still want access to the recap. This can be helpful when schedules overlap or when a meeting is useful to monitor without joining in real time. It can help you stay connected to outcomes and shared materials while keeping your calendar more manageable. [...] Updated Jun 02, 2026\n\nVersion 1.0\n\nadmin\n\nexchange\n\noffice 365\n\noutlook for windows\n\noutlook on the web\n\nVicki_Milton's avatar\n\nVicki\\_Milton\n\nIcon for Microsoft rankMicrosoft\n\nJoined September 06, 2019\n\nView Profile\n\nOutlook Blog \n\nWelcome to the Outlook Blog! Learn best practices, news, and trends and directly from the Outlook team. [...] ## Blog Post\n\nOutlook Blog\n\n5 MIN READ\n\n# 15 productivity features in the new Outlook for Windows\n\nVicki_Milton's avatar\n\nVicki\\_Milton\n\nIcon for Microsoft rankMicrosoft\n\nJun 03, 2026\n\nHello, Outlook community. I’m Vicki Milton, a Principal Product Manager on the Outlook team.\n\nOver the last year, we’ve added important capabilities across areas such as offline support, shared mailboxes, and PST files. Alongside those milestones, we've continued to deliver smaller improvements that help people work more efficiently throughout the day.\n\nThis article highlights 15 productivity features in the new Outlook for Windows that can help you stay organized, reduce routine effort, and keep important work moving.\n\n## Mail features",

    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/industries/health-industries/library/develop-a-deep-understanding-of-consumers.html",
      "title": "Develop a deep understanding of consumers: Owning the doctor’s visit is no longer enough: PwC",
      "content": "{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\n{{item.publishDate}}\n\n#### {{item.title}}\n\n{{item.text}}\n\nLinkedin Follow\nFacebook Follow\nX Follow\nSocialFollow_Youtube\nInstagram Follow\nSocialFollow_TikTok\n\n© 2017\n- 2026 PwC. All rights reserved. PwC refers to the PwC network and/or one or more of its member firms, each of which is a separate legal entity. Please see www.pwc.com/structure for further details. [...] ## 5. Preserve trust.\n\nCombine clinical, behavioral, consumer, and contextual data as strategic assets supported by trust and governance. The convergence of these data sources introduces real privacy, cybersecurity, and ethical risks that can erode consumer confidence if not tightly managed. Industry-leading organizations respond by establishing enterprise‑level data and AI governance, making transparency a competitive differentiator, and treating trust as a measurable outcome rather than a compliance exercise. The ability to govern data responsibly is what often allows providers to scale innovation without sacrificing credibility. Getting the foundation right, first, and early, allows for the value of data to be realized. [...] One reason that this dissatisfaction has not reshaped provider economics is that credible alternatives (such as digital-first providers) have largely been limited to low‑acuity care so far.5 Still, consumers are getting acclimated to looking for alternative providers. This shift reflects the broader reality that patients assess care across sites, settings, and time, from the first sniffle to the last medical bill. A provider that concentrates only on patient experience within the four walls of a visit misses significant pieces of what helps shape patient trust.\n\n#### Reasons to believe",

      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/event-processing/entity-graph",
      "title": "Using the Entity Context Graph (ECG) | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "`<variable_name>.graph.metadata.vendor_name`\n`Google Threat Intelligence`\n`<variable_name>.graph.metadata.product_name`\n`GTI Feed`\n`<variable_name>.graph.metadata.threat.threat_feed_name`\n`Remote Access Tools`\n`<variable_name>.graph.entity.file.sha256`\n`<variable_name>.graph.entity.file.sha1`\n`<variable_name>.graph.entity.file.md5`\n\n##### Example search\n\n`graph.metadata.source_type =\"GLOBAL_CONTEXT\"\ngraph.metadata.product_name = \"GTI Feed\"\ngraph.metadata.threat.threat_feed_name = \"Remote Access Tools\"`\n\n### Enrich entities with information from Safe Browsing threat lists [...] `<variable_name>.graph.metadata.vendor_name`\n`Google Threat Intelligence`\n`<variable_name>.graph.metadata.product_name`\n`GTI Feed`\n`<variable_name>.graph.metadata.threat.threat_feed_name`\n`Benign Binaries`\n`<variable_name>.graph.entity.file.sha256`\n`<variable_name>.graph.entity.file.sha1`\n`<variable_name>.graph.entity.file.md5`\n\n##### Example search\n\n`graph.metadata.source_type =\"GLOBAL_CONTEXT\"\ngraph.metadata.product_name = \"GTI Feed\"\ngraph.metadata.threat.threat_feed_name = \"Benign Binaries\"`\n\n#### Data about remote access tools [...] `Entity graph`\n\nTo build a comprehensive ECG, prioritize data sources that contribute\nhigh-value identifiers and relationship data. For example:",

    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/investigate-detections-in-search",
      "title": "Investigate detections in Search | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Investigate detections in Search\n\nThis guide is for security analysts who want to query, filter, and analyze\ndetections within Google Security Operations Search. This capability lets you search\nacross all alerting and non-alerting detections using any detection attribute\nand get a holistic workflow for threat investigation.\n\n## Common use cases\n\nThis section lists some common use cases.\n\n### Entity-based threat hunting\n\nObjective: Identify all detections triggered by a specific entity (for\nexample, a principal IP address or hostname) across different rules over a set\nperiod. [...] ### Incident scope identification\n\nObjective: Quantify how many times a specific event has resulted\nin a detection or alert within the last month.\n\nValue: Provides visibility into the frequency and severity of\nspecific threats. This helps you determine the complete scenario of an attack\nand decide whether a detection is a persistent threat or an isolated incident.\n\n## Key terminology\n\nSearch: The core search functionality in Google SecOps\nused to analyze security telemetry.\n\nDetections: Security findings generated by the system based on rules\napplied to raw logs and UDM events.\n\n## Before you begin\n\nYou must have the standard SecOps Search permissions and roles\nassigned to your user account to execute queries and view detection details.\n\n## Execute a search on detections [...] Need more help? Get answers from Community members and Google SecOps professionals.Updated. FYI, this section is a part of the new journey-based doc template that we follow.\n\nExcept as otherwise noted, the content of this page is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 License, and code samples are licensed under the Apache 2.0 License. For details, see the Google Developers Site Policies. Java is a registered trademark of Oracle and/or its affiliates.\n\nLast updated 2026-06-03 UTC.\n\n### Products and pricing\n\n### Support\n\n### Resources\n\n### Engage",
      "score": 0.008219693,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "content": "Skip to Ask Learn chat experience \n\nThis browser is no longer supported.\n\nUpgrade to Microsoft Edge to take advantage of the latest features, security updates, and technical support.\n\nDownload Microsoft Edge   More info about Internet Explorer and Microsoft Edge\n\nRead in English   Edit  \n\n---\n\nAccess to this page requires authorization. You can try signing in or changing directories.\n\nAccess to this page requires authorization. You can try changing directories.\n\n# Conditional Access: Conditions\n\n## Overview\n\nIn a Conditional Access policy, admins use one or more signals to improve policy decisions.\n\nScreenshot of available conditions for a Conditional Access policy in the Microsoft Entra admin center. [...] Path: `HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\\Software\\Policies\\Google\\Chrome\\ExtensionInstallForcelist`\n Name: `1`\n Type: `REG_SZ (String)`\n Data: `ppnbnpeolgkicgegkbkbjmhlideopiji;\n\n##### macOS\n\nmacOS devices using the Enterprise SSO plugin require the Microsoft Single Sign On extension to support SSO and device-based Conditional Access in Google Chrome.\n\nFor MDM based deployments of Google Chrome and extension management, refer to Set up Chrome browser on Mac and ExtensionInstallForcelist.\n\n### Supported mobile applications and desktop clients\n\nAdmins can select Mobile apps and desktop clients as client app.\n\nThis setting has an effect on access attempts made from the following mobile apps and desktop clients: [...] ## Device state (deprecated)\n\nThis condition is deprecated. Customers should use the Filter for devices condition in the Conditional Access policy to satisfy scenarios previously achieved using the device state condition.\n\nImportant\n\nDevice state and filters for devices can't be used together in Conditional Access policy. Filters for devices provide more granular targeting, including support for targeting device state information through the `trustType` and `isCompliant` property.\n\n## Filter for devices",
      "score": 0.007066401,
      "raw_content": null
    }
