      "title": "Delivery Senior Consultant, Workday HCM Payroll/Benefits Module(s)",
      "content": "### Position Summary\n\nOur Deloitte Human Capital team helps organizations create value through people performance. We work with clients to reimagine work, the workforce, and the workplace across the enterprise and to transform their HR functions with AI and emerging technology. With the rapid pace of change in today’s world, you will help clients answer questions like: How do I access, develop, and motivate my workforce? What should my AI strategy be for the HR function? Do I have the right organization and culture to enable performance? Join our team to make work better for humans and humans better at work.\n\nWork You'll Do\n\nAs a Delivery Senior Consultant for our team, you will be responsible for:\n\nA successful candidate would possess these skills:\n\nThe Team [...] The Team\n\nDeloitte’s Government & Public Services (GPS) practice – our people, ideas, technology and outcomes – is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise.\n\nOur HR Strategy & Technology offering develops leading global HR strategies and implements HR technologies that drive transformation, maximize the workforce experience, and sustain HR effectiveness and value while navigating a world of disruption. [...] © 2026. See  Terms of Use  for more information.\n\nDeloitte refers to one or more of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee (\"DTTL\"), its network of member firms, and their related entities. DTTL and each of its member firms are legally separate and independent entities. DTTL (also referred to as \"Deloitte Global\") does not provide services to clients. In the United States, Deloitte refers to one or more of the US member firms of DTTL, their related entities that operate using the \"Deloitte\" name in the United States and their respective affiliates.     {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/be/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] A financial services company is building agentic workflows to automatically capture meeting actions from video conferences, draft communications to remind participants of their next steps, and track follow-through.\n An air carrier is using AI agents to help customers complete the most common transactions, such as rebooking a flight or rerouting bags, freeing up human agents to address more complex matters.\n A manufacturer is using AI agents to support new product development initiatives, leveraging AI to find the optimal balance between competing objectives such as cost and time-to-market.\n In the public sector, AI agents are being used to cover workforce shortages, partnering with human workers to complete key processes.",
      "score": 0.058094297,
      "raw_content": null

    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/tag/outlook%20for%20windows",
      "title": "Tag:\"outlook for windows\" | Microsoft Community Hub",
      "content": "nested folders. Now, when you share a folder, Outlook can automatically apply the visibility permissions needed for its parent folders. This can reduce setup effort, help avoid access issues for recipients, and make folder sharing easier to complete with confidence. Calendar and meeting features For many people, the workday is shaped by meetings, schedule changes, and the need to stay aligned on what comes next. Outlook includes calendar and meeting capabilities that can help simplify planning, reduce coordination overhead, and make follow-up easier. Follow a meeting: Stay informed without attending live. The Follow RSVP option lets you indicate that you will not attend a meeting but still want access to the recap. This can be helpful when schedules overlap or when a meeting is useful to [...] OnAppointmentSendHandler ); })(this); Observed behavior The handler logs successfully. typeof fetch === \"function\". typeof XMLHttpRequest === \"function\". However: fetch(...) starts, but no response callback is logged. In earlier tests, fetch remained pending until our timeout marker. XMLHttpRequest reaches readyState: 1 after open(), but does not return useful response data. In some runs it eventually reports: status: 0, empty response, or timeout. Server-side logs show the request does not reach the server in the failing cases. event.completed(...) works and Outlook receives the completion. Things already ruled out / tested We are using absolute URLs, not relative URLs. We tested both:   We tested through an HTTPS tunnel pointing to localhost. We tested trusted local HTTPS certificates; [...] response', { elapsedMs: Date.now() - fetchStartedAt, ok: response.ok, status: response.status, statusText: response.statusText, url: response.url }); return response.text(); }, function (error) { console.log('[test] fetch error', { elapsedMs: Date.now() - fetchStartedAt, name: error && error.name, message: error && error.message }); } ); var xhrStartedAt = Date.now(); var xhr = new XMLHttpRequest(); xhr.timeout = 4500; xhr.onreadystatechange = function () { console.log('[test] xhr readyState', { elapsedMs: Date.now() - xhrStartedAt, readyState: xhr.readyState, status: xhr.status, statusText: xhr.statusText, responseURL: xhr.responseURL, responseTextLength: xhr.responseText ? xhr.responseText.length : 0 }); }; xhr.onload = function () { console.log('[test] xhr load', { elapsedMs:",

    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/about/press-room/deloitte-collaborates-with-google-wiz-ai-defense.html",
      "title": "Deloitte collaborates with Google Cloud and Wiz on AI powered ...",
      "content": "Deloitte provides industry-leading audit, consulting, tax and advisory services to many of the world’s most admired brands, including nearly 90% of the Fortune 500® and more than 9,000 U.S.-based private companies. At Deloitte, we strive to live our purpose of making an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. We bring together distinct talents, technologies, disciplines, and an ecosystem of alliances to help tackle today’s most complex business challenges and drive long-term progress. Deloitte is proud to be part of the largest global professional services network serving our clients in the markets that are most important to them. Bringing more than 180 years of service, our network of member firms spans more than 150 countries and territories. Learn how Deloitte’s [...] About Deloitte [...] © 2026. See Terms of Use for more information.\n\nDeloitte refers to one or more of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee (\"DTTL\"), its network of member firms, and their related entities. DTTL and each of its member firms are legally separate and independent entities. DTTL (also referred to as \"Deloitte Global\") does not provide services to clients. In the United States, Deloitte refers to one or more of the US member firms of DTTL, their related entities that operate using the \"Deloitte\" name in the United States and their respective affiliates. Certain services may not be available to attest clients under the rules and regulations of public accounting. Please see www.deloitte.com/about to learn more about our global network of member firms.",
      "score": 0.048699286,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/outlook/15-productivity-features-in-the-new-outlook-for-windows/4522796",
      "title": "15 productivity features in the new Outlook for Windows",
      "content": "## Calendar and meeting features\n\nFor many people, the workday is shaped by meetings, schedule changes, and the need to stay aligned on what comes next. Outlook includes calendar and meeting capabilities that can help simplify planning, reduce coordination overhead, and make follow-up easier.\n\n1. Follow a meeting: Stay informed without attending live. The Follow RSVP option lets you indicate that you will not attend a meeting but still want access to the recap. This can be helpful when schedules overlap or when a meeting is useful to monitor without joining in real time. It can help you stay connected to outcomes and shared materials while keeping your calendar more manageable. [...] Updated Jun 02, 2026\n\nVersion 1.0\n\nadmin\n\nexchange\n\noffice 365\n\noutlook for windows\n\noutlook on the web\n\nVicki_Milton's avatar\n\nVicki\\_Milton\n\nIcon for Microsoft rankMicrosoft\n\nJoined September 06, 2019\n\nView Profile\n\nOutlook Blog \n\nWelcome to the Outlook Blog! Learn best practices, news, and trends and directly from the Outlook team. [...] 1. Schedule Send: Write on your schedule and deliver at the right time. Schedule Send lets you prepare messages when it is convenient for you and send them later at a time that works better for the recipient. This can improve visibility, support more intentional communication, and reduce the need to rely on reminders or leave messages in Drafts.\n\n1. Simplified folder sharing: Share folders more simply. Sharing a mail folder has traditionally required extra permission steps, especially for nested folders. Now, when you share a folder, Outlook can automatically apply the visibility permissions needed for its parent folders. This can reduce setup effort, help avoid access issues for recipients, and make folder sharing easier to complete with confidence.\n\n## Calendar and meeting features",
      "score": 0.04725421,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/concept-conditional-access-conditions",
      "title": "Conditional Access - Microsoft Entra ID",
      "content": "| Office 2016 (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OneNote only). | SharePoint | macOS |\n| Office 2019 | SharePoint | Windows 10, macOS |\n| Office mobile apps | SharePoint | Android, iOS |\n| Office Yammer app | Yammer | Windows 10, iOS, Android |\n| Outlook 2019 | SharePoint | Windows 10, macOS |\n| Outlook 2016 (Office for macOS) | Exchange Online | macOS |\n| Outlook mobile app | Exchange Online | Android, iOS |\n| Power BI app | Power BI service | Windows 10, Android, and iOS |\n| Azure DevOps Services (formerly Visual Studio Team Services, or VSTS) app | Azure DevOps Services (formerly Visual Studio Team Services, or VSTS) | Windows 10, iOS, and Android | [...] | Client apps | Target Service | Platform |\n --- \n| Dynamics CRM app | Dynamics CRM | Windows 10, iOS, and Android |\n| Mail/Calendar/People app, Outlook 2016, Outlook 2013 (with modern authentication) | Exchange Online | Windows 10 |\n| MFA and location policy for apps. Device-based policies aren’t supported. | Any My Apps app service | Android and iOS |\n| Microsoft Teams Services - this client app controls all services that support Microsoft Teams and all its Client Apps - Windows Desktop, iOS, Android, WP, and web client | Microsoft Teams | Windows 10, iOS, Android, and macOS |\n| Office 2016 apps, Universal Office apps, Office 2013 (with modern authentication), OneDrive sync client | SharePoint Online | Windows 10 | [...] Exchange Web Services (EWS) - A programming interface used by Outlook, Outlook for Mac, and non-Microsoft apps.\n       IMAP4 - Used by IMAP email clients.\n       MAPI over HTTP (MAPI/HTTP) - Used by Outlook 2010 and later.\n       Offline Address Book (OAB) - A copy of address list collections that are downloaded and used by Outlook.\n       Outlook Anywhere (RPC over HTTP) - Used by Outlook 2016 and earlier.\n       Outlook Service - Used by the Mail and Calendar app for Windows 10.\n       POP3 - Used by POP email clients.\n       Reporting Web Services - Used to retrieve report data in Exchange Online.",
      "score": 0.03255596,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://careers.cognizant.com/global-en/jobs/00068835141/program-manager",
      "title": "Program Manager, Bangalore, Karnataka, India | Cognizant Careers",
      "content": "### These will help you stand out\n\n Exposure to enterprise GenAI solutions such as Salesforce Einstein GPT, ServiceNow Now Assist, SAP Joule, Workday AI, or Oracle AI Services\n Experience with hybrid and multi-cloud AI deployment models\n Knowledge of AI observability, agent lifecycle management, and interoperability standards\n Experience mentoring architects and leading high-performing AI engineering teams\n Active participation in AI communities, industry forums, or thought leadership initiatives\n\n### Why consider this role?\n\nWe're excited to meet professionals who are passionate about shaping the future of AI and driving real-world impact through innovation. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply and bring your unique perspective to the role.\n\n--- [...] ### In this role, you will:\n\n Define and own enterprise-wide architecture for Generative AI and Agentic AI solutions aligned with business goals\n Design end-to-end AI systems, from model selection and context engineering to application integration and deployment\n Lead development of reusable frameworks, accelerators, and multi-agent architectures for enterprise use cases\n Drive prototyping, PoCs, and MVP initiatives to validate AI solutions across industries\n Establish governance, standards, and best practices for AI lifecycle, ethics, security, and performance\n\n### Work model [...] ### What you need to have to be considered\n\n 15+ years of IT experience, including 5+ years in AI/ML and hands-on experience in Generative AI / Agentic AI\n Strong expertise in designing and implementing Generative AI and multi-agent systems at enterprise scale\n Proven experience in enterprise architecture, including frameworks such as TOGAF or Zachman\n Hands-on experience with cloud AI platforms such as AWS (SageMaker, Bedrock), Azure AI (Studio/Foundry), or Google Vertex AI\n Experience defining AI governance models, compliance frameworks, and enterprise AI strategies\n Ability to design scalable architectures integrating AI platforms across enterprise ecosystems\n Strong stakeholder management skills with experience working with senior leadership\n\n### These will help you stand out",
      "score": 0.4191967,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/careers/jobdetails?id=R00333881_en&title=Workday+Certified+Financials+Adaptive+Consultant",
      "title": "Workday Certified Financials Adaptive Consultant - Accenture",
      "content": "You are…\n\nAs a member of one of the world’s largest Workday-certified teams, you’ll be part of a team of advisory professionals who deliver more than just technology. From rapid deployments to entire transformations, you’ll deliver integrated, payroll, cloud-based HR and financial management solutions that put organizations on the fast track to a better way to work.\n\nAs a Workday Adaptive Insights Consultant, your primary responsibilities may include: [...] Identification of business requirements, functional design, process design (including scenario design, flow mapping), prototyping, testing, training, defining support procedures\n Lead workshops for client education\n Conduct working sessions with clients to gather, understand, and analyze business requirements\n Lead in architecting Workday Adaptive Insights solutions to ensure that all work streams are cohesive and cross functional impacts are considered and mitigated  Advise clients on industry standards and leading practices\n Demonstrate design options through the use of prototyping\n Understand and apply Workday and Accenture methodologies\n\nWhy should I join the Accenture Workday team? [...] Learn and grow continuously. Harness unmatched training and professional development to help you build and advance your Workday, consultative and delivery skills. Use our interactive calculator to find out how this fast-moving tech could transform your job.\n\nKeep up with the trends shaping the future of business—and stay ahead in a fast-changing world.\n\nNEWSLETTER\n\n##### Keep up with AI innovations for business\n\nAI is moving fast. Our bimonthly LinkedIn newsletter helps you do the same. Subscribe for breaking AI news and actionable insights.\n\nDive into our forward-thinking research and uncover new tech and industry trends\n\nREPORT\n\n##### New work, new world 2026 [...] Connect your processes, people and insights across the enterprise with AI-enabled IPA.\n\nAI-powered business processes for the modern enterprise.\n\n##### Always on, seamless and personalized support\n\nCognizant® Autonomous Customer Engagement offers AI-powered, human-supported instant assistance with effortless handoffs and streamlined issue resolution, enabling seamless transactions, natural intelligent responsiveness, and delighted customers.\n\nScalable and efficient cloud solutions for business transformation.\n\nEnterprise-grade acceleration: The last mile advantage for AI.\n\nTurn big visions into practical realities with expertise that takes you further.\n\nOutpace cyber adversaries with an AI-driven preemptive defense.\n\n##### Neuro® Cybersecurity",

    {
      "url": "https://www.cognizant.com/us/en/insights/insights-blog/frontier-ai-exposes-enterprise-architecture-flaws",
      "title": "Beyond cybersecurity: AI exposes your entire architecture - Cognizant",
      "content": "### The structural mistake most enterprises are making\n\nMost enterprises are running two parallel programs: AI modernization, owned by the CIO, and AI security, owned by the CISO. Both are moving. In most organizations I encounter, they share very little beyond a slide in the same board deck.\n\nThis produces a predictable failure mode. Modernization acquires new AI capability on top of an estate that security has not finished hardening, while security discovers exposure from deployments it was not consulted on. AI modernization and AI security are not parallel workstreams. They are two views of the same program—and the gap between them is where the risk lives. [...] ##### Keep up with AI innovations for business\n\nAI is moving fast. Our bimonthly LinkedIn newsletter helps you do the same. Subscribe for breaking AI news and actionable insights. [...] The benchmark: technical debt below 20% of the estate is the AI-era resilience threshold. A measurable target, not a transformation aspiration.\n\n### Three conversations worth having this quarter",
      "score": 0.04864503,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/chronicle/docs/investigation/investigate-detections-in-search",
      "title": "Investigate detections in Search | Google Security Operations",
      "content": "`detection.created_time.nanos > 621828000 AND detection.feedback_summary.priority = \"PRIORITY_CRITICAL\"`\n\nFind all detections where the investigation reason is marked as malicious:\n\n`detection.investigation.reason = \"REASON_MALICIOUS\"`\n\nBasic detection search:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\ndetection.detection.rule_type = \"SINGLE_EVENT\"\ndetection.detection.rule_name = /.+/`\n\nExport to datatable:\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name = /.+/\noutcome:\n$x = detection.id\nexport:\n%new_detection_table.write_row(\ntesting: $x\n)`\n\nStatistical query (search on aggregated results):\n\n`detection.detection.rule_name != \"\"\nmatch:\ndetection.detection.rule_name\noutcome:\n$detection_count = count(detection.id)\norder:\n$detection_count desc`\n\n## Enable programmatic API [...] Use this table to map specific issues to actionable fixes.\n\nIssue | Description | Fix || Exceeded result limit | The query returns more than 100,000 results (or 10,000 for statistical queries). | Refine your search query with filters or reduce the time range to narrow down the dataset. |\n| Exceeded time range | The search spans more than 365 days. | Adjust the start and end times in the search parameters to span one year or less. |\n| API sorting error | Sorting fails when using collection proto fields. | Ensure the sorting fields are prefixed with `detection.` instead of `collection.`. |\n\n`detection.`\n`collection.`\n\n### Validation and testing\n\nTo determine if detections search is enabled, check if the Run search button\nstays disabled after entering a query. If it does, contact Support. [...] Value: Lets you identify compromised entities that are\nconsistently generating alerts across the environment. Instead of manually\nchecking individual rules, you can perform a single search to see the full\nscope of an entity's suspicious activity.\n\n### Granular attribute analysis\n\nObjective: Search for detections based on specific fields, such as\nparticular network connection types or log sources, rather than just rule names.\n\nValue: Enables targeted searching within the detection fields. Since\nmultiple rules might trigger on similar network behaviors, searching by the\nspecific attribute helps you isolate related security findings that would\notherwise be buried across disparate rule sets.\n\n### Incident scope identification",
      "score": 0.028245157,
      "raw_content": null
    }
  ],
  "response_time": 2.44,
  "request_id": "b8860725-b4d2-4ac0-90b5-b78b664dbb70"
}

      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/ai/ai-in-finance.html",
      "title": "What is AI in Finance? | Workday IN",
      "content": "Companies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations.\n\nWe believe that for AI to truly deliver on its future promise, it must be trustworthy. That’s why Workday has a Machine Learning Trust program that provides governance in defining procedures for the development and management of AI at Workday. This work helps us deliver technology benefits to our customers in line with our core values.​ As part of our commitment to trustworthy AI, we embrace the following principles: [...] As organizations continue to place a greater focus on AI, it’s critical that business leaders can trust their AI. At Workday, our approach leverages ethical AI principles that are built into the architecture of our finance solutions.\n\nCompanies will often describe their products as “AI-powered” without a clear explanation of what that means. Workday is the only major cloud financial management provider that embeds AI into its foundation. That enables our applications to natively leverage AI as part of the workflow, rather than through complicated integrations. [...] Workday has embedded AI into the very core of our platform.     {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html",
      "title": "Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog",
      "content": "That’s why the Workday CLM product team is currently developing new AI tools to automate the drafting of contract playbooks based on existing contracts, with the goal of making playbook creation as simple as uploading sample documents (like existing agreements and policy notes) from which our AI can derive preferred and fallback language and criteria.\n\nWe’re also working to deliver users proactive insights over time about their teams’ adherence to those playbooks, so they can make informed decisions about keeping those playbooks current and optimized. Watch this space!\n\n## Staying Ahead of the Curve [...] The Workday Contract Negotiation Agent removes the friction of the drafting process, allowing you to focus on the strategic outcomes your contracts are actually meant to secure—achieving the true ends of your negotiation faster and more easily than ever before.\n\nWant to learn more? Book a demo today to see the Workday Contract Negotiation Agent in action.\n\nWhat Is Agentic AI?\n\nStill unsure on the difference between the types of AI agents, or just need a refresher on the basics? Workday has you covered.  \n   \n Learn More\n\nWorkday’s Bold Future With Responsible Agentic AI\n\nAgentic AI is here, which means the correct governance is crucial. Learn how Workday is doubling down on its commitment to responsible AI.\n\nRead Blog\n\nTrust by Design: How Workday Builds AI That Puts People First [...] It leverages sophisticated AI orchestration, enabling it to choose the best AI tool to support the task at hand. Instead of relying exclusively on one large language model (LLM), its capabilities grow naturally with the latest advances in AI.\n Its foundational, purpose-built AI helps ensure accurate results from LLMs by conducting core analysis of the customer’s contract repository and creating a trusted base of structured data to enable retrieval augmented generation (RAG).\n It’s fully customizable, enabling customers to create their own AI models to extract and generate all the contract data points they care about over time, rather than being limited to a finite set of core extractions.\n\n## Driving AI Responsibility",
      "score": 0.78314424,
      "raw_content": null
    },

    {
      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/hr/hris.html",
      "title": "What Is an HRIS? How IT and HR Are Evolving Together - Workday",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management [...] #### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Payroll\n\n### Planning\n\nThe planning system that integrates with any ERP/GL or data source.\n\n#### Planning\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Planning\n\n#### Close & Consolidation\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Operational Planning\n\n### Legal\n\nTake control of your contract data and drive results across your organization.\n\n#### Legal Overview\n\n#### Products\n\n#### Contract Intelligence\n\n#### Contract Lifecycle Management\n\n### Platform\n\nGain a flexible foundation that helps you move beyond the limitations of legacy ERP.\n\n#### IT Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Our Technology\n\n#### Extensions\n\n#### Orchestrate & Integrations\n\n#### Data Cloud\n\n#### Workday Extend\n\n### Workday ERP [...] HRIS: An HRIS collects and stores employee data. It compiles information on employee demographics, job status, contact information, compensation, and other areas.\n HRMS: An HRMS expands on the data management tools of an HRIS to include a wider range of software and utility. The term originated in the early twenty-first century to describe HR software suites supporting payroll, applicant tracking, benefits, and talent management.\n HCMS: An HCMS encompasses the functionality of an HRIS and HRMS while supporting a wider breadth of HR functions, including employee experience and analytics. HCM, in general, largely refers to HR suites designed with cloud computing at the core, ensuring they remain adaptive.",
      "score": 0.69399214,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/careers/jobdetails?id=R00333881_en&title=Workday+Certified+Financials+Adaptive+Consultant",
      "title": "Workday Certified Financials Adaptive Consultant - Accenture",
      "content": "You are…\n\nAs a member of one of the world’s largest Workday-certified teams, you’ll be part of a team of advisory professionals who deliver more than just technology. From rapid deployments to entire transformations, you’ll deliver integrated, payroll, cloud-based HR and financial management solutions that put organizations on the fast track to a better way to work.\n\nAs a Workday Adaptive Insights Consultant, your primary responsibilities may include: [...] Identification of business requirements, functional design, process design (including scenario design, flow mapping), prototyping, testing, training, defining support procedures\n Lead workshops for client education\n Conduct working sessions with clients to gather, understand, and analyze business requirements\n Lead in architecting Workday Adaptive Insights solutions to ensure that all work streams are cohesive and cross functional impacts are considered and mitigated  Advise clients on industry standards and leading practices\n Demonstrate design options through the use of prototyping\n Understand and apply Workday and Accenture methodologies\n\nWhy should I join the Accenture Workday team? [...] #### Technology jobs: Be the catalyst\n\nGet hands-on with the technologies that our clients need to reinvent, work in new ways and change the world for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n#### Jobs in technology platforms: Create foundations for the future\n\nYou’ll build the tech that transforms how business works for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n## Learn more about the hiring process at Accenture\n\n Benefits information\n Hiring process (Q&A)\n Interview tips\n Using AI",

    {
      "url": "https://apply.deloitte.com/en_US/careers/JobDetail/Delivery-Senior-Consultant-Workday-HCM-Payroll-Benefits-Module-s/354651",
      "title": "Delivery Senior Consultant, Workday HCM Payroll/Benefits Module(s)",
      "content": "### Position Summary\n\nOur Deloitte Human Capital team helps organizations create value through people performance. We work with clients to reimagine work, the workforce, and the workplace across the enterprise and to transform their HR functions with AI and emerging technology. With the rapid pace of change in today’s world, you will help clients answer questions like: How do I access, develop, and motivate my workforce? What should my AI strategy be for the HR function? Do I have the right organization and culture to enable performance? Join our team to make work better for humans and humans better at work.\n\nWork You'll Do\n\nAs a Delivery Senior Consultant for our team, you will be responsible for:\n\nA successful candidate would possess these skills:\n\nThe Team [...] The Team\n\nDeloitte’s Government & Public Services (GPS) practice – our people, ideas, technology and outcomes – is designed for impact. Serving federal, state, & local government clients as well as public higher education institutions, our team of professionals brings fresh perspective to help clients anticipate disruption, reimagine the possible, and fulfill their mission promise.\n\nOur HR Strategy & Technology offering develops leading global HR strategies and implements HR technologies that drive transformation, maximize the workforce experience, and sustain HR effectiveness and value while navigating a world of disruption. [...] © 2026. See  Terms of Use  for more information.\n\nDeloitte refers to one or more of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee (\"DTTL\"), its network of member firms, and their related entities. DTTL and each of its member firms are legally separate and independent entities. DTTL (also referred to as \"Deloitte Global\") does not provide services to clients. In the United States, Deloitte refers to one or more of the US member firms of DTTL, their related entities that operate using the \"Deloitte\" name in the United States and their respective affiliates. Certain services may not be available to attest clients under the rules and regulations of public accounting. Please see www.deloitte.com/about to learn more about our global network of member firms.",

      "title": "BI Engineering Specialist - Accenture",

    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/xx/en/what-we-do/services/ai/trusted-ai-framework.html",
      "title": "KPMG Trusted AI framework",
      "content": "Human-centric\n\nWe prioritize human impact as we deploy AI and recognize the needs of our clients and our people. We are embracing this technology to empower and augment human capabilities — to unleash creativity and improve productivity in a way that allows people to reimagine how they spend their days.\n\nTrustworthy\n\nWe will adhere to our principles and the ethical pillars that guide how and why we use AI across its lifecycle. We will strive to ensure our data acquisition, governance and usage practices upholds ethical standards and complies with applicable privacy and data protection regulations, as well as any confidentiality requirements. [...] ### Privacy\n\nAI solutions should be designed to comply with applicable privacy and data protection laws and regulations.\n\n### Sustainability\n\nAI solutions should be designed to be energy efficient, reduce carbon emissions and support a cleaner environment.\n\n### Explainability\n\nAI solutions should be developed and delivered in a way that answers the questions of how and why a conclusion was drawn from the solution.\n\n### Integrity\n\nData used in AI solutions should be acquired in compliance with applicable laws and regulations and assessed for accuracy, completeness, appropriateness and quality to drive trusted decisions.\n\n### Transparency [...] ### Transparency\n\nAI solutions should include responsible disclosure to provide stakeholders with a clear understanding of what is happening in each solution across the AI lifecycle.\n\n### Fairness\n\nAI solutions should be designed to reduce or eliminate bias against individuals, communities and groups.\n\n### Accountability\n\nHuman oversight and responsibility should be embedded across the AI lifecycle to manage risk and comply with applicable laws and regulations.\n\n### Reliability\n\nAI solutions should consistently operate in accordance with their intended purpose and scope and at the desired level of precision.\n\n### Security\n\nRobust and resilient practices should be implemented to safeguard AI solutions against bad actors, misinformation or adverse events.\n\n### Safety",
      "score": 0.06841928,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/be/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] While most are focused on educating employees, far fewer are re-architecting roles, workflows, and career paths. The most successful organisations reimagine jobs to seamlessly combine human strengths and AI capabilities, ensuring both aspects are used to their fullest potential. New roles—AI operations managers, human-AI interaction specialists, quality stewards, and others—signal a deeper shift: AI is now a structural component of how work is organised. Advanced organisations streamline workflows that AI can execute end-to-end, while humans focus on judgement, exception handling, and strategic oversight. The goal isn't replacing humans or merely assisting them, but creating complementary working relationships between humans and AI—where the combined output exceeds what either could [...] A financial services company is building agentic workflows to automatically capture meeting actions from video conferences, draft communications to remind participants of their next steps, and track follow-through.\n An air carrier is using AI agents to help customers complete the most common transactions, such as rebooking a flight or rerouting bags, freeing up human agents to address more complex matters.\n A manufacturer is using AI agents to support new product development initiatives, leveraging AI to find the optimal balance between competing objectives such as cost and time-to-market.\n In the public sector, AI agents are being used to cover workforce shortages, partnering with human workers to complete key processes.",
      "score": 0.058094297,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/industries/health-industries/library/develop-a-deep-understanding-of-consumers.html",
      "title": "Develop a deep understanding of consumers: Owning the doctor's ...",
      "content": "Intake data, scheduling behavior, missed appointments, and Social Determinants of Health indicators can be used today to tailor journeys, anticipate needs, and intervene earlier if organizations are set up to act on them. The real constraint is the operating model: who owns personalization, how insights trigger action, and how teams coordinate across care, access, and engagement. Treating personalization as an enterprise capability (not a technology feature) can unlock meaningful impact quickly while building the foundation for more advanced capabilities over time.\n\nInnovator’s move: Treat personalization as an enterprise autopilot. AI orchestrates cross‑team actions in real time, continuously adapting journeys using the data you already have, while governance defines the guardrails. [...] ## 5. Preserve trust.\n\nCombine clinical, behavioral, consumer, and contextual data as strategic assets supported by trust and governance. The convergence of these data sources introduces real privacy, cybersecurity, and ethical risks that can erode consumer confidence if not tightly managed. Industry-leading organizations respond by establishing enterprise‑level data and AI governance, making transparency a competitive differentiator, and treating trust as a measurable outcome rather than a compliance exercise. The ability to govern data responsibly is what often allows providers to scale innovation without sacrificing credibility. Getting the foundation right, first, and early, allows for the value of data to be realized.       "title": "Artificial Intelligence (AI) Solutions for Business | Workday BE",
      "content": "## Products. #### AI Masterclass. Discover superintelligence for the new world of work. #### Responsible AI. ### AI. #### Payroll.     {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/agentforce/operations/process-intelligence",
      "title": "What Is Process Intelligence? | Salesforce",
      "content": "A flat illustration of a woman interacting with a computer monitor displaying a digital brain with circuit patterns and refresh arrows, symbolizing AI machine learning, data processing, and continuous process optimization. # What is Process Intelligence? As business becomes more integrated with Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems and tools, it’s beneficial to employ AI’s capabilities to strengthen business processes. This article explains how process intelligence uses a digital twin of the organization to analyze activity data and uncover opportunities to forecast more accurately, cut costs, and make your operations better. On top of that, many teams are still relying on outdated reporting that shows what happened, but not how work actually unfolded or what to change next. Process intelligence is the practice of collecting system and task data, modeling it into a digital twin of the organization, and using AI to analyze and predict business processes. It combines process mining, task mining, and artificial intelligence process capabilities to transform raw data into actionable insights. What’s the difference between process intelligence and business intelligence (BI)?",
      "score": 0.98381,
      "raw_content": null,
      "from_cache": true
    },
    {
      "url": "https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work",
      "title": "Asana launches AI-powered products to help organizations manage ...",
      "content": "Asana Inc. announced today during the company’s Work Innovation Summit in London the launch of a new product suite that helps organizations manage work by humans and artificial intelligence agents using the same plan. This new set of tools includes three key parts: a chief of AI staff for every Asana user, three new applications for teams and a new generation of AI teammates. This AI agent acts as a layer-shared system for employees that understands goals, priorities and what work needs attention across teams and tools. With this kind of broad view, it can help work move along in a shared system where teams operate by letting humans manage what they do best and tracking what falls through the cracks. The Asana Work Graph is a data model created by the company that maps an organization’s tasks, projects, goals and relationships. ### A new generation of AI teammates. As every new generation of AI models rolls out, so do the harnesses for AI agents.",
      "score": 0.98293,

    {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/news/stories/agentic-marketing-teams-announcement",
      "title": "Salesforce Puts an AI Marketing Team in Every Marketer's Hands",
      "content": "Agents build pipeline, create content, and run campaigns so the marketer can set the strategy, own the results, and get back to making. Today at Connections, Salesforce gave marketers their own AI team, agents that collaborate with them to build pipeline, create content, and run campaigns. This frees marketers to be makers again — spending more time on the strategic and creative work of shaping the stories, campaigns, and experiences that move customers and drive growth. Rather than chasing reports, waiting on deliverables, and managing handoffs, marketers can work alongside agents that serve as analytical and creative partners — helping them validate ideas, qualify leads, create complete campaigns, and optimize customer experiences as behavior evolves. We’re more agile, we can test at a more refined level, and the scope of our personalization has exploded. Built on the world’s #1 CDP and Agentforce, Salesforce has the only agentic marketing platform that unifies customer and business data, content, conversations, and workflows across marketing, sales, service, and commerce.",
      "score": 0.97818,
      "raw_content": null,

    {
      "url": "https://blog.workday.com/en-us/agentic-contract-review-redlining-here.html",
      "title": "Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here - Workday Blog",
      "content": "# Agentic Contract Review and Redlining Is Here. AI-native, full-document review and redlining doesn’t just speed contract negotiations, it helps fulfill their true purpose. Using generative AI to speed contract review and redlining is not new. Customers of Workday Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) routinely ask it to review specific clauses against their preferred language and suggest edits to make them compliant. **But three years in AI terms is a generation.** Today we’re at a new threshold in agentic contract negotiation, and there’s no going back. The **Workday Contract Negotiation Agent** now reviews and edits entire documents at once, delivering accurate, automated pre-signature contract analysis and redlining like never before. That’s why it’s best to take advantage of innovative contract AI in the context of a complete contract management solution—one that has your full repository of executed contracts as well as your templates, playbooks, approved clause library, and pre-signature workflows.",
      "score": 0.97404,
      "raw_content": null,

      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/about/press-room/deloitte-collaborates-with-google-wiz-ai-defense.html",
      "title": "Deloitte collaborates with Google Cloud and Wiz on AI powered ...",
      "content": "# Deloitte Collaborates with Google Cloud and Wiz on Human-in-the-Loop, AI-Powered Cyber Defense. Deloitte today announced an expansion of its strategic alliance with Google Cloud and Wiz. By integrating Google Cloud’s AI Threat Defense solution with its Continuous Threat Exposure Management (CTEM) platform, Deloitte is delivering a consolidated solution for broad visibility and streamlined governance. Through this integrated offering, Deloitte is helping to deliver Google AI Threat Defense, which fuses the reasoning power of Gemini and other frontier models, the contextual risk prioritization of Wiz, the code remediation capabilities of Gemini and CodeMender and the frontline experience of Mandiant. Combined with Deloitte's CTEM platform and deep technical experience in building business-context-aware AI agents, together these organizations are helping clients adopt new agentic workflows that improve cyber defenses. The integrated solution combines Google Cloud and Wiz’s technologies with Deloitte’s experience and scale. By bringing together Google Cloud's agentic AI, Wiz's deep cloud context and Deloitte's security integration experience, we are helping to pioneer a new paradigm.",

      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/agentforce/operations/process-orchestration",
      "title": "What Is Process Orchestration? | Salesforce",
      "content": "A flat illustration of a woman interacting with a computer monitor displaying a digital brain with circuit patterns and refresh arrows, symbolizing AI machine learning, data processing, and continuous process optimization. # What is Process Orchestration and How to Use It? As businesses scale across systems, teams, and APIs, workflows often break down across handoffs and disconnected tools. In this article, we’re going to go over process orchestration in detail so you can make an informed decision about the best approach for your business. Process orchestration coordinates people, systems, workflows, and AI so complex processes run reliably end to end, reducing errors and keeping work moving without manual coordination. For example, at the enterprise level, process orchestration can span customer onboarding across CRM, billing, compliance, and AI review. ### Process Orchestration vs Workflow Automation. Some middleware is being eclipsed by AI-embedded process orchestration systems with far more robust capabilities, including enterprise-level visibility, full integration, and oversight of all systems and processes.",

      "raw_content": null,
      "from_cache": true

    {
      "url": "https://www.bain.com/insights/your-ai-budget-is-growing-your-returns-arent-heres-why",
      "title": "Your AI Budget Is Growing. Your Returns Aren't. Here's Why.",
      "content": "And rather than pausing to understand why, 90% of those same companies are now increasing their budgets again—this time for AI agents that will operate with even greater autonomy, complexity, and consequence. They are realizing the savings they targeted, deploying agents with genuine confidence, and funding the next wave from returns that actually materialized. The problem is the gap between what the investment case assumed and what is actually running. If your agentic AI business case was built on the economics of full automation and the reality is a system routing a significant share of decisions to a human queue, the CFO approved one set of numbers, and the organization is living with another. Rather than rush to full autonomy, the imperative here is to close the gap between the business case and the operating reality, and to be honest about the economics of what is actually in production vs.",

      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/be/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "Deloitte logo return to homepage. making an impact that matters. # The State of AI in the Enterprise. ## Deloitte's 2026 AI report tracking adoption and impact. Organisations today stand at the untapped edge of AI's potential. Our 2026 AI report reveals that success hinges on the ability to move boldly from ambition to activation. Get a tailored snapshot of your industry to understand where AI is making the biggest impact. ### What’s top of mind when it comes to AI in the enterprise? Improving productivity and efficiency top the list of benefits achieved from enterprise AI adoption so far, with two-thirds (66%) of organisations reporting gains. **Agentic AI:** While agentic AI is expected to have the highest impact in customer support, use cases for supply chain management, R&D, knowledge management, and cybersecurity are also seen as having particularly high potential. The State of AI in the Enterprise is a research series by the Deloitte AI Institute™ exploring trends, practices and challenges in scaling AI.",

      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "Search and knowledge management\n Virtual assistants/chatbots\n Content generation\n\nAgentic AI: While agentic AI is expected to have the highest impact in customer support, use cases for supply chain management, R&D, knowledge management, and cybersecurity are also seen as having particularly high potential. The enterprises we interviewed are deploying autonomous AI agents across multiple functions: [...] Agentic AI: While agentic AI is expected to have the highest impact in customer support, use cases for supply chain management, R&D, knowledge management, and cybersecurity are also seen as having particularly high potential. The enterprises we interviewed are deploying autonomous AI agents across multiple functions:\n\nPhysical AI: Physical AI applications span a wide range of industrial and commercial settings. Common use cases for physical AI include:\n\n#### Governance, data, and regulation\n\n### How do I manage AI governance, data, and regulation? [...] Organisational structures are beginning to flatten as AI absorbs routine execution tasks. Some companies are merging technology and people-leadership functions to ensure that systems and workforce design evolve together. The pace of AI change management varies by industry, but the direction is consistent: Roles, skills, and career paths should be rebuilt, not simply adjusted. Organisations should take an AI-native approach and redesign work holistically rather than layering AI onto legacy processes.\n\n### Key findings from this year's AI report\n\n# Discover Agentic AI\n\nLearn how Deloitte can help your organisation blend human expertise and autonomous intelligence, rethink business functions, drive smarter decisions, and generate value at scale.\n\nStart your Agentic Journey with Deloitte",
      "score": 0.6847074,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.forrester.com/blogs/the-state-of-agentic-ai-in-2026-companies-are-chasing-few-are-catching",
      "title": "The State of Agentic AI in 2026: Companies Are Chasing, Few Are ...",
      "content": "Forrester Logo\n\n# The State Of Agentic AI In 2026: Companies Are Chasing, Few Are Catching\n\nBrian Hopkins , VP, Emerging Tech Portfolio\n\nCraig Le Clair \n, Vice President, Principal Analyst\n\nJeff Pollard \n, VP, Principal Analyst\n\nRowan Curran \n, Principal Analyst\n\nLeslie Joseph \n, Principal Analyst\n\nThree-quarters of enterprise leaders tell us they’re adopting agentic AI. Only a small minority have it running in meaningful production beyond “agentish” chatbots and true scaled multiagent systems are rarer still. That’s the gap between the chase and the catch, and it’s the story of 2026. The technology is a runaway train. The enterprise is the heavy load it has to pull. [...] My colleagues and I just published The State Of Agentic AI, 2026, talking to the architects building agentic systems and digging through Forrester’s survey data to put meat on the bones of the story. Our read is that the technology has arrived and enterprise readiness hasn’t caught up. That shouldn’t surprise anybody. We’ve seen this story before. The harder question is whether readiness can ever catch a technology moving this fast.\n\n### Long-Horizon Agents Are No Longer On The Horizon [...] ### The Chase Is Easy — The Catch Is Expensive\n\nInterest is everywhere. Scale is rare. The reasons are stubbornly consistent, and they start with money. ROI uncertainty traps enterprise ambition in pilot mode, because most companies can’t justify production beyond narrow efficiency gains. Governance gaps drive agentic sprawl. More than half of enterprises report it even after adopting the NIST AI RMF, because a policy document can’t control an autonomous, tool-invoking system. And platform confusion freezes commitment while teams argue over whether to bet on a SaaS agent, an SI-built system, or a custom build.",
      "score": 0.5973971,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://go.oracle.com/LP=152046?src1=%3Aow%3Aevp%3Acpo%3A%3A%3A%3ARC_CORP260309P00029%3ALPD400443854&intcmp=CORP251218P00029%3Aow%3Aevp%3Acpo%3A%3A%3A%3ARC_CORP260309P00029%3ALPD400443854&elqTrackId=4dd0883ff9e345c29d1b61c784267067&elq=00000000000000000000000000000000&elqaid=152674&elqat=2&elqCampaignId=635939&elqak=8AF543C6A967451AC11F40B9C84911F38F4FFF312F17CE34DA30C2FD15599AB817F6",
      "title": "Day One and Beyond: Oracle AI Platform for Your Enterprise Applications",
      "content": "Discover how Oracle’s integrated suite—spanning ERP, HCM, SCM, EPM, and CX—connects data across your enterprise to turn everyday work into smarter outcomes.  \n   \n Oracle AI is ready out of the box. In many cases, customers can simply turn it on—no complex integrations, no custom models, no separate platforms to manage.  \n   \n These sessions are focused specifically on how embedded AI delivers real-world value through:  \n   \n • Agents, Agent Teams, and Agent Workflows: Discover how embedded AI agents accelerate and streamline routine tasks, enabling teams to focus more time on higher-value, strategic activities.  \n • Predictive Insights: Leverage recommendations and anomaly detection to stay ahead of risks. [...] Duration: 60 mins\n\n### Webinar Description\n\nStart using AI tools already included in Oracle Cloud HCM and see how classic, generative AI, and AI agents can boost efficiency without extra investment or complex setup.   \n  \n Explore the practical AI capabilities included in your Oracle Cloud HCM solution. Discover how classic AI, generative AI, and AI agents are available and ready to help boost efficiency across your HR processes. Learn how to start making the most of the AI tools at your fingertips without new investments or complex setup.\n\n  \n  \n\nAre You Ready For Oracle AI?\n\n Oracle AI Success Navigator\n Get started with Oracle AI)\n Cloud Application Readiness Center\n\n### Event Speaker\n\nDaria Hashemi-Rad\n\nDaria Hashemi-Rad HCM Solution Engineer\n\nGloria Brewster [...] Imagine being able to accurately forecast cash inflows and outflows by seamlessly leveraging transactional data directly from your accounting systems. Imagine proactively addressing cash shortfalls by intelligently optimizing working capital and leveraging alternative payment instruments with the help of AI-powered automation. Imagine AI agents that can reason, decide, and execute tasks on your behalf 24x7 — only involving your team when human intervention is needed, to help improve collections efficiency from slow-paying customers.  \n  \n You no longer have to imagine it.",

    },
    {
      "url": "https://community.ibm.com/community/user/blogs/aliannah-muzaffar/2026/06/03/activating-content-for-the-era-of-agentic-automati",
      "title": "Activating Content for the Era of Agentic Automation",
      "content": "This month, IBM announced IBM Content Cortex (planned availability in June 2026), a next-generation content services system designed to prepare, govern, and activate enterprise content for agentic automation. Content Cortex represents a new paradigm for transforming content repositories from passive systems of record into trusted foundations for AI-powered business operations.\n\n## Coming Soon: IBM Content Cortex\n\nCentralize Content\n\nOrganizations can bring together fragmented enterprise content into a trusted source of truth for both employees and AI agents\n\nGovern Content\n\nSecurity, compliance, retention, and governance remain embedded throughout the content lifecycle, ensuring that content remains trusted and usable as AI adoption expands\n\nActivate Content [...] When content is governed, discoverable, and AI-ready, agents can help organizations automate repetitive tasks while allowing employees to focus on higher-value work. In financial services, this could mean automatically organizing and classifying incoming loan documentation before underwriting begins, while for insurance companies, claims documents could be routed correctly, with updated metadata as soon as new information arrives. In the public sector, agencies could manage large volumes of incoming forms and supporting documentation without creating additional administrative overhead.\n\nContent growth doesn’t have to mean storage growth [...] #### By Aliannah Muzaffar posted 8 hours ago\n\n# \n\nAI agents have already changed the way work is being done, but agents are only as effective as the content they access. Enterprises already possess enormous stores of business-critical knowledge in documents, reports, correspondence, statements, images, case files….the list goes on. Businesses have plenty of information, but the challenge is that content remains fragmented, difficult to govern, and challenging to activate for AI-driven work.",
      "score": 0.39515805,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/migration-and-modernization/how-aws-is-using-agentic-ai-to-reinvent-infrastructure-modernization",
      "title": "How AWS Is Using Agentic AI To Reinvent Infrastructure Modernization | Migration & Modernization",
      "content": "For enterprises in regulated industries such as financial services, healthcare, and government, we have added the ability to bring your own Amazon S3 bucket for storing migration artifacts. Encrypt with your own keys, manage access through your own policies, and meet your data sovereignty requirements without changing how you use AWS Transform. Our optional Transform discovery tool runs entirely on premises without requiring cloud connectivity and makes it easy to anonymize discovery data exports, making it particularly well suited for regulated industries and organizations with strict data governance. Data is collected and stored locally on the virtual appliance. No data is transmitted to AWS unless you choose to upload the exported data. [...] Once your business case is ready, the next question tends to be the hardest one: where do we start? A large enterprise environment might have tens of thousands of servers running thousands of applications, each with years of tangled dependencies on databases, middleware, firewalls, and other systems. Figuring out which applications can move together, which ones must go first, and which will break everything else if sequenced wrong used to take migration teams months of manual effort, whiteboard sessions, and spreadsheet analysis. And even after all that work, a lot of it was still guesswork.\n\nAWS Transform replaces the uncertainty with precision and flexibility. [...] We have also addressed one of the most common sources of post-migration connectivity failures: DHCP. In many legacy enterprise networks, IP addresses were hard-coded whereas modern networks use dynamic assignments (DHCP). When migrating to AWS, security group rules targeting specific IPs can silently break when DHCP assigns a different address post-migration. Using network modernization, Transform now handles this automatically. Continue using static IP or move to DHCP. We’ll automatically generate appropriate security groups, document every adjustment and eliminate manual remediation that used to follow cutover. No more scrambling to restore connectivity after go-live.",
      "score": 0.37903407,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/about/press-room/deloitte-collaborates-with-google-wiz-ai-defense.html",
      "title": "Deloitte collaborates with Google Cloud and Wiz on AI powered ...",
      "content": "Utilizing an integrated platform with advanced AI-driven code analysis and auto-remediation agents, Deloitte can deliver powerful and scalable solutions for cyber teams tasked with protecting their organization's most important assets. In addition, the collaboration aims to help organizations with the following:\n\nKey quotes\n\nWe are at an inflection point where traditional approaches to security operations can no longer keep pace with the threat landscape. By bringing together Google Cloud's agentic AI, Wiz's deep cloud context and Deloitte's security integration experience, we are helping to pioneer a new paradigm. This human-led, AI-powered approach allows organizations to build resilience by design and manage cyber risk at the speed of business. [...] Through this integrated offering, Deloitte is helping to deliver Google AI Threat Defense, which fuses the reasoning power of Gemini and other frontier models, the contextual risk prioritization of Wiz, the code remediation capabilities of Gemini and CodeMender and the frontline experience of Mandiant. Combined with Deloitte's CTEM platform and deep technical experience in building business-context-aware AI agents, together these organizations are helping clients adopt new agentic workflows that improve cyber defenses.\n\nWhy this matters [...] — Adnan Amjad, US Cyber Leader, Deloitte\n\nYou can’t bring traditional security to an AI fight. AI Threat Defense combines Wiz’s multicloud visibility with Google’s AI and security expertise. Collaborating with Deloitte to deliver customizable, agentic defense helps us scale this offering, empowering organizations to stop AI-driven threats at machine speed.\n\n— Francis deSouza, Chief Operating Officer, Google Cloud and President, Security Products\n\nThis collaboration accelerates AI adoption for organizations at scale, making a human-led, AI-powered approach to vulnerability management a reality. In addition to this expanded alliance, Deloitte was awarded six 2026 Google Cloud Partner of the Year awards, including in Global Managed Security Service. Learn more about the collaboration here.",
      "score": 0.22859572,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.forrester.com/blogs/ai-is-forging-a-new-revops-identity",
      "title": "AI Is Forging A New RevOps Identity - Forrester",
      "content": "###### Categories\n\n### Get The Insights At Work Newsletter\n\n### Thanks for signing up.\n\nStay tuned for updates from the Forrester blogs.\n\n### AI, Buying Networks, Revenue Growth — Master It All\n\n#### Forrester’s B2B Summits connect you with analysts and insights to help you navigate buying shifts, win with AI, accelerate growth, and transform revenue.\n\n## The State Of Agentic AI In 2026: Companies Are Chasing, Few Are Catching\n\n## Move Over, WAF: The Web Application Protection Platform Takes Over\n\n## Get The Insights At Work Newsletter\n\n### Thanks for signing up.\n\nStay tuned for updates from the Forrester blogs.\n\n##### Who We Are\n\n##### What We Offer\n\n##### About\n\nForrester Logo\nForrester Logo\n\n##### [...] What RevOps Leaders Need To Do NowAI alters perceptions of personal competence, requires rebuilding confidence, and changes why and how RevOps is seen as the authority in the room. Leaders must actively manage the identity implications of AI adoption. Five steps matter most:\n\nThe Bottom LineAI is not taking authority away from RevOps — it is changing where it comes from, from systems to judgment, from expertise to wisdom, and from control to trust. Those RevOps leaders who guide their teams through this identity shift will become indispensable decision partners in the AI‑enabled enterprise.\n\nTo learn more about how Forrester’s RevOps analysts can help guide and support your AI journey, contact me directly at amcpartlin@forrester.com.\n\n###### Categories [...] Forrester Logo\n\n# AI Is Forging A New RevOps Identity\n\nAnthony McPartlin , Principal Analyst\n\nMost conversations about AI in revenue operations (RevOps) focus on efficiency, automation, and scale. Those benefits are real, but they’re not the most profound change AI is bringing to RevOps. The deeper shift is personal.",
