    {
      "url": "https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/ai/microsoft-iq",
      "title": "Unified Enterprise Intelligence for AI - Microsoft IQ",
      "content": "### Self-service HR agents for employees\n\nHelp desks are overwhelmed with routine questions, and employees waste time hunting for answers across policy docs, and word of mouth. Deploy an agent that instantly answers common HR and IT questions—grounded in your actual policies via Foundry IQ—and auto-creates tickets when human help is needed.  \n   \n Power users can build and publish this agent in Copilot Studio with point-and-click connectors to ServiceNow, Workday, and SharePoint. Developers who need full control can use Foundry and the Microsoft Agent Framework to integrate MCP servers, custom APIs, and enterprise-grade scaling—all surfaced through Teams and M365 Copilot.\n\nLearn more\n\nA women working on laptop.\n\n### AI-driven time-to-market acceleration [...] A women talking next to a laptop\n Maintain accuracy and consistency as agents reason over terabytes of enterprise data, generating knowledge as usage grows, so agents get smarter and more reliable over time, even as complexity increases.\n\n  A women pointing at a desktop screen\n\n### Intelligence without silos. Innovation without limits.\n\n Bring together how people work, how the business operates, and how to connect and reuse knowledge into a single, real-time view for your agents. Enable your AI to understand what’s in motion, what’s been decided, and what needs attention across workflows and your enterprise systems (CRM, ERP, HR, etc), all with the data and applications you already use. [...] Learn more\n\n### Fabric IQ\n\nProvides a shared semantic foundation of your business—aligning data, entities, and rules so agents reason consistently and drive accurate, context-aware decisions.\n\nLearn more\n\n### Foundry IQ\n\nTransforms fragmented data into governed, reusable knowledge—connecting agents to the right data, systems, and context to act effectively.\n\nLearn more\n\n### Web IQ\n\nDelivers fresh, real-world intelligence from across the web – including web pages, news, images, and videos.\n\nLearn more\n\nUse cases\n\n## Microsoft IQ is your IQ advantage\n\nSimplify revenue collection\n\n### Self-service HR agents for employees",
      "score": 0.767638,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://go.oracle.com/LP=152046?src1=%3Aow%3Aevp%3Acpo%3A%3A%3A%3ARC_CORP260309P00029%3ALPD400443854&intcmp=CORP251218P00029%3Aow%3Aevp%3Acpo%3A%3A%3A%3ARC_CORP260309P00029%3ALPD400443854&elqTrackId=4dd0883ff9e345c29d1b61c784267067&elq=00000000000000000000000000000000&elqaid=152674&elqat=2&elqCampaignId=635939&elqak=8AF543C6A967451AC11F40B9C84911F38F4FFF312F17CE34DA30C2FD15599AB817F6",
      "title": "Day One and Beyond: Oracle AI Platform for Your Enterprise ...",
      "content": "Duration: 60 mins\n\n### Webinar Description\n\nStart using AI tools already included in Oracle Cloud HCM and see how classic, generative AI, and AI agents can boost efficiency without extra investment or complex setup.   \n  \n Explore the practical AI capabilities included in your Oracle Cloud HCM solution. Discover how classic AI, generative AI, and AI agents are available and ready to help boost efficiency across your HR processes. Learn how to start making the most of the AI tools at your fingertips without new investments or complex setup.\n\n  \n  \n\nAre You Ready For Oracle AI?\n\n Oracle AI Success Navigator\n Get started with Oracle AI)\n Cloud Application Readiness Center\n\n### Event Speaker\n\nDaria Hashemi-Rad\n\nDaria Hashemi-Rad HCM Solution Engineer\n\nGloria Brewster [...] Discover how Oracle’s integrated suite—spanning ERP, HCM, SCM, EPM, and CX—connects data across your enterprise to turn everyday work into smarter outcomes.  \n   \n Oracle AI is ready out of the box. In many cases, customers can simply turn it on—no complex integrations, no custom models, no separate platforms to manage.  \n   \n These sessions are focused specifically on how embedded AI delivers real-world value through:  \n   \n • Agents, Agent Teams, and Agent Workflows: Discover how embedded AI agents accelerate and streamline routine tasks, enabling teams to focus more time on higher-value, strategic activities.  \n • Predictive Insights: Leverage recommendations and anomaly detection to stay ahead of risks. [...] Imagine being able to accurately forecast cash inflows and outflows by seamlessly leveraging transactional data directly from your accounting systems. Imagine proactively addressing cash shortfalls by intelligently optimizing working capital and leveraging alternative payment instruments with the help of AI-powered automation. Imagine AI agents that can reason, decide, and execute tasks on your behalf 24x7 — only involving your team when human intervention is needed, to help improve collections efficiency from slow-paying customers.  \n  \n You no longer have to imagine it.",
      "score": 0.629889,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/article/4169618/the-next-enterprise-architecture-asset-ontologies-for-ai.html",
      "title": "The next enterprise architecture asset: Ontologies for AI  | CIO",
      "content": "When Large Language Models and AI agents are deployed inside enterprises, they operate without continuous human oversight.  They operate autonomously, synthesizing answers, taking actions and reasoning across domains at machine speed.  At that point, ambiguity stops being tolerable.  If “customer” means different things in sales, finance and support, the AI will confidently act on the wrong interpretation or synthesize the wrong answer, which introduces risk, not efficiency. [...] This is why many enterprise GenAI initiatives stall after impressive demos.  The models are capable, but the environment is semantically unstable.  Enterprises are discovering, often painfully, that they have been outsourcing semantic alignment to humans for decades.  When AI agents act on behalf of those humans, the missing ontology becomes visible.\n\nIn the past, ontologies weren’t needed because systems didn’t need to understand; they needed to process.  AI, especially agentic AI, now requires systems that understand the business the way experienced employees do(what exists, how things relate and which rules govern behavior).  Therefore, ontologies are now required to represent a shared understanding of the business for people as well as machines. [...] To achieve this, we need an ontology in place that allows AI agents to reason without human guidance for every interaction.  The objective is agent reasoning, supported by system validation, rules and governance, with the ability to deliberately insert human‑in‑the‑loop activities where business processes require it.",
      "score": 0.5596998,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/profile/anirban-ghoshal/page/7",
      "title": "Anirban Ghoshal | Page 7 - CIO",
      "content": "With its acquisition of Dotmatics, a developer of software for managing data in life sciences research, Siemens hopes to help cut drug development costs.\n\n### Oracle’s AI Agent Studio is free for Fusion Cloud customers\n\nThe offering is expected to automate business processes, reducing manual effort and improving efficiency without heavy development overhead.\n\n### Salesforce takes on hyperscalers with Agentforce 2dx updates\n\nThe latest updates will help the CRM software provider leapfrog over its rivals in the agentic AI market, analysts say.\n\n### Salesforce’s AgentExchange targets AI agent adoption, monetization\n\nThe marketplace will initially feature action, topics, and templates from 200 partners for sales, service, finance, HR, productivity, and operations across various industries. [...] ## Topics\n\n# Anirban Ghoshal\n\n## Articles by Anirban Ghoshal\n\n### Salesforce supercharges Agentforce with embedded AI, multimodal support, and industry-specific agents\n\nFrom auto-updating CRM fields to new industry agents and employee tools, Salesforce’s Summer ’25 update positions Agentforce as a no-code AI engine woven into every enterprise workflow.\n\n### Workday’s new dev tools help enterprises connect with external agents\n\nAmong the additions are some improvements to Workday’s Developer Copilot, which can now generate application code snippets, data queries, and functional orchestrations.\n\n### SAP teams up with Alibaba to host Cloud ERP workloads in China\n\nThe partnership will also see Alibaba adopt SAP offerings to run its own business. [...] ### Why Microsoft is unifying data and AI within Fabric\n\nFabric is being transformed as a decision infrastructure layer to support faster decision making and easier development of agentic applications, analysts say.\n\n### New agentic AI tools bring new threat: agent sprawl\n\nVendors are lining up to offer enterprises tools to create AI agents for a plethora of different tasks — but it’s time to think about managing them.\n\n### Google’s AI innovations at Cloud Next 2025: What CIOs need to know\n\nThe announcements made at Google Cloud Next 2025 were designed to help CIOs adopt and scale agents while reducing integration complexity and costs.\n\n### Siemens grows its digital twin strategy into life sciences market",
      "score": 0.52042377,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://news.cognizant.com/2026-06-03-Cognizant-Accelerates-Enterprise-AI-Adoption-with-Snowflakes-Cortex-Powered-Intelligent-Agents",
      "title": "Cognizant Accelerates Enterprise AI Adoption with Snowflake's Cortex-Powered Intelligent Agents - Jun 3, 2026",
      "content": "Building on the success of engagements such as A+E Global Media, Cognizant plans to continue to deepen its investment in industry-specific CoCo skills, pre-built agent templates and cross-platform orchestration capabilities – supporting enterprises to scale their agentic AI programs on a proven, governed foundation.\n\nFor the latest news and announcements, including coverage from Snowflake Summit 2026, visit LinkedIn and X. [...] A+E Global Media™ provides a clear example of how enterprises are translating AI investment into measurable business outcomes. Cognizant successfully deployed an AI-powered conversational analytics agent built on Snowflake CoCo to transform Creative Airing Logs across the linear landscape. The solution streamlined a previously manual, multi-step analytics workflow by automating dataset preparation and supporting business users in asking natural language questions and easy investigation of trafficking discrepancies reported by agencies, as well as ad-hoc reporting on problematic product categories. [...] By replacing time-intensive reporting processes, the Cortex agent significantly improved operational efficiency across Commercial Operations and Legal & Business Affairs teams. Agent implementation has helped A+E Global Media reclaim approximately 200 hours of manual effort for business users while reducing labor costs and accelerating decision-making. Snowflake CoCo accelerated end-to-end workflow – from authoring complex SQL, to validating data quality and performing exploratory analysis – serving as an AI co-pilot that reduced development time, improved accuracy and streamlined key phases of delivery.",
      "score": 0.51076555,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work",
      "title": "Asana launches AI-powered products to help organizations manage ...",
      "content": "Rounding out the management and coordination side of the equation, Asana rolled out Dash. This AI agent acts as a layer-shared system for employees that understands goals, priorities and what work needs attention across teams and tools. With this kind of broad view, it can help work move along in a shared system where teams operate by letting humans manage what they do best and tracking what falls through the cracks.\n\nThis means picking up meetings, Slack threads, emails and other unstructured messaging through the Work Graph automatically. Decisions and follow-ups become normalized, instead of something that disappears into the back of inboxes. That way, digital teams act together, instead of falling behind. [...] The company also said it’s expanding its portfolio with industry-specific AI teammates, which are prebuilt with domain knowledge. These include agents that are fine-tuned to support work in high-value industries such as manufacturing and retail.\n\nFor teams who work in specific coordinated environments, such as service management, product development and client management, Asana is packaging this new agentic operating system into specialized systems.\n\nAsana Service Management provides a self-learning knowledge base for information technology, human resources, facilities and other service teams by unifying ticketing and project execution. Asana can move from ticket to project without losing context, allowing for rapid request resolution. [...] Siliconangle\n\nSiliconangle\n\nUPDATED 04:30 EDT / JUNE 04 2026\n\n AI\n\n### Asana launches AI-powered products to help organizations manage human and agent work\n\nby Kyt Dotson\n\nAsana Inc. announced today during the company’s Work Innovation Summit in London the launch of a new product suite that helps organizations manage work by humans and artificial intelligence agents using the same plan.\n\nCalling the suite an operating system for human-agent teams, Asana unveiled Agentic Work Management. This new set of tools includes three key parts: a chief of AI staff for every Asana user, three new applications for teams and a new generation of AI teammates.",
      "score": 0.459295,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-qwdqllmp4izkc?sr=0-10&ref_=beagle&applicationId=AWSMPContessa",
      "title": "AWS Marketplace: MAIAH Governance",
      "content": "Listing Thumbnail\n\n# MAIAH Governance\n\n## Overview\n\nPlay video\n\n## Product video\n\nIn an Agent-centric corporate landscape, the ultimate priority is no longer just deployment, but strategic governance. MAIAH functions as the Excellence Center for enterprise AI, providing a robust Control Tower to oversee autonomous operations. By aligning with OWASP Top 10 for LLM protocols and real-world operational demands, MAIAH ensures that every AI Agent remains secure, cost-efficient, and fully auditable, transforming raw AI potential into a managed corporate asset.\n\n### Highlights\n\n### Details\n\n### Introducing multi-product solutions\n\nYou can now purchase comprehensive solutions tailored to use cases and industries.\n\nMulti-product solutions\n\n## Features and programs [...] ### Content disclaimer\n\n## Usage information\n\n### Delivery details\n\n##### Software as a Service (SaaS)\n\nSaaS delivers cloud-based software applications directly to customers over the internet. You can access these applications through a subscription model. You will pay recurring monthly usage fees through your AWS bill, while AWS handles deployment and infrastructure management, ensuring scalability, reliability, and seamless integration with other AWS services.\n\n## Resources\n\n### Vendor resources\n\n## Support\n\n### Vendor support\n\nSupport Contact:\nai.agent@ecloudvalley.com\n\nOur support team is committed to addressing all requests within two business days. Assistance is available for a range of account-related issues, including billing inquiries and payment-related matters. [...] Multi-product solutions\n\n## Features and programs\n\n### Financing for AWS Marketplace purchases\n\nFinancing for AWS Marketplace purchases\n\n## Pricing\n\n### MAIAH Governance\n\n### 12-month contract (4)\n\n| Dimension | Description | Cost/12 months |\n --- \n| MAIAH Governance Freemium | Freemium | $0.00 |\n| MAIAH Governance Starter | Starter | $6,000.00 |\n| MAIAH Governance Professional | Professional | $12,000.00 |\n| MAIAH Governance Premium | Premium | $24,000.00 |\n\n### Vendor refund policy\n\nWe do not currently support refunds, but you can cancel at any time.\n\n### Custom pricing options\n\n## How can we make this page better?\n\nTell us how we can improve this page, or report an issue with this product.\n\n## Legal\n\n### Vendor terms and conditions\n\n### Content disclaimer\n\n## Usage information",
      "score": 0.3467409,

    {
      "url": "https://www.cio.com/article/4180868/your-ai-cloud-strategy-isnt-about-cost-its-about-gravity.html",
      "title": "Your AI cloud strategy isn't about cost. It's about gravity | CIO",
      "content": "### From our editors straight to your inbox\n\nPete Johnson\n\nPete Johnson is field CTO, AI for MongoDB, where he shapes AI strategy and developer-focused solutions. He is a 30+ year computer industry veteran with strong communication, organizational and management skills. Pete has held a variety of roles including chief architect at Hewlett-Packard, principal architect at Cisco and AI field CTO at CDW.\n\n## More from this author\n\n### The $570K canary: What AI coding agents reveal about enterprise AI’s real gaps\n\n### The CIO’s new job description: Chief transformation officer\n\n### AGI skepticism: Tellers vs. toll booth workers\n\n### Your dev team isn’t a cost center — it’s about to become a multiplier\n\n### The 37-point trust gap: It’s not the AI, it’s your organization\n\n## Show me more [...] Agents don’t just consume data. They live in it.\n\nMemory, context, retrieval, embeddings — the data isn’t an input to the workload. It largely is the workload. An agent reasoning about a customer’s situation is pulling in conversation history, organizational policies, product documentation and structured records on every turn. An agent writing code is pulling in the repository, the architectural decision records, the test suite and the relevant runtime telemetry. An agent doing financial analysis is pulling in market data, internal forecasts, regulatory filings and historical context — and then producing intermediate results that feed back into the next reasoning step.\n\nThe data isn’t a thing the workload references occasionally. It’s the substrate the workload is computing on. [...] The architectural pattern that emerged was elegant in its simplicity: applications in one region, data in another, users somewhere else entirely and the network in between papered over the seams. Latency budgets were measured in user-perceptible terms — a 200ms page load was acceptable, a 500ms one was a problem. Cross-region calls were a tax you paid for resilience or for putting compute close to the user.\n\nThat entire model assumed the application was the thing doing the work, and the data was the thing being acted upon.\n\nAI agents inverted that assumption.\n\n## AI inverted the ratio\n\nAgents don’t just consume data. They live in it.",
      "score": 0.31706718,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-skmyfqopi7oyu",
      "title": "AWS Marketplace: SPACE LENS: AI-Powered Video Intelligence & Auto-Analysis Solution",
      "content": "Co-innovated by MegazoneCloud and the AWS GenAIIC Partner Agent Factory (PAF) Link initiative, SPACE LENS is built on deep technical insights and a reusable asset (Recorded Voice Insight Extraction Webapp) from GenAIIC, including foundational components of Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Bedrock AgentCore (Runtime, Memory, and Gateway), and the Strands Agents SDK (Agents, Steering, and Evaluation). Through the PAF initiative, the GenAIIC team provided immersive expert guidance to ensure successful business validation, the development of enterprise-ready AI agents, and a seamless path to production. [...] Unlike conventional video analysis tools that invoke a single AI model or a fixed engine, SPACE LENS uses agentic orchestration to dynamically select the optimal combination of tools for each query. It delivers adaptive responses that span visual, audio, and combined context, enabling outcome-oriented experiences that continuously learn and improve.\n\nKEY DIFFERENTIATORS\n\nIntelligent Orchestration: \"Get the best analysis results without being an AI expert.\"\n\nMulti-Perspective Analysis: \"Understand the essence of video by integrating sight, sound, and context.\"\n\nData Assetization: \"Turn idle video files into immediately actionable knowledge assets.\"\n\nIndependent Scalability: \"Add the features you need instantly, without changing your existing environment.\"\n\nKEY BENEFITS\n\n### Highlights [...] ### Delivery details\n\n##### Software as a Service (SaaS)\n\nSaaS delivers cloud-based software applications directly to customers over the internet. You can access these applications through a subscription model. You will pay recurring monthly usage fees through your AWS bill, while AWS handles deployment and infrastructure management, ensuring scalability, reliability, and seamless integration with other AWS services.\n\n## Support\n\n### Vendor support\n\nSUPPORT CHANNELS\n\nSERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT\n\n### AWS infrastructure support",
      "score": 0.30080768,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/azure-ai-foundry-blog/azure-speech-at-build-2026-powering-voice-agents-with-real-time-and-life-like-ex/4524638",
      "title": "Azure Speech at Build 2026: Powering Voice Agents with Real-Time and Life-like Experiences | Microsoft Community Hub",
      "content": "Build Real-Time Voice Agent with Voice Live and Foundry Agent Service \n\nAs developers move beyond traditional chatbots, they are building a new class of real-time agents that can listen, reason, take actions, and respond naturally in live conversations. From customer support agents and virtual assistants to healthcare intake, retail concierge, field operations, in-car assistants and multilingual employee support, voice agents are becoming a key interface for how people interact with AI. \n\nAt Build 2026, we’re announcing major updates that support building enterprise-ready, voice agents at scale. [...] Hosted Agents with Voice Live is available in Public Preview. Some customers need full control over their agent’s runtime, orchestration framework, and execution model. For those scenarios, Microsoft AI Foundry supports Hosted Agents with Voice Live (public preview), so developers can build with the frameworks they prefer and deploy on managed infrastructure. Whether using Microsoft Agent Framework, LangChain, or a custom orchestration stack, they can host those agents on Foundry Agent Service and connect them directly to Voice Live. Both Response API and Invocations Protocol are supported. [...] Voice Live for Foundry Prompt Agents is now generally available. This is a strong fit for developers that want enterprise-ready capabilities with minimal operational overhead. Voice Live brings the essential pieces of voice interaction into a single API, from Speech to Text and Text to Speech to turn detection, interruption handling, avatars, and other conversational capabilities. Customers can combine real-time speech-to-speech interaction with managed agent orchestration, knowledge, memory, enterprise governance, observability, and scalable deployment - all within a single developer workflow.",
      "score": 0.2823327,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://kpmg.com/xx/en/what-we-do/services/ai/trusted-ai-framework.html",
      "title": "KPMG Trusted AI framework",
      "content": "Human-centric\n\nWe prioritize human impact as we deploy AI and recognize the needs of our clients and our people. We are embracing this technology to empower and augment human capabilities — to unleash creativity and improve productivity in a way that allows people to reimagine how they spend their days.\n\nTrustworthy\n\nWe will adhere to our principles and the ethical pillars that guide how and why we use AI across its lifecycle. We will strive to ensure our data acquisition, governance and usage practices upholds ethical standards and complies with applicable privacy and data protection regulations, as well as any confidentiality requirements. [...] #### \n\n###### \n\nKPMG Logo\n\n#### \n\n###### \n\n#### \n\n###### \n\nYour search term was too short.\n\nSearch terms must include 3 or more characters.\n\n# KPMG Trusted AI framework\n\nAI is transforming the world in which we live - impacting many aspects of everyday life, business and society.\n\n### Accelerate the value of generative AI with confidence\n\nArtificial intelligence (AI) is transforming the world in which we live—impacting many aspects of everyday life, business and society. KPMG Member Firms help organizations utilize the transformative power of AI, including its ethical and responsible use.\n\nAs KPMG harnesses the power of AI and accelerates adoption, we recognize that advanced technologies can introduce complexity and AI risks that should be addressed clearly and responsibly. [...] Throughout the global organization, we are committed to upholding ethical standards for AI solutions that align with our values and professional standards, and that foster the trust of KPMG clients, people, communities and regulators. We are steadfast in our commitment to act in a way that serves the public interest, honors public trust and demonstrates our commitment to professional excellence.\n\nKPMG Trusted AI is our strategic approach and framework to designing, building, deploying and using AI strategies and solutions in a responsible and ethical manner so we can accelerate value with confidence.\n\nKeep reading to discover our principles, ethical pillars and governance.\n\n## The KPMG Trusted AI principles\n\nWhat we aim to do",

      "title": "AWS Marketplace: AI & Data Readiness Assessment",
      "content": "Listing Thumbnail\n\n# AI & Data Readiness Assessment\n\n## Overview\n\nThe AI and Data Readiness Assessment from LSD Open helps organisations evaluate their data platforms, governance frameworks, and security posture to prepare for enterprise scale adoption of artificial intelligence and generative AI on Amazon Web Services.\n\nMany organisations are accelerating generative AI initiatives but lack the governed data, architecture, and operational foundations required to move from experimentation into production. This engagement identifies gaps between current capabilities and the requirements for safely operationalising AI systems at scale. [...] Through structured workshops and technical discovery, LSD Open assesses the full data landscape, including data pipelines, storage platforms, governance policies, and security controls. The assessment evaluates data maturity, accessibility, and data quality to determine readiness for AI driven use cases.\n\nThe engagement also evaluates platform readiness for modern AI workloads, including architectures required for retrieval augmented generation, semantic search, intelligent agents, and AI powered automation.\n\nThis assessment supports adoption of AWS services such as Amazon S3, Amazon Redshift, AWS Glue, Amazon Kinesis, Amazon OpenSearch, AWS Lake Formation, and Amazon Bedrock by establishing the required data and governance foundations. [...] Ideal for organisations in early to mid AI maturity stages looking to operationalise generative AI use cases on AWS.\n\n### Highlights\n\n### Details\n\n### Introducing multi-product solutions\n\nYou can now purchase comprehensive solutions tailored to use cases and industries.\n\nMulti-product solutions\n\n## Pricing\n\n### Custom pricing options\n\n## How can we make this page better?\n\nTell us how we can improve this page, or report an issue with this product.\n\n## Legal\n\n### Content disclaimer\n\n## Support\n\n### Vendor support\n\nLSD Open provides expert guidance throughout the AI and Data Readiness Assessment engagement.\n\nSupport includes:",

      "content": "Highlights ○ Intelligent table recognition: Convert tables embedded within your documents into clean and editable formats, eliminating manual data entry and streamlining analysis. ○ Customization at your fingertips: Tailor the extracted data to your specific needs, defining custom fields and applying calculations for enhanced insights. ○ Boost data processing efficiency: Automate time-consuming manual tasks, freeing up your team to focus on higher-value activities. ○ Gain a deeper understanding of your documents: Easily analyze trends, identify patterns, and make data-driven decisions with organized and accessible information. ○ Seamless integration: Our solution integrates seamlessly with existing workflows, allowing you to effortlessly incorporate document processing into your existing [...] Powered by state-of-the-art machine learning algorithms and seamlessly integrated with Amazon Textract and Amazon Comprehend, our product is set to redefine how organizations handle document-related tasks. ○ Amazon Textract serves as the cornerstone of our document processing solution, offering unparalleled capabilities in automated text extraction. This system effortlessly extracts valuable information from a wide array of documents, including invoices, contracts, forms, and more. ○ Amazon Comprehend allows our system to go beyond simple text extraction by analyzing and interpreting the extracted information. It can discern key entities, sentiments, and relationships within the documents, providing invaluable context for decision-making processes. [...] Listing Thumbnail\n\n# AI based Intelligent Document Processing\n\n## Overview\n\nUnleash the hidden potential of your documents with our product, a comprehensive and intelligent solution for managing your PDFs.       "content": "Not at all. Workday GO actually reduces complexity with streamlined processes, automation, global payroll, and integration. Workday AI agents including BP Optimise Agent, Payroll Agent, Self-Service Agent, and Deployment Agent are also available at go-live to augment your team’s capacity.\n\nWorkday GO unifies your data so you get an accurate and complete picture of your business in real time across HR and Payroll—rather than separate, siloed views.\n\nWorkday GO delivers its all-in-one solution for HR, global payroll, and finance to medium enterprise businesses through packages designed to fit their business and budget.       "title": "Artificial Intelligence (AI) Solutions for Business | Workday BE",
      "content": "Agentic HR\n\nDrive operational excellence by empowering your people, elevating managers and automating processes with AI built for HR.\n\nAgentic Finance\n\nGo from hindsight to insight with AI that surfaces exceptions, anticipates change and predicts the next best action.\n\nAgentic IT\n\nRealise ROI fast and deliver transformational outcomes by partnering with an AI platform driven by trust, agility and quality data you can always rely on.\n\nAgentic Legal\n\nTake control of your contract data with AI that gives you total visibility, so you can focus on shaping strategy.\n\n## Your people. Our agents. Exponential value.\n\nWhen humans and AI agents collaborate, you get rapid, measurable business results.\n\n## Your people. Our agents. Exponential value. [...] Experience a new way to work with Sana, superintelligence that automates work across HR, Finance, IT and beyond through the power of AI.\n\n## Faster insights. Better decisions. Automated actions.\n\nSana is where you build, orchestrate and manage agents across HR, Finance, IT and beyond. Built on your trusted Workday data and integrated with all your business apps, Sana unlocks a whole new way of working with AI.\n\n## Faster insights. Better decisions. Automated actions.\n\nSana is where you build, orchestrate and manage agents across HR, Finance, IT and beyond.       "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/ai/ai-in-finance.html",
      "title": "What is AI in Finance? | Workday IN",
      "content": "## Products\n\n### AI\n\nDiscover superintelligence for the new world of work.\n\n#### Artificial Intelligence\n\n#### Sana from Workday\n\n#### Agent System of Record\n\n#### Agents\n\n#### Responsible AI\n\n#### AI Masterclass\n\n### HR\n\nEmpower your people and adapt your workforce to the future of work.\n\n#### HR Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Human Capital Management\n\n#### Human Resource Management\n\n#### Workday Paradox\n\n#### Talent Management\n\n#### Workforce Management\n\n#### Employee Experience\n\n#### HR Service Delivery\n\n#### Employee Voice\n\n#### Workforce Planning\n\n#### Analytics & Reporting\n\n#### Local & Global Payroll\n\n### Finance\n\nDrive innovation, manage costs, and make smarter finance decisions, faster.\n\n#### Finance Solutions\n\n#### PRODUCTS\n\n#### Financial Management\n\n#### Spend Management [...] Applying AI to predictable finance processes and tasks that are traditionally labor intensive is essential for modernizing the financial services industry. For example, finance teams have traditionally spent an inordinate amount of time gathering information and reconciling throughout the month and at period end. AI focuses on oversight such as addressing anomalies, managing exceptions, and making recommendations so teams can focus their time on strategy.\n\n## What is AI in finance?\n\nThe world of work has changed, transforming every aspect of the business. HR has shifted to a skills-based economy. Finance has embraced touchless transaction processing. And IT must manage tools and systems for a distributed workforce. This transformation is inseparable from AI. [...] —ERP Business Analyst, IMC Financial Markets\n\n## What is AI in finance?\n\nThe world of work has changed, transforming every aspect of the business. HR has shifted to a skills-based economy. Finance has embraced touchless transaction processing. And IT must manage tools and systems for a distributed workforce. This transformation is inseparable from AI.\n\nAI in finance is the ability for machines to perform tasks that augment how businesses analyze, manage, and invest their capital. By automating repetitive manual tasks, detecting anomalies, and providing real-time recommendations, AI represents a major source of business value.",
      "score": 0.6133529,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://www.workday.com/en-in/topics/hr/hris.html",

Employee interface.\n\nWhen we talk about the future of HR, we often talk about manager and employee self-service. The benefit of an HRIS is that it streamlines HR requests into one platform. Employees can use one solution to view job role information, perform basic time and expense tracking, and update personal information. That makes an HRIS an essential part of modern HR service delivery.\n\nWhen considering which HR tool is best for your company, it’s worth evaluating how user-friendly the interface is. Employees increasingly expect workplace technology to have the same level of sophistication as their personal smart devices. Providing a user-friendly interface for your HR information solution can be a quick way to increase employee engagement.\n\n \n\n### 6. Benefits management. [...] ### 5. Employee interface.\n\nWhen we talk about the future of HR, we often talk about manager and employee self-service. The benefit of an HRIS is that it streamlines HR requests into one platform. Employees can use one solution to view job role information, perform basic time and expense tracking, and update personal information. That makes an HRIS an essential part of modern HR service delivery.\n\nWhen considering which HR tool is best for your company, it’s worth evaluating how user-friendly the interface is. Employees increasingly expect workplace technology to have the same level of sophistication as their personal smart devices.     {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/blog/field-service-facilities",
      "title": "A New Era of Field Service for Facilities Management",
      "content": "Leading organizations are also discovering a revenue opportunity: unified data transforms routine maintenance visits into upsell moments. Technicians can identify and recommend equipment upgrades, energy-efficiency retrofits, or lifecycle replacements while on-site. Facilities become growth drivers, not just cost centers.\n\n## Field Service Supercharged: Your Agentic AI Guide\n\nLearn how AI agents can help field service teams scale, work, and deliver service with confidence.\n\n#### 3. Move informal Knowledge into the Flow of Work [...] #### 4. Orchestrate a Hybrid Human-Agent-Robot Workforce\n\nThe future of facilities management orchestrates three types of workers: humans (handling complex issues, building relationships, and executing repairs), AI agents (automating triage, routing requests, analyzing risk), and autonomous robots (executing routine patrols and environmental sensing). When a robot detects an anomaly—pressure drop, temperature spike, moisture—it automatically triggers a work order routed to the right technician. Humans handle the exception; machines handle the routine. [...] ### Build Your 2025 Dreamforce Agenda\n\n### Operate with Confidence: Transform Your Field Service with Agentic and Conversational Analytics\n\n![Scheduling mishaps, administrative burden, and burnout are top mobile worker concerns. [Image: Adobe Stock]](\n\n### What 350 Mobile Workers Taught Us About Fixing Productivity (And Your Costs)\n\n### How Three Trailblazers Pivoted Their Careers for the Agentic AI Era\n\nServiceblazer Scott Luikart in his branded Trailblazer hoodie.\n\n## Breaking Free From Spreadsheets: How Scheduling Optimization Transformed a Field Service Operation\n\n## Why Asset Service Management Matters in Field Service\n\n## Break the Break-Fix Cycle: Get Proactive with Preventive Maintenance",
      "score": 0.47884622,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/nl/en/services/advisory/technology-and-ai/data-and-ai/ai-workforce.html",
      "title": "AI Workforce",
      "content": "# AI Workforce\n\nArtificial Intelligence plays a crucial role in modern organizations. It is a high-priority topic and is being applied in various ways. AI agents are being developed to support diverse functions and tasks, enabling employees to work more efficiently and streamline processes. The success of AI development and implementation heavily depends on the end users and their level of adoption. Unlike other technologies, AI requires a broader behavioral change, which demands a unique approach. How can we get the best out of our staff? Which tools and solutions are most effective for our employees? And how do we ensure that the provided opportunities are fully utilized?\n\n## KPMG AI Workforce [...] ## KPMG AI Workforce\n\nUnlocking the value of AI requires a new approach, defined by identifying and improving tasks that were previously difficult to automate. Without a targeted approach and clear success criteria, it will be challenging for organizations to effectively transform and improve their workforce. To accelerate value, we recommend a human-centered approach consisting of three essential steps:\n\n### 1. Identify Opportnunities for Your Workforce\n\n### 2. Empower your Employees\n\n### 3. Redesign Your HR Approach and Operations\n\nWorkforce image showing 3 phases, Reshape, identify and augment\n\n## AI Workforce core areas\n\nOur KPMG AI Workforce approach consists of several core areas that lead to optimal adoption and value creation with AI within your organization: [...] Identification of Opportunities\n\nContinuous identification of AI opportunities for employees, temporary workers, outsourcing models, shared services, and digital labor. Strategy, business case development, prioritization, and transformation roadmap.\n\nRedesign of Functions and Experience\n\nHuman-centered redesign of functions, roles, and departments to maximize the impact of AI. Identification of data, knowledge content, process impact, performance metrics, information security, and compliance considerations.\n\nEngagement and Adoption\n\nOur unique, human-centered approach accelerates the adoption of AI at both the business and functional levels and equips your employees with the necessary digital mindset, knowledge, and skills to leverage AI.\n\nLearning & development",
      "score": 0.4526091,
      "raw_content": null
    },

Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.\n\n### Equal Opportunity Statement.",

      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/ca/retail/retail-ai-agents",
      "title": "Retail AI Agents: Your Complete Guide | Salesforce CA",
      "content": "(opens in a new window)\n\nThe marketing, sales, service, and commerce tools you need to succeed — all in a single CRM suite.\n\n(opens in a new window)\n\nBuild enterprise-quality apps fast and get hands-on with Agentforce and Data Cloud.\n\n## New to Salesforce\n\nSalesforce is the #1 AI CRM, with AI agents, data, and CRM apps on a single, unified platform.\n\nLearn all about what CRM is, what it does, and how it can improve customer relationships.\n\nSalesforce’s category-leading apps for sales, service, marketing, commerce, IT, and more.\n\nLearn more about AI agents and how they can help your business.\n\nIt’s always-on digital labor augmenting every employee, department, and business process to improve customer experiences.\n\n## Blogs [...] AI agents, also known as intelligent agents, are software programmes that can observe their environment, make decisions, and take actions to achieve a specific goal. These agents are designed to act autonomously and adapt to changing situations. Examples of AI agents include chatbots and virtual personal assistants.\n\nOne key difference between AI agents and generative AI is their purpose. AI agents are created with a specific goal in mind, such as completing a task or providing assistance, while generative AI is focussed on exploration and creation. Another difference is their level of autonomy. AI agents are typically designed to act on their own, while generative AI systems require human input to function.\n\n## What is a retail AI agent? [...] ## What is a retail AI agent?\n\nRetail AI agents, powered by advanced machine learning algorithms and natural language processing (NLP), are software applications designed to assist retailers in automating various tasks and enhancing customer interactions. These agents have the ability to analyse large amounts of data, make predictions, and learn from customer behaviour to provide personalised recommendations and improve overall business efficiency.\n\nWith AI agents in retail, you can gain valuable insights into customer preferences and shopping habits, leading to more targeted marketing strategies and increased sales. Additionally, these agents can help retailers optimise inventory management and supply chain processes, reducing costs and improving the overall customer experience.",
      "score": 0.3499997,
      "raw_content": null
    },

You will work on cutting-edge agent technologies using Agentforce, Agent Script, and integrate with Salesforce CRM and Data Cloud.       "content": "Translate business requirements into AI-native technical architectures and communicate trade-offs via demos and deep-dives.  \n Document designs, experiments, and evaluation results for reproducibility and knowledge sharing.  \nProfessional & Technical Skills  \nMust-Have (AI Native Core)  \n Hands-on experience with LLMs, agent frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, Semantic Kernel, LangGraph, AutoGen), and vector DBs.  \n Strong Python skills for building agents, RAG services, data pipelines, and evaluation harnesses.  \n Deep understanding of transformer models, embeddings, prompt engineering, and model fine-tuning workflows.  \n Experience deploying AI systems to production with monitoring, error handling, retries, and fallback strategies.  \nGood to Have [...] Role Summary  \nWe are seeking a hands-on AI Native Engineer who can design, build, and deploy end-to-end agentic and LLM-powered systems. This includes constructing RAG pipelines, working with vector databases, building autonomous or semi-autonomous agents, integrating tools and skills, and developing evaluation frameworks for high-quality AI behaviour. You will prototype rapidly, experiment with models, and evolve solutions from PoC to production while working closely with cross-functional teams.  \n AI Powered Tech Talent   \n  \nKey Responsibilities  \nAgentic & LLM System Development  \n Build agentic AI systems, including agent orchestration, planning loops, tool calling, and memory modules. [...] Implement LLM toolchains, custom prompts, templates, evaluators, and multi-step reasoning workflows.  \n Develop autonomous/semi-autonomous agents for retrieval, summarization, decision support, or workflow automation.  \nRAG Pipelines & Vector Intelligence  \n Design and implement RAG pipelines end-to-end: ingestion, chunking, embeddings, indexing, vector search, hybrid retrieval, and grounding.  \n Integrate vector databases such as pgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate, or Milvus.  \n Optimize retrieval quality, latency, and factual accuracy using rerankers, retrieval evaluators, and freshness pipelines.  \nModel Integration & AI Ops  \n Integrate enterprise-grade AI APIs, foundation models, and transformer models into scalable systems.",

      "title": "The Unicorn Dilemma: Hire for Talent, Not Perfection",
      "content": "This is what I brought back from it, and this is what my peers are doing, and this is what I heard over and over and over again. We need to have a better interview guide, and we need to respect the intake meeting just a touch more, and I need to give that power with to the manager.\"\n\nAnd I think it depends on your organization, but you need to line it out, and I'd be remiss if I didn't say why don't you use AI?     {
      "url": "https://www.okta.com/company/careers/engineering/senior-software-engineer-ai-framework-services-7872569",
      "title": "Senior Software Engineer, AI Framework & Services | Okta",
      "content": "Design and implement backend systems, APIs, and services that bring identity and authorization capabilities to AI agent workflows.\n Own features end-to-end, from technical design through production, with attention to reliability, security, and developer experience.\n Build integrations that allow agents to securely acquire, use, and scope access credentials in real-world environments.\n Contribute to system design decisions, make thoughtful technical tradeoffs, and communicate them clearly to peers and partners.\n Help define patterns and abstractions for problems the industry hasn't fully solved yet: agent identity, delegated access, trust boundaries across agentic systems. [...] ## The Team: AI Frameworks & Services (AIFS)\n\nThe way software acts in the world is changing. AI agents don't just respond to users. They take actions, call APIs, hold credentials, and operate across systems on behalf of people and organizations. That shift raises a fundamental question: how do you extend identity to an agent?\n\nThe AI Framework & Services team sits within Auth0's AI Products organization and is focused on exactly that problem. We're building the product capabilities that let developers bring identity and authorization into agentic systems, treating agents as real principals with real access controls and real accountability. [...] ## Why You'll Love It Here\n\nThis is a team working at the edge of what identity means in a world of AI agents. You'll have real ownership of hard, unsolved problems, backed by Okta's platform, Auth0's developer relationships, and a team that values technical depth and customer impact in equal measure.\n\nYou'll help shape the product, not just implement a backlog. You'll work with people who care about getting the details right. And you'll be building something that matters to the developers and organizations trying to do AI responsibly.\n\n#LI-Hybrid  \nP24773\\_3426177",
      "score": 0.9219218,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "url": "https://careers.cognizant.com/latam-es/empleos/00068835141/program-manager",
      "title": "Program Manager, Bangalore, Karnataka, India | Cognizant Careers",
      "content": "### These will help you stand out\n\n Exposure to enterprise GenAI solutions such as Salesforce Einstein GPT, ServiceNow Now Assist, SAP Joule, Workday AI, or Oracle AI Services\n Experience with hybrid and multi-cloud AI deployment models\n Knowledge of AI observability, agent lifecycle management, and interoperability standards\n Experience mentoring architects and leading high-performing AI engineering teams\n Active participation in AI communities, industry forums, or thought leadership initiatives\n\n### Why consider this role?\n\nWe're excited to meet professionals who are passionate about shaping the future of AI and driving real-world impact through innovation. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply and bring your unique perspective to the role.\n\n--- [...] ### What you need to have to be considered\n\n 15+ years of IT experience, including 5+ years in AI/ML and hands-on experience in Generative AI / Agentic AI\n Strong expertise in designing and implementing Generative AI and multi-agent systems at enterprise scale\n Proven experience in enterprise architecture, including frameworks such as TOGAF or Zachman\n Hands-on experience with cloud AI platforms such as AWS (SageMaker, Bedrock), Azure AI (Studio/Foundry), or Google Vertex AI\n Experience defining AI governance models, compliance frameworks, and enterprise AI strategies\n Ability to design scalable architectures integrating AI platforms across enterprise ecosystems\n Strong stakeholder management skills with experience working with senior leadership\n\n### These will help you stand out [...] ### In this role, you will:\n\n Define and own enterprise-wide architecture for Generative AI and Agentic AI solutions aligned with business goals\n Design end-to-end AI systems, from model selection and context engineering to application integration and deployment\n Lead development of reusable frameworks, accelerators, and multi-agent architectures for enterprise use cases\n Drive prototyping, PoCs, and MVP initiatives to validate AI solutions across industries\n Establish governance, standards, and best practices for AI lifecycle, ethics, security, and performance\n\n### Work model",

      "url": "https://careers.servicenow.com/jobs/744000130098534/software-engineer-developer-experience-moveworks",
      "title": "Software Engineer, Developer Experience — Moveworks, Mountain View | ServiceNow Careers",
      "content": "In December 2025, Moveworks was acquired by ServiceNow, marking a pivotal milestone in our journey to create a single front door to work for all business systems. By combining ServiceNow’s leading workflow automation with Moveworks’ Reasoning Engine and natural language capabilities, we deliver the AI platform for every person and every workflow. Built to go beyond basic summaries to deliver meaningful business impact. Together, our AI acts across enterprise systems to turn conversations into completed work.\n\nBy joining our team, you’ll be at the forefront of the AI transformation, backed by the global scale of ServiceNow and the agility of a high-growth company. We are looking for world-class talent to help us extend agentic AI to every employee across every corner of the business. [...] # Software Engineer, Developer Experience — Moveworks\n\n### Company Description\n\nMoveworks\n\nMoveworks is the Agentic AI Assistant platform that empowers the entire workforce.\n\nOur platform enables employees to converse with all of their business systems through natural language to quickly find answers and automate tasks. Powered by the world's most advanced LLMs, our proprietary models, and a sophisticated Agentic AI platform, we're transforming how work gets done by allowing AI to take initiative, streamline complex workflows, and continuously learn and adapt. [...] ### Job Description\n\nThe Developer Experience team sits within Moveworks' Platform org, but operates with a vertical mandate: we build products and platforms that serve developers — specifically the developers at Moveworks customer environments who are building on top of the Moveworks Agentic AI platform.\n\nOur north star is supply. Every developer we enable, every integration they ship faster, every higher-quality skill or workflow they publish — that compounds into more value for the employees who rely on the assistant every day. More builders → more things built → better assistant → more demand. We own that flywheel.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.reuters.com/world/china/microsoft-expected-showcase-new-pc-cloud-ai-tools-developer-conference-2026-06-02",
      "title": "Microsoft teases new era of AI-driven devices at annual ...",
      "content": "At its ‌annual software developer conference, Microsoft Build in San Francisco, executives showcased a broader shift in company strategy, as it pushes to replace the traditional model of navigating software with one in which AI agents carry out complex tasks autonomously. By pairing those agents with new devices, powerful PCs and its own models, Microsoft is trying to control more of the end-to-end AI system — called a stack — and lock in enterprise customers, as competition from rivals ​OpenAI and Anthropic intensifies.\n\nThe Reuters Iran Briefing newsletter keeps you informed with the latest developments and analysis of the Iran war. Sign up here.\n\nAdvertisement · Scroll to continue [...] The goal is to make OpenClaw — which has gained popularity in China and helped rival Apple sell Mac computers — safe for businesses to use on computers with sensitive corporate data. On stage during a demo, executives showed how a corporate IT department could prevent users from inadvertently ​deleting all the files on their desktops.\n\n\"You can ​totally run OpenClaw inside your company now,\" ⁠Peter Steinberger, the software engineer who created OpenClaw, said on stage.\n\n## NEW AGENTS, AI MODELS\n\nMicrosoft said it would introduce a new AI agent called Scout within its Copilot software that can carry out tasks such as gathering emails or messages that require decisions by the user to move forward.\n\nAdvertisement · Scroll to continue\n\nReport AdImage 7 [...] Advertisement · Scroll to continue\n\nMicrosoft showed off a new computer called the Surface RTX Spark Dev Box loaded with an Nvidia (NVDA.O), opens new tab chip. CEO Satya Nadella said the computer was ​a \"dream machine\" and he was on the wait list to buy it.\n\nExecutives also revealed Project Solara, a family of prototypes that includes devices the ⁠size of a smart speaker or keycard badge, based on chips from Qualcomm (QCOM.O), opens new tab and MediaTek (2454.TW), opens new tab.\n\nThese devices have screens and microphones, but instead of running a traditional operating system and apps like a ​smartphone, they will host AI agents that talk to cloud-computing systems to carry out specific tasks, such as documenting a medical visit with a nurse.\n\nAdvertisement · Scroll to continue",

      "title": "[PDF] Wealth.AI - Capgemini",
      "content": "across key markets.41 • Raymond James pursued a targeted acquisition of OakWood Financial Services in March 2026 to expand its advisor base and specialist capabilities in estate planning, tax structuring, and retirement planning; these services remain central to HNWI relationships, particularly for intergenerational wealth transfer.42 A common pattern appears across both financial and non-financial sectors: expanding services broadens the client relationship and enhances loyalty, but realizing their full value requires proper coordination and management of the entire client experience. Relationship managers need the right skills, tools, and organized workflows to oversee the full customer journey. RMs are key players in operating model redesign, and their role is crucial in delivering [...] The demand signal from RMs is clear. More than three-quarters (76%) want digital and AI-enabled systems that automate routine work and provide proactive client insights; further, 61% want access to an integrated ecosystem of specialists across financial and non-financial services. These preferences highlight a fundamental challenge: as clients’ needs become more complex and service offerings expand, traditional advisory models struggle to deliver the personalized experiences that HNWIs expect. [...] investments 2. Supercharge RMs by shifting their focus and responsibilities from execution to orchestration, providing clients with the right expertise at the right moment.",

For more information, please see our Personnel and Job Candidate Privacy Notice at . [...] What you will own\n\nThree forcing functions converge in FY27. Regulators demand phishing-resistant credentials by default (NIST SP 800-63B Rev 4, EU PSD2, FedRAMP High). Attackers bypass passwords and SMS at industrial scale. AI agents need machine-verifiable credentials that humans can govern. The Senior PM in this role decides how Okta meets all three. Phishing-resistant authenticators: passkeys (synced and device-bound), FIDO2 WebAuthn, PIV smart cards, Yubikey OTP and FIDO. Non-phishing-resistant authenticators: SMS, voice, email magic link, security questions, password policy.\n\nA day in the life [...] Solve authorization for agents and phishing.\n\nSolve authorization for agents and phishing.\n\nRegister now\n\nRegister now\n\nSolve authorization for agents and phishing.\n\n+1 (800) 425-1267\n\nFree trial\n\nContact us\n\nContact us\n\nLogin\n\nQuestions?     {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/be/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "A financial services company is building agentic workflows to automatically capture meeting actions from video conferences, draft communications to remind participants of their next steps, and track follow-through.\n An air carrier is using AI agents to help customers complete the most common transactions, such as rebooking a flight or rerouting bags, freeing up human agents to address more complex matters.\n A manufacturer is using AI agents to support new product development initiatives, leveraging AI to find the optimal balance between competing objectives such as cost and time-to-market.\n In the public sector, AI agents are being used to cover workforce shortages, partnering with human workers to complete key processes. [...] Search and knowledge management\n Virtual assistants/chatbots\n Content generation\n\nAgentic AI: While agentic AI is expected to have the highest impact in customer support, use cases for supply chain management, R&D, knowledge management, and cybersecurity are also seen as having particularly high potential. The enterprises we interviewed are deploying autonomous AI agents across multiple functions: [...] Agentic AI: While agentic AI is expected to have the highest impact in customer support, use cases for supply chain management, R&D, knowledge management, and cybersecurity are also seen as having particularly high potential. The enterprises we interviewed are deploying autonomous AI agents across multiple functions:\n\nPhysical AI: Physical AI applications span a wide range of industrial and commercial settings. Common use cases for physical AI include:\n\n#### Governance, data, and regulation\n\n### How do I manage AI governance, data, and regulation?",
      "score": 0.5278769,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.okta.com/company/careers/okta-for-ai-agents/principal-forward-deployed-engineer-okta-for-ai-agents-7961339",
      "title": "Principal Forward Deployed Engineer - Okta for AI Agents | Okta",
      "content": "The annual base salary range for this position for candidates located in California (excluding San Francisco Bay Area), Colorado, Illinois, New York, and Washington is between:$240,000—$330,000 USD\n\nThe Okta Experience\n\n Supporting Your Well-Being\n Driving Social Impact\n Developing Talent and Fostering Connection + Community [...] About Okta for AI Agents\n\nOkta secures access for 20,000 organizations and billions of users. Okta for AI Agents extends that work to the agentic shift. Deploying an AI agent is not like deploying traditional software. You are putting professional work output into production, and it needs deep integration, continuous tuning, and change management. Every agent needs an identity, a scope, an audit trail, and a way to be shut down when it goes wrong. Most enterprises have not built this yet. We are.\n\nWe hire builders who see the cracks in enterprise agent identity that everyone else has learned to live with.\n\nThe Role [...] Okta is the leading independent provider of identity for the enterprise. The Okta Identity Cloud enables organizations to securely connect the right people to the right technologies at the right time. With over 7,000 pre-built integrations to applications and infrastructure providers, Okta customers can easily and securely use the best technologies for their business. More than 19,300 organizations, including JetBlue, Nordstrom, Slack, T-Mobile, Takeda, Teach for America, and Twilio, trust Okta to help protect the identities of their workforces and customers.\n\n###### Follow Okta\n\n Share on Twitter   Share on Linkedin   Share on Youtube\n\n### Apply",
      "score": 0.4256297,
      "raw_content": null
    },

      "content": "As VP of Product – Enterprise Agentic Compliance you will shape SAP's AI-first product direction and drive delivery of trusted, enterprise-ready agent-powered",

    {
      "url": "https://www.okta.com/company/careers/engineering/senior-software-engineer-ai-framework-services-7872569",
      "title": "Senior Software Engineer, AI Framework & Services - Okta",
      "content": "## Why You'll Love It Here\n\nThis is a team working at the edge of what identity means in a world of AI agents. You'll have real ownership of hard, unsolved problems, backed by Okta's platform, Auth0's developer relationships, and a team that values technical depth and customer impact in equal measure.\n\nYou'll help shape the product, not just implement a backlog. You'll work with people who care about getting the details right. And you'll be building something that matters to the developers and organizations trying to do AI responsibly.\n\n#LI-Hybrid  \nP24773\\_3426177 [...] Design and implement backend systems, APIs, and services that bring identity and authorization capabilities to AI agent workflows.\n Own features end-to-end, from technical design through production, with attention to reliability, security, and developer experience.\n Build integrations that allow agents to securely acquire, use, and scope access credentials in real-world environments.\n Contribute to system design decisions, make thoughtful technical tradeoffs, and communicate them clearly to peers and partners.\n Help define patterns and abstractions for problems the industry hasn't fully solved yet: agent identity, delegated access, trust boundaries across agentic systems. [...] ## Nice to Haves\n\n Experience with identity, authentication, or authorization systems (OAuth 2.0, OIDC, FAPI, token-based access control).\n Familiarity with AI agent frameworks, orchestration tools, or the Model Context Protocol (MCP).\n Exposure to agentic application patterns: tool use, multi-step workflows, agent-to-agent communication.\n Experience with containerized environments (Docker/Kubernetes) and cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or Azure).\n Prior work on developer-facing APIs, SDKs, or integrations where developer experience was a first-class concern.\n Contributions to open-source projects relevant to identity, security, or AI tooling.\n\n## Why You'll Love It Here",

    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/mw/en/services/legal/services/legal-implications-of-generative-ai.html",
      "title": "The legal implications of Generative AI | Legal | Deloitte Global",
      "content": "There are a number of key points that will likely be required to be addressed and understood — from liability to AI regulations.\n\n#### The path ahead\n\nWhile the competitive advantage of Generative AI is enticing, adoption of this powerful, differentiating technology demands attention to the risks that could imperil an enterprise’s brand, reputation, stakeholder trust, or critically, its compliance with legal and regulatory obligations.\n\n### The path ahead\n\nWhile the competitive advantage of Generative AI is enticing, adoption of this powerful, differentiating technology demands attention to the risks that could imperil an enterprise’s brand, reputation, stakeholder trust, or critically, its compliance with legal and regulatory obligations. [...] # The legal implications of Generative AI\n\n## In collaboration with the Deloitte AI Institute, considerations for intellectual property, data protection, contracts for corporations and Generative AI policy.\n\n## The current enthusiasm for AI adoption is being fuelled in part by the advent of Generative AI\n\nWhile definitions can vary, the EU AI Act defines Generative AI as \"foundation models used in AI systems specifically intended to generate, with varying levels of autonomy, content such as complex text, images, audio, or video.\" (Art. 28b (4) AI Act)\n\nAs businesses explore how to use these new tools, there are potential concerns for enterprise stakeholders, particularly legal and compliance professionals. [...] While the competitive advantage of Generative AI is enticing, adoption of this powerful, differentiating technology demands attention to the risks that could imperil an enterprise’s brand, reputation, stakeholder trust, or critically, its compliance with legal and regulatory obligations.\n\n### Our thinking\n\n### Generative AI: A guide for corporate legal departments\n\n### Generative AI and prompt engineering\n\n### 2025 Global Tax Policy Survey\n\n### Discover the future of contract management\n\nOpens in new window\n\n### Get in touch\n\nDonna Bartlett\n\n#### Donna Bartlett\n\nWillem-Jan Cosemans\n\n#### Willem-Jan Cosemans\n\nMatt Saunders\n\n#### Matt Saunders\n\nDr. Till Contzen\n\n#### Dr. Till Contzen\n\nMaria-Alexandra Papoutsi\n\n#### Maria-Alexandra Papoutsi\n\nPietro Boccaccini\n\n#### Pietro Boccaccini",

      "content": "Search plays a foundational role in this mission.     {
      "url": "https://www.okta.com/company/careers/ga/people-technology-manager-recruiting-tech-learning-platforms-7927644",
      "title": "People Technology Manager - Recruiting Tech & Learning Platforms | Okta",
      "content": "Data Integrity, Reporting & Insights: Establish and maintain data integrity standards, validation rules, and monitoring processes. Ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and consistency across systems. Partner with analytics teams to align on reporting frameworks and enable actionable insights.\n AI Enablement & Innovation: Leverage AI-enabled capabilities (e.g., candidate matching, chatbots, recommendations, content generation) to optimize recruiting and learning processes. Evaluate and implement AI-driven tools and features across systems. Identify high-impact use cases for AI to improve efficiency, personalization, and decision-making. Partner with stakeholders to drive responsible and scalable adoption of AI solutions. [...] At Okta, we view our people systems as critical infrastructure. In this role, you will design and manage a scalable, reliable ecosystem across our recruiting technology stack and Learning Management System (LMS), ensuring seamless workflows, high-quality data, and strong system performance globally.\n\nYou will operate at the intersection of systems architecture, automation, and user experience—building solutions that improve efficiency, enable better decision-making, and support global growth. This role also requires strong AI fluency, with a focus on leveraging AI-driven capabilities to enhance recruiting processes, learning experiences, and operational automation.\n\nWhat You'll Own [...] Deep expertise in recruiting systems (ATS, CRM, interview platforms) and Learning Management Systems (LMS)\n Strong experience in system configuration, architecture, and administration\n Proven ability to design and implement integrations using APIs, middleware, and data pipelines\n Experience leading system implementations and managing medium-to-large complexity technical initiatives\n Strong understanding of data models, reporting, and system governance\n Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot complex issues and perform root cause analysis\n Experience leveraging automation to improve operational efficiency\n AI fluency, including familiarity with AI/ML applications and AI-enabled features in recruiting and learning platforms",
      "score": 0.04075789,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/uk/en/services/consulting/analysis/beyond-accuracy-deloittes-journey-to-robust-genai-model-validation.html",
      "title": "Beyond Accuracy: Deloitte’s Journey to Robust GenAI Model Validation | Deloitte UK",
      "content": "Drawing on practical experience from a global financial institution’s GenAI validation journey, this publication shares how organisations can rethink validation to address risks that extend beyond model performance, including prompt injection, hidden functionality, hallucinated reasoning, semantic ambiguity, evolving system behaviour, and conversational inconsistency.\n\nThrough real-world case studies spanning AI Code Assistants, AI-powered Due Diligence platforms, Intelligent Email Classification, and Conversational Banking Assistants, the article demonstrates how seemingly high-performing GenAI systems can still introduce significant business, operational, and regulatory risks when deployed at enterprise scale.\n\nReaders will gain insights into how hidden model behaviours can lead to: [...] The publication also explores why GenAI validation must evolve from accuracy testing to behavioural assurance, combining technical testing with governance and control design to ensure AI systems operate within defined business, regulatory, and operational boundaries.\n\nRather than presenting a purely theoretical framework, this article offers a practical perspective grounded in real validation experience, highlighting how organisations can establish scalable and sustainable validation capabilities while strengthening trust, accountability, and resilience in GenAI adoption.\n\nDownload the full article to explore Deloitte’s perspective on building robust, risk-aligned GenAI model validation capabilities in an increasingly AI-driven       "title": "PEO Benefits | Offer benefits and stay compliant when you expand into new states",
      "content": "Want more exclusive business insights like this delivered to your inbox?Subscribe now\n\n### Get started with the nation’s largest PEO\n\nGet Pricing\n\nRelated resources\n\ntool\n\n## Prioritizing workplace health and safety as your employees return to work\n\nLearn more\n\nFAQ\n\n## Recruitment process outsourcing (RPO)\n\nLearn more\n\nanalyst report\n\n## NelsonHall: ADP Comprehensive HR and Comprehensive Benefits ROI and Impact\n\nRead now\n\nGet started with ADP\n\nWe'll be in touch shortly to discuss  \n your HR challenges.\n\nOr call us at 866-360-2454\n\nStay up to date\n\nGet exclusive business insights delivered straight to your inbox.\n\nYour privacy is assured.\n\nHi there, what brings you here today? [...] Registering your business through the state’s retirement savings portal (e.g., CalSavers in California, Illinois Secure Choice in Illinois)\n Submitting employee information (names, SSNs or tax IDs, contact details)\n Setting up payroll deductions at the state’s default contribution rate (often 5%, with automatic annual increases unless employees opt out)\n Uploading ongoing payroll files so employee contributions can be transmitted to the program\n Tracking employee eligibility and opt-outs [...] ### State auto-IRA multistate example\n\nOperating in multiple states makes compliance more complex. For instance, a business with employees in both California and Illinois would need to:\n\n Register separately with CalSavers and Illinois Secure Choice\n Track different enrollment deadlines based on employee headcount in each state\n Monitor separate penalty structures\n Upload payroll files to two distinct platforms\n Manage employee communications under two different state regulatory frameworks\n\nEach program operates independently, with its own administrative systems, timelines, and enforcement rules. For small businesses without dedicated HR staff, this can create additional oversight and payroll coordination requirements.\n\n## The fairness factor: ensuring benefits equity",

      "title": "20 tricks for more efficient Android messaging - Computerworld",
      "content": "## More from this author\n\n### 10 Android Circle to Search superpowers you probably never noticed\n\n### 8 critical questions about the Googlebook, Android, and ChromeOS\n\n### Give yourself an on-demand Android taskbar\n\n### 25 great uses for an old Android device\n\n### Android reminders, reinvented\n\n### The smartest ways to sync your Android and computer clipboards\n\n### How to create your own custom Android air gesture\n\n### Chrome, Vivaldi, and the challenge of changing browsers\n\n## Show me more\n\n### Open source Euro-Office productivity suite to launch June 9\n\nImage\n\n### Meta considers becoming a hyperscaler\n\nImage\n\n### AI hiring monoculture is delivering racial bias at scale\n\nImage\n\n### Microsoft Copilot Growth, ClaudeBleed Risk, LinkedIn GDPR Complaint | Ep. 84\n\nImage [...] Image\n\n### Chrome Gemini, AI Agents, CISA Infrastructure Cyber Resilience | Ep. 83\n\nImage\n\n### AI Triage Gains, Model Reviews, Ask Jeeves Shutdown | Ep. 82\n\nImage\n\n### AI is making business scams harder than ever to spot\n\nImage\n\n### Microsoft Copilot Growth, ClaudeBleed Risk, LinkedIn GDPR Complaint | Ep. 84\n\nImage\n\n### Chrome Gemini, AI Agents, CISA Infrastructure Cyber Resilience | Ep. 83\n\nImage\n\n### About\n\n### Policies\n\n### More\n\n### Our Network [...] A while back, a crafty reader alerted me to the fact that you could use Gemini’s Messages integration to effectively give yourself free in-flight Wi-Fi access even when you haven’t paid for the privilege. It’s a heck of a clever hack and a moment when you genuinely may want to activate Gemini within the Messages app, at least for a little while.\n\nI’ve got a complete breakdown of how it works and how you can put it to use on your next flight.\n\n## Android Messages trick #9: Calendar connection\n\nWhether from the sky or on the regular ol’ ground, the next time you’re working to plan a meeting or event with a fellow Homo sapien in Messages, make yourself a mental note of this:",

      "title": "Hole in GitHub's browser-based VSCode editor could lead to stolen ...",
      "content": "### GitHub scales back bug bounties, reminds users security is their responsibility too\n\n### GitHub shifts Copilot to usage-based billing, signaling a new cost model for enterprise AI tools\n\n## Other Sections\n\nHoward Solomon is a Toronto-based freelance reporter who writes on IT and cybersecurity issues.\n\nHoward is a former editor of IT World Canada and Computing Canada. An IT journalist over 30 years, he has also written for ITBusiness.ca and Computer Dealer News. Before that he was a staff reporter at the Calgary Herald and the Brampton (Ontario) Daily Times.\n\nHoward Solomon is a Toronto-based freelance reporter who writes on IT and cybersecurity issues. [...] Howard is a former editor of IT World Canada and Computing Canada. An IT journalist over 30 years, he has also written for ITBusiness.ca and Computer Dealer News. Before that he was a staff reporter at the Calgary Herald and the Brampton (Ontario) Daily Times.\n\n## More from this author\n\n### Lack of response to critical vulnerability in Gogs is a reminder of the limits of open source projects\n\n### 13 new critical holes in JavaScript sandbox allow execution of arbitrary code\n\n### More fake extensions linked to GlassWorm found in Open VSX code marketplace\n\n### Malicious pgserve, automagik developer tools found in npm registry\n\n### Anthropic employee error exposes Claude Code source\n\n### Crooks are hijacking and reselling AI infrastructure: Report [...] The problem, Askar says, is that this functionality is achieved by github.com POSTing over an OAuth token to github.dev that allows it to interact with GitHub on your behalf. “The token is not scoped to the particular repo you interacted with, meaning it has full access to every other repo that you have access to,” he wrote in the blog.\n\n“The presence of this token, and the fact that this web app is running almost the entire brunt of VSCode’s million line Typescript codebase, makes it a great target for anyone looking into VSCode bugs,” he wrote.\n\n## The exploit",
