    {
      "url": "https://www.servicenow.com/community/now-assist-forum/generating-a-formatted-knowledge-article-from-a-word-document/td-p/3553735",
      "title": "Generating a formatted Knowledge Article from a Wo... - ServiceNow Community",
      "content": "This seems quite straight-forward in Copilot/Gemini/Claude, but with Now Assist, it seems quite difficult.\n\nThanks!\n\n# \n\nMark Manders\n\nan hour ago\n\nAny reason why you aren't just importing the word document the old fashioned way? No AI required, so no cost, no changing of content, no nothing.\n\n# \n\nSwarnadeepNandy\n\n16m ago\n - last edited \n\n9m ago\n\nHi @Mark Manders ,\n\nThanks for you answer. [...] 9m ago\n\nHi @Mark Manders ,\n\nThanks for you answer.\n\nWe are currently doing that, but in the era of AI, we want to apply a particular layer of styling on the Knowledge Article content.  \nFor eg: We are applying a unified styling for all the Knowledge Articles for HR, with a particular header & sub-header styling and collapsible sections.  \n  \nSo, what we were trying is upload the document to Now Assist/LLM and as it to style according to some guidelines.  \n  \nThere are ofcourse manual way of doing it, but we are tying to leverage Gen AI to do this styling activity.\n\nKind Regards,  \nSwarnadeep Nandy\n\n# \n\nSwarnadeepNandy\n\n8m ago [...] The catch  \nBecause these are policy documents, the wording has to stay exactly the same after the transformation. We're not looking for the AI to summarize, rephrase, or \"improve\" the text — the legal/policy content must be preserved verbatim. What we want the AI to help with is the formatting and structuring (headings, sections, layout into the KB template), not rewriting the substance.  \nSo it's a bit different from the typical \"summarize this document\" or \"answer a question about this document\" use case. It's closer to: reproduce this content faithfully, but lay it out as a structured Knowledge article.\n\nLimitations we've run into  \nWhen using the Document Intelligence tool, the actions available seem to be limited to three:",
      "score": 0.081996456,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-ceynik4hp4r5y",
      "title": "AWS Marketplace: OutcomeOps AI Assist",
      "content": "Select video\n\n##### OutcomeOps as a Kiro Power\n\nOutcomeOps is a Context Engineering and Organizational Intelligence platform that turns an enterprise's own code, documents, and tickets into a queryable knowledge layer for AI workflows. Instead of asking developers and architects to copy-paste sensitive context into hosted AI tools, OutcomeOps brings the AI to the context: deployed via Terraform into the customer's own AWS account, powered by Amazon Bedrock, with every byte of source code, ADR, Confluence page, Jira ticket, SharePoint document, and Teams thread staying inside the customer's security perimeter. [...] Keywords: Context Engineering, Organizational Intelligence, AI for enterprise, code generation, code-map, AI code review, PR analysis, ADR, architecture decision records, runbook generation, migration planning, AWS Bedrock, Claude, single-tenant AI, data sovereignty, regulated industries, aerospace, defense, healthcare, financial services, Terraform, Confluence integration, Jira integration, SharePoint integration, Microsoft Teams integration, MCP, Model Context Protocol, RAG, retrieval augmented generation, source provenance, air-gapped AI, customer-owned infrastructure.\n\n### Highlights\n\n### Details\n\n### Introducing multi-product solutions\n\nYou can now purchase comprehensive solutions tailored to use cases and industries.\n\nMulti-product solutions\n\n## Features and programs [...] At the core of the platform are workspaces. A workspace connects repositories, documentation systems, and ticketing tools into a single shared context that teams query together. OutcomeOps automatically generates code-maps from connected repositories and keeps them current as code evolves, with first-class language support for Java, TypeScript, Python, C# / .NET, and ABAP. Code-maps describe applications running anywhere (AWS, Azure, GCP, on-premises, or SAP) so teams can analyze and plan migrations from any source environment, even when OutcomeOps itself runs only on AWS. MCP server support is opt-in per query, letting teams selectively bring in real-time tool context from their existing systems without expanding the platform's standing data access.",

      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "Artificial Intelligence (AI) Solutions for Business | Workday BE",
      "content": "Agentic HR\n\nDrive operational excellence by empowering your people, elevating managers and automating processes with AI built for HR.\n\nAgentic Finance\n\nGo from hindsight to insight with AI that surfaces exceptions, anticipates change and predicts the next best action.\n\nAgentic IT\n\nRealise ROI fast and deliver transformational outcomes by partnering with an AI platform driven by trust, agility and quality data you can always rely on.\n\nAgentic Legal\n\nTake control of your contract data with AI that gives you total visibility, so you can focus on shaping strategy.\n\n## Your people.     {
      "url": "https://markets.ft.com/data/announce/detail?dockey=600-202606040430BIZWIRE_USPRX____20260604_BW472500-1",
      "title": "Asana Unveils Operating System for Human-Agent Teams – Company Announcement - FT.com",
      "content": "“Many vendors are repositioning around AI agents, but layering agents onto coordination-centric systems does not address the core challenge of enterprise execution. As organizations move toward more dynamic, cross-system workflows, the need is shifting from coordination to Adaptive Work Orchestration, where humans and AI operate against shared context, with embedded governance and continuous visibility. Asana’s Agentic Work Management approach reflects this shift, combining a structured work graph with orchestration capabilities to support execution across people, systems, and intelligent agents at scale,” says Riana Barnard, Industry Analyst at Frost & Sullivan.\n\nCustomers adopting Agentic Work Management [...] With StackAI, acquired in May 2026, customers can extend Agentic Work Management beyond the Work Graph into the systems where work actually happens. AI Studio, AI Teammates, and StackAI together let customers orchestrate complex, multi-step workflows across CRMs, ERPs, collaboration tools, support systems, contracts, databases, and custom infrastructure — not just within Asana. Teams can plan work, coordinate humans and agents through one shared system, and execute across the enterprise with the context, governance, and handoffs production use demands.\n\nNew applications coming soon: the OS packaged for the teams that need it most\n\nAsana is packaging its operating system for the teams whose work is most dependent on coordinated execution. [...] \"For 18 years, Asana has solved one of the hardest problems in business: helping teams coordinate at scale across goals, decisions, and handoffs. The foundation we built - the Enterprise Work Graph, shared memory, multiplayer coordination, and governance - is precisely what the agentic era requires. Asana's OS is how AI moves from helping individuals work faster to supercharging entire organizations,\" said Dan Rogers, CEO of Asana.",
      "score": 0.49626526,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://venturebeat.com/technology/googles-new-open-source-gemma-4-12b-analyzes-audio-video-and-runs-entirely-locally-on-a-typical-16gb-enterprise-laptop",
      "title": "Google's new open source Gemma 4 12B analyzes audio, video — and runs entirely locally on a typical 16GB enterprise laptop - VentureBeat",
      "content": "Multimodal Autonomous Agent Workflows: If your engineering roadmap involves autonomous agents interacting with real-world inputs, Gemma 4 12B is uniquely positioned to serve as the reasoning engine. The combination of native function calling, robust coding capabilities, and the capacity to ingest real-time audio and variable-resolution images makes it highly suitable for agentic tasks. Google has simultaneously released a dedicated Gemma Skills Repository to explicitly support agentic development with these new models. [...] Furthermore, Gemma 4 12B includes a native \"thinking\" mode to map out step-by-step reasoning before generating a response. It also features out-of-the-box support for native function calling and system prompts, which are essential prerequisites for building highly capable autonomous software agents.\n\n## The Enterprise Verdict: Should You Adopt Gemma 4 12B?\n\nThe short answer is yes, provided your operational needs align with edge computing, strict data privacy, or agentic automation. However, adoption should not be a blanket replacement for all existing AI infrastructure. Instead, technical leaders should view Gemma 4 12B as a specialized tool optimized for specific deployment conditions. [...] Available immediately for download on Hugging Face and Kaggle and for use on Google AI Edge Gallery, Gemma 4 12B packs a 256K token context window, native agentic tool-use capabilities, and an explicit step-by-step reasoning mode into a highly optimized footprint that bridges the gap between mobile edge models and heavy data-center infrastructure.\n\n## The Architectural Shift: Understanding the Encoder-Free Advantage\n\nGemma 4 12B is highly relevant to enterprise architecture due to its novel \"Unified\" structure.\n\nTraditional multimodal systems typically utilize discrete, separate encoders to translate audio waveforms and visual data into representations that the core language model can process.",

      "raw_content": null
    },

      "title": "Documentación de flujos de trabajo  |  Workflows  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "Accede a más de 20 productos gratuitos para casos de uso comunes, incluidas APIs de IA, VMs, almacenes de datos\ny mucho más.\n\n## Recursos de documentación\n\n### Comenzar\n\nCrea e implementa un flujo de trabajo con la consola\n\nCrea e implementa un flujo de trabajo con gcloud CLI\n\nCrea e implementa un flujo de trabajo con Terraform\n\nEjecuta un flujo de trabajo con una biblioteca cliente\n\n### Reference\n\nReferencia de sintaxis\n\nReferencia de la biblioteca estándar\n\nAPI de REST de ejecuciones\n\nReferencia de conectores\n\n### Recursos\n\nNotas de la versión\n\nCuotas y límites\n\nPrecios\n\nObtén asistencia\n\n## Recursos relacionados\n\n### Ejecuta una traducción por lotes con el conector de Cloud Translation [...] Realiza una iteración paralela con bucles for.\n\n### Espera eventos con devoluciones de llamada\n\nAlmacenar los detalles de la devolución de llamada en una base de datos de Firestore\n\n### Pasos para volver a intentarlo\n\nSe reintentan los pasos con una política de reintentos predeterminada.\n\n## Videos relacionados\n\nSalvo que se indique lo contrario, el contenido de esta página está sujeto a la licencia Atribución 4.0 de Creative Commons, y los ejemplos de código están sujetos a la licencia Apache 2.0. Para obtener más información, consulta las políticas del sitio de Google Developers. Java es una marca registrada de Oracle o sus afiliados.\n\nÚltima actualización: 2026-06-03 (UTC)\n\n### Productos y precios\n\n### Asistencia\n\n### Recursos\n\n### Interactúa [...] Google Cloud Documentation\n\nGoogle Cloud Documentation\n\n# Documentación de flujos de trabajo\n\nPuedes usar Workflows para ejecutar servicios en el orden que definas: un flujo de trabajo.\nCombina la potencia de los servicios de Google Cloud , los servicios personalizados alojados en Cloud Run Functions o Cloud Run, y cualquier API basada en HTTP para crear aplicaciones flexibles sin servidores.\nWorkflows no requiere administración de infraestructura y se ajusta a la demanda sin interrupciones, incluida la reducción vertical a cero.\n\nVisita la página del producto de Workflows para obtener más información.\n\n#### Comienza tu prueba de concepto con un crédito gratis de $300\n\n#### Sigue explorando con más de 20 productos siempre gratuitos.",
      "score": 0.01729751,

    {
      "url": "https://docs.cloud.google.com/workflows/docs?hl=it",
      "title": "Documentazione di Workflows  |  Google Cloud Documentation",
      "content": "Accedi a oltre 20 prodotti gratuiti per casi d'uso comuni, tra cui API AI, VM, data warehouse\ne altro ancora.\n\n## Risorse di documentazione\n\n### Inizia\n\nCrea e implementa un flusso di lavoro utilizzando la console\n\nCrea ed esegui il deployment di un flusso di lavoro utilizzando gcloud CLI\n\nCrea ed esegui il deployment di un workflow utilizzando Terraform\n\nEsegui un flusso di lavoro utilizzando una libreria client\n\n### Riferimento\n\nRiferimento per la sintassi\n\nRiferimento libreria standard\n\nAPI REST Executions\n\nRiferimento connettori\n\n### Risorse\n\nNote di rilascio\n\nQuote e limiti\n\nPrezzi\n\nAssistenza\n\n## Risorse correlate\n\n### Esegui una traduzione batch utilizzando il connettore Cloud Translation [...] Esegui l'iterazione parallela utilizzando i cicli for.\n\n### Attendi gli eventi utilizzando i callback\n\nMemorizza i dettagli del callback in un database Firestore.\n\n### Procedura per riprovare\n\nRiprova i passaggi utilizzando una policy di ripetizione predefinita.\n\n## Video correlati\n\nSalvo quando diversamente specificato, i contenuti di questa pagina sono concessi in base alla licenza Creative Commons Attribution 4.0, mentre gli esempi di codice sono concessi in base alla licenza Apache 2.0. Java è un marchio registrato di Oracle e/o delle sue consociate.\n\nUltimo aggiornamento 2026-06-03 UTC.\n\n### Prodotti e prezzi\n\n### Assistenza\n\n### Risorse\n\n### Coinvolgi [...] ### Esegui un job Batch utilizzando Workflows\n\nScopri come utilizzare Workflows per pianificare ed eseguire un job Batch che esegue sei attività in parallelo su due VM di Compute Engine.\n\n### Carica i dati da Cloud Storage a BigQuery utilizzando Workflows\n\nScopri come eseguire flussi di lavoro serverless utilizzando Workflows, Cloud Functions e Firestore\nper caricare dati non elaborati, come i log eventi, da Cloud Storage a BigQuery.\n\n### Richiama Cloud Run\n\nEsegui un job Cloud Run che elabora i dati degli eventi salvati in Cloud Storage.\n\n### Utilizza un connettore\n\nEsegui una query su un set di dati BigQuery e scrivi i risultati in un foglio di lavoro Fogli utilizzando i connettori.\n\n### Iterazione in parallelo\n\nEsegui l'iterazione parallela utilizzando i cicli for.",

      "url": "https://aws.amazon.com/solutions/case-studies/personio-case-study",
      "title": "Personio Success Story",
      "content": "As generative AI gained momentum, Personio identified an opportunity to transform how HR teams handle day-to-day operational tasks. The software provider wanted to build an AI assistant that could simplify everyday work while meeting enterprise requirements for scale, flexibility, and data privacy. At the same time, the solution needed to support access to multiple foundation models (FMs) and keep pace with rapid changes in AI. [...] After implementing AI in its development workflows, Personio has increased its own teams’ productivity as well. “Since adopting AWS services for AI development, we’ve improved development velocity and response quality,” says Bannon. “This helps us confidently test new features and deliver results that customers can trust.”\n\nLooking ahead, Personio plans to expand its use of generative AI to develop specialized HR agents that can both provide insights and take action, extending these capabilities across more workflows. “Using AWS, we will build the next generation of our intelligent HR solution to help organizations focus on what matters: their people,” says Bannon.\n\nMissing alt text value\n\nAlex Bannon\n\n## AWS Services Used\n\n### Amazon Bedrock [...] Search\n\n# Building an AI HR assistant using Amazon Bedrock with Personio\n\nHR tech company Personio built an AI-powered assistant on Amazon Bedrock to automate requests and improve workflows.\n\n## Benefits\n\n## Overview\n\nAs demand for AI-driven experiences increased, human resources (HR) software provider Personio sought to simplify everyday tasks. The company took its first steps into generative AI by incorporating intelligent capabilities into its product to improve how HR teams access and manage employee data. Working alongside Amazon Web Services (AWS), Personio built an AI-powered assistant that handles 70 percent of routine HR requests.",

    },

      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/eu/sales/ai/generative-ai-for-sales",
      "title": "How Generative AI for Sales Will Improve Your Job | Salesforce EU",
      "content": "### Generating insight and analytics:\n\nThe Trends report found that 96% of sales pros agree data is essential to keep up with customer expectations. Generative AI analyses customer data so reps can prioritise the most promising leads. It also creates accurate sales forecasts by analysing historical sales data and identifying patterns and trends. With those deep insights into customer behaviour, preferences, and purchasing patterns, sales reps can individualise their approach.\n\n### Personalised sales skills training: [...] ### 5. Speed up data analysis\n\nSales reps can spend huge parts of their day generating, pulling, and analysing data — and with so much to look at, it’s easy to miss big-win deal opportunities. Generative AI acts like a personal data analyst to uncover patterns and relationships in your CRM data almost instantaneously, pointing you towards high-value deals and those likely to close. Even better, it adapts to changing dynamics and customer information in real time, helping you modify your sales approach to make it more effective.\n\n### 6. Analyse lead scoring and prioritisation [...] ### Creating close plans:\n\nClosing can be a challenging part of the sales process: the Trends report says 79% of sales teams agree that personalisation is difficult to execute at scale. Generative AI solves that, creating an action plan tailored for your customer and your sales process. It can also provide a bulleted summary of calls and identify customer sentiment, so you have better context for moving deals to close.\n\n### Generating insight and analytics:",

    {
      "url": "https://jobs.sap.com/job/Prague-5-Senior-Data-Scientist-%28Generative-AI-&-LLM-Systems%29-158-00/1371429333",
      "title": "Senior Data Scientist (Generative AI & LLM Systems) Job Details | SAP",
      "content": "SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose-driven and future-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best. [...] We win with inclusion  \nSAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world. [...] What you`ll do:\n\nWe are seeking a Data Scientist focused on AI integration to help embed machine learning and generative AI capabilities directly into our web delivery platforms. This role bridges data science, software engineering, and product delivery, translating AI opportunities into scalable features used by real customers. The ideal candidate combines strong machine learning expertise with practical engineering skills and can collaborate closely with web developers, product owners, and platform teams to deploy AI-driven capabilities into production environments.  This is a hands-on role.",
      "score": 0.35871297,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/mw/en/services/legal/services/legal-implications-of-generative-ai.html",
      "title": "The legal implications of Generative AI | Legal | Deloitte Global",
      "content": "### Personal data and confidentiality\n\nGenerative AI systems both ingest and generate large amounts of data, including images, text, speech, video, code, business plans, and technical formulae. Training, testing, uploading, analysing, consulting, or otherwise processing such input and output data requires various levels of protection.\n\nIn our report, we turn to some of the main challenges organisations face when using personal and confidential data in Generative AI systems, as well as the measures they could adopt to mitigate the relevant legal risks. [...] #### Personal data and confidentiality\n\nGenerative AI systems both ingest and generate large amounts of data, including images, text, speech, video, code, business plans, and technical formulae. Training, testing, uploading, analysing, consulting, or otherwise processing such input and output data requires various levels of protection. In our report, we turn to some of the main challenges organisations face when using personal and confidential data in Generative AI systems, as well as the measures they could adopt to mitigate the relevant legal risks.\n\n### Personal data and confidentiality [...] Across jurisdictions, there are several common personal data principles and protections that are highly affected by Generative AI systems. When using Generative AI, organisations should pay specific attention to the following aspects of the solutions they use: transparency, data minimisation, lawfulness, sensitive data, and individual rights.\n\n#### Contractual terms\n\nGiven the legal risks associated with the use of Generative AI in a business context, when licensing or otherwise entering into a contract that relates to a Generative AI solution, careful consideration to the terms of the contract under which the solution is procured is important. There are a number of key points that will likely be required to be addressed and understood—from liability to AI regulations.",
      "score": 0.30837816,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/mw/en/about/people/profiles.pboccaccini+eea08663.html",
      "title": "Pietro Boccaccini | Director | Milano",

    {
      "url": "https://www.deloitte.com/be/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html",
      "title": "The State of AI in the Enterprise - 2026 AI report | Deloitte Belgium",
      "content": "Collection\n\nmaking an impact that matters\n\n### Our History\n\nAbout Us\n\ntwo girls smiling with books in hand\n\n### World Impact\n\nCollection\n\nmaking an impact that matters\n\n### Our History\n\nAbout Us\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\nAbout Us\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nService\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\nAbout Us\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nService\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\nAbout Us\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nService\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\nAbout Us\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nService [...] One-third (34%) of surveyed organisations are starting to use AI to deeply transform—creating new products and services or reinventing core processes or business models. Another third (30%) are redesigning key processes around AI. The remaining third (37%) are using AI at a more surface level, with little or no change to existing processes. While each are capturing productivity and efficiency gains, only the first group are truly reimagining their businesses rather than optimising what already exists.\n\nAdditionally, different types of AI technologies yield different expectations for impact.  \nGenerative AI (GenAI): The areas of GenAI that leaders believe will have the most impactful effects on their industries are: [...] ### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nService\n\nA person is lying on their stomach on the wooden deck\n\n### 2025 Impact Report\n\nAbout Us\n\n### Empowering Generative AI through trust\n\nService\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\n### TMT Predictions 2026\n\nGreen circle with an equal sign in the center\n\n### Inclusion at Deloitte\n\nCommitment to society\n\n### Deloitte’s commitment to society\n\nAbout Us\n\nGreen circle with an equal sign in the center\n\n### Inclusion at Deloitte\n\nCommitment to society\n\n### Deloitte’s commitment to society\n\nAbout Us\n\nGreen circle with an equal sign in the center\n\n### Inclusion at Deloitte\n\nCommitment to society\n\n### Deloitte’s commitment to society\n\nAbout Us",

    {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/ca/retail/retail-ai-agents",
      "title": "Retail AI Agents: Your Complete Guide | Salesforce CA",
      "content": "## What is the difference between AI agents and generative AI?\n\nAs technology continues to advance and consumer expectations evolve, retail AI agents and generative AI are becoming essential tools for retailers. While both involve the use of algorithms and data to create intelligent systems, they differ in their approach and purpose.\n\nGenerative AI can create new content or generate new outputs based on a set of rules or training data. This type of AI is commonly used by retailers to create marketing content and respond to customer service queries. [...] AI agents, also known as intelligent agents, are software programmes that can observe their environment, make decisions, and take actions to achieve a specific goal. These agents are designed to act autonomously and adapt to changing situations. Examples of AI agents include chatbots and virtual personal assistants.\n\nOne key difference between AI agents and generative AI is their purpose. AI agents are created with a specific goal in mind, such as completing a task or providing assistance, while generative AI is focussed on exploration and creation. Another difference is their level of autonomy. AI agents are typically designed to act on their own, while generative AI systems require human input to function.\n\n## What is a retail AI agent? [...] ## Slack\n\nBring your people, agents data, apps and Salesforce solutions to where work happens in Slack.\n\n## Small Business\n\nFind more customers, win their business, and keep them happy with tools that help you work smarter, not harder.\n\n## Data\n\nBring together, understand, and act on all your data, from any source.\n\n## Agentforce 360 Platform\n\nBuild and customize your Agentforce and Customer 360 with the Agentforce 360 Platform.\n\n## Net Zero\n\nSustainability, simplified with Agentforce.\n\n## Customer Success\n\nReach your goals with our customer success experts.\n\n## Partner Apps & Experts\n\nGet the most out of Salesforce with partners, agents, apps, and consultants.\n\n###### Discover the #1 AI CRM\n\n###### Discover the #1 AI CRM\n\n## Industries\n\n## Automotive\n\nDrive a lifetime of loyalty.",
      "score": 0.16210158,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/agentforce/operations/process-intelligence",
      "title": "What Is Process Intelligence? | Salesforce",
      "content": "Salesforce is the world’s #1 AI-powered CRM provider. See how Agentforce can streamline multiple aspects of your business and request a demo\n\n today.\n\n## Fundamentals of Agentic AI\n\nA flat vector illustration of a robotic arm shaking hands with a human hand in a suit sleeve, set against a purple background with white clouds.\n\n### What is Agentic AI?\n\nA digital illustration of a tablet screen showing a chat interface between a human user and a friendly AI robot character.\n\n### Agentic AI vs. Generative AI\n\nA digital vector illustration of a tablet displaying a chat interface with message bubbles between a human avatar and a small, friendly AI robot.\n\n### What are Autonomous Agents? [...] ### How do AI agents work with process intelligence?\n\nAI agents use process intelligence to understand and execute complex business workflows. By analyzing real-time data and system logs, agentive AI can gain context to effectively automate tasks and make informed decisions. [...] ### Process intelligence vs business intelligence\n\nWhat’s the difference between process intelligence and business intelligence (BI)? Business intelligence generally focuses on reporting metrics and dashboards. Process intelligence reveals how workflows are actually operating across systems.\n\nEssentially, BI tells you what is happening across an organization using high-level, static, and historical data, while process intelligence tells you how and why specific operational processes are happening with real-time, dynamic data. Effective process intelligence connects operational flow to automation and AI-driven action.\n\n### The digital twin of the organization",
      "score": 0.072564535,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.salesforce.com/agentforce/operations/process-orchestration",
      "title": "What Is Process Orchestration? - Salesforce",
      "content": "## Why Agentforce for Process Orchestration\n\nAgentforce provides the advantage of a completely unified platform, tying together AI agents, workflow, and CRM. It features proven, embedded intelligence, enterprise-grade governance, and seamless CRM integration with multiple systems. See how process orchestration with Agentforce can improve your team’s workflow and performance.\n\nRequest a personalized demo\n\n!\n\n## Fundamentals of Agentic AI\n\nA flat vector illustration of a robotic arm shaking hands with a human hand in a suit sleeve, set against a purple background with white clouds.\n\n### What is Agentic AI?\n\nA digital illustration of a tablet screen showing a chat interface between a human user and a friendly AI robot character.\n\n### Agentic AI vs. Generative AI [...] ### Large Language Models in Orchestration\n\nLLMs work by interpreting unstructured input (such as raw text) by breaking it into small, numerical pieces called tokens, assigning each token a numerical vector, and using “self-attention” mechanisms to understand relationships between words. This process transforms chaotic text into mathematical patterns to predict the most likely next word.\n\nThis capability can be used for functions such as summarization of long or complicated reports, meeting transcripts, and more, as well as drafting documents or outlines within workflows.\n\nLLMs act in this fashion as support players in our “orchestra” analogy rather than the conductor or composer. Read more about large language models vs generative AI.\n\n## Use Cases Across Industries [...] ### Agentic AI vs. Generative AI\n\nA digital vector illustration of a tablet displaying a chat interface with message bubbles between a human avatar and a small, friendly AI robot.\n\n### What are Autonomous Agents?\n\nA flat vector illustration of a dark blue tablet screen showing a chat conversation between a person's avatar and a friendly purple AI robot.\n\n### What are Agentic Workflows?\n\n## Ready to take the next step with Agentforce?\n\n### Build agents fast.\n\nTake a closer look at how agent building works in our library.\n\n### Get expert guidance.\n\nLaunch Agentforce with speed, confidence, and ROI you can measure.\n\n### Talk to a rep.\n\nTell us about your business needs, and we’ll help you find answers.\n\n## Process Orchestration FAQs\n\n### What is process orchestration?",

      "url": "https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/prodview-skmyfqopi7oyu",
      "title": "AWS Marketplace: SPACE LENS: AI-Powered Video Intelligence & Auto-Analysis Solution",
      "content": "Listing Thumbnail\n\n# SPACE LENS: AI-Powered Video Intelligence & Auto-Analysis Solution\n\n## Overview\n\nOpen image\n\nExpand\n\n## GenAIIC\n\nSPACE LENS, AI-Driven Intelligence for Every Frame and Word\n\nPreviously, traditional video analysis workflows required teams to manually review hours of footage and independently evaluate which AI engine best suited their analytical needs. SPACE LENS significantly reduces this complexity.\n\nSPACE LENS is an advanced video intelligence solution built to transform how organizations analyze and extract value from video content.\n\nNow, when a user simply enters a query, the AI autonomously determines the optimal analysis path, whether Visual-centric, Audio-centric, or Comprehensive, ensuring the most efficient results without manual intervention. [...] ### Delivery details\n\n##### Software as a Service (SaaS)\n\nSaaS delivers cloud-based software applications directly to customers over the internet. You can access these applications through a subscription model. You will pay recurring monthly usage fees through your AWS bill, while AWS handles deployment and infrastructure management, ensuring scalability, reliability, and seamless integration with other AWS services.\n\n## Support\n\n### Vendor support\n\nSUPPORT CHANNELS\n\nSERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT\n\n### AWS infrastructure support [...] Co-innovated by MegazoneCloud and the AWS GenAIIC Partner Agent Factory (PAF) Link initiative, SPACE LENS is built on deep technical insights and a reusable asset (Recorded Voice Insight Extraction Webapp) from GenAIIC, including foundational components of Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Bedrock AgentCore (Runtime, Memory, and Gateway), and the Strands Agents SDK (Agents, Steering, and Evaluation). Through the PAF initiative, the GenAIIC team provided immersive expert guidance to ensure successful business validation, the development of enterprise-ready AI agents, and a seamless path to production.",

      "title": "Human Capital Real Insights",
      "content": "## Subscribe to Human Capital Real Insights: Delivering in today's world of work\n\nSubscribe to Human Capital Real Insights for access to research, analysis, and events to help you gain strategic insight and position your enterprise for a competitive advantage.\n\n## Meet our team\n\n### \n\nKPMG Human Capital Advisory Services provides innovative, multi-faceted people solutions to help organizations navigate the challenges they face today. Work with us to ensure your workforce is future ready\n\nImage of Katie Dahler\nImage of Evan Metter\nImage of John Doel\nImage of Leila Shin\nImage of Dimitris Papageorgiou\nImage of Katie Dahler\nImage of Evan Metter\nImage of John Doel\nImage of Leila Shin\nImage of Dimitris Papageorgiou\n\nThank you\n\n## Subscribe to Human Capital Real Insights [...] KPMG’s Human Capital Real Insights program provides access to research, analysis, and events to help you gain strategic insight and position your enterprise for competitive advantage.\n\nOur insights can help you with today's HR and Workforce Transformation imperatives:\n\n## Human Capital Advisory Services\n\nTo become future ready, enterprises must reinvent the world of work and empower an ever-evolving workforce.\n\n## Payroll at the tipping point: The case for C-suite elevation\n\nPayroll often accounts for 40–60% of operating expenses, yet many organizations have not modernized how it is governed or led.\n\n## Human capital advisory client stories\n\nExplore how KPMG has clients navigate the evolving workplace, manage talents, and position HR as a source of innovation. [...] ## How We Work\n\nWe bring together passionate problem-solvers, innovative technologies, and full-service capabilities to create opportunity with every insight.\n\n## Careers & Culture\n\nWhat is culture? Culture is how we do things around here. It is the combination of a predominant mindset, actions (both big and small) that we all commit to every day, and the underlying processes, programs and systems supporting how work gets done.\n\n###### Relevant Results\n\n###### \n\n# Human Capital Real Insights\n\n## At KPMG, we work with clients to transform their business\n\nBy organizing work more effectively, identifying emerging capabilities such as AI, and attracting, developing and retaining their top talent, we work with clients to transform their business.",

  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/intelligent-enterprise.html",
      "title": "Intelligent enterprise: Connect AI, strategy and operations | PwC",
      "content": "An AI operating model defines how AI systems, workflows, people, and business decisions function together across the enterprise. It establishes how workflows, how decisions move across functions, and how humans and AI interact at scale.\n\nGovernance is the accountability layer within that system. It defines decision rights, delegation boundaries, escalation paths, and the controls needed to ensure AI-driven actions remain reliable, auditable, and aligned to business intent. [...] AI governance is the accountability architecture that defines how AI systems make decisions across an enterprise. It establishes who holds authority, what autonomous systems are permitted to do, how risks are managed, and how outcomes remain explainable. As AI becomes part of how businesses operate, governance becomes the difference between AI that creates value and AI that creates exposure.\n\nStrategy sets direction, technology connects data and AI, operations execute decisions in real time, and governance establishes decision rights, accountability, and risk guardrails. Together, they operate as a single system, enabling aligned decisions, connected systems, and scalable AI performance across the enterprise. [...] Playback of this video is not currently available\n\n## Frequently asked questions\n\nAn intelligent enterprise connects strategy, technology, operations, and governance into one system, linking AI, data, and decisions across the business. Leading organizations aren’t just investing in AI; they’re redesigning how the enterprise works together.\n\nMany AI pilots fail to scale because organizations are under-architected, not under-invested. Data often stays siloed, decisions don’t flow across the business, and governance lags behind autonomy. Scaling AI is a design problem, not a technology problem.",
      "score": 0.4562407,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://aws.amazon.com/solutions/case-studies/personio-case-study",
      "title": "Personio Success Story - AWS",
      "content": "As generative AI gained momentum, Personio identified an opportunity to transform how HR teams handle day-to-day operational tasks. The software provider wanted to build an AI assistant that could simplify everyday work while meeting enterprise requirements for scale, flexibility, and data privacy. At the same time, the solution needed to support access to multiple foundation models (FMs) and keep pace with rapid changes in AI. [...] The solution reduced the time to manage HR reporting by 21 percent and day-to-day processes by 20 percent, freeing HR teams to focus on strategic, meaningful work. To meet data residency and sovereignty requirements, Personio stores data within the European Union, maintaining data protection standards under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model.\n\n## About Personio\n\nHR tech company Personio helps organizations across Europe manage employee data, streamline processes, and support day-to-day operations. Its AI-powered platform spans the entire employee life cycle, from core HR to talent and payroll.\n\n### Opportunity | Using AWS to build an AI HR assistant for Personio [...] Search\n\n# Building an AI HR assistant using Amazon Bedrock with Personio\n\nHR tech company Personio built an AI-powered assistant on Amazon Bedrock to automate requests and improve workflows.\n\n## Benefits\n\n## Overview\n\nAs demand for AI-driven experiences increased, human resources (HR) software provider Personio sought to simplify everyday tasks. The company took its first steps into generative AI by incorporating intelligent capabilities into its product to improve how HR teams access and manage employee data. Working alongside Amazon Web Services (AWS), Personio built an AI-powered assistant that handles 70 percent of routine HR requests.",
      "score": 0.4475333,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.pwc.com/us/en/intelligent-enterprise/building-the-intelligent-enterprise-age-of-ai.html",
      "title": "The Intelligent Enterprise in the age of AI - PwC",
      "content": "Operations is the backbone of an intelligent enterprise, where AI-based insights become tangible, shaping the flow of physical assets to improve execution. Rather than simply improving efficiency in areas like inventory and asset management, operations decisions also drive growth, by tapping into customer demand signals. Supply chains become more resilient and responsive, and operations leaders have a clearer sense of how decisions on the factory floor will flow through to the P&L. [...] hero image\n\n#### Key takeaways:\n\nIf you could design a business from scratch today, you’d take a different approach. You’d build it to run as one coherent system, starting with an employee or team, then using AI to help orchestrate how cloud, data, and application investments work together across the enterprise. Finance, technology, and operations are completely interconnected. Humans and AI agents collaborate as a new unified service delivery model. Security is embedded into each business unit, function, and product offering, and is built to evolve with the business. The result is a more connected enterprise where leaders can make faster decisions, respond to change with greater confidence, and create value at scale. [...] We call this the intelligent enterprise: where strategy, technology, operations, and governance run as one integrated system, with AI as the core, and managed services helping you run, adapt, and evolve as your business changes.\n\n## What the future enterprise looks like\n\nDesigning an intelligent enterprise entails more than just integrating systems. It also requires governance: clear accountability, trusted data, and the ability to make smarter, faster decisions to improve business performance.",
      "score": 0.38200366,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/sg/en/campaigns/you-can-with-ai/consumer-retail-and-leisure.html",
      "title": "AI in Consumer, Retail & Leisure - KPMG International",
      "content": "### AI Strategy & Value Realisation\n\n###### Identify high‑impact AI use cases across services, policy, risk, and operations\n\n###### Prioritise initiatives based on public value, feasibility, and risk\n\n###### Scale AI from pilots to secure, enterprise‑wide adoption\n\n### Trusted & Responsible AI (Public Trust by Design)\n\n###### Embed governance, security, and regulatory alignment into AI by design\n\n###### Manage model risk, data quality, privacy, and third‑party exposure, especially for sensitive public data\n\n###### Build confidence with boards and regulators through transparent, auditable AI decisions\n\n### AI Enablement for the Public Service Workforce\n\n###### Enable officers to use AI confidently in day to day roles, not as isolated specialists [...] Consumer, Retail & Leisure organisations are navigating rapid shifts in customer expectations, margin pressure, supply‑chain volatility and workforce disruption. Artificial intelligence (AI) is now central to how leading organisations respond, enabling smarter decisions, more personalised experiences, and resilient operations at scale. KPMG helps Consumer, Retail & Leisure leaders move beyond experimentation to embed AI responsibly across the enterprise, from strategy to execution.\n\nJames Wilson\n\nPartner​, Technology Consulting, Advisory and Head of Consumer & Retail\n\nKPMG in Singapore\n\n## Make AI work for you.\n\n##### Move from AI pilots to scalable AI solutions with the KPMG AI Centre of Excellence.\n\nAI CoE\n\n## The Government Sector value propositions through KPMG’s four-door Framework [...] ###### Redesign work and operating models so humans and AI collaborate effectively\n\n###### Build AI literacy and adoption across frontline, policy, risk, and corporate functions\n\n### Built to scale securely across government\n\n###### Build AI ready data and technology foundations aligned to government architectures\n\n###### Move beyond fragmented tools to scalable, secure enterprise platforms\n\n###### Enable AI to scale safely across agencies, functions, and channels\n\n## The Consumer, Retail & Leisure sector value propositions through KPMG’s Four-Door Framework\n\n### VALUE\n\nAI Strategy & Value Realisation\n\n### TRUST\n\nTrusted & Responsible AI (Trust by Design)\n\n### PEOPLE\n\nAI Enablement for the Consumer, Retail & Leisure Workforce\n\n### DATA & TECH",

      "content": "AI CoE\n\n## The Government Sector value propositions through KPMG’s four-door Framework\n\n### AI Strategy & Value Realisation\n\n###### Identify high‑impact AI use cases across services, policy, risk, and operations\n\n###### Prioritise initiatives based on public value, feasibility, and risk\n\n###### Scale AI from pilots to secure, enterprise‑wide adoption\n\n### Trusted & Responsible AI (Public Trust by Design)\n\n###### Embed governance, security, and regulatory alignment into AI by design\n\n###### Manage model risk, data quality, privacy, and third‑party exposure, especially for sensitive public data\n\n###### Build confidence with boards and regulators through transparent, auditable AI decisions\n\n### AI Enablement for the Public Service Workforce [...] ### TRUST\n\nTrusted & Responsible AI (Banking trust by design)\n\n### PEOPLE\n\nHuman‑AI ways of working (adoption is the multiplier)\n\n### DATA & TECH\n\nBuilt to scale (foundations that industrialise AI)\n\n## Discover more: KPMG’s insights on “AI in Banking”\n\n### Financial Services: KPMG Global tech report 2026\n\n### The future of risk in banking\n\n### Banking Strategic Benchmarking Insights Report 2025\n\nA comparative peer analysis and insight into the latest market trends\n\n### Transform your banking operations for a competitive future\n\nDriving modernisation to build agile, data-driven enterprises with KPMG and SAP\n\n## Start scaling AI with confidence today!\n\n#### AI in Banking\n\nExplore practical use cases, governance considerations, and scaling strategies for the public sector.     {
      "url": "https://kpmg.com/nl/en/services/advisory/technology-and-ai/data-and-ai/ai-workforce.html",
      "title": "AI Workforce",
      "content": "Identification of Opportunities\n\nContinuous identification of AI opportunities for employees, temporary workers, outsourcing models, shared services, and digital labor. Strategy, business case development, prioritization, and transformation roadmap.\n\nRedesign of Functions and Experience\n\nHuman-centered redesign of functions, roles, and departments to maximize the impact of AI. Identification of data, knowledge content, process impact, performance metrics, information security, and compliance considerations.\n\nEngagement and Adoption\n\nOur unique, human-centered approach accelerates the adoption of AI at both the business and functional levels and equips your employees with the necessary digital mindset, knowledge, and skills to leverage AI.\n\nLearning & development [...] ## KPMG AI Workforce\n\nUnlocking the value of AI requires a new approach, defined by identifying and improving tasks that were previously difficult to automate. Without a targeted approach and clear success criteria, it will be challenging for organizations to effectively transform and improve their workforce. To accelerate value, we recommend a human-centered approach consisting of three essential steps:\n\n### 1. Identify Opportnunities for Your Workforce\n\n### 2. Empower your Employees\n\n### 3. Redesign Your HR Approach and Operations\n\nWorkforce image showing 3 phases, Reshape, identify and augment\n\n## AI Workforce core areas\n\nOur KPMG AI Workforce approach consists of several core areas that lead to optimal adoption and value creation with AI within your organization: [...] # AI Workforce\n\nArtificial Intelligence plays a crucial role in modern organizations. It is a high-priority topic and is being applied in various ways. AI agents are being developed to support diverse functions and tasks, enabling employees to work more efficiently and streamline processes. The success of AI development and implementation heavily depends on the end users and their level of adoption. Unlike other technologies, AI requires a broader behavioral change, which demands a unique approach. How can we get the best out of our staff? Which tools and solutions are most effective for our employees? And how do we ensure that the provided opportunities are fully utilized?\n\n## KPMG AI Workforce",

      "content": "More\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nAmerica in motion\n\nExecutive leadership hub - What’s important to the C-suite?\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nTech Effect\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nMenu\n\nFeatured\n\nShared success benefits\n\nLoading Results\n\nNo Match Found [...] ### What is care engineering?\n\nCare engineering is a disciplined approach to analyzing, designing, and improving operational workflows to help drive efficiency, quality, and performance. Care engineering investigates how care is performed today, identifying bottlenecks and waste with the aim of redesigning processes to achieve the organization’s goals. This process uses data, insights from stakeholders, and leading practices to streamline operations, reduce costs, and improve outcomes and patient, caregiver, and employee experience.\n\nPwC, “A vision for 2035: The future of healthcare providers: Empowering care, elevating outcomes,” January 2026, .\n\nAAMC, “AAMC Report Reinforces Mounting Physician Shortage”, June 2021, [...] While systems can begin with focused pilots and disciplined baselining, these steps typically operate within the constraints of an existing care model. Real success can come from institutionalizing transparency, standardization, and governance across sites.\n\nInnovators redesign care pathways as scalable operating platforms, embedding real-time intelligence, dynamic staffing, and integrated decision logic into how the system runs. This shift often moves beyond benchmarking and incremental improvement toward structural cost redesign, positioning the organization to sustain margin performance under tightening reimbursement conditions.\n\n### What is care engineering?",

      "url": "https://www.accenture.com/us-en/careers/jobdetails?id=R00316273_en",
      "title": "Change Lead – People & HR Transformation",
      "content": "### Preferred Qualifications\n\n Experience in enterprise HR transformation or HR technology implementation\n Familiarity with AI-enabled HR tools (e.g., talent intelligence, automation, predictive analytics)\n Background in consulting or large-scale organizational change programs\n Certifications in change management (e.g., Prosci, CCMP)\n\n### About the Company / Team\n\nYou will join Accenture’s Talent & Organization team, working at the intersection of people, technology, and transformation. The team focuses on enabling workforce change, driving HR innovation, and accelerating AI adoption across organizations. You will collaborate with experts in HR strategy, AI innovation, and organizational change to help clients build a future-ready workforce. [...] #### Consulting jobs: Lead change\n\nIndustry understanding. Deep insights. Big ideas.       "title": "Accenture PLC, CSAX:GER summary - FT.com",
      "content": "Accenture plc is a global professional services company. It is engaged in providing a range of services and solutions across strategy and consulting, technology, operations, Industry X and Song. It serves clients and manages its business through three geographic markets: Americas, EMEA (Europe, Middle East and Africa) and Asia Pacific. It operates business processes on behalf of clients for specific enterprise functions, including finance and accounting, sourcing and procurement, supply chain, marketing and sales, and human resources, as well as industry-specific services, such as platform trust and safety, banking, insurance, network and health services. Its services include Cloud consulting services, customer services, cybersecurity consulting, data and artificial intelligence, digital [...] ## Will the IT consulting share price rout ever end?\n\nAccenture made a fortune from previous tech revolutions but investors think AI could kill it, not make it stronger\n\n## Europe’s Climate Leaders 2026: interactive listing\n\nAnnual list highlights companies making progress on cutting carbon emissions\n\n## How AI threatens the giants of consulting\n\nThe technology opens the door for smaller, well-funded challengers to take market share from the Big Four and others\n\n## Post Office replaces Fujitsu after Horizon scandal\n\nAccenture and OneView Commerce win £500mn contracts to run and create alternative to notorious IT system\n\n## Anthropic and Freshfields agree deal to create legal AI tools\n\nTech group will tap ‘magic circle’ law firm’s expertise as it builds products that can be sold to rivals [...] consulting, data and artificial intelligence, digital engineering and manufacturing, managed services, metaverse solutions, Infrastructure and Capital Projects, Finance consulting, Supply chain consulting, and others.",

      "content": "#### Technology jobs: Be the catalyst\n\nGet hands-on with the technologies that our clients need to reinvent, work in new ways and change the world for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n#### Jobs in technology platforms: Create foundations for the future\n\nYou’ll build the tech that transforms how business works for the better.\n\nLearn more\n\n## Learn more about the hiring process at Accenture\n\n Benefits information\n Hiring process (Q&A)\n Interview tips\n Using AI [...] California requires additional notifications for applicants and employees. If you are a California resident, live in or plan to work from Los Angeles County upon being hired for this position, please click here for additional important information.\n\nPlease read Accenture’s Recruiting and Hiring Statement for more information on how we process your data during the Recruiting and Hiring process.\n\nWe work with one shared purpose: to deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity. Every day, more than 775,000 of us help our stakeholders continuously reinvent. Together, we drive positive change and deliver value to our clients, partners, shareholders, communities, and each other. [...] We believe that delivering value requires innovation, and innovation thrives in an inclusive and diverse environment. We actively foster a workplace free from bias, where everyone feels a sense of belonging and is respected and empowered to do their best work.\n\nAt Accenture, we see well-being holistically, supporting our people’s physical, mental, and financial health. We also provide opportunities to keep skills relevant through certifications, learning, and diverse work experiences. We’re proud to be consistently recognized as one of the World’s Best Workplaces™.\n\nJoin Accenture to work at the heart of change.     {
      "url": "https://community.ibm.com/community/user/blogs/aliannah-muzaffar/2026/06/03/activating-content-for-the-era-of-agentic-automati",
      "title": "Activating Content for the Era of Agentic Automation - IBM Community",
      "content": "When content is governed, discoverable, and AI-ready, agents can help organizations automate repetitive tasks while allowing employees to focus on higher-value work. In financial services, this could mean automatically organizing and classifying incoming loan documentation before underwriting begins, while for insurance companies, claims documents could be routed correctly, with updated metadata as soon as new information arrives. In the public sector, agencies could manage large volumes of incoming forms and supporting documentation without creating additional administrative overhead.\n\nContent growth doesn’t have to mean storage growth [...] This month, IBM announced IBM Content Cortex (planned availability in June 2026), a next-generation content services system designed to prepare, govern, and activate enterprise content for agentic automation. Content Cortex represents a new paradigm for transforming content repositories from passive systems of record into trusted foundations for AI-powered business operations.\n\n## Coming Soon: IBM Content Cortex\n\nCentralize Content\n\nOrganizations can bring together fragmented enterprise content into a trusted source of truth for both employees and AI agents\n\nGovern Content\n\nSecurity, compliance, retention, and governance remain embedded throughout the content lifecycle, ensuring that content remains trusted and usable as AI adoption expands\n\nActivate Content [...] #### By Aliannah Muzaffar posted 8 hours ago\n\n# \n\nAI agents have already changed the way work is being done, but agents are only as effective as the content they access. Enterprises already possess enormous stores of business-critical knowledge in documents, reports, correspondence, statements, images, case files….the list goes on. Businesses have plenty of information, but the challenge is that content remains fragmented, difficult to govern, and challenging to activate for AI-driven work.",
      "score": 0.6369636,

    },

    {
      "url": "https://community.ibm.com/community/user/blogs/gunjan-ghangare/2026/06/03/ibm-bob-more-than-an-ai-coding-assistant",
      "title": "IBM Bob: More Than an AI Coding Assistant",
      "content": "IBM Bob helps make that process less intimidating.\n\nBy analyzing legacy codebases and assisting with modernization efforts, Bob can help organizations move from older technologies to modern architectures much faster than traditional approaches. What once took months of effort can potentially be reduced to days, allowing teams to focus on innovation instead of maintenance.\n\n## Working Like a Real Team Member\n\nOne of the most interesting aspects of IBM Bob is its agentic approach.\n\nThink about a typical software team. You have architects designing systems, developers building features, reviewers checking quality, and testers validating functionality.\n\nIBM Bob brings a similar mindset into the development environment. [...] IBM Bob understands this reality.\n\nBuilt as an AI-first development partner and agentic IDE, Bob works alongside developers, helping them navigate complex projects while keeping the bigger picture in mind. It understands project context, adapts to different workflows, and supports teams throughout the development process.\n\n## Giving Legacy Systems a New Life\n\nMany organizations still rely on applications written decades ago. Technologies like Java, COBOL, and RPG continue to power banks, healthcare systems, government services, and critical business operations around the world.\n\nModernizing these systems is often seen as a daunting task.\n\nIBM Bob helps make that process less intimidating. [...] IBM Bob addresses these concerns with built-in security checks, compliance-aware workflows, audit-ready reporting, and human oversight mechanisms. Developers remain in control while benefiting from AI-driven assistance.\n\nThis balance between innovation and responsibility is what makes enterprise AI adoption possible.\n\n## Choosing the Right AI for the Right Job\n\nNot every AI model is great at every task.\n\nIBM Bob recognizes this by intelligently routing requests to different models, including IBM Granite, Anthropic Claude, Mistral, and Llama.\n\nInstead of forcing developers to choose between models, Bob helps ensure the right model is used for the right task. This improves efficiency, reduces costs, and creates a smoother development experience.\n\n## Looking Ahead",
      "score": 0.37360206,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://community.ibm.com/community/user/discussion/who-is-accountable-when-an-ai-agent-acts-autonomously-and-the-framework-fails",
      "title": "Who Is Accountable When an AI Agent Acts Autonomously and the Framework Fails? | IBM Zero Trust Connect",
      "content": "The problem is that Zero Trust was built around human identity at a session boundary. Agentic AI systems do not authenticate that way. They act, delegate, spawn sub-tasks, inherit permissions, and disappear after completing an objective. None of the existing IAM, PAM, or network security control layers were designed to observe that behavior.\n\nAccording to Gravitee's 2026 State of AI Agent Security report, only 47.1% of deployed AI agents are actively monitored or secured. The Agentic Trust Framework, published through the Cloud Security Alliance in February 2026, exists specifically because the governance layer has not caught up to the infrastructure. [...] Annie Liu's profile image\n\n#### Office\n\nIf you need immediate assistance please contact the Community Management team\n\n  support@communitysite.ibm.com\n\n Monday - Friday: 8AM - 5 PM MT\n\n#### Community Links\n\nAbout Us\n\nTerms of Use\n\nCommunity Netiquette\n\nSitemap\n\nFAQ\n\n#### IBM Links\n\nIBM Training\n\nIBM Developer\n\nDocumentation\n\nGlobal message icon [...] The design question is no longer just who is requesting access. It is what is requesting access, under whose authority, with what documented oversight, and what the accountability chain looks like when that agent acts autonomously and the framework fails.\n\nWhere does the existing Zero Trust model hold in the context of agentic AI, and where does it need to be rebuilt?\n\n#### New Best Answer\n\n## Related Content\n\n#### Who Is Accountable When an AI Agent Acts Autonomously and the Framework Fails?\n\nIsabella Boggs's profile image\n\n#### Same Resolver. Smarter Policy.\n\nAnnie Liu's profile image\n\n#### The Identity Layer Your Network Stack Is Missing\n\nAnnie Liu's profile image\n\n#### From Network Trust to Identity‑First Connectivity: DNS as a Control Plane for Access\n\nAnnie Liu's profile image",

      "content": "“Attackers aren’t reinventing playbooks, they’re speeding them up with AI,” said Mark Hughes, Global Managing Partner for Cybersecurity Services at IBM, in a news release related to the report. “The core issue is the same: businesses are overwhelmed by software vulnerabilities. [...] In a LinkedIn post, IBM Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Arvind Krishna said the company applauds the administration’s focus on AI security and on the open-source software ecosystem that supports much of today’s digital infrastructure.\n\n“As AI becomes more foundational to all of us, including business and government, securing it — and the open source software our society depends on — is essential to maintaining trust and ensuring U.S. leadership,” Krishna wrote.\n\n## Industry welcomes security focus\n\nThe order arrives as governments and technology companies sharpen their focus on the security implications of frontier AI models, which can perform increasingly complex tasks and may play a larger role in protecting critical digital infrastructure.",

      "url": "https://research.ibm.com",
      "title": "IBM Research",
      "content": "News\n\n  Leron Gil, Sanskriti Deva, and Robert Davis\n\n  + Quantum Community\n ### Qiskit Global Summer School 2026: Registration now open\n\n  \n\n  News\n\n  Marina Caputo, Olivia Lanes, Marcel Pfaffhauser, and Robert Davis\n\n  + Qiskit\n  + Quantum Community\n Read more news on our blog\n\n## Tools + Code\n\n BeeAI Framework\n\n  ### BeeAI Framework\n\n  Open-source framework for building, deploying, and serving powerful agentic workflows at scale.\n\n  View project →\n Docling\n\n  ### Docling\n\n  An open-source tool designed to streamline document preparation for generative AI applications.\n\n  View project →\n Granite open-source models\n\n  ### Granite open-source models\n\n  A family of AI models engineered to ensure trust and scalability in AI-driven applications.\n\n  View project →\n Qiskit SDK [...] # The future of computing is now\n\n## Inventing What’s Next\n\nLearn more\n\nIBM Research is helping solve problems at the frontier of science with quantum-centric supercomputing.\n\n## Recent news\n\n ### Why IBM is investing $10 billion into quantum computing\n\n  \n\n  Explainer\n\n  IBM Quantum\n\n  + Quantum Enablement\n ### Renowned mathematician Subhash Khot joins IBM Research\n\n  News\n\n  Mike Murphy\n\n  + Mathematical Sciences\n  + Quantum Algorithms\n ### Ponder This Challenge - June 2026 - The Superhero Team Movies\n\n  \n\n  Puzzle\n\n  Gadi Aleksandrowicz\n\n  + Ponder This\n ### Ponder This: June Challenge\n\n  Puzzle\n ### New Classroom Accounts expand quantum access for educators\n\n  \n\n  News\n\n  Leron Gil, Sanskriti Deva, and Robert Davis [...] View project →\n Qiskit SDK\n\n  ### Qiskit SDK\n\n  Easily build and transpile circuits with 100+ qubits with the stable, high-performance Qiskit SDK.\n\n  View project →",

    {
      "url": "https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/04/asana-launches-ai-powered-products-help-organizations-manage-human-agent-work",
      "title": "Asana launches AI-powered products to help organizations manage ...",
      "content": "The company also said it’s expanding its portfolio with industry-specific AI teammates, which are prebuilt with domain knowledge. These include agents that are fine-tuned to support work in high-value industries such as manufacturing and retail.\n\nFor teams who work in specific coordinated environments, such as service management, product development and client management, Asana is packaging this new agentic operating system into specialized systems.\n\nAsana Service Management provides a self-learning knowledge base for information technology, human resources, facilities and other service teams by unifying ticketing and project execution. Asana can move from ticket to project without losing context, allowing for rapid request resolution. [...] Siliconangle\n\nSiliconangle\n\nUPDATED 04:30 EDT / JUNE 04 2026\n\n AI\n\n### Asana launches AI-powered products to help organizations manage human and agent work\n\nby Kyt Dotson\n\nAsana Inc. announced today during the company’s Work Innovation Summit in London the launch of a new product suite that helps organizations manage work by humans and artificial intelligence agents using the same plan.\n\nCalling the suite an operating system for human-agent teams, Asana unveiled Agentic Work Management. This new set of tools includes three key parts: a chief of AI staff for every Asana user, three new applications for teams and a new generation of AI teammates. [...] APPS - BY MIKE WHEATLEY . 2 HOURS AGO\n\nNvidia snaps up Kumo AI, a predictive AI startup known for its extreme accuracy\n\nAI - BY MIKE WHEATLEY . 5 HOURS AGO\n\nIn policy paper, OpenAI diverges from White House on AI safety\n\nAI - BY JAMES FARRELL . 7 HOURS AGO\n\nSpaceX hopes to raise $75B when world's biggest-ever IPO lifts off next week\n\nEMERGING TECH - BY MIKE WHEATLEY . 7 HOURS AGO\n\nObservability provider Coralogix nabs $200M investment\n\nAI - BY MARIA DEUTSCHER . 8 HOURS AGO\n\n##### CUBE EVENT COVERAGEhelp\\_outline\n\n##### LATEST FROM THECUBE\n\n##### Sign Up for Our Weekly Newsletter\n\n##### UPCOMING CUBE EVENTS\n\n##### RECENT CUBE EVENTS\n\n##### Snowflake Summit 2026\n\n##### Freshworks Refresh 2026\n\n##### IBM Think 2026\n\n##### Dell Technologies World 2026\n\n##### KB4-CON 2026",

Subscribe to follow.\n\nQuantcast\nQuantcast\n\n### Cookies [...] “What we have learned over time is that the machine user is outpacing the human user in consumption of analytics data,” Jajoo said. “The ML features, the feedback loops to production services or the AI agent workflows are outpacing the analytics user. When you do that, you cannot adopt a monolithic environment, which is holding you back and not letting you enable new use cases on top of it.” [...] With new Majorana 2 quantum chip, Microsoft claims dramatic breakthrough in qubit stability\n\nTrump signs scaled-back version of AI executive order\n\n##### LATEST STORIES\n\nGenerative AI music startup Suno AI raises over $400M at a $5.4B valuation\n\nAI - BY KYT DOTSON . 4 HOURS AGO\n\nWP Engine bolts bot management onto Global Edge Security as AI crawlers surge\n\nSECURITY - BY DUNCAN RILEY . 6 HOURS AGO\n\nQuobly raises $133M for its silicon spin qubit technology\n\nEMERGING TECH - BY MARIA DEUTSCHER . 14 HOURS AGO\n\nSema4.ai's autonomous agent-building platform gets simpler to use, adds deeper business context and more\n\nAI - BY MIKE WHEATLEY . 19 HOURS AGO\n\nWith new Majorana 2 quantum chip, Microsoft claims dramatic breakthrough in qubit stability\n\nEMERGING TECH - BY MIKE WHEATLEY . 20 HOURS AGO",

      "url": "https://www.ibm.com/think/insights/rethinking-readiness-how-enterprise-security-needs-to-plan-for-frontier-ai-models",
      "title": "How enterprise security needs to plan for frontier AI models | IBM",
      "content": "### Your weekly news podcast for cybersecurity pros\n\nWhether you're a builder, defender, business leader or simply want to stay secure in a connected world, you'll find timely updates and timeless principles in a lively, accessible format. New episodes on Wednesdays at 6am EST.\n\n## How frontier models map your vulnerabilities\n\nWith the advent of agentic attacks, what matters most is not just which vulnerabilities exist, but how machine‑intelligent systems perceive them. Frontier models can:\n\nThis situation is not hypothetical. Attackers are already using these capabilities.\n\n## Building a new taxonomy of AI driven enterprise risk and continuous monitoring\n\nIf organizations want to stay ahead, they must adopt assessments built for this new reality. [...] And that’s the leadership mindset enterprises need now: readiness is moving from reactive to architectural. From managing vulnerabilities to understanding systemic exposure and what it means to the business. From patch cycles to machine‑aware resilience.\n\nLearn more about this assessment by inquiring here\n\nWatch the webinar: The shift to autonomous security: How governed AI is redefining enterprise security\n\n## Author\n\nPartner & Offering Leader - Cyber Strategy & Risk\n\nIBM Consulting\n\nRegister for this webinar to learn how AI governance helps organizations manage risk, meet evolving regulations and build trusted, responsible AI at scale.\n\n## Resources [...] Access this Gartner guide to learn how to manage the complete AI inventory and secure your AI workloads with guardrails. It also shows how to reduce risk and manage the governance process to achieve AI trust for all AI use cases in your organization.\n\nFollow clear steps to complete tasks and learn how to effectively use technologies in your projects.\n\nIdentity and access management (IAM) is a cybersecurity discipline that deals with user access and resource permissions.\n\nProtect your most critical data—discover, monitor and secure sensitive information across environments while automating compliance and reducing risk.\n\nTransform your security program with solutions from the largest enterprise security provider.",
      "score": 0.17034262,
      "raw_content": null
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.ibm.com/careers/blog/once-an-ibmer-always-an-ibmer",
      "title": "Once an IBMer, Always an IBMer: Stay Connected, Stay Curious, Come Back Like an IBMer | IBM",
      "content": "HR Brazil PwD Campaign Photos\n\n## Staying Connected\n\nNot everyone wants to come back and that’s also completely okay. But staying connected can still be meaningful.  \nIt might look like:\n\nThere’s no one “right way” to stay connected. But our door is always open.  \nIf you are interested, stay connected on our LinkedIn page!  \nStay connected by joining our Talent Network and set up job alerts tailored to your interests.\n\nHere’s how to set up your personalized alerts:\n\nYou’ll start receiving job alerts to your email based on your chosen frequency.\n\n0_leadspace_hp-app-steps-and-tips\n\n## Rediscover your potential at IBM\n\nIBM was never meant to be just one step in your career. It’s something you build on, something you carry with you. And sometimes something you come back to. [...] Being like an IBMer isn’t tied to your email address or org chart. It’s about:\n\nIBM Alumni\n\n## Equal Opportunity Means Everyone, Including Alumni\n\nOne thing that doesn’t change: our commitment to fairness.\n\nAt IBM, equal opportunity isn’t limited to current employees. It applies to everyone - including alumni.  \nWhat that means in practice:\n\nWe’re always happy to welcome IBMers back. Because when you come back, you bring something different, something stronger.  \nYou return with:\n\nAnd at the same time, you don’t start from zero.  \nYou already understand:\n\nThe mix of familiarity and fresh perspective is incredibly powerful.\n\nHR Brazil PwD Campaign Photos\n\n## Staying Connected [...] # Once an IBMer, Always an IBMer: Stay Connected, Stay Curious, Come Back Like an IBMer\n\nA professionally dressed woman sits on a red bench in a contemporary office lobby. The background features a bright blue wall with a large illuminated abstract graphic design. She faces the camera with her hands resting on the bench, conveying a confident and relaxed posture. The space has a clean, modern aesthetic with bold color contrasts and minimal decor.\n\nCareers aren’t straight lines anymore, and they shouldn’t be.\n\nPeople grow, explore, take risks, change directions. Sometimes that means continuing your journey somewhere new. But what you build along the way does not just disappear.\n\nAnd at IBM, we truly believe that. Because as you might have heard already: “Once an IBMer, always an IBMer.",

    {
      "url": "https://www.bcg.com/publications/2026/ceos-need-to-shape-where-quantum-creates-value",
      "title": "CEOs Need to Shape Where Quantum Creates Value | BCG",
      "content": "Some examples of these projects include HSBC and IBM using quantum computing to improve automated bond trading; AstraZeneca, IonQ, AWS, and Nvidia simulating drug molecule synthesis; and Airbus, BMW, and Quantinuum modeling fuel cell efficiency. As more companies start to experiment with quantum, the enterprise spend is also increasing in depth: More than 60% of surveyed enterprises spend more than $1 million annually, an 11-percentage-point increase from our 2022 survey. The $1 million mark is an important milestone because our analysis showed that companies investing beyond this level are three times more likely to develop intellectual property (IP), twice as likely to be experimenting with AI, and twice as likely to be engaged in a public-private partnership. [...] © Boston Consulting Group 2026. All rights reserved.\n\nFor information or permission to reprint, please contact BCG at permissions@bcg.com. To find the latest BCG content and register to receive e-alerts on this topic or others, please visit bcg.com. Follow Boston Consulting Group on Facebook and X (formerly Twitter).\n\nSaved To My Saved Content\n\nStay ahead with BCG insights on digital, technology, and data   You are subscribed to BCG's eAlert on digital, technology, and data. Manage Subscriptions\n\nDownload Article\n\nShare\n\nSaved To My Saved Content   Download Article [...] The spending patterns that have emerged reflect an increasing sophistication in the marketplace. (See Exhibit 3.) Between 2022 and 2024, enterprises increased the proportion of their spending on algorithm and software development from 21% to 40%, targeting algorithm innovation rather than simply running test codes on prototype machines. In addition, IBM reports that its users programmed 10 times more qubits in 2024 than in 2022—signaling a shift toward more complex, application-scale use cases. As we will discuss later in the article, this shift will be a critical enabler to bridge the potential value gap.\n\nWeekly Insights Subscription\n\nStay ahead with BCG insights on digital, technology, and data\n\n## Technical Roadmaps Accelerate Across the Board",
      "score": 0.024574842,
      "raw_content": null
    },
